solution: If the app needs to work cross-tenant: change signInAudience on the app registration in the home tenant to 'AzureADMultipleOrgs' or 'AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount'. If the customer doesn't own the app, contact the app owner to update signInAudience. If single-tenant is intended, no action needed - external SPs will error. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/howto-convert-app-to-be-multi-tenant
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Failed to add identifier URI error: 'All newly added URIs must contain a tenant verified domain, tenant ID, or appId, as per tenant policy.' Occurs when updating Application ID URI (identifierUris) in App Registration > Expose an API blade.
rootCause: Identifier URI protection enforcement (deployed May 19, 2025) blocks non-default identifier URIs to prevent impersonation attacks. The tenant's defaultAppManagementPolicy has uriAdditionWithoutUniqueTenantIdentifier or nonDefaultUriAddition restriction enabled.
solution: Options: (1) Use default URI format: api://{appId} or api://{tenantId}/{appId}. (2) Configure app to receive v2 tokens (set requestedAccessTokenVersion=2, but cannot revert to v1 afterwards). (3) Use SAML protocol for SSO. (4) Grant per-app exemption using appManagementPolicies API or GrantAppExemption.ps1 script. (5) Grant caller exemption using GrantCallerExemption.ps1. (6) Admin can disable protection entirely (not recommended) using DisableIdentifierUriProtection.ps1. Reference: https://aka.ms/identifier-uri-formatting-error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: SignInAudience value 'AzureADMultipleOrgs' not allowed as per assigned policy. Error when creating or updating app registration with multi-tenant audience in Azure portal.
rootCause: Tenant admin has configured app management policy (defaultAppManagementPolicy or custom appManagementPolicy) with audience restriction that blocks creation of multi-tenant apps. The 'allowedAudience' restriction type disallows the requested signInAudience value.
solution: Contact tenant admin to either: (1) Update the policy to allow the desired signInAudience value. (2) Create a custom appManagementPolicy with the audience restriction disabled and assign it to the specific app. (3) Use single-tenant (AzureADMyOrg) if multi-tenant is not needed. Policy API: PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/policies/defaultAppManagementPolicy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: AADSTS70001: Application '{GUID}' is disabled. User cannot sign in to an application that was previously working.
rootCause: Microsoft Identity Protection has detected the application as malicious (consent phishing, suspicious activity, or violation of services agreement) and globally disabled the Application and Service Principal objects by setting 'disabledByMicrosoftStatus' to 'DisabledDueToViolationOfServicesAgreement'.
solution: 1. Check the 'disabledByMicrosoftStatus' property via Microsoft Graph GET on the application or service principal. 2. Check Audit Logs for 'Update Application/Update service principal' activity initiated by 'Azure Active Directory Protection Service'. 3. If customer believes this is a false positive, file an IcM: Team: Identity Protection Service, Path: Identity Reputation Management and Abuse Prevention\Triage. Template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=B203t1. Note: Disabled apps cannot be deleted by admin or Microsoft Support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD Malicious OAuth App Disablement

symptom: Application cannot access protected resources or obtain new access tokens. Enterprise app shows state as disabled/deactivated. Users cannot sign in to the application.
rootCause: Application has been deactivated by setting 'isDisabled' property to true on the App Registration object (via portal Deactivate button, Graph API, or App lifecycle manager agent). This is different from 'accountEnabled' (portal toggle) and 'disabledByMicrosoftStatus' (Microsoft-initiated).
solution: To re-enable: use Microsoft Graph API PATCH to set isDisabled to false or null on the application object: PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/myorganization/applications/{appObjectId} with body {"isDisabled": false}. To verify state: GET the application or service principal and check isDisabled, accountEnabled, and disabledByMicrosoftStatus properties. Note: changes may take up to 60 minutes to take effect, and existing tokens remain valid until expiry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Application Deactivation (Disablement)

symptom: Microsoft internal employee (MSIE) submits Azure support ticket requesting to be added as Owner to an application in the Microsoft tenant (microsoft.onmicrosoft.com).
rootCause: The employee incorrectly submitted an external Azure support ticket instead of using the internal helpdesk process.
solution: Direct the Microsoft employee to TechWeb (https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/sites/TechWeb) where they can chat with internal helpdesk and create an internal ticket. Close the case as no further action is required on the support side.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Microsoft employee wants to be added as an Owner to an app - Microsoft Tenant

symptom: Client application receives error 'The client application {appId} is missing service principal in the tenant {tenantId}' when acquiring token via OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow (app-to-app authentication)
rootCause: Entra ID enforced blocking of SP-Less authentication — cross-tenant token acquisition without a client Service Principal in the target tenant. Step 1 (Feb 2023): blocked for all 1P resource apps; Step 2 (Mar 2023): blocked for all 3P resource apps except a legacy allowlist. This is a security enforcement because SP-Less tokens lack oid, role, and group claims, posing authorization vulnerabilities.
solution: Create a Service Principal (Enterprise Application) for the client app in the target tenant. Steps: (1) Admin consent URL or PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId '{appId}'; (2) Consent to required permissions using requiredResourceAccess property; (3) Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/create-service-principal-cross-tenant. ICM escalation: Service=ESTS, Team=Incident Triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Product updates to Service Principal less Authentication

symptom: SP-Less authentication works for App A but fails for App B in the same tenant — inconsistent behavior across applications
rootCause: SP-Less authentication is deprecated. Some legacy resource apps (e.g., MS Graph, Azure Bot Framework) are in an allowlist that still permits SP-Less tokens. New apps and most 3P resource apps are blocked. 1P client apps can still get tokens without SP in any tenant.
solution: Do not rely on SP-Less authentication behavior. Create Service Principals for both client and resource apps in the target tenant. Use requiredResourceAccess property to bundle consent requests. For resource app owners: review authorization logic and migrate away from SP-Less patterns. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/create-service-principal-cross-tenant
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Product updates to Service Principal less Authentication

symptom: Sign-in logs show Service Principal ID as 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 for certain app sign-ins
rootCause: SP-Less clients don't have a Service Principal in the tenant, so the SP ID shows as all-zeros placeholder. This is expected for legacy-allowed resource apps (e.g., MS Graph). The SP-Less token lacks oid, role, and group claims — it only proves AuthN (correct secret), not AuthZ. This is a visibility change, not a behavior change.
solution: No security or functionality issue. To improve visibility and log consistency: (1) Create Service Principals for approved client apps in the tenant; (2) Review sign-in logs to monitor SP-Less scenarios; (3) For untrusted apps: create SP for resource app, enable assignment required, and only allow explicitly approved client apps. Reference: IcM 494632598.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Product updates to Service Principal less Authentication

symptom: User receives AADSTS50105 error — 'User is not assigned to a role for the application' when signing into an Enterprise Application
rootCause: The 'Assignment required' property is enabled on the Enterprise Application's Service Principal (appRoleAssignmentRequired=true), but the user or their group has not been assigned to the app. Note: Changes to 'Assignment required' setting are NOT visible in standard Audit logs (known bug — Bug 3424404).
solution: (1) Assign the user or group to the Enterprise Application via Entra portal > Enterprise Apps > Users and groups; (2) If unsure whether 'Assignment required' was recently changed, use Kusto query on MSODS (cluster: msodsuswest) — query GlobalIfxRestBusinessCommon with the internalCorrelationId to see SP update operations, and GlobalIfxUlsEvents with tagId=='05je' to see property changes; (3) Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/entra-id/app-integration/error-code-AADSTS50105-user-not-assigned-role
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/Troubleshooting/User assignment troubleshooting flow

symptom: AADSTS700016: Application with identifier '31359c7f-bd7e-475c-86db-fdb8c937548e' (PnP Management Shell) was not found in the directory. Customer gets this error when using PnP PowerShell or CLI for Microsoft 365.
rootCause: As of 9/9/2024, PnP Management Shell multitenant app was disabled. The application is no longer available as a shared multitenant registration, so customers must register the application in their own tenant.
solution: Follow instructions at https://pnp.github.io/blog/post/changes-pnp-management-shell-registration/ to register PnP Management Shell in own tenant, or use own Entra ID identity per https://pnp.github.io/cli-microsoft365/user-guide/using-own-identity/. Route case to SharePoint Online: SharePoint/SharePoint Online/Identity and Authentication/App Authentication. Identity can help with app registration questions via collab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Applications Experiences Support Team Boundaries

symptom: AADSTS700016: Application with identifier 'd1ddf0e4-d672-4dae-b554-9d5bdfd93547' was not found in the directory when running Intune PowerShell example scripts.
rootCause: Intune PowerShell example scripts reference a deprecated app ID. Customers need to register their own Microsoft Entra ID app registration and update scripts accordingly.
solution: Register a new App Registration in Entra ID with required Intune API permissions, then update PowerShell scripts with the new App ID. Refer to https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/fundamentals/in-development. Route updating scripts to Intune team; AADSTS errors and app registration help can be provided by Entra Identity via collab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Applications Experiences Support Team Boundaries

symptom: SAML Token Encryption certificate upload fails when configuring token encryption for an enterprise application in Azure AD.
rootCause: Wrong certificate format used for upload. The certificate must be an X.509 certificate file in .cer format containing only the public key. Binary or PFX formats will fail.
solution: Export/obtain the certificate as an X.509 .cer file (public key only). For applications registered via App Registration blade (not Enterprise Apps), the Token Encryption menu is disabled — use app manifest, Microsoft Graph API, or PowerShell to configure encryption instead. Upload the .cer file through Enterprise Apps > Token Encryption, then activate encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/How to/Azure AD SAML Token Encryption

symptom: SAML token encryption configured but tokens are not being encrypted, or application fails to decrypt tokens after enabling SAML token encryption in Azure AD.
rootCause: TokenEncryptionKeyID attribute in the application manifest is not set or does not match the keyId of the uploaded encryption certificate. Azure AD uses this attribute to identify which certificate to use for encrypting tokens.
solution: Verify tokenEncryptionKeyId in the app manifest matches the keyId of the active encryption certificate in keyCredentials. Use Graph API: PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/applications/<objectId> with body {"tokenEncryptionKeyId":"<matching-keyId>"}. Can also verify via PowerShell: $app = Get-MgApplication -ApplicationId <id>; $app.TokenEncryptionKeyId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/How to/Azure AD SAML Token Encryption

symptom: SAML SSO fails — token is sent encrypted by Azure AD but the application cannot decrypt it. User sees authentication failure or error after Azure AD redirects back to the app.
rootCause: Key mismatch: the public key certificate uploaded to Azure AD for encryption does not correspond to the private key configured in the application's keystore. Azure AD encrypts using AES-256 with the uploaded public key, but the app cannot decrypt without the matching private key.
solution: 1) Use Fiddler to capture the SAML response and verify the token is encrypted. 2) Verify the public key uploaded to Azure AD matches the private key in the application. 3) If mismatch, re-generate a key pair: upload the public cert (.cer) to Azure AD and install the private key in the app's keystore. 4) Use Kusto diagnostic queries with CorrelationId to search for TokenEncryptionKeyID in ESTS DiagnosticTracesIfx to confirm encryption is being applied. ICM escalation for token issuance: ESTS/Incident Triage; for portal config: IAM Services/Enterprise App Management.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/How to/Azure AD SAML Token Encryption

symptom: AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error during sign-in. Application request includes prompt=consent parameter, causing consent prompt even when admin consent already granted.
rootCause: Application sends prompt=consent in authorization request, forcing consent prompt regardless of existing grants.
solution: Ask app vendor/owner to remove prompt=consent from authorization request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: AADSTS90094/90095 error. Microsoft detects risky permissions. Audit Logs show Risky application detected.
rootCause: Risk detection flagged application permissions as risky, blocking user consent.
solution: Review risk detection in Audit Logs. If app valid, admin should approve.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: AADSTS90094/90095 error. Enterprise app has User assignment required=Yes, admin consent not granted.
rootCause: User assignment required disallows all user consent because consenting causes self-assignment.
solution: (1) Disable User assignment required, let user consent, re-enable. Or (2) Admin grants consent via /authorize with Consent on behalf of your organization checked.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: AADSTS90094/90095 error. Permissions in scope not admin consented. Grant admin consent button does not resolve.
rootCause: Grant admin consent uses /adminconsent endpoint which only grants permissions in App Registration. Dynamic scope permissions not covered. Files.Read.All, Sites.Read.All always require admin consent.
solution: Use /authorize endpoint with dynamic consent: Global admin navigates to URL with all scopes, checks Consent on behalf of org, clicks Accept.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: User prompted for consent twice during sign-in. First consent, then login, then second consent with some scopes missing.
rootCause: Dynamic scopes: first call evaluates existing Graph delegations, second call evaluates dynamic scopes. By-design (ICM: 250523706).
solution: By-design. App can minimize by registering all permissions in App Registration and pre-consenting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS65001: user/admin has not consented. Or AADSTS90008: app misconfigured, must require Sign in and read user profile.
rootCause: Application missing Microsoft Graph User.Read or openid delegated permission, or permission not consented.
solution: 1. Add User.Read delegated permission. 2. Admin consent with Consent on behalf of org. 3. For missing scope permissions, use /authorize with dynamic consent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS650059: Application not configured for use in tenant. SignInAudience AzureADMyOrg limiting its use.
rootCause: App changed from multi-tenant to single-tenant. External SPs cannot authenticate. ESTS enforcement after July 14, 2025.
solution: Change signInAudience to AzureADMultipleOrgs in home tenant app registration. If customer does not own app, contact app owner.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Failed to add identifier URI: All newly added URIs must contain tenant verified domain, tenant ID, or appId. Error in Expose an API blade.
rootCause: Identifier URI protection enforcement (May 2025) blocks non-default URIs. defaultAppManagementPolicy has nonDefaultUriAddition restriction enabled.
solution: (1) Use default URI: api://{appId}. (2) Switch to v2 tokens. (3) Use SAML. (4) Per-app exemption via appManagementPolicies. (5) Caller exemption. (6) Disable protection (not recommended). Ref: https://aka.ms/identifier-uri-formatting-error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: SignInAudience value AzureADMultipleOrgs not allowed as per assigned policy. Error creating multi-tenant app.
rootCause: Tenant admin configured app management policy with audience restriction blocking multi-tenant apps.
solution: Contact tenant admin to update policy or create custom exemption. Or use AzureADMyOrg.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: AADSTS70001: Application is disabled. User cannot sign in to previously working application.
rootCause: Microsoft Identity Protection detected app as malicious, set disabledByMicrosoftStatus=DisabledDueToViolationOfServicesAgreement. Only in Public clouds.
solution: Check disabledByMicrosoftStatus via Graph GET. Check Audit Logs for Azure AD Protection Service. File IcM to Identity Protection Service. Disabled apps cannot be deleted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD Malicious OAuth App Disablement

symptom: Application cannot access resources or obtain new tokens. Enterprise app shows disabled/deactivated.
rootCause: isDisabled=true set on App Registration. Different from accountEnabled and disabledByMicrosoftStatus.
solution: PATCH .../beta/.../applications/{appObjectId} with {isDisabled:false}. Verify via GET. Changes take up to 60 minutes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Application Deactivation (Disablement)

symptom: Microsoft internal employee submits Azure support ticket to be added as Owner to app in Microsoft tenant.
rootCause: Employee incorrectly submitted external support ticket.
solution: Direct to TechWeb for internal helpdesk. Close case.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Microsoft employee wants to be added as an Owner to an app - Microsoft Tenant

symptom: ADSTS700021: No matching federated identity record found for presented assertion. Occurs when using workload identity federation (FIC) with GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, or other OIDC providers. Error includes assertion issuer, subject, and audience mismatch details.
rootCause: The issuer, subject, or audience claim in the external OIDC token does not match the federated identity credential configuration on the Azure AD application. Common causes: GitHub workflow Organization/Repository/Branch mismatch, wrong audience value, or incorrect subject identifier format.
solution: 1) Verify the FIC configuration matches exactly: issuer URL (e.g., https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com for GitHub), subject identifier (e.g., repo:org/repo:ref:refs/heads/main), and audience (recommended: api://AzureADTokenExchange). 2) For GitHub Actions: ensure Organization, Repository, and Entity type (Branch/Tag/Environment) in the FIC match the workflow trigger. 3) Check if the correct app registration is being used (ClientId/TenantId in workflow secrets).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/Troubleshooting/Federated identity credentials TSG

symptom: 403 error 'The size of the object has exceeded its limit. Please reduce the number of values and retry your request' when adding a federated identity credential to an application via Graph API or Azure Portal.
rootCause: Maximum of 20 federated identity credentials allowed per application object. This is a platform limit.
solution: Either delete an existing federated identity credential from the application object, or create a new application object and add the federated identity credential there. If using GitHub Actions across many repos/branches, consider using Environment-based subjects to reduce the number of FICs needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/Troubleshooting/Federated identity credentials TSG

symptom: AADSTS700211: No matching federated identity record found for presented assertion issuer when using Azure CLI 'az login --federated-token' for cross-tenant access with managed identity and FIC. PowerShell Connect-AzAccount works fine but Azure CLI fails.
rootCause: Azure CLI's '--federated-token' parameter and Azure PowerShell's '-AccessToken' parameter have different functionalities. PowerShell's -AccessToken directly uses the access token for REST API calls. Azure CLI's --federated-token expects a managed identity (MSI) OIDC token to be used for token exchange, not an already-exchanged access token from the target tenant.
solution: When using Azure CLI with FIC for cross-tenant managed identity: pass the Managed Identity token (from the source tenant) to 'az login --federated-token', NOT the access token obtained from the target tenant. For PowerShell, use 'Connect-AzAccount -AccessToken $TenantBToken.access_token' directly with the target tenant's access token.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/Troubleshooting/Federated identity credentials TSG

symptom: AADSTS7002104: 'Symmetric secrets may not be set on Service Principals to authenticate this application.' Error appears in sign-in logs. Client requests using symmetric keys on Microsoft first-party Application Service Principals fail after June 15, 2024.
rootCause: Microsoft disabled symmetric key authentication on first-party application Service Principals as part of SFI (Security First Initiative). Symmetric keys rely on shared secrets that are vulnerable to interception. Enforcement started June 15, 2024 (MC792991, MC792982).
solution: Replace symmetric keys with asymmetric keys using Add-MgServicePrincipalKey. To identify affected SPs, run PowerShell: Get-MgServicePrincipal -All and filter KeyCredentials where Type='Symmetric'. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.graph.applications/add-mgserviceprincipalkey for migration guidance. ICM escalation: Service=ESTS, Team=Incident Triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/First_Party_Applications/How to/Disablement of Symmetric keys for Microsoft Entra first party applications Service Principals

symptom: MyApps launcher page (launcher.myapps.microsoft.com) shows failure with Go to the My Apps Portal error when launching a password-SSO or linked app
rootCause: App launch failure on the MyApps launcher page; backend telemetry needed to identify the specific cause
solution: Use Kusto cluster idsharedscus.southcentralus.kusto.windows.net, database IAMK8s, query AllDiagnosticEvent table with the correlation ID and timestamp from the error screenshot. Filter by operation_id to get detailed telemetry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Kusto logs for MyApps

symptom: Password-based SSO app returns unable to save credentials error when admin or user tries to store credentials
rootCause: Hard limit of 48 keys per user per password-based SSO app has been exceeded
solution: Remove credentials for an app the user is no longer using to free up key slots. Each user is limited to 48 saved keys across all password-based SSO apps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Password_and_Linked_SSO/Troubleshooting/Password SSO Troubleshooting

symptom: Password-based SSO app launches but hangs on loading spinner, fails to submit credentials to the application login form
rootCause: SSO mode is set to Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on disabled instead of password-based SSO
solution: Navigate to Enterprise Applications > select the app > Single sign-on > change mode to Password-based Sign-on, enter the Sign-on URL, and save.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Password_and_Linked_SSO/Troubleshooting/Password SSO Troubleshooting

symptom: Password-based SSO submits credentials to the application login form but the app does not sign the user in; user is returned to the login page
rootCause: Application login form structure or field labels have changed; stored metadata no longer matches the current form
solution: Gallery apps: verify metadata update needed, open ICM with App Name/ID/Sign-on URL. Non-gallery apps: use Jarvis (SAW required) AppManagement/Password SSO action to retrieve and update metadata, then recapture sign-in fields using manual capture with MyApps Secure Sign-In Extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Password_and_Linked_SSO/Troubleshooting/Password SSO Troubleshooting

symptom: Publisher Verification error PublisherDomainMismatch: domain used for email verification in Partner Center does not match the app Publisher Domain or any verified domains on the tenant
rootCause: Mismatch between the domain verified in MPN Partner Center and the Publisher Domain configured on the app registration in Entra ID
solution: 1) Ensure the tenant where the app is registered is added as a secondary tenant on the CPP Account in Partner Center. 2) Set the app Publisher Domain to match the domain used for email verification in the CPP account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Publisher_Verification/How to/Azure AD Publisher Verification for app developers

symptom: Publisher Verification error MPNAccountNotFoundOrNoAccess: The MPN ID does not exist or the user does not have access to it
rootCause: MPN ID is incorrect, the Entra ID tenant is not associated with the MPN account in Partner Center, or the signed-in user lacks required admin role
solution: 1) Verify MPN ID at Partner profile page. 2) Add tenant at tenant management page (partner.microsoft.com/dashboard/account/v3/tenantmanagement). 3) Ensure user has Account Admin, MPN Admin, or Global Admin role in Partner Center user management.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Publisher_Verification/How to/Azure AD Publisher Verification for app developers

symptom: Publisher Verification error UnableToAddPublisher: A verified publisher cannot be added to this application
rootCause: Microsoft security risk assessment flagged the user or app/MPN account as risky (Identity Protection signals)
solution: Have the user perform password reset via SSPR portal (https://aka.ms/sspr). Wait up to 24 hours for the risky user state to clear. Also check Identity Protection limitation where risky user blocked by policy may not appear in reports.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Publisher_Verification/How to/Azure AD Publisher Verification for app developers

symptom: Publisher Verification error InteractionRequired: multi-factor authentication required to proceed, even when MFA is enabled but not required in the session
rootCause: MFA was not performed in the current session before attempting publisher verification; MFA must be performed in the same session
solution: Perform Azure MFA in the same session before retrying publisher verification. Note: Publisher Verification supports only Azure MFA; no plans to support other MFA providers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Publisher_Verification/How to/Azure AD Publisher Verification for app developers

symptom: FIDO2 security key registration on Apple (macOS/iOS) fails with error 'Something went wrong. You may want to try a different security key, or contact your administrator' when the key has no PIN configured
rootCause: Apple does not support provisioning a new PIN on the FIDO2 device. If the security key has not been set up with a PIN on another platform, Apple cannot initialize it.
solution: Have the user first configure the PIN on the FIDO2 security key using a Windows device, then return to the Apple device to complete registration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD FIDO2 on Non-Windows OSes

symptom: FIDO2 security key authentication via NFC fails on Android devices in both browser and native app scenarios
rootCause: Android does not currently support NFC for FIDO2 security keys. Only USB interface is supported on Android (Chrome and Edge only).
solution: Use USB security keys on Android instead of NFC. NFC is only supported on iOS and Windows. For Android native apps, ensure Microsoft Authenticator (6.2405.3618+) or Intune Company Portal (5.0.6262.0+) is installed as broker, and device runs Android 14+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD FIDO2 on Non-Windows OSes

symptom: After enabling Issuer Hints in CBA authentication policy, users fail to sign in with certificate-based authentication. Organizations with TLS inspection on firewalls/proxies see authentication failures.
rootCause: Enabling Issuer Hints changes the CBA sign-in endpoint from certauth.login.microsoftonline.com to t{tenantId}.certauth.login.microsoftonline.com. If TLS inspection rules only allow the old endpoint, the new tenant-specific endpoint is blocked.
solution: Before enabling Issuer Hints, update firewall/proxy TLS inspection exceptions to use wildcard *.certauth.login.microsoftonline.com (or *.certauth.login.microsoftonline.us for Azure Government). Organizations not doing TLS inspection are not affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD Native Certificate Based Authentication

symptom: After changing Required Affinity Binding to High at tenant level in CBA policy, all users in the tenant are locked out of certificate-based authentication
rootCause: Changing Required Affinity Binding to High requires that all user objects have correct high-affinity binding values (SKI or SHA1PublicKey) in their certificateUserIds attribute. Without these values, no certificates can match the high-affinity requirement.
solution: 1) Ensure certificateUserIds attribute of all user objects have the correct high-affinity binding value (SKI or SHA1PublicKey). 2) Add a Username binding rule of SKI or SHA1PublicKey to match user accounts. Only change to High after verifying user data is properly populated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD Native Certificate Based Authentication

symptom: Windows interactive CBA login fails with NGC error AADSTS130004: UserPrincipal doesn't have the NGC key configured. AzureAdPrt shows No with Attempt Status 0xc000006d.
rootCause: The Windows version does not support Azure AD Native CBA for interactive login. Requires Windows 10 20H1+, Windows 11 21H2+, or Windows Server 2019+.
solution: Upgrade Windows to a supported version: Windows 10 20H1+, Windows 11 21H2+, or Windows Server 2019+. Verify with dsregcmd /status to check PRT acquisition status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD Native Certificate Based Authentication

symptom: Get-UserSMSSignInSettings returns 'notAllowedByPolicy' for a user with a unique phone number in the tenant
rootCause: The user has a valid phone number but is not within scope of the Text message sign-in policy. The policy must explicitly include the user or a group they belong to.
solution: Add the user directly to the Text message sign-in policy under Authentication method policy in Azure AD > Authentication Methods, or add them to a group that is assigned to this policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD SMS Sign-in

symptom: SMS Sign-in fails to enable for a user. Admin sees 'Unable to set sign-in method' notification. Audit log shows Failure with Status reason DirectoryUniquenessException.
rootCause: Phone number collision: another user in the tenant already has the same phone number registered. The AlternativeSecurityId attribute requires a unique Type|IdentityProvider|Key combination, which fails when the number is already provisioned.
solution: Identify the existing user with that phone number (use Kusto query or check Audit logs with Global Admin/Privileged Auth Admin who can see the objectId in the PhoneNumberCollision notification). Remove the duplicate number from the other user before re-provisioning.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD SMS Sign-in

symptom: SMS Sign-in previously worked for a user but suddenly stopped. No check mark next to Phone on Authentication Methods blade indicating 'SMS sign-in ready'. AlternativeSecurityId may still be present.
rootCause: The user was removed from the Text message policy or from membership in a group assigned to the policy.
solution: Re-add the user directly to the Text message policy, or add them to a group that is assigned to the policy. The mapping will indicate 'SMS sign-in ready' once again.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD SMS Sign-in

symptom: Entering a phone number in the Microsoft Teams credential prompt on Windows/macOS does not activate the Next button, preventing SMS Sign-in
rootCause: SMS Sign-in is not supported on Teams Windows and macOS desktop apps. Support requires adoption of the new auth library which has not been completed for these platforms.
solution: Workaround: Enter the phone number in format ##########@contoso.onmicrosoft.com, then click 'Use another account' at the disambiguation prompt and re-enter the phone number. Alternatively, use Teams for Android, iOS, or web where SMS Sign-in works.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD SMS Sign-in

symptom: CBA login fails with error 'The Certificate Revocation List (CRL) downloaded from {uri} has exceeded the maximum allowed size ({size} bytes)'. User must retry.
rootCause: CRL foreground download exceeds the size limit (20MB public cloud, 45MB USGov) or takes longer than 11 seconds. Large CRLs that exceed foreground download limits cause login failures.
solution: 1) Reduce CRL size by using delta CRLs for frequent updates. 2) Add 'next CRL publish' field to CRLs to enable background downloads before expiry. 3) After first failure, service auto-adjusts to higher background limit (45MB public, 150MB USGov). 4) If CRL >50MB public or >150MB USGov, request manual increase via ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Cert Revocation Lists

symptom: CBA CRL check fails because CRL distribution point uses LDAP path (e.g., ldap://...)
rootCause: Entra ID only supports HTTP-based CRL paths. LDAP paths are not supported for CRL distribution points. The CRL must also be accessible anonymously without authentication.
solution: Change CRL distribution points to use HTTP-based URLs. Ensure the CRL endpoint is publicly accessible and does not require authentication. Update the CRL path in Entra ID CA configuration accordingly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Cert Revocation Lists

symptom: CBA CRL validation fails or produces unexpected results when the same CRL path is configured for multiple Certificate Authorities in Entra ID
rootCause: CRL paths must be unique to each CA and the CRL must be signed by the associated CA. Using the same CRL path for multiple CAs causes validation to fail because the CRL signature won't match all CAs. Also note: each CA cert shows the CRL distribution point for its issuer, not its own.
solution: Configure unique CRL paths for each CA in Entra ID. Ensure each CRL is signed by the corresponding CA. Be careful to use the correct CRL path - each CA's own cert shows the CDP for its issuer, not its own CRL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Cert Revocation Lists

symptom: ADSTS700021: No matching federated identity record found for presented assertion when using workload identity federation (FIC).
rootCause: Issuer, subject, or audience claim mismatch with FIC configuration.
solution: Verify FIC issuer URL, subject identifier, and audience match exactly. For GitHub Actions check Organization/Repository/Branch.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/Troubleshooting/Federated identity credentials TSG

symptom: 403 size exceeded limit when adding federated identity credential to application.
rootCause: Max 20 FIC per application object.
solution: Delete existing FIC or use another application object.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/Troubleshooting/Federated identity credentials TSG

symptom: AADSTS700211 when using az login --federated-token for cross-tenant managed identity. PowerShell works but Azure CLI fails.
rootCause: Azure CLI federated-token expects MSI OIDC token, not access token. Different from PowerShell AccessToken param.
solution: Pass MSI token to az login --federated-token, not the access token from target tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/Troubleshooting/Federated identity credentials TSG

symptom: AADSTS7002104: Symmetric secrets may not be set on Service Principals to authenticate this application.
rootCause: Microsoft disabled symmetric key auth on 1P SP as part of SFI, enforced June 2024.
solution: Replace symmetric keys with asymmetric keys via Add-MgServicePrincipalKey. ICM: ESTS/Incident Triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/First_Party_Applications/How to/Disablement of Symmetric keys for Microsoft Entra first party applications Service Principals

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with AADSTS1001000: Unable to acquire certificate policy from tenant
rootCause: Wrong user's UPN was entered on the sign-in page and the device owner's certificate was presented and selected instead of the correct user's certificate.
solution: In ASC Sign-in Auth Diagnostic, check the PerRequestLogs of the GetCredentialType call to find the ObjectID/PUID of the account attempting sign-in. Verify this matches the SubjectName on the user certificate. Instruct user to enter the correct UPN matching their certificate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with AADSTS1001003: Unable To Acquire Value Specified In Binding From Certificate
rootCause: User selected the wrong certificate from the certificate picker during sign-in. The selected certificate does not contain the value required by the certificate-to-user attribute binding configuration.
solution: Instruct user to swipe through the certificate list and select the correct one issued to them by the Certificate Authority. On Android, only the user's certificate (with GUID) is shown. On iOS, all certificates including device certificates are shown — select the one showing the user's name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: After enabling X509Certificate authentication method in Entra ID, federated users performing CBA are no longer redirected to ADFS; they authenticate directly in Azure AD
rootCause: When X509Certificate is enabled as an authentication method in the Entra ID policy, users in scope are authenticated via Azure AD CBA instead of being redirected to ADFS for federated CBA.
solution: To restore federated CBA for specific users, change the scope of the X509Certificate authentication method to target only select users/groups, and ensure the users who need federated CBA are NOT members of the included groups.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with AADSTS50034: The User Account {EmailHidden} Does Not Exist In The <TenantId> Directory. Sign-in logs show X.509 Certificate authentication succeeded but ESTS fails the user lookup.
rootCause: The user account type does not align with the userproperty value specified on the certificate-to-user attribute binding. For example, cloud/synced users may need userPrincipalName while synced users with Alternate Security ID need onPremisesUserPrincipalName.
solution: For cloud and synced users NOT using Alternate Security ID: change the userproperty on the binding to userPrincipalName. For synced users using Alternate Security ID: change the userproperty to onPremisesUserPrincipalName.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with AADSTS130501: Sign in was blocked due to User Credential Policy
rootCause: The X509Certificate Authentication Method policy is not enabled for all users in the tenant, and the signing-in user is not a member of any group assigned to the policy.
solution: Verify the user is a member of a group assigned to the X509Certificate Authentication Method policy, or directly add them to the policy, then retry sign-in.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with generic 'Sign-in failed' error page showing only a Browser ID. Sign-in logs show error code 7000214 with failure reason 'Certificate has been revoked.'
rootCause: The user's certificate has been revoked and is listed in the published CRL (Certificate Revocation List).
solution: Confirm certificate revocation status in the CRL. If the revocation is intentional, issue a new certificate. The generic error message is a known UX issue — check sign-in logs for error code 7000214 to confirm revocation as the cause.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Uploading CA certificates via the Entra ID Portal Certificate Authorities blade fails with 'Certificate uploading failed' — no HTTP activity is captured in browser/Fiddler trace. Only occurs in tenants creating Certificate Authorities for the first time (no existing CAs from PowerShell).
rootCause: Portal bug (Bug 1940514) affecting tenants with no pre-existing Certificate Authorities. Tenants that already had CAs created via PowerShell are not affected.
solution: Use PowerShell to upload CA certificates as a workaround. The bug fix was deployed starting June 2022.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Edge browser throws ERR_SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_SIGNATURE_FAILED when attempting CBA over TLS 1.3, immediately after user selects certificate and enters PIN correctly
rootCause: SmartCard (hardware or virtual MS VSC) or TPM does not support the RSA-PSS algorithm required for TLS 1.3 client certificate authentication.
solution: 1) Use a smartcard that supports RSA-PSS algorithm (e.g., WHfB Cert Trust instead of MS Virtual SC, depends on TPM). 2) Upgrade TPM firmware to a version supporting RSA-PSS — contact TPM vendor. Reference: KB article on TPM-based RSA cert failures with TLS 1.3. Note: Edge TLS troubleshooting is Developer Browser Team scope; SmartCard troubleshooting is Directory Services team scope.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA authentication fails because Azure AD cannot download the CRL (Certificate Revocation List) — either the CRL file exceeds the 20MB size limit (45MB for Federal) or the download takes longer than 11 seconds
rootCause: The CRL file published by the CA is too large for Azure AD to download and cache within its constraints (max 20MB commercial / 45MB Federal, max 11 second download time).
solution: Keep certificate lifetimes reasonable and clean up expired certificates to reduce CRL size. If blocked by CRL size limit, as workaround: remove the CRL path and disable CRL checking for the specific CA until the limit is increased. Also note: OCSP is not supported — only HTTP CRL works for revocation checking.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA authentication is NOT blocked for a revoked certificate when all CA certificates are only in the new PKI Trust Store and the old Certificate Authority store is empty
rootCause: When the old Certificate Authority store returns null (has no CAs), revocation checking is not enforced even if the CRL in the new store lists the certificate as revoked. A fix was checked in but blocked by seasonal lockdown.
solution: Workaround: Upload a certificate (even a dummy certificate) to the old certificate store. As long as the old store does not return null, revocation checking will be enforced. The fix is expected to complete rollout by January 2025. Documented in MS Learn under 'Step 1: Configure the certificate authorities with PKI-based trust store (Preview)'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: In the new PKI Trust Store, clicking 'Deleted CAs' under a specific PKI container shows all deleted CAs across all containers instead of only the CAs from that container
rootCause: During public preview, the certificateAuthorityDetail objects lack a property associating them with their parent PKI container, so filtering is not possible.
solution: This is a known public preview limitation. By GA, a parent association property will be added to filter deleted CAs to their parent PKI container.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: After adding, updating, or deleting a Certificate Authority in the new PKI Trust Store, users experience up to 10 minutes delay before CBA authentication works with certificates from the affected CA
rootCause: CA CRUD operations have a propagation delay (~3 minutes typical, up to 10 minutes) for issuer hints to be set in the ESTS cache. New CAs must be cached before their certificates appear in the cert picker.
solution: This is expected behavior. Wait up to 10 minutes after CA changes. To minimize impact: upload CAs to new store while they still exist in old store (ESTS uses old CAs until new ones are cached). Rolling an expired CA (same subject name) has no visible impact as the subject persists in cache.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: Attempting to permanently (hard) delete a soft-deleted PKI container before the 30-day retention period fails with HTTP 400 Bad Request. Audit event shows 'Delete PublicKeyInfrastructure' failure with DirectoryObjectNotFoundException.
rootCause: Hard deletion of soft-deleted PKI containers is not supported during the public preview phase.
solution: Wait for GA when hard deletion will be fully supported. During preview, PKI containers can only be soft-deleted and will be automatically purged after 30 days.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: Application JSON metadata parser fails after Microsoft added cloud_instance_name tag to JWK signing key metadata for US Government cloud (GCCH/DoD) endpoints
rootCause: New cloud_instance_name property added to JWK metadata as part of public/gov cloud signing key isolation; application parser does not comply with RFC 7517 which requires ignoring unknown members
solution: Application developer must fix their metadata parser to be RFC 7517 compliant - ignore unknown members in JWK. Per RFC 7158, a JSON parser MUST accept all conforming texts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/US Government (GCCH DOD) Public Cloud Signing Key Split

symptom: Verified ID Face Check: User gets "Your organization hasn't authorized you to get a Verified ID" error with 401 Unauthorized when clicking Get my Verified ID on myaccount.microsoft.com. Browser trace shows POST to api.myaccount.microsoft.com returns 401 with "Provided access token contains no scp".
rootCause: The "My Profile" servicePrincipal (AppId: 8c59ead7-d703-4a27-9e55-c96a0054c8d2) is missing from the tenant.
solution: Workaround: Manually add the My Profile servicePrincipal via PowerShell: Connect-MgGraph -scopes Application.ReadWrite.All; New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId "8c59ead7-d703-4a27-9e55-c96a0054c8d2"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID Face Check

symptom: Verified ID Face Check iOS: Users on iOS get "User Key for iOS App Integrity is not found" (Error 400, code AppIntegrity_iOS_KeyNotFound) after Face Check scan completes. Intermittent failure.
rootCause: iOS App Integrity key metadata becomes stale or missing; Authenticator deletes the app integrity metadata and retries on next attempt.
solution: Have customer upload Authenticator logs and provide the Easy ID for ASC analysis. The error is intermittent - retrying usually succeeds after Authenticator auto-deletes and regenerates the integrity key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID Face Check

symptom: Verified ID Onboarding: Overview blade fails with 403 "The Verifiable Credentials Issuer Service application service principal is not found" even with P2 license.
rootCause: Bug in provisioning for tenants that previously provisioned Verified ID. Missing client API app SP causes re-provisioning to fail due to insufficient Graph permissions.
solution: File ICM (ref: 335009327). PG fix tracked in bug 2041925. Requires PG intervention to manually provision missing service principals.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID: DID registration / domain verification fails. HTTPS issues with did-configuration.json hosting. SSL certificate errors in Postman.
rootCause: HTTPS not properly configured on site hosting did-configuration.json; unsupported SSL certificate (wildcard GoDaddy certs, LetsEncrypt); or Azure CDN Custom domain HTTPS not enabled.
solution: Verify https://{domain}/.well-known/did-configuration.json is accessible. Test with Postman (SSL check enabled). For Azure CDN: enable Custom domain HTTPS (8hr cooldown). Check supported SSL cert.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID: "Refresh verification status" fails with "Your domain could not be validated" even though did-configuration.json is accessible in browser.
rootCause: Stale did.json file from previous deleted Verified ID configuration. The verificationMethod/authentication/assertionMethod entries are outdated.
solution: Re-download both did.json and did-configuration.json from the Domain blade in Verified ID portal. Replace files in .well-known/ folder. Click Refresh registration then Refresh verification status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID: Credential creation fails with "Your organization already has a credential with this name" / objectAlreadyExist error.
rootCause: Known issue when organization deletes Verified ID config and recreates with same credential names. Backend cleanup takes time.
solution: Choose a different Credential name. Known issue to be resolved when new API removes name uniqueness constraint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Issuance: "attestations.missing" / "Credential subject in output Verifiable Credential cannot be empty" when requesting IdTokenHint credential. B2B guest user scenario.
rootCause: B2B guest account does not have First name and Last name populated. Credential requires givenName/Surname from user profile.
solution: Edit the guest user account properties in the resource tenant - supply First name and Last name. Re-attempt credential request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Issuance: "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')" in sample app. API returns "No acceptable roles were found in access token".
rootCause: Verifiable Credentials Service Request API permissions not granted on App registration. Access token missing VerifiableCredential.Create.All role.
solution: Grant Verifiable Credentials Service Request API permissions on the App registration and ensure admin consent is given.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Issuance: "ServiceUnreachableException failed to sign digest" in Authenticator when scanning QR code.
rootCause: Key Vault permissions incorrect for Verifiable Credential Issuer Service. Missing Create, Delete, Sign permissions for Keys.
solution: Grant the Verifiable Credential Issuer Service the required Key Vault permissions: Create, Delete, and Sign for Keys.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Issuance: 403 "At least one unexpected OpenID token attestation encountered: https://self-issued.me" when adding IdToken credential.
rootCause: IdToken flow payload incorrectly modified to include pin and claims sections (only for IdTokenHint flow).
solution: Remove pin and claims sections from issuance request payload for IdToken flow. These are only for IdTokenHint scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Issuance: "token_validation.invalid_openid_token" / "No OpenId token claim found matching required claim family_name" for B2C users.
rootCause: B2C user account does not have First/Last name populated. Credential rules definition requires these claims.
solution: Edit user account properties to supply First name and Last name, then re-attempt credential request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Issuance: 504 Service Error. Users in specific regions fail while VPN/US-based users succeed. Authenticator logs show timeout on mobileappcommunicator.auth.microsoft.com.
rootCause: Customer network/firewall blocks inbound traffic from certain Azure regions. Verified ID service callback URI must be accessible globally.
solution: Check network/firewall rules. Ensure callback URI accessible globally. Use FQDNs in firewall rules. Download Azure IP Ranges and Service Tags for required IPs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Verification: "This app or website may be risky" warning in Authenticator digital wallet when presenting credential.
rootCause: Domain verification is not working for the Verified ID organization.
solution: Verify the Verified ID Organization domain meets requirements per docs: how-to-dnsbind#verified-domain.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Verification: "You'll have to add the credential first then try again" - Share button disabled despite credential being issued.
rootCause: Credential type mismatch between issued VC type and Verifier presentation request (e.g., "SelfAttested" vs "SelfAttestedCredential").
solution: In Verified ID portal, update Rule definition vc.type to match expected type. Delete invalid credential from Authenticator, wait 15 min for cache, reissue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID General: "No OpenID token claim found matching required claim given_name" in IDToken flow after successful sign-in.
rootCause: Missing API permissions, missing Token configuration claims, or user attribute not populated while rules definition has required=true.
solution: Check: 1) API permissions include profile/openid/email/offline_access with admin consent. 2) Token configuration has claims added. 3) User attributes populated or set required=false.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID: Question mark appears in front of claim name on issued credential in Authenticator app.
rootCause: Typo in credential Display definition claim path (e.g., "vc.credentialSubject.lasttName" instead of "lastName").
solution: In Verified ID portal Properties tab, correct the typo in Display definition claim. Click Update. Delete credential from Authenticator, wait 15 min, reissue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID Setup: Creating did.json or did-configuration.json fails during Verified ID configuration.
rootCause: Administrator does not have KEY SIGN permissions on Key Vault, even if they created it.
solution: Modify Key Vault access policy to grant the administrator SIGN permissions for Keys.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: Verified ID: Domain validation fails or Authenticator shows security warning even though did.json and did-configuration.json are accessible on web server.
rootCause: Files are served through HTTP redirect (302) instead of directly (200). W3C DID standards require these files to be accessible without redirects.
solution: Ensure did.json and did-configuration.json return 200 OK directly without any HTTP redirects. Use F12 tools or curl to verify.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Verified ID/How to/Verified ID

symptom: MySecurityInfo prompts user to register a Microsoft Authenticator passkey even when authentication strength policy explicitly excludes Microsoft Authenticator passkeys. User attempts to register and the registration fails.
rootCause: UI/UX inconsistency in MySecurityInfo portal. The page does not filter out Microsoft Authenticator passkey option based on the authentication strength advanced options configuration. Policy enforcement is correct (registration fails), but the prompt is misleading.
solution: Instruct user to select 'Security key' instead of Microsoft Authenticator passkey option when prompted on MySecurityInfo. The registration for a supported FIDO2/WebAuthn security key will succeed. Track Bug 3039303 for permanent fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: FIDO2 security key model details show as 'unknown or invalid' in Entra ID portal and Microsoft Graph API (listFido2AuthenticationMethod). The model column is empty or shows invalid value despite key working for authentication.
rootCause: The FIDO2 key vendor has not onboarded/registered the key's AAGUID with Entra ID. Entra ID uses an AAGUID-to-model mapping to display model names. Without the mapping, model info cannot be resolved.
solution: This is a display-only limitation with no functional impact on authentication. If model visibility is required for inventory/compliance, contact the FIDO2 key vendor and request they onboard the AAGUID to Microsoft. Reference ICM341190948.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: FIDO2 interactive logon appears unresponsive after touching the security key on Windows sign-in screen. No error, no PIN prompt, no progress. Commonly occurs on laptops connected to docking stations with external monitors when laptop lid is closed.
rootCause: Power management behavior when the laptop lid is closed may interfere with the UI session state required for FIDO2 logon. Registry setting LidNotificationsAreReliable may influence this behavior. Under investigation.
solution: Open the laptop lid and retry the FIDO2 logon. If issue persists, disconnect from docking station and attempt logon directly on laptop. Ensure device has latest Windows updates and firmware.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: On 'Add a passkey' page in MySecurityInfo, clicking 'Next' does nothing. No prompt to insert key, no PIN prompt, no progress. Occurs when CA policy enforces FIDO2 via authentication strengths without specifying AAGUIDs, while Authentication methods policy restricts FIDO2 by AAGUID.
rootCause: Policy mismatch between Conditional Access authentication strength (no AAGUIDs specified = allow all) and Authentication methods policy (FIDO2 restricted by specific AAGUIDs). The intersection results in no effective eligible authenticator, producing a no-op Next button.
solution: Create a custom authentication strength that explicitly lists the same AAGUIDs allowed in the Authentication methods policy. Ensure both policies are aligned. If Microsoft Authenticator passkeys are intended, enable that provider in the authentication strength. Retest after policy propagation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: FIDO2 browser logon stops working on specific Windows device — PIN dialog never appears or times out. Same security key works on other machines. WebAuthN Operational log shows Event ID 2226 with Error 0x5B4 (timeout). Device has Citrix Workspace/Receiver installed.
rootCause: Incomplete auto-install or auto-update of Citrix client (Workspace/Receiver) without admin elevation leaves USB redirection / security key components in a broken state. These components intercept or block CTAP traffic needed for WebAuthn, causing PIN prompt failures and timeouts.
solution: If Citrix is not required, uninstall it and retest. If Citrix is required, perform an elevated repair/reinstall of the latest supported Citrix client with administrator privileges, then reboot. Alternatively, disable Citrix USB redirection feature via policy if not needed. Verify fix by checking WebAuthN logs for absence of 0x5B4 timeout errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: User signs in to Hybrid Join or Entra ID Join PC with Windows Hello for Business (WHfB/NGC), then accesses resource with CA policy requiring FIDO2 authentication strength. After successful FIDO2 key presentation, authentication fails with AADSTS90117: Invalid request.
rootCause: KeyID claim conflict between the WHfB credential and the FIDO2 credential during Conditional Access token exchange. Current token handling logic does not reconcile both credentials in this scenario. Tracked by engineering work item 2167915.
solution: Option 1: Instruct users to sign in to the PC using the FIDO2 key (instead of WHfB) when accessing resources that require FIDO2 authentication strength. Option 2: Modify the CA policy to allow WHfB in addition to FIDO2 in the authentication strength configuration. Track fix via ADO work item 2167915.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: FIDO2 security keys appear as 'Disabled' on My Security Info page (aka.ms/mysecurityinfo) under Security info section. Keys continue to work for sign-in and authentication despite the Disabled label.
rootCause: UI rendering bug in the MySecurityInfo portal incorrectly marks FIDO2 keys as Disabled. The underlying authentication method remains active in Entra ID. Tracked by Incident 627758907.
solution: No action required. Inform users the 'Disabled' label is a visual bug only. Do not delete or re-register keys unless there is an actual sign-in failure. Verify key is shown as Enabled in Entra ID Authentication methods admin view. Track fix via ICM 627758907.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Tenant with Passkey Profiles enabled sees 'Windows Hello (preview)' option when editing a passkey profile in Entra portal, suggesting Entra Passkeys on Windows feature is available. Feature is not yet in public preview.
rootCause: UI prematurely exposed the Windows Hello (Entra Passkeys on Windows) option in the passkey profile editor before the feature reached public preview. PG confirmed fix to remove this option from UI.
solution: Inform customer the Windows Hello (Entra Passkeys on Windows) feature is not yet available despite the UI option. PG fix to remove this option was in deployment with ETA end of Jan 30 2026.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Cross-device FIDO2 authentication (caBLE) fails when using iOS device (e.g., scanning QR code with camera to authenticate via passkey). Apple sysdiagnose logs show HTTP 400 status code returned from cable.auth.com, NSURLErrorDomain Code=-1011, caBLE transaction failed.
rootCause: Connectivity issue to cable.auth.com endpoint. The caBLE (cloud-assisted BLE) tunnel service returns HTTP 400 during WebSocket handshake, causing the cross-device FIDO2 authentication to fail. May be caused by network filtering, proxy, or firewall blocking the cable.auth.com endpoint.
solution: Verify network connectivity to cable.auth.com endpoint. Check firewall/proxy rules allow HTTPS traffic to cable.auth.com. Collect Apple sysdiagnose logs for detailed analysis. Use 'log show' command on macOS to filter for CFNetwork/caBLE/FIDO predicates around the failure timestamp.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Data analysis

symptom: FIDO2 security key interactive logon fails with message 'This security key doesn't look familiar. Please try a different one'. WebAuthN event log shows CTAP response 0x2E (CTAP2_ERR_NO_CREDENTIALS).
rootCause: The security key does not have credentials registered for the relying party (login.microsoft.com). This occurs when the key was never enrolled for the user/tenant, was reset, or the credentials were deleted. The 0x2E CTAP2 error means no valid credentials found on the key for the requested RP.
solution: Re-register the FIDO2 security key at aka.ms/mysecurityinfo. If key was previously working, check if it was reset (which erases all credentials). Verify the key is enrolled for the correct user/tenant. Check WebAuthN event log Event ID 2225 for the CTAP response details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Data analysis

symptom: FIDO2 security key PIN entry fails repeatedly. CBOR-decoded CTAP2 response to clientPIN getRetries command shows 0 attempts remaining ({3: 0}). Key is locked due to excessive incorrect PIN entries.
rootCause: User has exhausted all PIN retry attempts on the FIDO2 security key. The key firmware locks PIN entry after maximum retries are exceeded, making the key unusable until reset.
solution: The FIDO2 security key must be reset to restore functionality. WARNING: Resetting the key erases ALL credentials stored on it. After reset, the user must re-register the key at aka.ms/mysecurityinfo. To reset: Windows Settings > Accounts > Sign-in options > Security key > Manage > Reset security key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Data analysis

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with AADSTS1001000 'Unable to acquire certificate policy from tenant' in ASC Sign-in Auth Diagnostic
rootCause: Wrong user UPN entered on the sign-in page; device owner's certificate was presented and selected for a different user account
solution: Check PerRequestLogs of GetCredentialType call in ASC Sign-in Auth Diagnostic to find the ObjectID/PUID. Verify this account matches the SubjectName on the user certificate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in returns AADSTS1001003 'Unable To Acquire Value Specified In Binding From Certificate' after user selects a certificate at certauth.login.microsoftonline.com
rootCause: User selected the wrong certificate from the certificate picker during CBA sign-in
solution: Select the correct user certificate from the picker. On Android, only user certificates with a GUID are shown. On iOS, all certificates (including device certs) are displayed — select the one showing the user's name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Federated users are no longer redirected to ADFS for CBA after enabling X509Certificate authentication method in Entra ID; they are authenticated directly by Azure AD CBA instead
rootCause: Enabling the X509Certificate authentication method policy causes all users in scope to use native Azure AD CBA, overriding federated CBA via ADFS
solution: Scope the X509Certificate authentication method policy to specific users/groups only. Ensure users who need federated CBA via ADFS are excluded from the policy scope.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with AADSTS50034 'The User Account Does Not Exist In The Directory' despite X.509 certificate authentication succeeding at certauth endpoint (sign-in logs show Authentication method=X.509 Certificate, Succeeded=true)
rootCause: User account type does not align with the userproperty value specified in the certificate-to-user attribute binding in the CBA policy
solution: For cloud/synced users NOT using Alternate Security Id: set userproperty to 'userPrincipalName'. For synced users using Alternate Security Id: set userproperty to 'onPremisesUserPrincipalName'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with AADSTS130501 'Sign in was blocked due to User Credential Policy'
rootCause: The user is not included in the X509Certificate Authentication Method policy — the policy is not enabled for all users and this user is not a member of any assigned group
solution: Add the user to a group assigned to the X509Certificate authentication method policy, or directly add them to the policy, then retry sign-in.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with generic 'Sign-in failed' error screen showing only a Browser ID; Sign-in logs show error code 7000214 with failure reason 'Certificate has been revoked'
rootCause: User's certificate has been revoked and the revocation is recorded in the published CRL
solution: Check sign-in logs for error code 7000214 to confirm certificate revocation. The generic error screen is a known UX issue — a bug has been filed to improve the error message. The user needs a new, valid certificate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Upload of CA certificates fails in the Entra portal Certificate authorities blade for tenants creating Trusted Certificate Authorities for the first time. Error shows 'Certificate uploading failed' with no HTTP activity in browser trace.
rootCause: Bug 1940514 — portal issue occurs when no CA created via PowerShell previously exists in the tenant
solution: Use PowerShell to upload CA certificates as a workaround. The bug fix (code checked in June 13, 2022) has been deployed to production.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Edge browser throws ERR_SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_SIGNATURE_FAILED error during CBA over TLS 1.3, immediately after user selects certificate and enters PIN
rootCause: SmartCard (hardware or virtual MS VSC) or TPM does not support the RSA-PSS algorithm required for TLS 1.3 client certificate authentication
solution: Option 1: Use a smartcard that supports RSA-PSS algorithm (e.g., WHfB Cert Trust instead of MS Virtual SC). Option 2: Upgrade TPM firmware to a version supporting RSA-PSS — contact TPM vendor. Note: Edge TLS troubleshooting is handled by Developer Browser Team (BoringSSL); SmartCard troubleshooting is Directory Services team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA authentication is NOT blocked for a revoked certificate when all CA certificates exist only in the new PKI trust store and the old Certificate Authority store is empty
rootCause: When the old CA store returns null (is empty), revocation checking is bypassed even if CAs exist in the new trust store. Fix was checked in but seasonal lockdown delayed rollout.
solution: Workaround: Upload a certificate (even a dummy certificate) to the old certificate store. As long as the old store does not return null, revocation checking will be enforced. This is publicly documented under Step 1 of CBA configuration docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: Deleted CAs tab under a specific PKI container in the new trust store shows ALL deleted CAs from all containers, not scoped to the selected PKI container
