rootCause: During public preview, certificateAuthorityDetail objects lack a property associating them with their parent PKI container
solution: Known preview limitation. Will be fixed by GA when a parent association property is added to certificateAuthorityDetail objects.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: Users experience up to 10-minute CBA authentication delay after Certificate Authority create/update/delete operations in the new PKI trust store. New CA certs don't appear in certificate picker immediately.
rootCause: CA issuer hints cache propagation delay — typically ~3 minutes but stated as 10 minutes to allow buffer. ESTS needs time to refresh its CA cache.
solution: Expected behavior — wait for cache propagation (up to 10 minutes). Only affects users with certs from the modified CA. Other users are unaffected. Workaround: keep CAs in both old and new stores during migration so ESTS uses cached old CAs while new ones propagate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: Hard deletion of a soft-deleted PKI container in the new trust store fails with HTTP 400 Bad Request. Audit event shows Status=Failure with StatusReason=DirectoryObjectNotFoundException.
rootCause: Feature limitation during public preview — permanent deletion of soft-deleted PKI containers is not yet supported
solution: Known preview limitation. Will be supported by GA. Currently, soft-deleted PKI containers will be automatically purged after the 30-day retention period.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with AADSTS50034: The User Account does not exist in the directory. Sign-in logs show X.509 Certificate auth succeeded but ESTS fails user lookup.
rootCause: The user account type does not align with the userproperty value on the certificate-to-user attribute binding. Cloud/synced users need userPrincipalName; synced users with Alternate Security ID need onPremisesUserPrincipalName.
solution: For cloud and synced users NOT using Alternate Security ID: change userproperty to userPrincipalName. For synced users using Alternate Security ID: change userproperty to onPremisesUserPrincipalName.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA sign-in fails with generic Sign-in failed error page showing only Browser ID. Sign-in logs show error code 7000214 with failure reason Certificate has been revoked.
rootCause: The user certificate has been revoked and is listed in the published CRL (Certificate Revocation List).
solution: Confirm certificate revocation status in the CRL. If intentional, issue a new certificate. The generic error message is a known UX issue - check sign-in logs for error code 7000214 to confirm revocation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Uploading CA certificates via Entra ID Portal Certificate Authorities blade fails with Certificate uploading failed - no HTTP activity in browser/Fiddler trace. Only in tenants creating CAs for the first time.
rootCause: Portal bug (Bug 1940514) affecting tenants with no pre-existing Certificate Authorities created via PowerShell.
solution: Use PowerShell to upload CA certificates as workaround. Bug fix deployed starting June 2022.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA authentication fails because Azure AD cannot download the CRL - either CRL file exceeds 20MB size limit (45MB for Federal) or download takes longer than 11 seconds
rootCause: CRL file published by the CA is too large for Azure AD to download and cache (max 20MB commercial / 45MB Federal, max 11 second download time).
solution: Keep certificate lifetimes reasonable and clean up expired certs to reduce CRL size. If blocked by CRL size limit: remove CRL path and disable CRL checking for the specific CA as workaround. Note: OCSP not supported - only HTTP CRL works for revocation checking.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: CBA authentication NOT blocked for revoked certificate when all CA certificates are only in new PKI Trust Store and old Certificate Authority store is empty
rootCause: When old Certificate Authority store returns null (has no CAs), revocation checking is not enforced even if CRL in new store lists cert as revoked.
solution: Workaround: Upload a certificate (even dummy) to old certificate store. As long as old store does not return null, revocation checking will be enforced. Fix expected to complete rollout by January 2025.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: In new PKI Trust Store, clicking Deleted CAs under a specific PKI container shows all deleted CAs across all containers instead of only CAs from that container
rootCause: During public preview, certificateAuthorityDetail objects lack a property associating them with their parent PKI container, so filtering not possible.
solution: Known public preview limitation. By GA, a parent association property will be added to filter deleted CAs to their parent PKI container.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: After adding/updating/deleting a Certificate Authority in the new PKI Trust Store, users experience up to 10 minutes delay before CBA authentication works with certificates from affected CA
rootCause: CA CRUD operations have propagation delay (~3 min typical, up to 10 min) for issuer hints to be set in ESTS cache.
solution: Expected behavior. Wait up to 10 minutes after CA changes. To minimize: upload CAs to new store while they still exist in old store (ESTS uses old CAs until new ones cached). Rolling expired CA with same subject name has no visible impact.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: Hard deleting a soft-deleted PKI container before 30-day retention fails with HTTP 400 Bad Request. Audit shows Delete PublicKeyInfrastructure failure with DirectoryObjectNotFoundException.
rootCause: Hard deletion of soft-deleted PKI containers is not supported during public preview.
solution: Wait for GA when hard deletion will be supported. During preview, PKI containers can only be soft-deleted and auto-purge after 30 days.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: New Trust Store

symptom: Federated user signed into local Windows client using WHfB certificate trust gets stuck in an infinite sign-in loop when connecting to AVD/RDP session host via the new RDP authentication protocol. User selects account at credential prompt but is redirected back to credential prompt repeatedly. Sign-in logs show single-factor authentication Success and MFA requirement satisfied by claim in the token. ASC Authentication Diagnostics shows StaticUiPageController:TokenBrokerAddAccountProgress with ErrorNumber 0.
rootCause: The user attempting to connect to the Azure Virtual Desktop has not been assigned the Azure RBAC role 'Virtual Machine Administrator Login' or 'Virtual Machine User Login' on the target VM or resource group.
solution: Add the user to either the 'Virtual Machine Administrator Login' or 'Virtual Machine User Login' Azure RBAC role on the target VM, resource group, or subscription.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/AVD or RDP Sign in using WHfB or FIDO2/AVD or RDP Sign in using WHfB or FIDO2 Limitations known issues

symptom: After disabling Windows Hello for Business via GPO (Computer or User > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Windows Hello for Business > Use Windows Hello for Business = Disabled), previously configured PCs still have WHfB credentials and users can still sign in with WHfB PIN/biometrics.
rootCause: Changing the GPO to Disabled only prevents new provisioning. It does not remove existing WHfB credentials (NGC container and biometric data) from machines that were previously configured.
solution: 1. Set GPO 'Use Windows Hello for Business' to Disabled to prevent new enrollment. 2. On each configured machine, launch elevated cmd.exe and run: certutil -deleteHelloContainer (this deletes the NGC container and biometric data). 3. Restart the PC. Note: Run 'certutil -user -key -csp ngc' first to see what keys are in the container before deleting, as it may contain other keys.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O1 Disabling WHfB

symptom: When users click 'I Forgot My PIN' on the Windows logon screen, they get the error: 'This feature is not supported in your organization. Please contact your administrator'.
rootCause: The Microsoft PIN reset service is not properly configured in the tenant, or the client device does not have internet access to connect to the PIN Reset Service.
solution: 1. Verify the PIN Reset Service configuration following: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/hello-for-business/pin-reset 2. Ensure the client has internet access to connect to the PIN Reset Service endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O2 PIN reset from logon screen

symptom: WHfB PIN reset from the Windows logon screen does not work on Windows 10 Professional edition. The 'I Forgot My PIN' option may not appear or does not function correctly.
rootCause: PIN reset from logon screen requires Windows 10 Enterprise edition (build 1709 or later). Windows 10 Professional does not support this feature.
solution: Upgrade client devices to Windows 10 Enterprise edition build 1709 or later to enable PIN reset from the logon screen.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O2 PIN reset from logon screen

symptom: Users who set their WHfB PIN before the PIN reset policy was applied cannot use the 'I Forgot My PIN' feature from the logon screen. The PIN reset from login screen does not work for these users.
rootCause: During initial PIN provisioning (before PIN reset policy), the recovery key is not encrypted with the PIN Reset Service's public key. The PIN Reset Service requires the recovery key to be encrypted with its public key to function.
solution: Users must first reset their PIN manually from Settings > Accounts > Sign In options > PIN > Change > I forgot My Pin. This re-provisions the recovery key with the PIN Reset Service public key encryption. After this, the PIN reset from the logon screen will work on subsequent attempts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O2 PIN reset from logon screen

symptom: WHfB provisioning fails when migrating from Hybrid Azure AD joined Key Trust to Certificate Trust deployment. Event 362 in User Device Registration log shows 'User has successfully authenticated to the enterprise STS: No'. AAD Operational log shows Event 1118 'Enterprise STS Logon failure. Status: 0xC0000250' and Event 1081 'OAuth response error: interaction_required' with error MSIS9699. ADFS Admin log shows Event 1021 with error MSIS9462: 'Enable MicrosoftPassportAuthentication in the Global Policy'.
rootCause: Microsoft Passport Authentication is not enabled in ADFS Global Authentication Policy. The Certificate Trust model requires ADFS to support OAuth JWT Bearer requests from WHfB, which needs the MicrosoftPassportAuthentication method enabled.
solution: 1. Launch ADFS management console. 2. Browse to Services > Authentication Methods. 3. Click Edit under 'Primary Authentication Methods'. 4. Under Intranet, select 'Microsoft Passport Authentication'. 5. Save. Next time the user logs in, WHfB will provision a new certificate from the configured CA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O3 WHfB move from KT to CT

symptom: After running ADPREP /DOMAINPREP for Windows Server 2016 AD schema upgrade, the Key Admins (RID 526) and Enterprise Key Admins (RID 527) security groups cannot be resolved to friendly names. They appear as SIDs instead of group names.
rootCause: The RID 526 and 527 cannot be resolved to friendly names until a Windows Server 2016 domain controller holds the PDC Emulator FSMO role. If the PDC role is still on a pre-2016 DC, name resolution fails.
solution: Move the PDC Emulator FSMO role to a Windows Server 2016 Domain Controller: Move-ADDirectoryServerOperationMasterRole -Identity <2016DC> -OperationMasterRole PDCEmulator. Reference: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4033233
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O4 Key admins not shown after schema upgrade

symptom: Users migrated from one AD domain/forest to another domain/forest are unable to authenticate using WHfB PIN. The existing WHfB key pair is bound to the original domain/forest and becomes invalid after migration.
rootCause: WHfB keys are bound to the user's original domain identity. After migration to a different domain/forest, the key pair no longer matches the new identity, causing authentication failure.
solution: 1. Run 'certutil -deletehellocontainer' on the device (check existing keys first with 'certutil -user -key -csp ngc'). 2. If the device also migrated: run 'dsregcmd /leave'. 3. Migrate device and user to new domain. 4. Run 'dsregcmd /join' on the new domain. 5. Re-enroll in WHfB from lock screen using new UPN (avoid cached profile).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O5 User device migrated from different forest

symptom: WHfB provisioning starts but fails with error 0x801C0451 (DSREG_E_USER_TOKEN_SWITCH_ACCOUNT). This can occur after user/device migration or account switch scenarios.
rootCause: Stale WAM (Web Account Manager) token cache contains tokens from the previous account/domain, causing a token mismatch during WHfB provisioning.
solution: Workaround: Clear WAM cache by either: (a) Delete files at %LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy\AC\TokenBroker\Accounts\*.* or (b) Reset WAM cache using the M365 doc method. Then reboot the machine - provisioning should work. For existing migrated users where NgcSet:Yes in dsregcmd /status, run 'certutil -deleteHelloContainer' at device level. For RCA, engage WAM team with aka.ms/WAMHot data collected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O5 User device migrated from different forest

symptom: FIDO2 registration on Apple fails: Something went wrong when key has no PIN
rootCause: Apple cannot provision new PIN on FIDO2 device
solution: Configure PIN on Windows first then use on Apple
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD FIDO2 on Non-Windows OSes

symptom: FIDO2 NFC fails on Android in browser and native apps
rootCause: Android does not support NFC for FIDO2. Only USB (Chrome/Edge)
solution: Use USB keys. For native apps install Authenticator 6.2405.3618+ on Android 14+
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD FIDO2 on Non-Windows OSes

symptom: CBA fails after enabling Issuer Hints - TLS inspection blocks new endpoint
rootCause: Issuer Hints changes endpoint to t{tenantId}.certauth.login.microsoftonline.com
solution: Add *.certauth.login.microsoftonline.com to TLS exceptions before enabling
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD Native Certificate Based Authentication

symptom: CBA High Affinity Binding locks out all users
rootCause: High affinity requires SKI/SHA1PublicKey in certificateUserIds on all users
solution: Populate certificateUserIds first then change to High
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD Native Certificate Based Authentication

symptom: Windows CBA fails AADSTS130004: NGC key not configured. AzureAdPrt=No
rootCause: Windows too old for Native CBA. Need Win10 20H1+/Win11 21H2+/Server 2019+
solution: Upgrade Windows. Verify with dsregcmd /status
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD Native Certificate Based Authentication

symptom: SMS Sign-in: notAllowedByPolicy despite valid phone
rootCause: User not in Text message policy scope
solution: Add user to policy or assigned group

symptom: SMS Sign-in fails: Unable to set method. DirectoryUniquenessException
rootCause: Phone number collision - another user has same number
solution: Find conflicting user via Kusto/Audit, remove duplicate, re-provision

rootCause: User removed from Text message policy or group
solution: Re-add user to policy or assigned group

symptom: Teams Next button inactive for SMS Sign-in on Win/Mac
rootCause: SMS Sign-in not supported on Teams Windows/macOS desktop
solution: Enter as ##########@contoso.onmicrosoft.com then Use another account. Or use mobile/web
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Azure AD SMS Sign-in

symptom: CBA login fails: CRL exceeded maximum allowed size
rootCause: CRL foreground download exceeds limit (20MB public/45MB USGov) or >11sec
solution: Use delta CRLs. Add next CRL publish field. ICM for >50MB
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Cert Revocation Lists

symptom: CBA CRL check fails - LDAP path used
rootCause: Entra ID only supports HTTP-based CRL paths
solution: Change to HTTP URLs accessible anonymously

symptom: CBA CRL validation fails - same path for multiple CAs
rootCause: CRL paths must be unique per CA. CA cert shows issuer CDP not its own
solution: Use unique CRL paths per CA
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Cert Revocation Lists

symptom: Windows Hello for Business biometric data (fingerprint/facial recognition) is lost after Windows in-place upgrade (UIP). Users must re-enroll biometrics after OS upgrade.
rootCause: The biometric device (fingerprint reader or camera) has 'Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power' enabled in Device Manager power management settings. This causes biometric signatures to not survive the upgrade process.
solution: Before performing the in-place upgrade, uncheck 'Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power' for both the Biometric fingerprint reader and Camera (facial recognition) devices in Device Manager. If the power management tab is not present, biometrics will survive the upgrade by default.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O6 stored biometric lost in place upgrade

symptom: WHfB Cloud Kerberos Trust does not work when Certificate Trust policies are also configured. Certificate Trust takes precedence and Cloud Kerberos Trust is silently ignored.
rootCause: Cloud Kerberos Trust is incompatible with Certificate Trust. When certificate trust policies are set via GPO or MDM, they are enforced instead of Cloud Kerberos Trust.
solution: Remove Certificate Trust policies (GPO or MDM) before deploying Cloud Kerberos Trust. Only one trust type should be configured at a time. Check both Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Hello for Business policies and Intune WHfB configuration profiles.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Limitations and known issues

symptom: User cannot sign in with WHfB Cloud Kerberos Trust on a Microsoft Entra hybrid joined device if they have never previously signed in with DC connectivity on that device.
rootCause: Cloud Kerberos Trust requires an initial sign-in with Domain Controller connectivity to establish the cached credential. Without this first DC-connected sign-in, the cloud kerberos trust authentication path is unsupported.
solution: Ensure the user performs their first sign-in on the hybrid joined device while connected to the corporate network with DC connectivity. Subsequent sign-ins can work with cached credentials. This is a known limitation of Cloud Kerberos Trust - not a bug.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Limitations and known issues

symptom: WHfB Cloud Kerberos Trust users experience account lockout issues when Credential Guard is enabled on the device.
rootCause: Known interaction issue between WHfB Cloud Kerberos Trust and Windows Credential Guard causing repeated authentication failures that trigger account lockout policies.
solution: See KB article: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/486bdf30-5b19-d543-d7cc-658a14a5e1fd for workaround. Consider disabling Credential Guard or switching to Key Trust as alternatives until a fix is available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Limitations and known issues

symptom: WHfB on-premises (Cert Trust / Key Trust) provisioning fails with error 0X801C044F. Users cannot set PIN; redirected to ADFS which shows 'An error occurred', then 'Error code: 0X801C044F'. Event 324 (User Device Registration) shows HResult 0x801c044f, Event 1098 (AAD Operational) shows 'interaction_required / MSIS9700: The JWT bearer request requires fresh authentication', Event 364 (ADFS Admin) shows 'The requested authentication method ngcmfa is not valid for relying party trust Device Registration Service'.
rootCause: MFA authentication method 'ngcmfa' is not enabled on the ADFS Device Registration relying party. AllowedAuthenticationClassReferences is missing 'ngcmfa' — either it was modified or never properly configured. Get-AdfsDeviceRegistration shows AllowedAuthenticationClassReferences only has 'wiaormultiauthn' without 'ngcmfa'.
solution: Run on ADFS server: set-AdfsDeviceRegistration -AllowedAuthenticationClassReferences ngcmfa, wiaormultiauthn. Then have users log off and log back in — PIN provisioning should succeed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P6 WHfB Onprem provisioning error 0X801C044F

symptom: Windows Hello for Business key-based authentication fails when only Windows Server 2019 Domain Controllers are used. Users see error 'That option is temporarily unavailable. For now, please use a different method to sign in.'
rootCause: Known issue with Windows Server 2019 DCs affecting WHfB key trust authentication. The DC does not properly handle WHfB key-based sign-in requests.
solution: Install KB4487044 on the Windows Server 2019 Domain Controllers. See https://support.microsoft.com/en-gb/help/4487044/windows-10-update-kb4487044 and public doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/hello-for-business/hello-deployment-issues#resolving-server-2019-key-trust-authentication-issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S7 WHfB authentication fails WS2019 DC

rootCause: Dual-headed account (UPN mapped to both AAD and MSA). Keychain entries prevent re-authentication.

symptom: FIDO2 security key model details show as unknown or invalid in Entra ID portal and Microsoft Graph API (listFido2AuthenticationMethod).
rootCause: The FIDO2 key vendor has not onboarded/registered the key AAGUID with Entra ID. Entra ID uses an AAGUID-to-model mapping to display model names.
solution: Display-only limitation with no functional impact. If model visibility is required, contact the FIDO2 key vendor to onboard the AAGUID to Microsoft. Reference ICM341190948.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: On Add a passkey page in MySecurityInfo, clicking Next does nothing. Occurs when CA policy enforces FIDO2 via authentication strengths without specifying AAGUIDs, while Authentication methods policy restricts FIDO2 by AAGUID.
rootCause: Policy mismatch between CA authentication strength (no AAGUIDs specified) and Authentication methods policy (FIDO2 restricted by specific AAGUIDs). The intersection results in no effective eligible authenticator.
solution: Create a custom authentication strength that explicitly lists the same AAGUIDs allowed in the Authentication methods policy. Ensure both policies are aligned. Retest after policy propagation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: FIDO2 browser logon stops working on specific Windows device - PIN dialog never appears or times out. Same key works on other machines. WebAuthN log shows Event ID 2226 with Error 0x5B4 (timeout). Device has Citrix Workspace/Receiver installed.
rootCause: Incomplete auto-install or auto-update of Citrix client without admin elevation leaves USB redirection / security key components in a broken state, intercepting or blocking CTAP traffic for WebAuthn.
solution: If Citrix not required, uninstall and retest. If required, elevated repair/reinstall of latest Citrix client then reboot. Alternatively disable Citrix USB redirection if not needed. Verify by checking WebAuthN logs for absence of 0x5B4 errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: User signs in to Hybrid/Entra ID Join PC with WHfB/NGC, then accesses resource with CA policy requiring FIDO2 strength. After successful FIDO2 key presentation, fails with AADSTS90117: Invalid request.
rootCause: KeyID claim conflict between WHfB credential and FIDO2 credential during CA token exchange. Current token handling does not reconcile both credentials. Tracked by ADO work item 2167915.
solution: Option 1: Sign in to PC using FIDO2 key instead of WHfB for resources requiring FIDO2 strength. Option 2: Modify CA policy to allow WHfB in addition to FIDO2. Track fix via ADO 2167915.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: FIDO2 security keys appear as Disabled on My Security Info page (aka.ms/mysecurityinfo). Keys continue to work for sign-in despite the Disabled label.
rootCause: UI rendering bug in MySecurityInfo portal incorrectly marks FIDO2 keys as Disabled. Underlying authentication method remains active. Tracked by Incident 627758907.
solution: No action required. Inform users the Disabled label is a visual bug only. Do not delete or re-register keys unless actual sign-in failure. Verify key is Enabled in Entra ID admin view.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Tenant with Passkey Profiles sees Windows Hello (preview) option when editing passkey profile in Entra portal. Feature is not yet in public preview.
rootCause: UI prematurely exposed Windows Hello (Entra Passkeys on Windows) option before feature reached public preview.
solution: Inform customer the feature is not yet available. PG fix to remove this option was in deployment with ETA end of Jan 30 2026.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Cross-device FIDO2 authentication (caBLE) fails on iOS device. Apple sysdiagnose logs show HTTP 400 from cable.auth.com, NSURLErrorDomain Code=-1011, caBLE transaction failed.
rootCause: Connectivity issue to cable.auth.com endpoint. caBLE tunnel service returns HTTP 400 during WebSocket handshake. May be caused by network filtering/proxy/firewall blocking cable.auth.com.
solution: Verify network connectivity to cable.auth.com. Check firewall/proxy rules allow HTTPS to cable.auth.com. Collect Apple sysdiagnose logs for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Data analysis

symptom: FIDO2 security key interactive logon fails with This security key does not look familiar. WebAuthN event log shows CTAP response 0x2E (CTAP2_ERR_NO_CREDENTIALS).
rootCause: Security key has no credentials registered for the relying party (login.microsoft.com). Key was never enrolled, was reset, or credentials deleted.
solution: Re-register the FIDO2 security key at aka.ms/mysecurityinfo. If previously working, check if it was reset. Check WebAuthN Event ID 2225 for CTAP response details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Data analysis

symptom: FIDO2 security key PIN entry fails. CBOR-decoded CTAP2 response shows 0 PIN retries remaining. Key is locked.
rootCause: User exhausted all PIN retry attempts. Key firmware locks PIN entry after maximum retries exceeded.
solution: Reset the FIDO2 security key (WARNING: erases ALL credentials). After reset, re-register at aka.ms/mysecurityinfo. Reset via Windows Settings > Accounts > Sign-in options > Security key > Manage > Reset.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Data analysis

symptom: FIDO2 interactive logon fails with error 0xc000006d / 0xC0048585. AAD Operational Event Log shows Status: 0xC0048585. Environment uses proxy (e.g. Skyhigh Security / McAfee Web Gateway) that blocks NCSI traffic.
rootCause: NCSI (Network Connection Status Indicator) traffic to http://www.msftconnecttest.com/connecttest.txt is blocked by proxy or firewall. Windows incorrectly determines there is no internet access, which breaks FIDO2 interactive logon.
solution: Allow NCSI traffic through proxy/firewall. Ensure http://www.msftconnecttest.com/connecttest.txt is accessible from the device. Follow Microsoft guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/privacy/manage-connections-from-windows-operating-system-components-to-microsoft-services#14-network-connection-status-indicator
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: issues with fixes/FIDO2: blocked NCSI traffic

symptom: FIDO2 security key registration or sign-in fails in browser. Device logs (edge://device-log/ or chrome://device-log/) show CBOR parsing errors with Unexpected CtapDeviceResponseCode: 18 and CBOR decoding of authenticator data extensions failed.
rootCause: Firmware bugs in certain FIDO2 security keys (e.g. Nitrokey fw 2.2 AAGUID c39efba6, Feitian K9 fw 4503 AAGUID ee041bce) produce malformed CBOR responses that fail browser-side parsing.
solution: Update security key firmware if supported (e.g. Nitrokey 2.2 to 2.4). If no firmware update available (e.g. Feitian K9 fw 4503), advise customer to contact vendor for replacement. Verify fix by re-checking device logs after update.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: issues with fixes/FIDO2: buggy security keys

symptom: Cross-device passkey authentication or registration fails with Devices could not connect. WebAuthN Operational log shows Event ID 2336 with error 0x80072EE2 (WININET_E_TIMEOUT) during Ctap Hybrid Protocol setup.
rootCause: Corporate firewall or web proxy blocks access to FIDO2 tunnel service domains: Cable.auth.com (Apple) and Cable.ua5v.com (Google). The cryptsvc service (svchost) running under NT Authority\NetworkService needs machine-level proxy to reach these destinations.
solution: Unblock Cable.auth.com (Apple) and Cable.ua5v.com (Google) in firewall/proxy. For proxy environments, configure machine-level proxy for NetworkService using bitsadmin /util /setieproxy networkservice AUTOSCRIPT, then deploy resulting registry key via GPO. Alternative: use same-device passkey registration in MS Authenticator app.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: issues with fixes/FIDO2: devices couldnt connect

symptom: FIDO2 cached interactive logon fails off corporate network on Windows 11 24H2 Entra hybrid joined devices. Error: Sorry, try that again. There was an issue with the server. Works on-network or with VPN.
rootCause: GPO 'Always wait for the network at computer startup and logon' or 'Run logon scripts synchronously' causes cached FIDO2 logon to fail when device has no line-of-sight to domain controllers (off-corp). Regression observed January 2025 on Windows 11 24H2. Bug 54160686.
solution: Remove or relax the two GPOs on affected devices and retest cached FIDO2 logon off-network. If GPOs are required: enable pre-logon VPN for DC reachability, scope GPOs to exclude FIDO2-dependent devices, or redesign logon scripts to run asynchronously/post-logon.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: issues with fixes/FIDO2: logon fails outside corpnet

symptom: Passkeys not presented as sign-in option in RDP sessions. WebAuthN Operational log shows Event 2103 with Error 0x8001011B (Access is denied) for GetAllPlatformCredentials and GetAssertion on RDP client side; Event 1005 on destination.
rootCause: Windows 11 passkeys privacy settings have been disabled for the relevant app/website (e.g. Entra ID). This blocks enumeration and use of passkeys within RDP sessions.
solution: Enable passkeys privacy settings in Windows Settings app for the relevant app/website. For RDP sessions, check and enable settings on the RDP client side. Navigate to Windows Settings > Privacy > Passkeys and ensure the target is enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: issues with fixes/FIDO2: passkeys privacy settings

symptom: Adding security key at aka.ms/mysecurityinfo fails immediately without prompt. HAR shows POST to /api/post/newfido with error_code=RangeError and Too many credentials.
rootCause: User has too many orphaned Windows Hello for Business (WHfB) credentials that count toward the 10-key registration limit. Orphaned WHfB entries are remnants of device reimages/replacements or stale join states.
solution: Remove orphaned WHfB credentials using WHfBTools PowerShell module: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/using-whfbtools-powershell-module-for-cleaning-up-orphaned-windows-hello-for-business-keys-779d1f3f-bb2d-c495-0f6b-9aeb940eeafb. After cleanup and directory propagation, retry enrollment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: issues with fixes/FIDO2: rangeerror add securitykeys

symptom: Repeated PIN prompts when enrolling YubiKey credential, enrollment never completes. WebAuthN Operational log (Event 2225) shows Response: 03 (CTAP1_ERR_INVALID_LENGTH) during makeCredential.
rootCause: User principal name (UPN) exceeds 64 characters. YubiKey rejects the makeCredential request when user.name length exceeds the limit. CTAP spec error 0x03 = CTAP1_ERR_INVALID_LENGTH.
solution: Shorten UPN to 64 characters or fewer (reduce alias or domain suffix). After directory propagation, retry YubiKey credential enrollment. Verify fix by checking WebAuthN logs for absence of Response: 03.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: issues with fixes/FIDO2: yubikey provision fail

symptom: Upload of CA certificates fails in the Entra portal Certificate authorities blade for new tenants. Error shows 'Certificate uploading failed' with no HTTP activity in browser trace.
rootCause: Bug 1940514 - portal issue occurs when no CA created via PowerShell previously exists in the tenant
solution: Use PowerShell to upload CA certificates as a workaround. The bug fix has been deployed to production.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA: Limitations and known issues

symptom: Federated user with WHfB certificate trust gets stuck in infinite sign-in loop connecting to AVD/RDP session host. Selects account at credential prompt but loops back. Sign-in logs show SF auth Success + MFA satisfied by claim. ASC shows TokenBrokerAddAccountProgress ErrorNumber 0.
rootCause: User not assigned Azure RBAC Virtual Machine Administrator Login or Virtual Machine User Login role on target VM.
solution: Add user to Virtual Machine Administrator Login or Virtual Machine User Login RBAC role on target VM/resource group/subscription.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/AVD or RDP Sign in using WHfB or FIDO2/AVD or RDP Sign in using WHfB or FIDO2 Limitations known issues

symptom: After disabling WHfB via GPO, previously configured PCs still have WHfB credentials and users can still sign in with PIN/biometrics.
rootCause: GPO Disabled only prevents new provisioning. Does not remove existing NGC container and biometric data.
solution: 1. Set GPO Disabled. 2. Each machine: elevated certutil -deleteHelloContainer. 3. Restart PC. Check keys first: certutil -user -key -csp ngc.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O1 Disabling WHfB

symptom: Users click I Forgot My PIN on logon screen and get error: This feature is not supported in your organization.
rootCause: PIN reset service not configured or client lacks internet access.
solution: Verify PIN Reset Service config per MS docs. Ensure client has internet access to endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O2 PIN reset from logon screen

symptom: WHfB PIN reset from logon screen does not work on Windows 10 Professional.
rootCause: Requires Windows 10 Enterprise build 1709+. Pro edition not supported.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10 Enterprise build 1709 or later.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O2 PIN reset from logon screen

symptom: Users who set WHfB PIN before PIN reset policy applied cannot use I Forgot My PIN from logon screen.
rootCause: Recovery key not encrypted with PIN Reset Service public key during initial provisioning before policy.
solution: Reset PIN manually first: Settings > Accounts > Sign In options > PIN > Change > I forgot My Pin. This re-encrypts recovery key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O2 PIN reset from logon screen

symptom: WHfB provisioning fails migrating Key Trust to Certificate Trust. Event 362: authenticated to enterprise STS: No. AAD Event 1118 0xC0000250, Event 1081 interaction_required MSIS9699. ADFS Event 1021 MSIS9462.
rootCause: Microsoft Passport Authentication not enabled in ADFS Global Auth Policy.
solution: ADFS > Services > Auth Methods > Edit Primary > Intranet: enable Microsoft Passport Authentication.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O3 WHfB move from KT to CT

symptom: After AD schema upgrade to 2016, Key Admins (RID 526) and Enterprise Key Admins (RID 527) show as SIDs not friendly names.
rootCause: RID 526/527 need Windows Server 2016 DC holding PDC Emulator FSMO role to resolve.
solution: Move PDC Emulator to 2016 DC: Move-ADDirectoryServerOperationMasterRole -Identity <DC> -OperationMasterRole PDCEmulator.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O4 Key admins not shown after schema upgrade

symptom: Users migrated from one AD domain/forest to another cannot authenticate using WHfB PIN.
rootCause: WHfB keys bound to original domain identity, invalid after migration.
solution: 1. certutil -deletehellocontainer. 2. If device migrated: dsregcmd /leave. 3. Migrate. 4. dsregcmd /join. 5. Re-enroll WHfB with new UPN.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O5 User device migrated from different forest

symptom: WHfB provisioning fails with 0x801C0451 (DSREG_E_USER_TOKEN_SWITCH_ACCOUNT) after migration or account switch.
rootCause: Stale WAM token cache from previous account causes token mismatch.
solution: Clear WAM cache: delete BrokerPlugin TokenBroker Accounts files or use M365 method. Reboot. For NgcSet:Yes: certutil -deleteHelloContainer. RCA: WAM team aka.ms/WAMHot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O5 User device migrated from different forest

symptom: Passwordless Authenticator sign-in with login_hint fails for managed accounts — user is prompted for password instead of Authenticator notification
rootCause: PSI with login_hint is not supported for managed accounts. Only SurfaceHub supports login_hint with PSI. Work on this feature is in progress.
solution: Remove login_hint from the sign-in request, or have users enter their username manually to trigger the passwordless flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: PSI registration succeeds but feature becomes disabled immediately after first use when Alternate Login ID is enabled. Error in Authenticator logs: 'Keystore does not contain a key pair with the expected alias'
rootCause: By design — Broker component uses preferred_username claim from IdToken to set userName. Applications using preferred_username are not supported with Alternate Login ID feature.
solution: Alternate Login ID and PSI are incompatible. Do not use both simultaneously. See public docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/howto-authentication-use-email-signin#preview-limitations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: User completes number match for PSI but gets error 130500: 'Your company policy requires that you use a different method to sign in. Disable phone sign-in on your account in Microsoft Authenticator.' Sign-in log shows error code 130500 (PhoneSignInBlockedByUserCredentialPolicy).
rootCause: User is not in scope of the Microsoft Authenticator passwordless sign-in policy (Default User Credential Policy).
solution: In the Microsoft Authenticator passwordless sign-in policy, verify the user or a group containing the user is included. If 'Select users' targets a group, verify user membership. User can also remove credential from Authenticator app via 'Disable phone sign-in' or delete from aka.ms/mysecurityinfo.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: Federated users with PSI enabled bypass ADFS sign-in — always prompted for Authenticator notification instead of being redirected to ADFS. On-premises CA policies and Pass-through authentication stop working.
rootCause: PSI notification logic prevents federated users from being redirected to ADFS. PSI only works for tenants syncing password hashes to Entra ID, bypassing ADFS entirely.
solution: Ensure password hash sync is enabled for PSI users. Users needing ADFS workflows should click 'Use your password instead'. Exception: if login_hint is specified, user is autoforwarded to ADFS bypassing PSI credential.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: User stops receiving PSI push notifications on device after denying login requests twice within 24 hours.
rootCause: Anti-abuse throttling mechanism — system stops sending push notifications after 2 denials within a 24-hour period to prevent MFA fatigue attacks.
solution: User can open Authenticator app and select 'Check for notifications' to manually retrieve the pending prompt.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: After accepting Terms of Use (ToU) Conditional Access policy, PSI sign-in fails on iPhone with error 'Something went wrong. Please try again later' in Authenticator app — occurs at the PIN/biometric step after entering correct number match.
rootCause: Known bug with ToU Conditional Access Policies and PSI interaction on iOS (tracked in Bug 628199).
solution: Fixed in Bug 628199. Update Microsoft Authenticator app to the latest version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: Error 'The policy did not save successfully' when saving Microsoft Authenticator authentication method policy. Browser trace shows 400 Bad Request for PATCH call to graph.microsoft.com/beta/policies/authenticationmethodspolicy/authenticationMethodConfigurations/MicrosoftAuthenticator.
rootCause: Too many individual users or groups being added to the policy in a single save operation.
solution: Replace individual users/groups with a single group containing all target users, then click Save again in the same operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: Users with multiple passwordless accounts on one Android device intermittently fail to receive PSI authentication notifications.
rootCause: Company Portal app installed on the device takes over as broker instead of Authenticator app. Multi-account PSI requires updated broker library protocol that Company Portal has not yet onboarded to. Check Authenticator logs for 'isAuthenticatorBroker: false' to confirm.
solution: Uninstall Company Portal to ensure Authenticator app is used as broker. This conflict is resolved at GA when Company Portal onboards the new broker library. On Android 6.2404.2872+, multiple PSI accounts are supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: Passwordless sign-in notifications not appearing on user device when sign-in originates from unfamiliar location. Failed MFA sign-in event recorded in Entra Sign-in logs. ESTS Kusto shows PSIBLK flag in RamAdhocDebuggingInfo.
rootCause: By design — Entra ID smart lockout blocks PSI notifications from unfamiliar locations to prevent MFA fatigue attacks. Location becomes 'familiar' after 14 days or 10 logins. Determined by D Protection ESTS Risk Assessment Module (RAM).
solution: If user initiated the sign-in from an unfamiliar location, open Authenticator app and swipe down to see the prompt and enter number matching code manually. Location familiarity builds over 14 days / 10 logins. Check ESTS Kusto: AllPerRequestTable | where RamAdhocDebuggingInfo contains 'PSIBLK' to confirm risk-based blocking.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/Whats New

symptom: AADSTS50107: The requested federation realm object does not exist - occurs during B2B SAML/WS-Fed guest sign-in
rootCause: Three possible causes: (1) IDP-initiated sign-on attempted, which is not supported by Entra External ID SAML Federation; (2) No matching SAML/WS-Fed federation configuration found in the resource tenant for the realm in the token; (3) External SAML IDP sent SAMLResponse to wrong endpoint (e.g., /common instead of /login.srf)
solution: (1) Use SP-initiated sign-on via Entra supported endpoints only; (2) Verify federation config exists in resource tenant via ASC Graph Explorer: GET /directory/federationConfigurations/graph.samlOrWsFedExternalDomainFederation; (3) Ensure IDP sends SAMLResponse to https://login.microsoftonline.com/login.srf (workforce) or https://<tenantID>.ciamlogin.com/login.srf (External ID)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration - Federation with SAML or WS-Fed identity providers for guest users

symptom: AADSTS5000819: Email address claim is missing or does not match domain from an external realm / AADSTS500089: SAML 2.0 assertion validation failed - during B2B SAML federation sign-in
rootCause: SAML Response from external IDP is missing required attributes or sent to incorrect destination URL. Required: (1) Correct Destination URL, (2) NameID with email format, (3) emailaddress claim attribute, (4) AudienceRestriction matching https://login.microsoftonline.com/<tenantID>/
solution: Capture HAR/Fiddler trace, decode SAMLResponse sent to login.microsoftonline.com/login.srf, verify all 4 required SAML attributes are present with correct values. If missing, reconfigure the external SAML IDP to emit them. Also verify email domain matches a configured Entra SAML federated identity provider.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration - Federation with SAML or WS-Fed identity providers for guest users

symptom: AADSTS5000811: Unable to verify token signature. The signing key identifier does not match any valid registered keys - during B2B SAML federation sign-in
rootCause: SAML Response signed with a certificate that does not match the signing certificate configured in the Entra External ID SAML Federation configuration
solution: (1) Capture Fiddler trace, decode SAMLResponse, extract X509Certificate value; (2) Compare with signingCertificate in ASC Graph Explorer GET /directory/federationConfigurations/graph.samlOrWsFedExternalDomainFederation; (3) If mismatch, customer contacts SAML IDP for updated metadata XML and reconfigures Entra SAML Federation signing certificate per docs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration - Federation with SAML or WS-Fed identity providers for guest users

symptom: Invitation redemption failed - An error has occurred. Please retry again shortly - on invitations.microsoft.com during B2B SAML federation invite redemption
rootCause: Underlying AADSTS authentication error on login.microsoftonline.com/login.srf is relayed to invitations.microsoft.com, causing a generic failure message
solution: Capture HAR/Fiddler, inspect invitations.microsoft.com request Webforms section to find the relayed AADSTS error code and correlation ID. Troubleshoot the root cause AADSTS error (commonly AADSTS50107, AADSTS5000819, AADSTS5000811). Use ASC Auth Troubleshooter with correlation ID for further analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration - Federation with SAML or WS-Fed identity providers for guest users

symptom: B2B invite redemption prompt: You are currently signed in as user1@domain.com, however, user2@domain.com was invited. Do you want to switch? - during SAML federation invitation redemption
rootCause: Email claim in the SAMLResponse (http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/emailaddress) does not match the invited guest user's email address in mail/proxyAddress attribute
solution: Capture HAR/Fiddler, decode SAMLResponse, check emailaddress attribute value matches the invited email found in the Guest user object's mail/proxyAddress. If mismatch, either the IDP needs to emit the correct email or the invitation was sent to the wrong address.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration - Federation with SAML or WS-Fed identity providers for guest users

symptom: B2B guest users with SAML/WS-Fed federation are redirected to an Entra tenant login page instead of the configured SAML federated Identity Provider
rootCause: Guest's domain (e.g., contoso.com) is a DNS-verified domain on another Entra tenant. Default redemption order prioritizes Entra (azureActiveDirectory) above SAML/WS-Fed federation (externalFederation)
solution: (1) Verify SAML federation config exists via ASC Graph Explorer; (2) Confirm domain is Entra-verified by checking https://login.microsoftonline.com/contoso.com/.well-known/openid-configuration; (3) Resource tenant admin reorders redemption order via Cross-Tenant Access Settings to prioritize externalFederation above azureActiveDirectory. Verify via GET /policies/crossTenantAccessPolicy/default?$select=invitationRedemptionIdentityProviderConfiguration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration - Federation with SAML or WS-Fed identity providers for guest users

symptom: Unable to configure SAML/WS-Fed direct federation for a domain - API error: Invalid domain. Domain should match the passiveSignInUri
rootCause: Known bug: when the federation domain (e.g., mydomain.com) differs from the passive authentication endpoint domain (e.g., login.mydomain.com), the API rejects the configuration. Also fails if the domain is DNS-verified on Azure AD or auth URL domain is not in the allowed list.
solution: Workaround for domain mismatch bug: add DNS TXT record in format DirectFedPassiveSignInUri=https://login.mydomain.com/adfs (note: must use DirectFedPassiveSignInUri= format, not DirectFedAuthUrl= as shown in some docs). Also verify: (1) domain is not DNS-verified on another Azure AD tenant; (2) auth URL domain matches target domain or is in allowed list (accounts.google.com, pingidentity.com, login.pingone.com, okta.com, my.salesforce.com).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration - Federation with SAML or WS-Fed identity providers for guest users

symptom: Google Workspace SAML federated B2B guest user gets 403 error: app_not_configured_for_user / Service is not configured for this user
rootCause: Google Workspace does not have a SAML relying party application configured with the correct Entra Entity ID (https://login.microsoftonline.com/<tenant id>), or the application is not enabled for the authenticating user in Google admin console
solution: Google Workspace IDP admin follows https://support.google.com/a/answer/6301076 to: (1) Create/configure SAML app with Entity ID set to https://login.microsoftonline.com/<tenant id> where tenant ID is the Entra tenant with SAML federation configured; (2) Enable the app for all users or specifically for the authenticating user in Google admin console
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration - Federation with SAML or WS-Fed identity providers for guest users

symptom: B2B invitation fails due to proxyAddress or mail attribute conflict — invited user's email matches existing user/group/contact in the inviting tenant
rootCause: ProxyAddress or mail attribute conflict: the invited email address already exists on another User, Group, or Contact object's mail or proxyAddresses attribute in the inviting tenant
solution: In Azure Support Center → Tenant Explorer, search Users/Groups/Contacts in the inviting tenant for the invited email address. If a match is found, check the object's mail and proxyAddresses for the conflicting attribute. Customer must remove the conflict before re-inviting the user.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B collaboration TSG: Searching B2B Service Logs

symptom: B2B service logs on aadb2bprod.westus2 Kusto cluster are PII-redacted since August 2023; unable to see meaningful PII details (email addresses) in B2B troubleshooting logs
rootCause: PII redaction policy applied to B2B service logs starting 2023-08, making Kusto query results not useful for identifying specific users
solution: Track scenarios/ICMs/Cases in CSS AAD Log Access Tracking Spreadsheet (https://aka.ms/cssaadlogaccess). For PII details, request B2B PG or TA to run Geneva/Jarvis queries — requires AME/AAD-TA security group membership and SAW access with ame.gbl accounts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B collaboration TSG: Searching B2B Service Logs

symptom: Azure AD B2C returns HTTP 429 throttling error — customer traffic exceeds per-tenant or per-IP RPS limits (Gateway throttling or B2C downstream service throttling)
rootCause: B2C request rate exceeds Azure AD B2C service throttling limits. Two types: Gateway throttling (AAD Gateway layer) and B2C throttling (downstream services like SMS/Phone MFA)
solution: 1) Collect tenant ID, policy name, timestamps + correlation IDs of 429 errors. 2) Use ASC troubleshooter: [Identity][B2C] Scoping TSG → B2C Tenant Throttling HTTP 429 Errors. 3) For rate limit increase: customer must first enable B2C Custom Domain + integrate WAF partner (Akamai/Azure WAF/CloudFlare) with IP rate limiting and bot protection. 4) Under 500 RPS increase can bypass WAF requirement. 5) Raise ICM with CID TA Triage using template (tmpl=Y1j3q3). 6) Pricing: ~$10K/mo for 500 RPS, ~$25K/mo for 1500 RPS (proposal, not finalized).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C tenant throttling, service limit increase and default domain block handbook

symptom: Azure AD B2C custom policy XML file exceeds 1024 KB (1 MB) size limit — unable to upload policy
rootCause: Single B2C custom policy file exceeds the default 1024 KB maximum policy file size limit
solution: Raise ICM to request policy file size increase to 2 MB. No additional cost to customer. Example ICM: incident/224729690.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C tenant throttling, service limit increase and default domain block handbook

symptom: Azure AD B2C tenant count per subscription exceeds default limit of 20 — cannot create more B2C tenants
rootCause: Default Azure AD B2C tenant creation limit is 20 per subscription
solution: Raise ICM to request B2C tenant creation limit increase. Example ICM: incident/500873133.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C tenant throttling, service limit increase and default domain block handbook
