Ensure root CA cert in Trusted Root store. 6. Wait 30 min after PIN provisioning for AAD Connect sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S2 Unable to access onprem resources AADJ

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust: Login with PIN fails with error 'The request is not supported'. KDC Event ID 19 (PKINIT missing suitable certificate) or Event ID 29 (KDC cannot find suitable certificate for smart card logon) on Domain Controller.
rootCause: Domain Controller does not have a certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU, or has multiple certificates and is using an older one without the correct EKU. Alternatively, the issuing CA certificate is not in the NTAuth store.
solution: 1. Verify 2016 DC has cert with Kerberos Authentication EKU. 2. If multiple certs exist, import new cert to 'Active Directory Domain Service' store via MMC and restart DC to clear cache. 3. Verify NTAuth store: certutil -viewstore -enterprise NTAuth. 4. Export DC cert (.cer) and validate: certutil -verify -urlfetch <cert.cer>. 5. Fix any revocation or root trust failures. 6. Enable CAPI2 Operational logs if still failing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S3 The request is not supported error

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust: Sign-in with PIN fails with error 0x00000bb. User provisioned PIN successfully but cannot authenticate.
rootCause: msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute not populated on user object in on-premises AD. Possible causes: (1) AAD Connect delta sync not completed (need 30 min minimum), (2) AAD Connect schema not updated to include msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute, (3) machine cannot locate/communicate with Server 2016+ DC.
solution: 1. Wait 30 min and trigger delta sync on AAD Connect server. 2. Check msDS-KeyCredentialLink on user: if empty after sync, verify AAD Connect schema includes the attribute ($ADConnector = Get-ADSyncConnector | ? {$_.name -notmatch '.onmicrosoft.com'} | Select -expand AttributeInclusionList). 3. Refresh schema in AAD Connect if needed. 4. Verify client can reach 2016+ DC. 5. Ensure DC cert in Trusted Root store on client. 6. If still failing, collect NGC trace via Auth trace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S4 Error message 0x00000bb sign in with PIN

symptom: WHfB: Login with PIN fails with 'Sign-in failed. The KDC certificate could not be validated.' Kerberos Event ID 9 on client: 'The client has failed to validate the domain controller certificate... The revocation function was unable to check revocation because the revocation server was offline.'
rootCause: The CRL (Certificate Revocation List) distribution point for the DC certificate issuing CA is unreachable from the client, or the CRL has expired and not been republished.
solution: 1. Verify the CRL distribution point URL is accessible from the client. 2. Check CRL validity dates. 3. If connectivity valid, republish the CRL from the issuing CA. 4. Verify with: certutil -verify -urlfetch <exported-dc-cert.cer>.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: WHfB: All options under Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options are grayed out. Users cannot change or reset PIN, set up fingerprint, etc.
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions has Value set to 0, which disables all sign-in options. This may be deployed via Group Policy or baked into the base/golden image.
solution: Set the registry key Value to 1: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions\Value = 1. Check if this is being set by Group Policy and update the GPO accordingly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust cross-forest: Login fails with 'Credential cannot be verified' when user logs in with UPN (e.g. BOB@MYDOMAIN.COM) but succeeds with DOMAIN\USERNAME format. Environment has devices in one forest and users in another forest with trust.
rootCause: When credentials are serialized in UPN format, the Kerberos client attempts to locate a Server 2016 DC in the device's domain (which may only have 2012 R2 DCs). When using DOMAIN\USERNAME, Kerberos client correctly locates a 2016 DC in the user's domain. NGC cache behavior affects credential serialization format.
solution: Deploy Windows Server 2016 or above DCs in ALL realms in the trust referral path between devices and users (not just the user's domain). This is the supported configuration per Microsoft WHfB requirements. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/hello-for-business/hello-identity-verification
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S6 WHfB sign in cross tenant

symptom: WHfB PIN registration fails with error 0x800706d9 after completing MFA successfully. Process Monitor shows 'Access Denied' when writing to registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Authentication\LogonUI\NgcPin. No WHFB sign-in options appear for manual setup.
rootCause: The NgcCtnrSvc (Microsoft Passport Container) service was disabled on the affected machine. This service is required for WHfB PIN operations as it manages the Hello container where the private key associated with the PIN is stored.
solution: 1. Set registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NgcCtnrSvc\Start to 3 (Manual). 2. Restart the machine. 3. The Microsoft Passport Container service will start successfully. 4. WHfB sign-in options will appear and PIN provisioning will succeed. Tip: Always verify NgcCtnrSvc and NgcSvc service status early in WHfB troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S8 WHFB PIN Registration Failure (Error 0x800706d9)

symptom: Windows 24H2/25H2 behavior change: FIDO2 security keys without a PIN set are no longer functional for authentication after October patch.
rootCause: Windows 24H2 introduced a behavior change requiring PIN on FIDO2 security keys. However, Entra ID is NOT impacted because it requires User Verification (UV) for passkey/FIDO2 registration, meaning keys are always registered with a PIN.
solution: If the case involves FIDO2 keys without PIN in non-Entra scenarios (home-grown apps, custom IDPs, non-Entra web auth), route to Directory Services team: Windows/Windows 11/{version}/Windows Security Technologies/Biometric, Passwordless Authentication, SSO, and Windows Hello. Entra ID cases are not affected by this change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S9 Windows 24H2 Security Key PIN Requirement Issue

symptom: WHfB PIN Reset: User clicks 'I Forgot My PIN' on login screen and gets error 'This feature is not supported in your organization. Please contact your administrator'.
rootCause: Either (1) PIN Reset Service is not properly configured, (2) client has no internet access to reach PIN Reset Service, or (3) user set their PIN before the PIN reset policy was applied (recovery key was not encrypted with PIN Reset Service public key).
solution: 1. Verify PIN Reset Service configuration per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/hello-for-business/pin-reset. 2. Ensure client has internet access. 3. If PIN was set before policy: user must first reset PIN from Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options > PIN > Change > I forgot My PIN. After this reset, PIN Reset Service public key encrypts the recovery key, and login-screen PIN reset will work next time. Note: PIN reset from login screen requires Windows 10 1709+ Enterprise (not Professional).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Built in features

symptom: WHfB login fails with The request is not supported error on hybrid key trust deployment
rootCause: Domain controller does not have a certificate with the Kerberos Authentication EKU, or older certificates are being used instead of the correct one. Can also occur if CRL is not current or reachable.
solution: 1) Ensure 2016 DCs have cert with Kerberos Authentication EKU. 2) If multiple certs exist, import new cert to AD DS certificate store via MMC and restart DC to clear lsass cache. 3) Ensure CRL is current and reachable. 4) Verify issuing cert is in NTAuth store: certutil -viewstore -enterprise NTAuth.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S3 The request is not supported error

symptom: KDC Event ID 19/29 on DC: KDC is unable to use PKINIT protocol or cannot find suitable certificate for smart card logons
rootCause: DC cannot validate its own certificate due to CRL revocation check failure, root trust failure, or issuing CA certificate not in NTAuth store.
solution: 1) Export DC cert as .cer, run certutil -verify -urlfetch. 2) Fix revocation or root trust issues. 3) Verify issuing cert in NTAuth store. 4) Enable CAPI2 logs for further analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S3 The request is not supported error

symptom: WHfB PIN login fails with KDC certificate could not be validated - client Event ID 9: revocation server was offline
rootCause: CRL Distribution Point for the DC certificate is not reachable from client, or CRL is expired/not published.
solution: Verify CRL is reachable and up to date from the client machine. If connectivity is valid, republish CRL from the issuing CA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: All options under Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options are grayed out - cannot change/reset WHfB PIN
rootCause: Registry AllowSignInOptions Value set to 0, disabling all sign-in options. May be deployed via Group Policy or golden image.
solution: Set registry AllowSignInOptions Value to 1. Check if Group Policy or base image is enforcing Value=0.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: WHfB hybrid key trust cross-forest: Login fails with Credential cannot be verified when using UPN format, but works with DOMAIN\username
rootCause: NGC credential provider serializes UPN-format credentials differently - Kerberos client locates DC in device domain (no 2016+ DCs) instead of user domain.
solution: Deploy Windows Server 2016+ DCs in ALL realms in the trust referral path. Workaround: login with DOMAIN\USERNAME format.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S6 WHfB sign in cross tenant

symptom: WHFB PIN registration fails with error 0x800706d9 after completing MFA - NgcCtnrSvc service disabled
rootCause: NgcCtnrSvc (Microsoft Passport Container) service is disabled. Handles private key storage in Hello container. When disabled, Access Denied occurs writing to NgcPin registry.
solution: Set registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NgcCtnrSvc Start=3 (Manual). Restart machine. Always check NgcCtnrSvc and NgcSvc service status first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S8 WHFB PIN Registration Failure (Error 0x800706d9)

symptom: Windows 24H2 FIDO2 security key behavior change - keys without PIN no longer work after October patch
rootCause: Windows 24H2/25H2 October patch introduced mandatory PIN for FIDO2 keys. Entra ID NOT impacted (requires UV for registration).
solution: Entra ID NOT impacted. Route to Directory Services team. Affects home-grown apps, custom IDPs, non-Entra scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S9 Windows 24H2 Security Key PIN Requirement Issue

symptom: WHfB PIN Reset: I Forgot My PIN on login screen returns This feature is not supported in your organization
rootCause: PIN Reset Service not configured, or client has no internet access to reach the endpoint.
solution: Verify PIN Reset Service config. Ensure client has internet connectivity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Built in features

symptom: WHfB PIN Reset from login screen does not work on Windows 10 Professional edition
rootCause: PIN Reset from login screen requires Windows 10/11 Enterprise edition (build 1709+). Professional SKU not supported.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10/11 Enterprise edition.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Built in features

symptom: WHfB PIN Reset from login screen fails after policy configured - works from Settings but not lock screen
rootCause: User provisioned PIN before PIN Reset policy was applied. Recovery key not encrypted with PIN Reset Service public key.
solution: Reset PIN from Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options > PIN > Change > I forgot My PIN first. This re-encrypts recovery key. Subsequent lock-screen resets will work.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Built in features

symptom: Hybrid user unable to access on-premises resources from Azure AD Joined PC using Windows Hello for Business (Key Trust). Login works but local/AD resources are inaccessible.
rootCause: Missing or misconfigured domain controller certificate (Kerberos Authentication EKU), user public key not synced back from AAD to on-prem AD via AAD Connect (msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute), CRL not accessible from client, or root CA certificate not in client trusted store.
solution: 1) Ensure DC has certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU per docs. 2) Verify public key writeback via repadmin /showobjmeta. 3) Verify DC is locatable via nltest /dsgetdc:FQDN. 4) Ensure root CA cert is in client trusted root store. 5) Verify CRL accessibility. 6) Wait 30 min after provisioning for AAD Connect sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S2 Unable to access onprem resources AADJ

symptom: WHfB sign-in fails with error 'The request is not supported'. System event log shows KDC Event ID 19 or 29 indicating KDC cannot find suitable certificate for smart card logon.
rootCause: Domain controller lacks certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU, or DC is using an older certificate instead of the correct one. DC certificate may fail validation (revocation check failure, root trust failure, or issuing CA not in NTAuth store).
solution: 1) Verify 2016 DC has certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU. 2) If multiple certs exist, import correct cert to AD DS store and restart DC. 3) Verify CRL is accessible via certutil -verify -urlfetch. 4) Verify issuing CA is in NTAuth store via certutil -viewstore -enterprise NTAuth. 5) Enable CAPI2 logs for further diagnostics.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S3 The request is not supported error

symptom: WHfB PIN provisioning succeeds but sign-in with PIN fails with error 'Something went wrong, 0x00000bb' in Hybrid Azure AD Key Trust deployment.
rootCause: msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute not synced from AAD to on-prem AD (AAD Connect delta sync not completed or schema not updated), client unable to reach Server 2016 DC, or DC certificate validation failure.
solution: 1) Wait 30 min for AAD Connect sync. 2) Trigger delta sync on AAD Connect server. 3) Verify msDS-KeyCredentialLink is populated on user object. 4) If not, refresh AAD Connect schema. 5) Verify client can reach 2016 DC. 6) Verify DC certificate trust chain and CRL. 7) If still failing, collect NGC tracing within Auth trace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S4 Error message 0x00000bb sign in with PIN

symptom: WHfB PIN login fails with 'the KDC certificate could not be validated'. System event log shows Kerberos Event ID 9: 'The revocation function was unable to check revocation because the revocation server was offline.'
rootCause: CRL Distribution Point (CDP) for the domain controller certificate is unreachable or the published CRL is expired/stale.
solution: Verify the CRL is reachable from the client. If connectivity is valid, republish the CRL from the issuing CA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: All options under Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options are grayed out. Users cannot change/reset WHfB PIN from settings.
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions has Value set to 0, disabling all sign-in options. May be deployed via Group Policy or baked into the golden image.
solution: Set the registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions Value to 1. Check if a Group Policy is enforcing this setting or if the base image has it set to 0.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust login fails with 'Credential cannot be verified' in cross-forest trust deployment when user logs in using UPN format (user@domain.com). Same user logging in with DOMAIN\USERNAME format succeeds.
rootCause: NGC credential provider serializes credentials in UPN format, causing Kerberos client to look for a Server 2016 DC in the device domain (which may only have 2012 R2 DCs). When using DOMAIN\USERNAME, Kerberos correctly locates a 2016 DC in the user domain.
solution: Deploy Windows Server 2016 or above domain controllers in ALL realms in the trust referral path between the device domain and the user domain. This is a PG-confirmed requirement for WHfB Hybrid Key Trust in cross-forest scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S6 WHfB sign in cross tenant

symptom: FIDO2 security keys without PIN stop working after Windows 24H2 update (October 2025 patch). Users prompted for PIN on security keys that previously worked without one.
rootCause: Windows 24H2 behavior change requiring PIN on FIDO2 security keys. Entra ID is NOT impacted because it always requires User Verification (UV) for passkey/FIDO2 registration. Affected scenarios: home-grown apps, custom IDPs, non-Entra web auth where UV was optional.
solution: If using Entra ID: not impacted (UV always required). For non-Entra scenarios: route case to Directory Services team with SAP: Windows/.../Windows Security Technologies/Biometric, Passwordless Authentication, SSO, and Windows Hello.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S9 Windows 24H2 Security Key PIN Requirement Issue

symptom: Customer asks if WHfB key-trust self-signed certificate 30-year validity period can be changed
rootCause: The WHfB key-trust certificate validity period is non-configurable by design. It is a self-signed certificate not trusted by any entity.
solution: Inform customer the validity is non-configurable. The self-signed cert is not trusted externally, so the long validity is not a security concern.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: WHfB policies configured via both Group Policy and Intune produce unexpected results (e.g., wrong minimum PIN length)
rootCause: When GPO and Intune both configure WHfB policies, GPO takes precedence over Intune for conflicting settings.
solution: Use only one management method (GPO or Intune) for WHfB policies to avoid conflicts. If both must be used, ensure GPO settings match desired outcome since GPO wins.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User cannot find REMOVE button for PIN in Windows Settings and needs to remove WHfB PIN
rootCause: The REMOVE button may not be visible depending on policy configuration.
solution: Ensure user can logon with username/password first. Run 'certutil -deleteHelloContainer' under user context to delete Hello container. Before deleting, run 'certutil -user -key -csp ngc' to check what keys exist (other keys may need reprovisioning).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User forgot PIN and wants to reset from the Windows logon screen
rootCause: PIN reset from logon screen requires Windows 10 Enterprise edition and the PIN reset service configured in Azure AD.
solution: For Enterprise: configure PIN reset service per MS docs. For non-Enterprise: user must logon with password, go to Settings > Account > Sign-in options > I forgot my PIN (destructive reset, ~30 min before new PIN usable).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User does not know password and asks for destructive PIN reset from Windows logon screen
rootCause: Destructive PIN reset from logon screen is not supported by design. It would destroy the credential and prevent logon.
solution: User must recover password first, then logon with password to perform PIN reset via Settings. Destructive reset cannot be done from logon screen.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Devices are both AAD Registered and Hybrid Azure AD Joined, causing nondeterministic Conditional Access evaluation
rootCause: AAD Registered + Hybrid Joined is an unsupported dual state. Hybrid join usually takes precedence but dual state can cause nondeterministic device evaluation.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10 v1803+ which auto-cleans the AAD registered state. Do not AAD Register devices that will be Hybrid Joined. If already in dual state, remove the AAD registration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Need to know biometric and PIN failed attempt limits for WHfB lockout behavior
rootCause: Non-configurable limits: Biometric locks after 3 failed matches. PIN allows 4 attempts, then shows captcha (A1B23C), then 1 more attempt, then Windows reboots.
solution: These limits are hardcoded and non-configurable. After biometric lockout, user must sign in with another method. After PIN lockout sequence, device reboots.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: WHfB authentication fails on AADJ or Workplace Joined devices; DC certificate does not have KDC EKU
rootCause: AADJ and standalone/workplace-joined devices require KDC EKU on domain controller certificate for strict KDC validation. Hybrid AADJ trusts KDC via domain join so it works without EKU, but AADJ/WPJ does not.
solution: Deploy DC certificates with KDC EKU using autoenrollment. This ensures WHfB works for all join types. Enable strict KDC validation policy on hybrid devices for additional security.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Customer asks if WHfB is supported with Azure AD Domain Services (AAD DS)
rootCause: Azure AD Domain Services does not have the feature to support WHfB.
solution: WHfB is not supported with Azure AD Domain Services. Customer must use traditional AD DS or consider alternative authentication methods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: WHfB credential queried via Graph API shows null displayName for windowsHelloForBusinessMethods
rootCause: Null displayName indicates either the user is not the registered owner of that device, or the device has been removed from Azure AD.
solution: Use ?$expand=device parameter to get definitive device ID and state. Check device object status in Azure AD. PowerShell: Get-MgUserAuthenticationWindowHelloForBusinessMethod -UserId {upn} -Expand device
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Querying for passwordless credentials via Graph

symptom: WHfB credential queried via Graph API shows device ID as all-zeros (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000)
rootCause: Device ID of all-zeros indicates the device on which the credential was provisioned has been deleted from Azure AD. The credential is orphaned and unusable.
solution: The orphaned credential can be safely removed. Query with ?$expand=device to confirm. Use Graph API or PowerShell to manage credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Querying for passwordless credentials via Graph

symptom: Microsoft Authenticator method queried via Graph API returns device: null for microsoftAuthenticatorMethods
rootCause: Device null for microsoftAuthenticatorMethods indicates the device has been removed from Azure AD.
solution: Confirm device removal in Azure AD. User may need to re-register Authenticator on a current device. Query: GET /users/{id}/authentication/microsoftAuthenticatorMethods?$expand=device. PowerShell: Get-MgUserAuthenticationMicrosoftAuthenticatorMethod -UserId {upn} -Expand device
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Querying for passwordless credentials via Graph

symptom: WHfB policies deployed via both GPO and Intune produce unexpected configuration on Windows client devices (e.g. PIN length differs from Intune setting)
rootCause: When Group Policy and Intune are both used to configure Windows Hello for Business, GPO always takes precedence over Intune settings
solution: Use only one management tool for WHfB configuration. If both are required, be aware GPO settings override Intune (e.g. GPO minimum PIN length 6 overrides Intune minimum PIN length 4). Review and align policies across both management tools.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: PIN REMOVE button no longer appears in Windows Settings menu; user cannot remove Windows Hello PIN
rootCause: UI change or policy removed the REMOVE option from Settings
solution: Confirm user can logon with username/password first (local admin rights required). Run 'certutil -user -key -csp ngc' to inspect Hello container. Then run 'certutil -deleteHelloContainer' under user context to delete all keys. Note: Hello container may contain other keys (WHfB, FIDO2) that will need reprovisioning.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User forgot Windows Hello for Business PIN and cannot sign in; no Enterprise edition for self-service PIN reset from logon screen
rootCause: PIN reset from logon screen requires Windows 10 Enterprise and Azure AD PIN reset service. Without Enterprise, only destructive PIN reset via Settings is available.
solution: With Win10 Enterprise: use PIN reset from logon screen (requires PIN reset service in Azure AD). Without Enterprise: logon with username/password, Settings > Account > Sign-in options > 'I forgot my PIN' (destructive reset, ~30 min wait). Without knowing password: no recovery from logon screen (by design).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Windows Hello for Business does not work with Azure AD Domain Services (AADDS)
rootCause: Azure AD Domain Services does not support Windows Hello for Business. This is a known platform limitation.
solution: WHfB is not supported with AADDS. Use alternative authentication methods or consider on-premises AD with Azure AD Connect for WHfB deployment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Devices AAD Registered via 'Add a Work Account' show nondeterministic auth and CA evaluation after attempting Hybrid Azure AD Join (dual state)
rootCause: AAD Registered + Hybrid AADJ is not a supported device state. Dual registration causes nondeterministic evaluation of device compliance and identity.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10 1803+ which automatically cleans up AAD Registered state when Hybrid Joined. Do not manually attempt both join types. Hybrid AADJ should take precedence but dual state can cause access issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: WHfB authentication fails on AADJ or AAD Registered devices; DC certificate lacks KDC EKU (Enhanced Key Usage)
rootCause: Non-domain-joined devices (AADJ/Workplace Join) enforce Strict KDC validation by default, requiring KDC EKU on DC certificate. Hybrid AADJ trusts KDC via domain membership but AADJ/WPJ does not.
solution: Deploy domain controller certificate with KDC EKU using autoenrollment. Enable Strict KDC validation policy for Hybrid AADJ for consistent behavior. Always deploy KDC certificates with proper EKU regardless of join type to prevent future breakage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User locked out after failed biometric or PIN attempts on Windows Hello; behavior is non-configurable
rootCause: Windows Hello enforces non-configurable lockout: biometric locks after 3 failed matches, PIN allows 4 attempts then requires anti-hammering code
solution: Biometric: locked after 3 failed matches, unlocks after successful sign-in. PIN: 4 attempts, enter anti-hammering code (e.g. A1B23C) for 1 more attempt, then Windows reboots. Not configurable, by design.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Windows Web Sign-in with Passkey (device-bound passkey in MS Authenticator) fails with 'Something went wrong' after scanning QR code
rootCause: Corporate firewall/proxy blocks FIDO2 tunnel service domains: Cable.auth.com (Apple) and Cable.ua5v.com (Google)
solution: Unblock Cable.auth.com (Apple devices) and Cable.ua5v.com (Google/Android devices) in corporate firewall/proxy. Required for cross-device passkey authentication via QR code.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Web Sign-in on Windows

symptom: Customer claims they never received or cannot see a pending Azure Customer Lockbox consent request in the Azure Portal, despite support engineer seeing Pending customer approval in ASC.
rootCause: For Tenant-scoped Lockbox requests (ResourceType=2), only active Entra Global Administrators at request creation time can see the request. For Subscription-scoped requests (ResourceType=1), only active Subscription Owner or Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role holders at subscription scope can see them. Inheriting roles from management groups or resource group scope is NOT sufficient.
solution: 1) In ASC, use F12 Dev Tools to capture GetLockboxConsentRequestStatus API response and identify ResourceType (1=Subscription, 2=Tenant) and ResourceId. 2) For Subscription: verify via Check Access API that user has Owner or Lockbox Approver role at subscription scope. 3) For Tenant: verify user is active Global Administrator. 4) If still unable, collect HAR trace + PSR and submit ICM to Azure LockBox Triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Customer LockBox/TSG Obtaining pending consent request details for Azure Lockbox

symptom: User assigned the Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role cannot see pending Lockbox requests or receive notification emails for subscription-scoped Lockbox requests.
rootCause: The Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role must be assigned and active on the user account BEFORE the Lockbox consent request is created. If assigned after the request is sent, user will not receive the notification and cannot approve.
solution: Ensure Azure Customer Lockbox Approver or Owner role is active at subscription scope prior to request creation. If using PIM, activate the role before the request. Verify Customer Lockbox is enabled from Azure Admin Center. Check activity logs at Customer Lockbox Activity Log blade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Customer Lockbox Approver Role For Subscriptions

symptom: Duplicate Lockbox notification emails are sent when primary email and Other Emails field contain the same email address.
rootCause: Azure Lockbox Alternate Email sends notifications to both primary and other email addresses without deduplication when identical.
solution: Known issue. Ensure primary email and Other Emails values are different to avoid duplicates.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Lockbox Alternate Email

symptom: Lockbox notification emails only sent to first email in Other Emails despite multiple alternate addresses configured.
rootCause: Azure Lockbox Alternate Email feature only sends notifications to the first entry in Other Emails list, ignoring additional entries.
solution: Known issue. Place the most important alternate email as the first entry in Other Emails field.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Lockbox Alternate Email

symptom: Two identical Lockbox notification emails sent to alternate email when primary email field is empty but Other Emails is configured.
rootCause: When primary email not set and only Other Emails is configured, the Lockbox notification system sends two copies to the alternate address.
solution: Known issue. Set a primary email on the user account to avoid duplicate notifications.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Lockbox Alternate Email

symptom: User not receiving Azure Lockbox notification email for pending consent requests despite alternate email configured.
rootCause: Owner/Global Administrator/Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role must be applied to the approver account BEFORE the request is sent. No notification is generated if role was not active at request creation time.
solution: Confirm Lockbox is enabled for the tenant. Verify alternate email in Other Emails property. Ensure Owner/GA/Lockbox Approver role was active before request submission. If using PIM, ensure role was activated prior to request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Lockbox Alternate Email

symptom: Entra Customer Lockbox Tenant Add in ASC error: The tenant is not in the same region as the support case.
rootCause: Case region is determined by EUDB onboarding status, not tenant location. Non-EU case cannot add a remote EU tenant via Tenant Add due to data residency requirements. Only the case-creation tenant is exempted.
solution: Customer must create a separate support case directly from the resource tenant. Tenant Add does not support cross-region scenarios except for the case-creation tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Entra Customer Lockbox Tenant Add in ASC error: Tenant access check request failed with status code: Forbidden.
rootCause: The support case is not open or not assigned to the engineer attempting the tenant add.
solution: Verify the support case is still open and the engineer performing Tenant Add is the assigned case owner.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Entra Customer Lockbox Tenant Add in ASC error: Support case creator is not authorized to approve access to resource tenant data.
rootCause: The customer who opened the support case does not have an external identity (guest account) with a required admin role in the remote tenant.
solution: Case contact must have an external identity in the remote tenant with an approved admin role. Create a guest account from the remote tenant and assign a valid admin role before retrying Tenant Add.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Entra Customer Lockbox Tenant Add in ASC error: Insufficient case information.
rootCause: System cannot retrieve case creator information and is unable to send Entra Lockbox access request.
solution: Verify case has complete contact information. If persists, escalate via ICM to Azure LockBox Triage with case details, tenant ID, and timestamp.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Entra Customer Lockbox Tenant Add in ASC error: Diagnostic data not approved.
rootCause: Customer did not allow collection of diagnostic information when creating the support case.
solution: Ask customer to navigate to their support case page in Azure Portal and give consent under Advanced diagnostic information, then retry Tenant Add.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Customer reports they cannot see pending Customer Lockbox consent request in Azure Portal (Pending Requests blade is empty)
rootCause: For tenant-scoped requests, only active Entra Global Administrators at the time the request was created can see it. For subscription-scoped requests, only active subscription Owners or users with Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role at request creation time can see it. Roles inherited from management group or assigned at resource group scope are NOT sufficient for subscription-level requests.
solution: 1) Use browser DevTools (F12->Network) on ASC Refresh button to inspect GetLockboxConsentRequestStatus API response - check ResourceType (1=Subscription, 2=Tenant), ResourceId, CreatedAtUTC, RequestExpiryUTC. 2) Verify customer has correct roles assigned BEFORE the request was created. 3) For subscription-scoped: Owner or Lockbox Approver must be assigned directly at subscription scope. 4) If still failing: collect HAR trace + PSR, submit ICM to Azure LockBox\Triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Customer LockBox/TSG Obtaining pending consent request details for Azure Lockbox

symptom: User with Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role cannot see or approve subscription-level lockbox requests
rootCause: The Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role was not active on the account before the lockbox request was created. The role must be established before the Lockbox request is created.
solution: Ensure the Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role is assigned and active (including via PIM) BEFORE lockbox requests are sent. Verify: 1) Customer Lockbox is enabled from Azure Admin Center. 2) The role shows as active on the user account. 3) Check Customer Lockbox activity logs for any activity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Customer Lockbox Approver Role For Subscriptions

symptom: Customer receives duplicate Lockbox notification emails when primary email and Other Emails field contain the same email address
rootCause: Known product issue: Lockbox Alternate Email feature sends separate notifications to both primary and alternate email without deduplication check
solution: Known issue, no workaround. Ensure primary email and Other Emails values are different to avoid duplicates.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Lockbox Alternate Email

symptom: Lockbox notifications are sent only to the first email address in Other Emails field despite multiple email addresses configured
rootCause: Known product issue: Lockbox Alternate Email feature only reads the first entry in the Other Emails array
solution: Known issue. Workaround: place the most important alternate email address as the first entry in Other Emails.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Lockbox Alternate Email

symptom: Two duplicate Lockbox notification emails sent to alternate email address when primary email is not set on the user account
rootCause: Known product issue: when primary email is empty and Other Email is set, the system sends two copies of the notification to the alternate address
solution: Known issue. Workaround: set a primary email on the account to avoid duplicate notifications.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Lockbox Alternate Email

symptom: Customer Lockbox alternate email notification not received by the configured alternate email address
rootCause: Required roles (Owner/Global Administrator/Azure Customer Lockbox Approver) were not active on the approver account before the lockbox request was sent, OR Lockbox is not enabled for the tenant, OR alternate email not correctly configured in Other Emails field
solution: Verify: 1) Lockbox is enabled for the tenant. 2) Alternate email is correctly configured in the user Other Emails attribute in Entra ID. 3) Required roles (Owner/Global Admin/Lockbox Approver) were assigned to the approver account BEFORE the request. Also check Using Customer LockBox with PIM guide.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Azure Lockbox Alternate Email

symptom: Entra Lockbox Tenant Add fails with error: The tenant is not in the same region as the support case
rootCause: EUDB data boundary restriction. If customer is not onboarded to EUDB, the case is non-EU, but the resource tenant is EU. Add Tenant across EU/non-EU boundary is not supported due to legal requirements.
solution: Customer must create a separate support case directly from the resource tenant. Cross-region tenant add is not supported by design for EUDB compliance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Entra Lockbox Tenant Add fails with error: Tenant access check request failed with status code Forbidden
rootCause: The support case is not open or is not assigned to the engineer attempting to add the tenant in ASC Tenant Explorer
solution: Verify the support case is still open and assigned to the current engineer. Re-open or reassign the case if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Entra Lockbox Tenant Add fails with error: Support case creator is not authorized to approve access to resource tenant data
rootCause: The customer who opened the support case does not have an external identity with a required admin role in the remote tenant being added
solution: Customer needs to create a guest account in the remote tenant with one of the authorized admin roles for the case creator. The external identity must exist with proper roles before retrying.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Entra Lockbox Tenant Add fails with error: Insufficient case information
rootCause: System cannot retrieve information about the case creator from the support case metadata
solution: Escalate via ICM to Azure LockBox\Triage with case details, tenant IDs, and timestamps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Entra Lockbox Tenant Add fails with error: Diagnostic data not approved
rootCause: Customer did not consent to collection of diagnostic information when creating the support case
solution: Ask customer to give consent in Advanced diagnostic information in their support case page, then retry the tenant add.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Entra Customer Lockbox/Microsoft Entra Customer Lockbox for Tenant Add Scenarios in Azure Support Center (ASC)

symptom: Customer using PIM (Privileged Identity Management) cannot see pending Azure Customer Lockbox request or receive notification email, even after activating Owner/Global Admin role via PIM.
rootCause: When PIM is enabled, the user must have active (elevated) Owner or Global Admin rights AT THE TIME the Microsoft engineer submits the Lockbox access request. If PIM rights are activated after the request is submitted, the request will not appear in the Lockbox blade and no notification email will be sent. Re-adding rights after removal also does not restore visibility of existing requests.
solution: 1) Customer activates GA role (for tenant requests) or Owner/Lockbox Approver role (for subscription requests) via PIM FIRST. 2) Notify the Microsoft engineer that they may now request access. 3) Microsoft engineer submits access request. 4) Temporary GA/Owner can approve via email or Lockbox pending tab in Azure Portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Lockbox Workflow in Pictures

symptom: User with inherited Owner role on a subscription does not receive Azure Customer Lockbox notification email and cannot approve or reject access requests.
rootCause: An inherited Owner role (e.g., from a management group) is NOT recognized by Customer Lockbox for subscription-scoped requests. Only directly assigned Owner, Global Admin, or Azure Customer Lockbox Approver roles at the subscription scope count for Lockbox notifications and approvals.
solution: Assign the Owner role (or Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role) directly at the subscription scope. Do not rely on inherited roles from management groups or resource group scope for Lockbox approval.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Lockbox Workflow in Pictures

symptom: Microsoft engineer Lockbox access request is stuck in pending state with no customer notification, and engineer is blocked from re-requesting access.
rootCause: The Microsoft engineer submitted the Lockbox access request BEFORE the customer enabled PIM or activated their Owner/GA role. The request is stuck pending with no notification sent to any customer admin. The engineer cannot submit a new request until the original one times out after 4 days.
solution: Wait for the stuck request to time out (4-day automatic timeout). Then: 1) Customer enables PIM and activates proper roles first (GA for tenant, Owner/Lockbox Approver for subscription). 2) Notify Microsoft engineer to submit a new access request only after roles are active.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Lockbox Workflow in Pictures

symptom: APIRunner501 alert triggered: API Runner is not receiving at least two 501 NotImplemented responses every 3 minutes for 3 or more of the 5 buckets.
rootCause: Expected behavior is one 501 response per ContextType (tenant, subscription) every 3 minutes for each environment from runnerScenario GetContextNotInWhitelist. When this expected pattern of 501 responses is not met across multiple buckets, the alert fires indicating a potential runner health issue.
solution: Investigate why expected 501 responses are missing. Check if runner scenarios are executing correctly and that GetContextNotInWhitelist scenario is running as expected for both tenant and subscription ContextTypes across all environments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/APIRunner501

symptom: Lockbox runner failure for a request but no corresponding lockbox logs found for that request ID.
rootCause: The issue is at JIT processing level. JIT evaluates the request against DSCM policies to determine auto approve/reject/manual approve. For runner requests the policy should auto-approve. The request may be approved without going through customer notification if the subscription is associated with an internal Microsoft service in Service Tree.
solution: 1) Query JIT Kusto logs by request ID to check request status. 2) Check DSCM policy evaluation at jitaccess.security.core.windows.net/PolicyEvaluationOutcomeAnalysis.html?requestId={id}. 3) Verify subscription in aka.ms/servicetree for internal service association. 4) Search JITProcessorWorkerRole logs for 'shouldsend' or 'topic' to verify Lockbox topic posting. 5) If JIT policy issue confirmed, transfer ICM to JIT team with cluster, request ID, timestamp, and policy evaluation link.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/Debugging no log issues

symptom: Customer with PIM enabled does not receive Customer Lockbox notification email; pending request not visible in Azure portal Lockbox blade
rootCause: When PIM is enabled, if a Microsoft employee requests lockbox access before the customer activates proper Owner/GA rights via PIM, the request does not reach the customer. Even if rights are activated after the request, the customer will not see the pending request or receive an email.
solution: Customer must first activate GA (for tenant requests) or Owner/GA/Azure Customer Lockbox Approver (for subscription requests) via PIM, then notify the Microsoft engineer to submit the access request. The sequence matters: PIM activation must happen before the lockbox request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Lockbox Workflow in Pictures

symptom: Customer with inherited Owner role does not receive Customer Lockbox approval email and cannot approve or reject lockbox access requests
rootCause: An inherited owner role is NOT recognized by Customer Lockbox. Only directly assigned Owner, Global Admin, or Azure Customer Lockbox Approver roles are eligible to receive lockbox notifications.
solution: Assign a direct (not inherited) Owner role, Global Admin role, or Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role to the user who needs to approve lockbox requests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Lockbox Workflow in Pictures

symptom: Customer Lockbox request stuck in pending for 4 days with no notification after PIM was enabled post-request submission
rootCause: If a Microsoft engineer submits a lockbox request before PIM is enabled (or before the customer activates rights via PIM), the request gets stuck. Enabling PIM or activating rights afterwards does not retroactively deliver the notification. The request must timeout after 4 days.
solution: Wait for the stuck request to timeout (4 days). Then: 1) Customer activates GA/Owner role via PIM first, 2) Customer notifies Microsoft engineer, 3) Engineer submits new access request. The PIM activation must precede the lockbox request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Lockbox Workflow in Pictures

symptom: APIRunner501 alert is triggered for Customer Lockbox API Runner
rootCause: The API Runner is not receiving at least two 501 NotImplemented responses every 3 minutes for 3 or more of the 5 monitoring buckets. Expected behavior is one 501 per ContextType (tenant, subscription) every 3 minutes per environment from runnerScenario GetContextNotInWhitelist.
solution: Verify that the API Runner health check scenario (GetContextNotInWhitelist) is returning the expected 501 NotImplemented responses. Check runner instances are running, investigate if the environment endpoints are reachable, and confirm the whitelist configuration is correct.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/APIRunner501

symptom: Runner failure for a lockbox request with no corresponding logs in Lockbox service; request appears to have been processed but no lockbox-side records exist
rootCause: The issue is at the JIT (Just-In-Time access) layer. JIT evaluates the request against DSCM policies before forwarding to Lockbox. If JIT auto-approves/auto-rejects based on policy, or if policy evaluation fails, the request may never reach the Lockbox service, resulting in no Lockbox logs.
solution: 1) Query JIT logs for the request ID, 2) Check policy evaluation at https://jitaccess.security.core.windows.net/PolicyEvaluationOutcomeAnalysis.html?requestId=<ID>, 3) Review DSCM policies at https://dscm.core.windows.net, 4) If JIT policy evaluation issue, transfer incident to JIT team with cluster info, request ID, timestamp, policy evaluation link, and expected DSCM policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/Debugging no log issues

symptom: Lockbox request is auto-approved without ever reaching customer notified state; customer never received lockbox approval email but access was granted
rootCause: The subscription is associated with an internal Microsoft service in Service Tree. When this happens, the JIT auto-approval policy matches and bypasses customer notification entirely.
solution: 1) Check Service Tree (https://aka.ms/servicetree) for the subscription ID to confirm if it is mapped to an internal Microsoft service, 2) Review JITProcessorWorkerRole logs filtered by shouldsend or topic to verify if posting to Lockbox topic was attempted, 3) Check AcisPortalWebRole logs with subscription ID for further context.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/Debugging no log issues

symptom: Customer using PIM (Privileged Identity Management) with Customer Lockbox for Azure does not receive email notification when Microsoft Support Engineer sends a data access request. The Lockbox request does not appear in Pending Requests in the Administration module. The user has Owner/Global Administrator/Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role as eligible via PIM but has not activated it.
rootCause: Customer Lockbox notifications and pending request visibility require the Owner, Global Administrator, or Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role to be actively assigned (not just PIM-eligible) at the time the data access request is sent. If the role is only eligible but not activated, the notification is never sent and the request is effectively lost until it expires after 4 days.
solution: This is expected behavior by design. The customer must activate their Owner/Global Administrator/Azure Customer Lockbox Approver role through PIM before the Microsoft Support Engineer sends the Lockbox data access request. The support engineer should inform the customer in advance to activate their PIM role. If the request was already sent without the role being activated, a new request cannot be sent until 4 days have passed. If the customer requires automatic notification regardless of PIM activation status, advise them to submit a DCR (Design Change Request).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Using Customer LockBox with PIM

symptom: Customer Lockbox alert 'IncorrectRequestProcessing' fires when requests are consistently seen in states other than Approved. Also check if LockboxApiRequestProcessing alert fired simultaneously as that is most likely the root cause.
rootCause: Request processing logic changed and terminated request before notifying customer, or request approval is not working, or on-demand request retrieval is failing.
solution: Investigate LockboxApiRequestProcessing alert first if concurrent. Use RequestStatus dashboard to check: what state the request is in (look for consistently non-zero graph), whether request approval metric is successful. Follow up with RAE if approval is failing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/IncorrectRequestProcessing

symptom: Customer Lockbox alert 'JIT-RequestSubmitted-SLA' fires when JIT pipeline fails to process new Lockbox requests for RAE notification - mismatch between request create count and request submitted count.
rootCause: JIT pipeline encountered an error processing Lockbox requests, preventing successful submission of customer notification through RAE.
solution: Use dashboard linked in ICM to narrow time range to 10-minute window where mismatch exists between #request create and #request submitted. Use DGREP query from ICM to find specific request IDs created but not submitted to RAE. Use sample request ID to drill down the error.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/JIT-RequestSubmitted-SLA

symptom: Customer Lockbox alert 'JITRunnerHeartbeatFailure' fires when a JIT Runner region has not reported any metric values for over 1 hour (sev 3) or over 2 hours (sev 2).
rootCause: Runners in the affected region were manually turned off, or an exception in runner code prevents metric emission.
solution: If runners are manually turned off, disable the monitor to suppress the alert. If runner code has an exception, investigate runner logs to identify and fix the exception preventing metric emission.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/JITRunnerHeartbeatFailure

symptom: Customer Lockbox alert 'JITRunnerIterationFailure' fires when there is a runner failure in any of the JIT runner instances.
rootCause: JIT runner instance encountered a failure during its iteration cycle.
solution: Check JIT Runner Dashboard in Jarvis. Review ICM logs (available ~15 minutes after incident). If no logs in ICM, check the specific instance logs around the time of metric emission. See also: Debugging Runners guide at SupportabilityWork/Azure Security wiki.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/JITRunnerIterationFailure

symptom: Customer Lockbox alert 'LockboxApiRequestProcessing' fires when post-processing of requests created via API hits an unexpected exception.
rootCause: Unexpected exception during post-processing of API-created Lockbox requests.
solution: Use RequestProcessingSummary logs query in ICM enhancement to drill down to exception info. Check dashboard to determine if failure happened during last retry. If not last retry, request will auto-recover on next retry. If last retry failed, re-queue the request to service bus using Geneva action.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/LockboxApiRequestProcessing

symptom: Customer Lockbox alert 'RequestController500' fires when the number of HTTP 500 responses increases for requests served by the Requests Controller.
rootCause: Server-side error in the Lockbox Requests Controller causing HTTP 500 responses.
solution: Use FailedRequestSummary query from ICM to get sample log lines. Select the web request tracking ID column and filter logs with only that tracking ID (remove select command in DGREP query to see all columns). This gives all logs for the sample request to debug further.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/RequestController500

symptom: Customer Lockbox alert 'RunnerTrafficLoss' fires when there are no metrics reported by a runner on its availability view on the service.
rootCause: Lockbox runner instance stopped reporting metrics, causing a gap in availability monitoring.
solution: Use RunnerDrillDown dashboard link from ICM to identify which specific runner is seeing a drop in metrics. Use that runner instance name in the RunnerLogs query (added to ICM) to find out why there is a drop in metrics.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Customer LockBox/Specific Issues/RunnerTrafficLoss

symptom: Password Hash Sync (PHS) fails with connectivity issues in Connect Sync. Event viewer shows Critical/Warning/Error events from Directory Synchronization source for the domain.
rootCause: Network connectivity issue between Connect Sync server and Azure AD PHS endpoints, preventing password hash data from being uploaded.
solution: Verify and restore network connectivity. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/entra/entra-id/user-prov-sync/pwd-hash-sync-stops-work#more-information
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/DP Processes Guidelines and others/IdM (Account Managment & Sync)/Sync - Technical processes to collect information/Password Hash Sync issues using Connect Sync

symptom: Password Hash Sync not syncing passwords after on-premises password change in Connect Sync. Passwords filtered because User must change password at next logon was selected.
rootCause: Password changed in local AD with 'User must change password at next logon' enabled, causing temporary passwords to be filtered from PHS synchronization by default.
solution: Enable 'Synchronize temporary passwords' feature in Entra Connect. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/hybrid/connect/how-to-connect-password-hash-synchronization#synchronizing-temporary-passwords-and-force-password-change-on-next-logon
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/DP Processes Guidelines and others/IdM (Account Managment & Sync)/Sync - Technical processes to collect information/Password Hash Sync issues using Connect Sync

symptom: Password Hash Synchronization (PHS) fails after switching Azure AD Connect from staging to active mode. Users cannot log in with new passwords. Event ID 667 in Event Viewer: Password hash synchronization agent failed to create a key for decryption. FIPS policy is enabled on the server.
rootCause: FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) policy enabled on the Azure AD Connect server blocks the encryption algorithm used by PHS, causing Event ID 667 and preventing password hash synchronization.
solution: Disable FIPS enforcement in the Azure AD Connect config: 1) Navigate to %ProgramFiles%\Microsoft Azure AD Sync\Bin. 2) Open miiserver.exe.config. 3) Add <enforceFIPSPolicy enabled="false" /> under <runtime> in the <configuration> section. 4) Save and reboot the server. Verify PHS resumes by checking for Event IDs 656 and 657 in Event Viewer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/DP Processes Guidelines and others/IdM (Account Managment & Sync)/Sync - Technical processes to collect information/Password Hash Synchronization Failure Due to FIPS Policy

symptom: HTTP 400 Bad Request "Request too long" error when accessing a web application that uses Azure AD / Entra ID authentication. The error indicates the HTTP request header exceeds the server maximum allowed size.
rootCause: The HTTP request header size exceeds the web server limit. Common causes: (1) Access token is too long due to "groups" claim including many group memberships, (2) "wids" claim includes too many directory roles, (3) Too many or oversized cookies accumulated in the browser session.
solution: Reduce header size: (1) Remove "groups" claim from token config and use MS Graph API to check group membership instead, or use "roles" claim; (2) Reduce directory roles or remove "wids" claim; (3) Clear unnecessary cookies. Increase server header limit: For ASP.NET/IIS, add requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength in web.config under system.webServer/security/requestFiltering; For ASP.NET Core (Kestrel), set options.Limits.MaxRequestHeadersTotalSize in Program.cs (e.g. 128*1024 for 128KB).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/400 bad request Request too long

symptom: Need to validate if M365 Identity tenant is concierge (seat count < 300). IsConcierge in Case Buddy is unreliable for concierge validation.
rootCause: The IsConcierge attribute in Case Buddy and the Support Area Name attribute are unreliable for concierge validation. Only the DfM (Dynamics for Microsoft) IsConcierge attribute is accurate.
solution: Validate concierge status using IsConcierge attribute in Dynamics for Microsoft (DfM/DfMEU). If IsConcierge=Yes and no Premier/CSS/S500 customer program, transfer case to SAP: Office Products/Office 365/Office 365/Concierge. If customer has Premier or CSS/S500 program, keep in SCIM queues regardless of concierge status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/DP Processes Guidelines and others/M365 IDO/M365 Identity - Identify Concierge Volume

symptom: Misrouted case resolved - unclear which team (initial scoping or destination) should deliver LQR and close the case.
rootCause: No clear ownership rule applied when case root cause spans multiple teams or is a 3rd party issue.
solution: If root cause falls into different team scope, follow Procedure Case Closure FAQ (KB e44eb87d) to determine closure responsibility. If root cause is 3rd party, the initial scoping team delivers LQR and closes. Additional customer queries outside initial scope require a separate support ticket.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/DP Processes Guidelines and others/Misroutes and Reroutes Guidance/LQR and Case Closure Responsibility for Misrouted Cases

symptom: .NET application throws 'Could not load file or assembly' error (e.g., Microsoft.Identity.Client, Version=X.X.X.0) - assembly version mismatch or missing DLL at runtime
rootCause: The application has a dependency on a specific version of an assembly (e.g., MSAL Microsoft.Identity.Client) and the DLL is missing from deployment or a version mismatch exists between referenced and deployed assemblies
solution: 1) Verify assembly exists in bin folder, reinstall via NuGet 2) Ensure all projects reference same version 3) Update binding redirects in web.config/app.config 4) Add AutoGenerateBindingRedirects to .csproj 5) Clean/rebuild (delete bin/obj) 6) Check target framework 7) Use Fusion Log Viewer (fuslogvw) 8) Verify deployment includes all assemblies
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/DotNet Could not load file or assembly

symptom: MSAL AcquireToken calls appear to hang or freeze the .NET application, especially in WinForm/WPF apps on the UI Thread. App becomes unresponsive when calling AcquireTokenInteractive or AcquireTokenSilent.
rootCause: Async/await deadlock caused by blocking calls (.Result, .Wait()) on async methods that capture the UI thread SynchronizationContext, preventing continuation from executing
solution: Method 1: Use async/await all the way up (avoid .Result/.Wait()). Method 2: ConfigureAwait(false) on all await calls. Method 3: Task.Run(() => GetTokenAsync()).GetAwaiter().GetResult(). Method 4: ContinueWith(TaskScheduler.FromCurrentSynchronizationContext())
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/DotNet Handling Async Await

symptom: .NET app throws SocketException 'SSL connection could not be established' or 'existing connection forcibly closed by remote host' when calling Graph/Azure AD endpoints
rootCause: TLS protocol misconfiguration, insufficient connection limits (default ServicePointManager.DefaultConnectionLimit=2), or firewall/application gateway blocking connections
solution: Add ServicePointManager config: DefaultConnectionLimit=200, Expect100Continue=true, SecurityProtocol=Tls|Tls11|Tls12. Review firewall and application gateway logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/DotNet Network troubleshooting

symptom: .NET app throws 'System.Net.WebException: The request was aborted: Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel' calling Azure AD or Graph endpoints
rootCause: Application not configured to use TLS 1.2 or required TLS versions; SSL/TLS mismatch between client and server
solution: Set SecurityProtocol to include TLS 1.2: ServicePointManager.SecurityProtocol = SecurityProtocolType.Tls | SecurityProtocolType.Tls11 | SecurityProtocolType.Tls12 | SecurityProtocolType.Ssl3
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/DotNet Network troubleshooting

symptom: .NET app throws 'System.Net.WebException: The operation has timed out' when calling Microsoft Graph or Azure AD endpoints, requests eventually fail
rootCause: Insufficient connection limit (DefaultConnectionLimit=2 in .NET Framework), firewall/proxy delays, or backend processing exceeding client timeout
solution: Increase DefaultConnectionLimit to 200. Check Sign-in/Graph logs for backend latency (>20s = possible server issue). Relax app timeout. Review firewall/proxy config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/DotNet Network troubleshooting

symptom: ASP.NET/ASP.NET Core web app fails to complete authentication after successful Entra ID sign-in. Correlation and nonce cookies (.AspNetCore.OpenIdConnect.Nonce, .AspNetCore.Correlation) are not returned in POST to /signin-oidc.
rootCause: SameSite cookie attribute not set to None. OpenID Connect redirect from login.microsoftonline.com is cross-site POST; cookies with SameSite=Lax/Strict are not sent by the browser.
solution: Set SameSite=None on cookies. ASP.NET Core: ConfigureExternalCookie and ConfigureApplicationCookie with SameSite=None. ASP.NET: CookieAuthenticationOptions with SameSite=None. Alternative: set response_mode to query instead of form_post.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/Cookies not getting passed

symptom: ASP.NET web app authentication fails because authentication cookies exceed browser size limits (~4KB). Browser silently drops oversized cookies causing auth loop or silent failure.
rootCause: The id_token or session cookie value exceeds browser cookie size limit (~4KB). Occurs when token contains many claims or group memberships.
solution: ASP.NET: Enable cookie chunking via SystemWebChunkingCookieManager. Also reduce id_token size by limiting claims or using groups filter. ASP.NET Core handles chunking automatically.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/Cookies not getting passed

symptom: ASP.NET/ASP.NET Core web app generates HTTP redirect_uri instead of HTTPS when authenticating with Entra ID, causing AADSTS50011 redirect_uri mismatch. Common behind reverse proxy or load balancer.
rootCause: Web server generates redirect_uri from incoming request scheme which is HTTP when behind a proxy/LB that terminates TLS.
solution: ASP.NET Core: app.UseHsts() + app.UseForwardedHeaders(XForwardedFor|XForwardedProto). Or override: context.Request.Scheme = https. ASP.NET: RequireHttpsAttribute in global filters. Fallback: hard-code RedirectUri in OpenIdConnectAuthenticationOptions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/AspNet AspNetCore/Force HTTPS redirection

symptom: ASP.NET/ASP.NET Core web app always redirects to root page (/) after OpenID Connect sign-in instead of the originally requested page or custom destination like /Dashboard.
rootCause: Default OpenID Connect middleware redirects to root after auth callback. Original URL is not preserved across the authentication flow.
solution: Method 1: Microsoft Identity Web built-in redirect customization. Method 2: Customize OnTicketReceived to set ctx.ReturnUri. Method 3: Save URL in state param via OnRedirectToIdentityProvider, restore in OnTokenValidated, set ReturnUri in OnTicketReceived.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/AspNet AspNetCore/Redirect to custom page after signin

symptom: IDX10205: Issuer validation failed. The iss claim in the access token does not match validationParameters.ValidIssuer or ValidIssuers. Also appears as IDX10204 (ValidIssuer is null), IDX40001 (does not match valid issuers), or HTTP 401 The issuer is invalid.
rootCause: The iss claim in the token does not match the expected issuer in the application config. Common causes: wrong Authority URL, v1 vs v2 endpoint mismatch (v1 uses https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-id}/, v2 uses https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant-id}/v2.0), multi-tenant app using tenant-specific config, or B2C/CIAM issuer format differences.
solution: Ensure the Authority URL matches the token version. For v1 tokens: issuer is https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-id}/. For v2 tokens: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant-id}/v2.0. For B2C: https://{domain}.b2clogin.com/tfp/{tenant-id}/{policy-id}/v2.0/. For CIAM: https://{tenant-id}.ciamlogin.com/{tenant-id}/v2.0. For multi-tenant apps, set ValidateIssuer=false or implement custom issuer validator. Enable IdentityModelEventSource.ShowPII=true to see actual values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/IDX10205 Issuer Validation failed

symptom: IDX10214: Audience validation failed. The aud claim in the access token does not match validationParameters.ValidAudience or ValidAudiences. May surface as HTTP 401 in API calls.
rootCause: The aud claim in the access token does not match the expected audience configured in the application/API. Common with Microsoft.Identity.Web which auto-generates api://{client-id} format. Also occurs in API Management validate-jwt policies and Azure App Service EasyAuth.
solution: Ensure the audience config matches the aud claim in the access token. For ASP.NET Core with Microsoft.Identity.Web, check ClientId in appsettings.json (format: api://{client-id}). For APIM, verify audience in validate-jwt policy. For EasyAuth, check Client ID or Allowed Token Audiences. Common audience values: https://graph.microsoft.com, https://management.azure.com, api://{app-id}. Decode token at https://jwt.ms to check aud claim.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/IDX10214 Audience validation failed

symptom: IDX10311 or IDX21323: RequireNonce is true but nonce cookie is missing or cannot be validated. Error: validationContext.Nonce is null or OpenIdConnectProtocolValidationContext.Nonce was null while ID token contains a nonce.
rootCause: The OpenID Connect nonce cookie is missing, not marked as Secure, or the web app cannot recognize it. Common causes: redirect URI mismatch (http vs https, hostname, port), multiple app instances without ARR Affinity, outdated Microsoft.Owin or IdentityModel packages, or OWIN System.Web cookie integration issues.
solution: 1) Update Microsoft.Owin and Microsoft.IdentityModel.Protocols.OpenIdConnect to latest. 2) Verify redirect_uri consistency (same scheme, host, port). 3) For multi-instance apps, enable ARR Affinity. 4) Set CookieSecure=Always. 5) For ASP.NET, use SystemWebCookieManager from AspNetKatana wiki. 6) Set dynamic redirect_uri via RedirectToIdentityProvider notification. Last resort: RequireNonce=false (security trade-off).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/IDX10311_IDX21323

symptom: IDX10500: Signature validation failed. No security keys were provided to validate the signature. Token signature cannot be validated because IssuerSigningKeys is not configured in TokenValidationParameters.
rootCause: When using Microsoft.IdentityModel directly to validate tokens, the IssuerSigningKeys property in TokenValidationParameters is not set. The application has not fetched signing keys from the OIDC metadata endpoint. Middleware implementations handle this automatically.
solution: If using Microsoft.IdentityModel directly: fetch OpenIdConnectConfiguration from {authority}/.well-known/openid-configuration and set IssuerSigningKeys=publicOpenIdConfig.SigningKeys. If using middleware: verify Authority/Metadata URL points to correct Entra ID endpoint. For Microsoft.Identity.Web see github wiki. For ASP.NET Core see JWT Bearer docs. For OWIN ensure correct Bearer auth config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/IDX10500

symptom: Sustainsys.Saml2 configuration error: The value of the property cacheDuration cannot be parsed. The error is: the duration string does not start with P. Parser Error on metadata element.
rootCause: The cacheDuration property in sustainsys.saml2 metadata config uses time format like 0:0:42 instead of ISO 8601 duration format starting with P.
solution: Remove cacheDuration from sustainsys.saml2 metadata config (defaults to one hour). Or use ISO 8601 format (e.g. PT42S). Corrected: <metadata validDuration='7.12:00:00' wantAssertionsSigned='true'>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/How to Develop a SAML2 app

symptom: IDX10634: Unable to create the SignatureProvider. Application token validation fails when using custom issuer signing key with ValidateIssuerSigningKey=true and non-RSA256 algorithm.
rootCause: When TokenValidationParameters.ValidateIssuerSigningKey is set to true, it requires RSA256 algorithm. Custom signing keys using other algorithms will fail validation.
solution: Set ValidateIssuerSigningKey=false when using a hardcoded/custom signing key that does not need validation, or ensure the key uses RSA256. Alternatively provide an IssuerSigningKeyValidator delegate for custom validation logic.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/IDX10634 Unable to create the SignatureProvider

symptom: IDX40001: Issuer does not match any of the valid issuers provided for this application. Token issuer validation fails during token verification with Microsoft.IdentityModel.
rootCause: Token issuer claim does not match configured valid issuers in TokenValidationParameters. Same root cause as IDX10205 - issuer validation configuration mismatch.
solution: Follow same troubleshooting as IDX10205: verify TokenValidationParameters.ValidIssuers or ValidIssuer matches the token iss claim. Check tenant ID and authority URL. For multi-tenant apps, ensure issuer format matches (v1 vs v2 endpoint).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/IDX40001

symptom: Java IDE shows 'the import cannot be resolved' error after importing JAR packages or adding Maven dependencies for MSAL4J or Microsoft Graph SDK.
rootCause: IDE cache is stale after adding new JAR dependencies. The project needs to be cleaned and rebuilt to resolve the new imports.
