solution: Clean the project: VS Code - Java: Clean Java Language Server Workspace; Eclipse - Maven mvn clean install then Update Project then Project Clean.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/Java/Getting started Java

symptom: Java application fails with PKIX path building failed: SunCertPathBuilderException: unable to find valid certification path to requested target when connecting to Microsoft endpoints (login.microsoftonline.com, graph.microsoft.com).
rootCause: Java truststore/keystore is missing root CA certificates for Microsoft gateway servers. May also occur behind a proxy whose SSL certificates are not imported into Java truststore.
solution: Import Microsoft root certificates into Java truststore: keytool -importcert -file cert.crt -keystore cacerts -alias mycert -storepass changeit. Verify Azure TLS root certs per learn.microsoft.com/azure/security/fundamentals/tls-certificate-changes. For proxy: import proxy root cert. Debug with -Djavax.net.debug=all. Default truststore: Windows C:/Program Files/Java/jre/lib/security/cacerts, Linux /etc/pki/java/cacerts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/Java/PKIX path building failed

symptom: Java application using MSAL/Azure SDK throws java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError at runtime. Class is present at compile time but missing at runtime, causing authentication flow to fail.
rootCause: Missing JAR file dependency in the runtime classpath. The class exists during compilation but the JAR is not included in the runtime environment or deployment package.
solution: Identify the missing class from the error message. Verify the JAR file exists in the compile/build environment. Check runtime Java classpath for typos or missing JARs. For Java EE apps, verify EAR/WAR packaging and MANIFEST for all dependency JARs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/Java/java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError

symptom: Angular app git push fails with husky pre-push hook failure during ng lint step. Lint errors show comment-format violations (comment must start with a space) and npm ERR! code ELIFECYCLE.
rootCause: Angular TSLint/ESLint rules reject code comments that do not start with a space. The pre-push hook runs ng lint which enforces these rules and blocks the push on violations.
solution: Run 'ng lint {app-name} --fix' to auto-fix lint formatting errors, then retry git push.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/JavaScript/Angular

symptom: Customer wants to use Managed Identity with Azure AD B2C but it is not available or does not work. Managed Identity cannot authenticate to B2C-protected resources.
rootCause: Managed Identity does not support Azure AD B2C. B2C is not listed in the services supporting managed identities per Microsoft documentation.
solution: Managed Identity is not supported for B2C. Use alternative authentication methods such as client credentials with certificate/secret for B2C scenarios. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/managed-identities-azure-resources/managed-identities-status
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/Managed Identity/Developer Scenarios for Managed Identity

symptom: Managed Identity cannot access resources in a different directory/tenant. Customer asks if Managed Identity can work as a multi-tenant application.
rootCause: Managed Identity is designed as a single-tenant identity tied to the Azure resource home tenant. It cannot be configured as a multi-tenant application.
solution: Managed Identity cannot be used cross-tenant or as multi-tenant. For cross-tenant resource access, use standard app registrations with client credentials. See FAQ: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/managed-identities-azure-resources/managed-identities-faq
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/Managed Identity/Developer Scenarios for Managed Identity

symptom: Access token groups claim returns hasgroups:true (implicit flow with >5 groups) or _claim_sources with src1 Graph endpoint (other flows with >150 SAML / >200 JWT groups) instead of actual group GUIDs.
rootCause: Groups claim overage: implicit flow returns hasgroups:true when user has >5 groups; authorization code and other flows return _claim_sources endpoint reference when user has >150 groups (SAML) or >200 groups (JWT).
solution: Configure app registration groups claim to 'Groups assigned to the application' (recommended for large orgs). Alternatively, call Microsoft Graph /users/{userId}/memberOf endpoint to retrieve groups separately. Note: hasgroups value is a string ('true'/'false'), not boolean.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/More info about claims

symptom: Groups claim not returned in OAuth2 access token even though user is a member of groups. Token audience is Microsoft Graph or another first-party app.
rootCause: Groups claim requires groupMembershipClaims to be enabled in the resource (audience) application manifest. First-party apps like Microsoft Graph do not have this enabled. Only the audience app should consume the token.
solution: Enable groupMembershipClaims in your own application registration manifest. Ensure the access token audience is your app, not Microsoft Graph. Use the id_token for groups claim, or make a separate Microsoft Graph call to /users/{id}/memberOf.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/More info about claims

symptom: Groups claim is not available in Azure AD B2C user flow policy tokens. No groups are returned regardless of user group membership.
rootCause: Azure AD B2C user flow policies do not support the groups claim by design.
solution: Use B2C custom policies instead of user flows to include groups claim, or retrieve group membership via Microsoft Graph API call as a separate step after authentication.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/More info about claims

symptom: Access token wids claim contains role GUIDs; role ID b79fbf4d-3ef9-4689-8143-76b194e85509 returns error when looked up via Get-MgDirectoryRoleTemplate (resource does not exist).
rootCause: The wids claim lists Azure AD built-in role template IDs. GUID b79fbf4d-3ef9-4689-8143-76b194e85509 is the default User role assigned to all users. This special role is not a standard directory role template and cannot be queried via PowerShell.
solution: Use Get-MgDirectoryRoleTemplate for standard roles. For b79fbf4d-3ef9-4689-8143-76b194e85509, recognize it as the built-in User role (all users have it). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/roles/permissions-reference
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/More info about claims

symptom: Python Flask web app generates HTTP redirect URIs instead of HTTPS when using MSAL/Entra authentication, causing redirect_uri mismatch or insecure authentication flow.
rootCause: Flask url_for() generates HTTP URLs by default, especially when behind a reverse proxy or when the HTTPS scheme is not explicitly set in URL generation.
solution: Use url_for('authorization', _external=True, _scheme='https') to force HTTPS scheme in Flask redirect URIs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/Python/Python Force HTTPS

symptom: Web app enters infinite redirect loop (~15 cycles) between app and Entra ID (login.microsoftonline.com) before IdP throws authentication error. HTTP trace shows redirect_uri hostname or scheme (HTTP vs HTTPS) differs from where user originally accessed the app.
rootCause: Redirect URI hostname or scheme mismatch between the app access URL and the configured redirect_uri in Entra ID app registration. The browser redirect flow (app -> IdP -> app) requires consistent hostname and HTTPS scheme throughout.
solution: Verify redirect_uri passed to login.microsoftonline.com matches the app hostname and scheme (HTTPS). Hard-code redirect_uri in OIDC middleware options if needed: OpenIdConnectAuthenticationOptions.RedirectUri = "https://site.contoso.com/". Also configure PostLogoutRedirectUri.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/troubleshoot Infinite loop

symptom: ASP.NET OWIN/Katana app shows multiple duplicate OpenIdConnect.Nonce cookies in HTTP trace during authentication, causing infinite redirect loop. Each redirect cycle adds another nonce cookie.
rootCause: OWIN Katana bug: System.Web SessionStateModule Response.Cookies collection conflicts with OWIN response headers, causing cookie duplication (katanaproject.codeplex.com/workitem/197).
solution: Install latest Microsoft.Owin.Host.SystemWeb package and configure SystemWebChunkingCookieManager: app.UseCookieAuthentication(new CookieAuthenticationOptions { AuthenticationType = "Cookies", CookieManager = new Microsoft.Owin.Host.SystemWeb.SystemWebChunkingCookieManager() });
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/troubleshoot Infinite loop

symptom: Web app sets authentication cookie (.AspNet.Cookies) in Set-Cookie response header after redirect from Entra ID, but cookie is not passed in subsequent requests. App keeps redirecting to IdP in a loop.
rootCause: Authentication cookie exceeds browser cookie size limit and is silently dropped by the browser. Cookie is set by the server but never sent back by the client.
solution: Implement cookie chunking using SystemWebChunkingCookieManager to split large cookies into chunks within browser limits. Also configure HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite=None flags via CookiePolicyOptions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/troubleshoot Infinite loop

symptom: Web app deployed to multiple instances (e.g., Azure App Service with scale-out) enters authentication loop. Duplicate nonce or ARRAffinity cookies observed. Auth code flow token cache lost between requests.
rootCause: ARR (Application Request Routing) affinity not enabled or ARR cookie domain mismatch with custom domain. In-memory token cache on one instance not accessible from another instance handling the callback.
solution: Enable ARR affinity and verify ARR cookie domain matches custom domain. Use distributed token cache (SQL or Redis Cache) instead of InMemory for production. Check for duplicate ARRAffinity/nonce cookies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/troubleshoot Infinite loop

symptom: User gets "You do not have permission to view this directory or page" when authenticating to Azure App Service protected with EasyAuth (App Services Built-in Authentication).
rootCause: Misconfigured Azure AD settings in EasyAuth: invalid or expired client secret, incorrect audience in allowedAudiences, wrong issuer URL, or mismatched Client ID in authsettings.
solution: Check EasyAuth authsettings at resources.azure.com: navigate to Config/authsettings. Verify clientId, client secret validity, issuer URL (https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}), and allowedAudiences. Check Entra ID sign-in logs by the configured Client ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Integration Scenarios/Configure App Services for Entra Scenarios

symptom: Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) not working in ASP.NET Core app using Microsoft.Identity.Web despite configuring ClientCapabilities in appsettings.json per Microsoft docs. Access tokens lack xms_cc claim with cp1.
rootCause: Microsoft.Identity.Web does NOT bind ClientCapabilities from appsettings.json configuration. Public documentation is incorrect (escalated to PG). The config value is silently ignored.
solution: Set ClientCapabilities programmatically in Startup.cs: services.Configure<ConfidentialClientApplicationOptions>(options => { Configuration.Bind("AzureAd", options); options.ClientCapabilities = new[] { "cp1" }; }); Verify via jwt.ms that access token contains "xms_cc": ["cp1"].
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Integration Scenarios/Implementing and Validating Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) with Microsoft.Identity.Web

symptom: Web app using Microsoft Identity Web / OIDC Middleware behind Entra ID Application Proxy encounters authentication failures or redirect issues. Dual authentication conflicts between OIDC middleware and App Proxy pre-authentication.
rootCause: Using both OIDC Middleware/Microsoft Identity Web AND Application Proxy pre-authentication is contradictory. App Proxy is designed for apps that cannot authenticate themselves; when both are active, they conflict on redirect URIs and session management.
solution: Choose one: (1) Use OIDC Middleware and configure App Proxy to bypass authentication, OR (2) Remove OIDC Middleware and let App Proxy handle auth. If using OIDC with App Proxy passthrough, configure explicit redirect_uri via ConfidentialClientApplicationOptions and pass client_info=1 via OnRedirectToIdentityProvider.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Integration Scenarios/MSAL integration with Entra ID Application Proxy

symptom: Power BI fails with ADGraphGroupOperationResultSizeExceeded error when user is a member of 11,000+ groups.
rootCause: Microsoft Graph API getMemberGroups endpoint has a documented limit and returns error when group membership exceeds 11k. Power BI calls this API internally without handling the error.
solution: First-party integration limitation. The app should use transitiveMemberOf API with pagination instead of getMemberGroups. Cases should be owned by Power BI support team. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/group-list-transitivememberof
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Microsoft Graph First Party Known Issues/Microsoft Graph Power BI

symptom: Microsoft Graph API getMemberGroups (directoryObjects/{id}/getMemberGroups) returns ADGraphGroupOperationResultSizeExceeded error in Power BI or other first-party apps when user is member of 11000+ groups
rootCause: The getMemberGroups API has an inherent limit and fails when the user is a member of more than approximately 11,000 groups. This is expected behavior per API documentation.
solution: The first-party app should use the transitiveMemberOf API (GET /groups/{id}/transitiveMemberOf) instead of getMemberGroups to avoid the group count limit. Cases should be owned by the Power BI support team since this is a first-party integration issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Microsoft Graph First Party Known Issues/Microsoft Graph Power BI

symptom: Application receives 403 Forbidden with description 'Access blocked to AAD Graph API for this application' after Azure AD Graph retirement enforcement (Phase 1/2/3)
rootCause: Azure AD Graph API (graph.windows.net) is being retired in phases. Phase 1 blocks new apps, Phase 2 blocks all apps without blockAzureADGraphAccess=false extension, Phase 3 permanently blocks all apps including those with extensions.
solution: For Phase 1/2: Set blockAzureADGraphAccess to false via authenticationBehaviors property using MS Graph API or Graph Explorer to get temporary extension. For Phase 3 (permanent): Must migrate to Microsoft Graph API. Check Entra Recommendations for impacted apps. For AzureAD PowerShell: migrate to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK or Microsoft Entra PowerShell.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Graph API/Unblock AAD Graph APIs in App Registrations

symptom: Entra Recommendations alert shows first-party Microsoft applications in 'Migrate Applications from the Retiring Azure AD Graph APIs' recommendation, causing unnecessary customer concern about 1P apps they cannot control
rootCause: Entra Recommendations was not filtering non-actionable first-party applications from the AAD Graph migration recommendation. Issue surfaced around December 6, 2024 with ~50 cases/day.
solution: Product Engineering deployed a fix on 12/9/2024 to hide non-actionable 1P applications (completed by 12/11/2024). To verify if an app is 1P: check verifiedPublisher property on the service principal, click 'More details' to see if publisher is Microsoft, and cross-reference with the list of commonly used Microsoft application IDs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Graph API/Unblock AAD Graph APIs in App Registrations

symptom: S500 tenant applications fail with AAD Graph API 403 after Phase 2 enforcement rollout starting October 20, including Azure PowerShell and service principal authentication scenarios
rootCause: Phase 2 retirement enforcement extended to S500 tenants in Public Cloud. Apps registered in the tenant cannot call AAD Graph APIs unless blockAzureADGraphAccess is set to false.
solution: Set blockAzureADGraphAccess to false on impacted applications for temporary extension. For Azure PowerShell: run 'az account clear' after setting the flag to force token refresh. Update clients (Azure PowerShell, Terraform) to versions that do not use AAD Graph APIs. S500 tenants with the flag set can continue until February 2026.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Graph API/Unblock AAD Graph APIs in App Registrations

symptom: PATCH to passwordCredentials via AAD Graph API (graph.windows.net) fails with Request_BadRequest 'Property identifierUris is invalid' when trying to set custom password credential
rootCause: AAD Graph is deprecated; generating custom password credentials (setting your own password value) is no longer supported when the application has an App URI ID configured or is not set as a confidential client.
solution: Long-term: Migrate to MS Graph addPassword API (POST /applications/{id}/addPassword) which generates a random client secret. Short-term workaround: either enable the app as Confidential client, or remove the App URI ID from the application registration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Graph API/passwordCredentials

symptom: Azure AD Reporting API (auditLogs/signIns) returns 403 with Authentication_RequestFromNonPremiumTenantOrB2CTenant: 'Neither tenant is B2C or tenant doesn't have premium license'
rootCause: Three possible causes: (1) Tenant is B2C which cannot use Reporting API, (2) Tenant lacks Azure AD Premium P1/P2 license (EMS E3 includes P1, EMS E5 includes P2), (3) For application-based tokens, Directory.Read.All permission is missing or not consented - this can cause intermittent 403.
solution: Verify tenant has Azure AD Premium P1 or P2 subscription. For application (client_credentials) flow: ensure Directory.Read.All permission is added, admin consented, and present in 'roles' claim of the access token. B2C tenants cannot use Reporting API at all.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Azure AD Reporting API returns 403 with 'User is not in the allowed roles' when using delegated (user-based) access token
rootCause: The user calling the Reporting API is not assigned to any of the required admin roles. Non-admin users cannot use the Reporting API.
solution: Assign the user to one of: Security Administrator, Security Reader, Global Reader, Report Reader, or Global Administrator role. Check the latest list of required roles at the official documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Azure AD Reporting API returns 403 with 'Calling principal does not have required MSGraph permissions AuditLog.Read.All'
rootCause: The AuditLog.Read.All API permission is either not added to the app registration, not admin consented, or not present in the access token claims.
solution: Add AuditLog.Read.All to API permissions in app registration, grant admin consent, and verify it appears in the 'scp' claim (delegated) or 'roles' claim (application) of the access token.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Azure AD Reporting API (auditLogs/signIns) returns 429 Too Many Requests or UnknownError with 'This request is throttled' message, including transient throttling errors
rootCause: Application exceeds throttling limits: 5 requests per 10 seconds per app per tenant, or 122 per 10 seconds across all tenants. Transient 429 errors are also known to occur even below limits.
solution: Implement retry logic using Retry-After response header value. Reduce $top parameter (start at 900, decrease by 100 until stable). Narrow date range with $filter (e.g., createdDateTime ge/le). Simplify complex $filter queries. Run during off-peak hours. Combine multiple mitigations if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Azure AD Reporting API returns 504 Gateway Timeout when querying auditLogs/signIns or loading sign-in reports
rootCause: Query scope is too large causing server-side timeout, often due to broad date ranges, large $top values, or complex filter expressions.
solution: Apply same mitigations as 429 errors: reduce $top parameter, narrow date range with $filter, simplify filter parameters, run during off-peak hours. See also the known issue wiki for additional 504-specific guidance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Azure AD Graph API calls fail with HTTP 403 'Access blocked to AAD Graph API for this application' during Phase 3 permanent enforcement, even when blockAzureADGraphAccess is set to false. Previously worked during Phase 2 with the flag set to false.
rootCause: Phase 3 permanent enforcement of AAD Graph retirement ignores the blockAzureADGraphAccess authenticationBehaviors setting. Unlike Phase 2 where setting the flag to false allowed continued use until the cutoff, Phase 3 permanently blocks all AAD Graph API access regardless of configuration.
solution: Migrate applications to Microsoft Graph API immediately. blockAzureADGraphAccess can no longer be used for recovery in Phase 3. Check Entra Recommendations to identify impacted apps. For AzureAD PowerShell, migrate to MS Graph PowerShell SDK or Entra PowerShell. For Azure CLI, update to v2.66.0+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Graph API/Unblock AAD Graph APIs in App Registrations

symptom: Entra Recommendations 'Migrate Applications from the Retiring Azure AD Graph APIs' incorrectly shows Microsoft first-party (1P) applications as needing migration. Customers receive false alerts about 1st party apps.
rootCause: A change rolled out generated recommendations for 1P applications that should have been hidden. These Microsoft-owned apps have extensions configured and will not be impacted by retirement.
solution: Engineering deployed fix 12/9/2024 to hide non-actionable 1P apps. Check verifiedPublisher on service principal. Confirm against MS first-party app ID list. Cases should be handled in originating queue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Graph API/Unblock AAD Graph APIs in App Registrations

symptom: AzureAD PowerShell module commands (Connect-AzureAD, Get-AzureADUser, etc.) fail permanently after Phase 3 retirement enforcement for non-S500 tenants in Public Cloud.
rootCause: AzureAD PowerShell is permanently blocked for non-S500 tenants as part of AAD Graph retirement Phase 3. The module relied on Azure AD Graph APIs.
solution: Migrate to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK or Microsoft Entra PowerShell. Use compatibility mode (Enable-EntraAzureADAlias) for interim. Run az account clear after setting blockAzureADGraphAccess to force token refresh.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Graph API/Unblock AAD Graph APIs in App Registrations

symptom: Azure AD Graph API PATCH request to update passwordCredentials on an application returns error: 'Property identifierUris is invalid'. Error code: Request_BadRequest.
rootCause: When using AAD Graph API to PATCH passwordCredentials, including extraneous properties like identifierUris in the request body causes validation failure.
solution: Ensure PATCH body contains only passwordCredentials array. Use: PATCH graph.windows.net/myorganization/applications/{objectId}?api-version=1.6. Note: deprecated - use MS Graph addPassword action instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Graph API/passwordCredentials

symptom: Microsoft Graph Reporting API (auditLogs/signIns, directoryAudits) returns 403 Forbidden. Sub-errors include: 'Neither tenant is B2C or tenant doesn't have premium license', 'User is not in the allowed roles', 'Calling principal does not have required MSGraph permissions AuditLog.Read.All'.
rootCause: Multiple causes: (1) Tenant lacks Azure AD Premium P1/P2, (2) B2C tenant not supported, (3) User not in required admin role, (4) App missing AuditLog.Read.All or Directory.Read.All permission, (5) Permission not admin-consented.
solution: Verify P1/P2 license. Assign admin role (Security Admin/Reader, Global Reader, Report Reader). Add AuditLog.Read.All + Directory.Read.All for app-only. Grant admin consent. Check scp/roles claim in token.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Microsoft Graph Reporting API returns 429 'Too Many Requests' or 'UnknownError' with message 'This request is throttled' when querying auditLogs/signIns or directoryAudits. Transient 429 errors also occur.
rootCause: Reporting API throttling: 5 req/10s per app per tenant, 122/10s across all tenants. Complex filters and large result sets increase throttling risk.
solution: 1. Retry with Retry-After header. 2. Reduce $top (start 900, decrease by 100). 3. Narrow $filter time range. 4. Simplify $filter. 5. Run during off-peak hours. 6. Combine solutions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Microsoft Graph Reporting API returns 'Token not found: token is either invalid or expired' when paginating auditLogs results using skiptoken continuation token.
rootCause: Skiptokens expire within 5-10 minutes of issuance. Slow pagination causes token expiry.
solution: Implement exponential retry: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s. Process pages within 5-10 min. Reduce result set with $top/$filter to speed pagination.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Microsoft Graph Reporting API (auditLogs/signIns) returns fewer results than Azure portal or missing entries. Non-interactive sign-ins not appearing. userPrincipalName filter returns different results than portal.
rootCause: Multiple causes: (1) Audit log latency up to 2 hours, (2) v1.0 only shows interactive sign-ins, (3) userPrincipalName filter is case-sensitive causing mismatches.
solution: Allow 2hr propagation delay. Use beta endpoint with signInEventTypes/any(t:t eq 'nonInteractiveUser') for non-interactive. Use startsWith(userPrincipalName,...) instead of eq for case-insensitive matching.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/AAD Reporting API/aad reporting api tsg

symptom: Cannot add Azure AD Graph API permissions in Azure Portal App Registration blade - the 'Azure Active Directory Graph' option is missing from the 'Request API permissions' list under Microsoft APIs.
rootCause: AAD Graph option removed from App Registration UI as part of deprecation to block new apps from onboarding.
solution: Workaround 1 (Portal): APIs my organization uses > search 'Windows Azure Active Directory'. Workaround 2 (Manifest): Add resourceAppId '00000002-0000-0000-c000-000000000000' to requiredResourceAccess. Note: temporary workarounds, AAD Graph is being retired.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Azure AD Graph and ADAL Deprecation/Closing AAD Graph onramp in App Reg UX

symptom: Customer receives alert 'Migrate Microsoft first-party apps from the retiring Azure AD Graph APIs to Microsoft Graph' for 1st party applications the customer does not own and cannot take action on.
rootCause: Engineering change Dec 2024 generated alerts for 1P Microsoft-owned apps. Fix deployed 12/9/2024 to hide non-actionable 1P apps.
solution: MS 1P apps have extensions configured and will not be impacted. Use email template from wiki. Verify if truly 1P via verifiedPublisher or app ID list. Handle in originating queue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Azure AD Graph and ADAL Deprecation/Alert migration from AAD Graph to MS Graph

symptom: iOS/macOS app using MSAL Obj-C throws MSALErrorDomain Code=-51118. Token acquisition fails with no clear error message.
rootCause: Redirect URI misconfiguration: msauth.BUNDLE_ID://auth not properly set in MSAL config, app registration, or info.plist. Missing CFBundleURLSchemes or LSApplicationQueriesSchemes entries.
solution: Verify redirect URI format is msauth.BUNDLE_ID://auth and registered on app registration. Ensure info.plist has msauth.{package_name} under CFBundleURLSchemes and msauthv2/msauthv3 under LSApplicationQueriesSchemes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For iOS and macOS/MSAL Apple TSG

symptom: MSAL iOS/macOS app gets error "No cached preferred_network for authority" when authenticating with Azure AD B2C.
rootCause: B2C authority URL does not include the tfp (trust framework policy) path segment required by MSAL for proper authority validation and caching.
solution: Include tfp in the B2C authority URL: https://b2cdomain.b2clogin.com/tfp/b2cdomain.onmicrosoft.com/b2c_1_policy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For iOS and macOS/MSAL Apple TSG

symptom: MSAL iOS/macOS app returns "Authority validation is not supported for this type of authority" error when using Azure AD B2C authority.
rootCause: B2C login domain (e.g. b2cdomain.b2clogin.com) is not added to MSAL knownAuthorities configuration, so instance discovery validation rejects it.
solution: Add the B2C login domain to MSAL knownAuthorities. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/config-authority#change-the-default-authority
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For iOS and macOS/MSAL Apple TSG

symptom: Xcode compile error "no visible @interface for {DataType} declares the selector {DataField}" (e.g. NSData msidAES128DecryptedDataWithKey) when building iOS app with MSAL Obj-C library.
rootCause: Category name collision between MSAL internal categories (e.g. NSData+AES.h) and other frameworks in the project that use the same category names.
solution: Fork MSAL Obj-C repo and rename the conflicting category file (e.g. NSData+AES.h to NSData+msidAES.h). Update all internal imports referencing the old name. Consume MSAL manually via submodules.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For iOS and macOS/MSAL Apple TSG

symptom: iOS app using MSAL gets keychain errors: "cannot_access_publisher_keychain" or "Failed to delete broker key with error: -34018". App cannot acquire tokens or maintain SSO.
rootCause: Keychain sharing not properly configured in Xcode entitlements, or keychain group name mismatch between MSAL config and entitlements file.
solution: Enable Keychain sharing in Xcode entitlements with group "com.microsoft.adalcache". Verify MSAL uses the same keychain group via WithIosKeychainSecurityGroup(). Ensure Custom Entitlements path is set in Bundle Signing. Try: restart device, rebuild, remove/reinstall app, delete iCloud app data.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For iOS and macOS/MSAL Apple TSG

symptom: MSAL .NET throws "No account or login hint was passed to the AcquireTokenSilent call" in ASP.NET/ASP.NET Core web apps after application restart or cookie-based session continuation.
rootCause: MSAL token cache is empty (in-memory by default) when AcquireTokenSilent is called. User has valid auth cookie but MSAL cache was lost on app restart. No persistent token cache configured.
solution: Option 1: Implement persistent token cache (SQL, distributed, or file-based). See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/msal/dotnet/how-to/token-cache-serialization. Option 2: Catch MsalUiRequiredException and send Challenge/redirect to re-authenticate. For Microsoft Identity Web: use RejectSessionCookieWhenAccountNotInCacheEvents to auto-reject stale cookies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL No account or login hint was passed

symptom: MSAL instance discovery validation fails when using a custom or non-Entra identity provider authority. App cannot authenticate against non-Microsoft IdP endpoints.
rootCause: MSAL performs instance discovery by default, validating the authority against known Microsoft identity provider endpoints. Custom/third-party IdP authorities are not in the trusted endpoint list.
solution: Disable instance discovery in MSAL configuration. .NET: .WithInstanceDiscovery(false); Java: .instanceDiscovery(false); iOS: MSALGlobalConfig.enableInstanceDiscovery = NO; JS: auth.instanceDiscovery = false; Python: instance_discovery=False; Go: public.WithInstanceDiscovery(false). Note: using MSAL for non-Entra IdPs is not officially supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL Instance Discovery

symptom: Need to identify all applications using AAD Graph API in a tenant for deprecation/migration to Microsoft Graph
rootCause: AAD Graph API is deprecated; applications still using it need to be identified and migrated to Microsoft Graph
solution: Use Kusto query on msodsuswest.kusto.windows.net/msods: GlobalIfxRestBusinessCommon filtering by contextId, env_time, clientVersion not internal, directAccessSource=Gateway, firstPartyServicePrincipal=false, then distinct applicationId
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Azure AD Graph and ADAL Deprecation/How to find all apps using AAD Graph

symptom: User clicks Forgot password link during Azure AD B2C sign-in and app receives error AADB2C90118 instead of redirecting to password reset flow
rootCause: By design B2C redirects back to app with error AADB2C90118 when user clicks forgot password; app must catch this and redirect to password reset policy
solution: In MSAL.js subscribe to msal:loginFailure, check for AADB2C90118, then loginRedirect with Password Reset policy authority URL
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/B2C Development/B2C Handle ForgotPassword

symptom: Error AADB2C95032: Cannot mix RSA and Oct types in one key set when managing B2C Trust Framework KeySets via Graph API
rootCause: Attempting to mix different key types (RSA and Oct) in a single Trust Framework key set
solution: Ensure each key set contains only one key type - either RSA or Oct. Create separate key sets for different types
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/B2C Development/B2C Trust Framework Policy

symptom: Error uploading PKCS12 key to B2C Trust Framework KeySet via Graph API - value must not be null or white space for password
rootCause: PKCS12 certificate key uploaded without providing the required password parameter
solution: Include the PKCS12 password when uploading the key via Graph API Trust Framework KeySet endpoint
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/B2C Development/B2C Trust Framework Policy

symptom: Error AADB2C90136: request is missing parameters or has wrong parameter when using B2C Trust Framework Graph APIs
rootCause: Required parameters missing or incorrect in Graph API request for Trust Framework operations
solution: Check error message body for parameter details. Import Base and BaseExtension policies before dependent files. Ensure KeySet exists before importing custom policies
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/B2C Development/B2C Trust Framework Policy

symptom: MSAL prompt parameter (select_account or login) is ignored during Azure AD B2C authentication with federated IdP - user auto signed in
rootCause: B2C does not pass prompt parameter from MSAL to federated IdP by default. User may be authenticated via AAD-joined device or SSO session
solution: Configure B2C custom policy: Add ClaimType aadPrompt with PartnerClaimType=prompt, then InputClaim in AAD technical profile with DefaultValue=login or select_account. Verify via Fiddler
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/B2C Development/MSAL prompt ignored

symptom: Claims from external IdP not mapped correctly to Azure AD B2C tokens - e.g. unique_name not appearing as preferred_username
rootCause: B2C does not automatically map all claims from federated IdPs; requires custom policy with explicit OutputClaim mappings
solution: In Trust Framework policy: add ClaimType under ClaimsSchema, then OutputClaim in AAD TechnicalProfile mapping PartnerClaimType to ClaimTypeReferenceId. Account Management team scope
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/B2C Development/Mapping claims between B2C and the IdP

symptom: Need to pass extra query parameters like login_hint from Azure AD B2C to federated IdP but user flows do not support this
rootCause: B2C standard user flows do not support passing custom query parameters to federated IdPs; requires custom policy
solution: Use B2C custom policy: Add ClaimType LoginHint, then InputClaim with PartnerClaimType=login_hint DefaultValue={OIDC:LoginHint}. In MSAL.js use extraQueryParams
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/B2C Development/Pass extra param to IdP

symptom: Compilation errors in MSAL Android project due to misconfigured JDK version - build failures across codebase
rootCause: MSAL Android SDK requires JDK 11; any other version causes compilation failures
solution: Android Studio > Preferences > Build Tools > Gradle > set Gradle JDK to 11.0.15. Clean and rebuild
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/Configure the MSAL Android development envrionment

symptom: MSAL Android compilation errors after accepting Android Studio prompt to update Gradle version from 6.8
rootCause: Updating Gradle version from 6.8 breaks dependencies in MSAL Android project
solution: File > Project Structure > Project > set Gradle Version to 6.8. Ignore all upgrade prompts
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/Configure the MSAL Android development envrionment

symptom: Error insufficient memory for Java Runtime Environment when building MSAL Android project
rootCause: Default JVM memory allocation insufficient for MSAL Android build
solution: In gradle.properties add: org.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx2048m -XX:MaxPermSize=512m -XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/Configure the MSAL Android development envrionment

symptom: Gradle daemon keeps restarting or never starts when building MSAL Android project
rootCause: Mismatch between Android Studio JDK and system JDK, or Gradle version reset from 6.8
solution: Verify Gradle 6.8. Download JBR 11 from JetBrains, replace Android Studio/jbr, set Gradle SDK and JAVA_HOME, invalidate cache restart
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/Configure the MSAL Android development envrionment

symptom: Error 401 Unauthorized accessing identitydivision.pkgs.visualstudio.com maven feed when building MSAL Android
rootCause: vstsMavenAccessToken in gradle.properties missing or incorrect for IdentityDivision DevOps maven feed
solution: Create PAT at identitydivision.visualstudio.com with Org=IdentityDivision Scopes=Packaging Read. Set vstsUsername=VSTS and vstsMavenAccessToken in gradle.properties
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/Configure the MSAL Android development envrionment

symptom: Cannot assign Microsoft Graph API permissions to Managed Identity or Service Principal from the Azure Portal app registration screen; the API permissions blade is not accessible
rootCause: Managed Identities and some service principals do not expose an API permissions UI in the Azure Portal, so permissions must be assigned programmatically
solution: Use Microsoft Graph PowerShell: Connect-Graph -scope "Directory.AccessAsUser.All", then Get-MgServicePrincipal to find the app and MS Graph resource (appId 00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000), then New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment to assign the required AppRoles. To remove, use Get-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment + Remove-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Assign MS Graph Permissions Manually

symptom: Microsoft Graph API returns 401 InvalidAuthenticationToken with message: Continuous access evaluation resulted in claims challenge with result: InteractionRequired and code: LocationConditionEvaluationSatisfied
rootCause: The access token is presented from a different IP address than where it was originally requested, and the new IP is blocked by a Conditional Access location policy. Common cause: split tunneling where login.microsoftonline.com and graph.microsoft.com traffic go through different gateways
solution: 1) Have user sign out and sign in again to get a fresh token from the correct IP. 2) If recurring, verify network configuration - ensure all Microsoft traffic goes through the same gateway (no split tunneling). The WWW-Authenticate header contains xms_rp_ipaddr showing the IP seen by Graph.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Continuous Access Evaluation in the MSGraph API

symptom: Azure Identity SDK-based clients (Azure CLI, Azure PowerShell, MS Graph PowerShell, Python SDK) receive unexpected CAE claim challenge errors from Microsoft Graph even though the application does not implement CAE handling
rootCause: Azure Identity SDK automatically includes the cp1 client capability in token requests, causing Azure AD to issue long-lived CAE tokens (20-28 hours). When CAE events occur, Graph returns claim challenges that the non-CAE-capable client cannot handle.
solution: Set environment variable AZURE_IDENTITY_DISABLE_CP1=true. Per-platform: MS Graph PowerShell: Disconnect-MgGraph, Remove-Item $env:USERPROFILE\.graph -Recurse -Force, reconnect. Python/CLI: export AZURE_IDENTITY_DISABLE_CP1=true. Java: System.setProperty("AZURE_IDENTITY_DISABLE_CP1", "true"). .NET: Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable("AZURE_IDENTITY_DISABLE_CP1", "true"). Java Graph SDK: pass false for isCAEEnabled in AzureIdentityAuthenticationProvider constructor.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Continuous Access Evaluation in the MSGraph API

symptom: Microsoft Graph .NET SDK always returns a successful status code for long-running operations (e.g., resetPassword API) without providing the async operation status or Location header
rootCause: The MS Graph .NET SDK does not support the longRunningOperation resource type, so it cannot automatically follow the Location header to track async operation status.
solution: Use the built-in HeadersInspectionHandlerOption with InspectResponseHeaders=true to capture the Location header from the response, then manually poll that URL for operation status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/DotNet/Known Issues and Limitations

symptom: Error "You are experiencing a delay in updating this permission on AAD. Try again after a few minutes." when trying to unconsent permissions in Microsoft Graph Explorer
rootCause: There is a propagation delay in Azure AD after consenting to a permission. Attempting to unconsent too soon triggers this error.
solution: Wait a few minutes after consenting before attempting to unconsent. Prerequisites: Directory.Read.All and DelegatedPermissionGrant.ReadWrite.All must be consented. After unconsenting, sign out and sign back in. Note: Some permissions like User.Read cannot be unconsented.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Graph Explorer Consent and Unconsent

symptom: Cannot use Microsoft Graph Explorer to access a different tenant as a guest user; Graph Explorer always signs into the home tenant
rootCause: By default, Graph Explorer authenticates against the user home tenant without specifying a target tenant.
solution: Append ?tenant={tenantname.onmicrosoft.com} to the Graph Explorer URL (e.g., https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/graph-explorer?tenant=contoso.onmicrosoft.com). Sign out first if already signed in, then sign in with the guest user credentials. Verify by calling GET /me - the userPrincipalName should contain EXT.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Graph Explorer Sign in as Guest

symptom: Browser console error "Permission was denied for this request to access the local address space" when using Microsoft Graph Explorer
rootCause: Chromium-based browsers enforce Private Network Access (PNA) security, blocking requests from less-trusted (public) to more-trusted (local/private) address spaces.
solution: For Edge: navigate to edge://flags/#block-insecure-private-network-requests, set to Disabled, restart browser. For Chrome: Settings > Privacy and security > Site settings > Local Network, enable "Sites can ask to access other devices on your local network", restart browser.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Graph Explorer TSG

symptom: MSAL.NET browser-based authentication dialog fails with error: the server or proxy was not found
rootCause: Network connectivity issue - proxy misconfiguration or TLS 1.2 not enabled on the client machine
solution: Check proxy settings (netsh winhttp show proxy), enable TLS 1.2 in Internet Options and registry via PowerShell script, restart. Review Fiddler capture for proxy errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/MSAL DotNet TSG

symptom: MSAL.NET MsalUiRequiredException ErrorCode current_broker_account: Only some brokers (WAM) can log in the current OS account
rootCause: WAM broker cannot perform silent auth when OS signed in with personal MS account or ListOperatingSystemAccounts not enabled
solution: Sign in with work/school account. Enable ListOperatingSystemAccounts in BrokerOptions. Use PublicClientApplication.OperatingSystemAccount for silent auth.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/MSAL DotNet TSG

symptom: MSAL.NET MsalServiceException: The wrong application (public or confidential) is being used with this authentication flow
rootCause: Redirect URI configured for wrong app type in app registration (web instead of desktop/public client)
solution: Enable app as public client in app registration, configure redirect URI as Mobile/Desktop type, not Web.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/MSAL DotNet TSG

symptom: MSAL.NET sign-in fails due to URL length too long, exceeds Chromium browser ~2000 char limit
rootCause: Too many scopes in single sign-in request causing authorization URL truncation
solution: Split scopes across multiple requests (e.g. 50 scopes -> two requests with 25 each).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/MSAL DotNet TSG

symptom: Could not load file or assembly Microsoft.Identity.Client - system cannot find the file specified
rootCause: Required MSAL DLL version missing from deployment or version mismatch with binding redirects
solution: Reinstall NuGet package, update binding redirects in web.config, clean+rebuild (delete bin/obj). Use Fusion Log Viewer (fuslogvw) to diagnose.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/MSAL DotNet TSG

symptom: MSAL.NET System.Security.Cryptography.CryptographicException: Keyset does not exist when using certificate auth
rootCause: Private key not persisted in certificate store - X509Certificate2 loaded without PersistKeySet flag
solution: Use X509KeyStorageFlags.PersistKeySet | Exportable when creating X509Certificate2. Consider Singleton pattern for certificate instance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/MSAL DotNet TSG

symptom: MSAL.NET IWA or federated sign-in fails - Integrated Windows Authentication not working with ADFS
rootCause: Client intranet zone not configured for ADFS URL, WIA not enabled, or ADFS AllowedUserAgents missing MSIE 7.0
solution: Add ADFS URL to Local Intranet zone, set Automatic logon in Intranet zone, enable Integrated Windows Auth in Advanced tab. On ADFS: add MSIE 7.0 to AllowedUserAgents.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/MSAL DotNet TSG

symptom: MSAL.NET MsalClientException: parsing_wstrust_response_failed when using AcquireTokenByUsernamePassword or IWA for federated users
rootCause: Federated IdP not returning valid SAML assertion in WS-Trust response - IdP misconfiguration or network timeout (504) to STS endpoint
solution: Collect Fiddler capture, review WS-Trust request/response, check ADFS logs. Verify STSAuthURL/MEXURL reachable. No MSAL-side fix - issue is in federated service config or network.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/MSAL parsing_wstrust_response_failed

symptom: MSAL returns id_token and refresh_token but no access_token when acquiring token from Azure AD B2C
rootCause: Invalid scope passed or API permission scopes not created/consented on the B2C app registration
solution: Create scopes on API app reg, grant permissions on client app reg, pass correct scope format. For B2C can use app-id as scope. Changes can take up to 2h to propagate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/Missing access token after acquiring token

symptom: MSAL B2C: No account matching the specified username / No matching account found in token cache
rootCause: B2C custom policy includes tid claim in OutputClaims which conflicts with MSAL cache account lookup
solution: Remove tid claim from B2C custom policy OutputClaims. Use alternative claim (issuerTenantId mapped to utid) if tenant ID needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/msal account not found in cache

symptom: Entra Connect in-place upgrade or fresh install fails: The directory synchronization state of the directory is invalid
rootCause: MSOnline retirement causes DirectorySynchronizationStatus=Other. Connect wizard depends on MSOnline for tenant auth.
solution: Use MS Graph PowerShell: Connect-MgGraph then Update-MgOrganization -OrganizationId tenantId -BodyParameter @{onPremisesSyncEnabled=true}. Resets DirSyncEnabledStatus. Retry install.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSOnline PowerShell - Deprecation and Retirement

symptom: Entra Connect cannot switch Staging mode via wizard after MSOnline PowerShell retirement
rootCause: Older Entra Connect versions (<2.4.18.0) depend on MSOnline for wizard operations, fails after retirement
solution: Install ADSyncTools module: Install-Module ADSyncTools; Import-Module ADSyncTools; Enable-ADSyncToolsStagingMode. Then upgrade Entra Connect to latest version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSOnline PowerShell - Deprecation and Retirement

symptom: MS Graph delta query for groups returns the same groups multiple times with empty values across pages; query appears to never end returning only nextLink and never deltaLink
rootCause: By design - internal USN watermark enumeration mechanism causes objects to re-emerge across pages when enumerating link changes (e.g., members). Overlapping watermark ranges produce duplicate objects in consecutive pages
solution: Set $top parameter to 999 to reduce pagination. Set Prefer header to 'return=minimal' to return only groups with changed properties. Keep following nextLinks until deltaLink appears - eventual convergence is guaranteed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Delta

symptom: MS Graph riskDetection/riskyUsers/signIns API queries using deprecated riskType enum property return errors or unexpected results
rootCause: Microsoft deprecated riskType enum property (deprecated 9/9/2020) and replaced it with riskEventType string property. For signIns API, riskEventTypes enum replaced with riskEventTypes_v2 string
solution: For Identity Protection APIs (riskDetection/riskyUserHistoryItem): use riskEventType (string) instead of riskType (enum). For signIns API: use riskEventTypes_v2 (string) instead of riskEventTypes (enum). Update search logic to match string values instead of numeric enum values. Use v1.0 endpoint as riskEventType is transitioning from beta to v1.0
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Identity Protection APIs/Deprecation of risk type enums and riskDetection or riskyUsers APIs

symptom: MS Graph API returns 'Insufficient privileges to complete the operation' despite having correct permissions consented
rootCause: Access token was issued before permissions were consented; the cached token does not contain the newly granted permissions. For managed identities, tokens are cached for 24 hours and cannot be refreshed
solution: Acquire a new access token after consent is granted. For managed identities, wait 24 hours for token cache to expire. Also check if user has PIM-activated role - verify the admin role GUID appears in the 'wids' claim of the access token
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Insufficient privileges

symptom: MS Graph returns 'Insufficient privileges' when updating sensitive user properties (accountEnabled, businessPhones, mobilePhone, passwordProfile, userPrincipalName, otherMails, onPremisesImmutableId)
rootCause: Sensitive user properties require additional specific API permissions beyond User.ReadWrite.All (e.g., User.EnableDisableAccount.All for accountEnabled, User-Phone for phone numbers, User-PasswordProfile for password, Directory.AccessAsUser.All for immutableId)
solution: Grant the specific additional permission for the sensitive property being updated. Refer to the sensitive actions permissions table at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/roles/permissions-reference#who-can-perform-sensitive-actions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Insufficient privileges

symptom: MS Graph returns 'Insufficient privileges' when updating group membership
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) Target is a Dynamic Group - manual member updates not supported; (2) Group is role-assignable - requires Global Admin/Privileged Role Admin + RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory; (3) Adding guest users requires User Admin or Group Admin role; (4) App-only token with servicePrincipal not set as group owner
solution: For dynamic groups: modify the dynamic membership rule instead. For role-assignable groups: assign Global Admin or Privileged Role Admin role + RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory. For guest users: assign User Admin or Group Admin role. For app-only: set servicePrincipal as group owner
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Insufficient privileges

symptom: Failed to delete application in Entra ID - 'Insufficient privileges to complete the operation' - application shows 'This application has been disabled by Microsoft'
rootCause: Application was disabled by Microsoft due to violation of services agreement (DisabledByMicrosoftStatus=2, IsDisabled=true). Created by compromised user or linked to malicious activity. Global Administrator cannot delete these apps
solution: Application cannot be deleted while in disabled state. Verify DisabledByMicrosoftStatus property in app manifest or via MSODS Explorer. The app cannot be used to authenticate while disabled. PG will not share detection details for security reasons. Refer customer to Identity Protection for Workload Identities documentation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Insufficient privileges

symptom: MS Graph returns 'Insufficient privileges' when using addKey/removeKey APIs with delegated permissions
rootCause: addKey and removeKey APIs do not support delegated permissions - only application permissions are supported
solution: Use application permissions (app-only token) instead of delegated permissions for addKey/removeKey operations. See IcM 690700259 for details
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Insufficient privileges

symptom: MS Graph CRUD operations (Create, Update, Delete) on Entra ID objects not reflected immediately - changes appear delayed
rootCause: Entra ID replication latency: CRUD operations can take up to 15 minutes in regular Entra ID tenants and up to 30 minutes in B2C tenants
solution: Wait up to 15 minutes (30 minutes for B2C tenants) for changes to propagate. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/entra/entra-id/app-integration/404-not-found-error-manage-objects-microsoft-graph
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Known Issues

symptom: MS Graph returns 401 error 'The calling app is not authenticated'
rootCause: Possible causes: (1) API does not support application permissions (app-only token); (2) Missing OID claim from servicePrincipalLess authentication; (3) Using public client instead of confidential client; (4) ServicePrincipal for client app does not exist in tenant
solution: Verify API supports application permissions if using app-only token. Ensure servicePrincipal exists in the tenant. Use confidential client instead of public client. Decode access token at jwt.ms to verify claims
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Known Issues

symptom: MS Graph returns 429 throttling error when calling authenticationMethods API
rootCause: authenticationMethods API has strict throttling limits: 5 requests per 10 seconds per app per tenant, 120 requests per 10 seconds per app across all tenants
solution: Implement rate limiting to stay within throttling bounds. Consider registering multiple apps and rotating MS Graph calls to distribute load. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/throttling-limits
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Known Issues

symptom: MS Graph signIns API filter by userPrincipalName using 'eq' operator returns no results even though sign-in events exist
rootCause: signIns API filter on userPrincipalName is case-sensitive. The UPN recorded in sign-in logs may differ in casing from the filter value (user may have typed or auto-completed with different capitalization)
solution: Use startsWith() instead of eq operator: /auditLogs/signIns?$filter=startsWith(userPrincipalName, 'user@contoso.com') - startsWith is not case sensitive
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Known Issues

symptom: MS Graph appRoleAssignments API does not return all results - no odata.nextLink in response for pagination
rootCause: appRoleAssignments API does not support standard OData pagination - nextLink is never returned regardless of result count. Also appRoleAssignedTo returns 500 when using skipToken with appId-based addressing
solution: Add $top parameter with high value: /users/{id}/appRoleAssignments?$top=999. Or filter on specific resource: $filter=resourceId eq {APP-ID}. For appRoleAssignedTo: use servicePrincipal Object ID instead of appId format
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph Known Issues

symptom: MSAL Android authentication fails with redirect URI mismatch error. The signature hash in the redirect URI does not match the app signing certificate. Common when debug keystore differs from release keystore or after publishing to Google Play Store.
rootCause: The Android app signature hash used in the redirect URI (msauth://<package>/<hash>) does not match the actual signing certificate. The hash may change after publishing to Google Play Store due to Google Play App Signing.
solution: Regenerate signature hash using keytool: keytool -exportcert -alias androiddebugkey -keystore ~/.android/debug.keystore | openssl sha1 -binary | openssl base64. For Play Store apps, get hash from Play Console > Setup > App Integrity > App signing. Update both the Entra ID app registration redirect URI and the MSAL config file. Ensure the hash is URL-encoded in the redirect URI but NOT in AndroidManifest.xml.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For Android/MSAL For Android TSG

symptom: MSAL Android app not redirected back to the application after completing authentication or MFA. User completes sign-in in browser/broker but app does not receive the auth result.
rootCause: The BrowserTabActivity intent filter in AndroidManifest.xml is misconfigured. Common issues: missing leading "/" in android:path for the signature hash, incorrect package name in android:host, or redirect_uri in MSAL config not matching the manifest.
solution: Verify AndroidManifest.xml has correct BrowserTabActivity with intent-filter: android:scheme="msauth", android:host="<package_name>", android:path="/<base64_signature_hash>" (note leading /). Ensure redirect_uri in MSAL config matches.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For Android/MSAL For Android TSG

symptom: MSAL Android error: "Requested account is from a different organization. Please make sure to use your organizational account." MsalClientException thrown during token acquisition.
rootCause: The home tenant of the signed-in account does not match the workplace join (device registration) account tenant.
solution: Open Microsoft Authenticator > Settings > Device Registration > Unregister device. Then re-register with the correct tenant account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For Android/MSAL For Android TSG

symptom: MSAL Android error: "More than one app is listening for the URL scheme defined for BrowserTabActivity in the AndroidManifest.xml". Multiple apps have same redirect URI scheme.
rootCause: Multiple apps on the device have registered the same msauth:// redirect URI scheme in their AndroidManifest.xml.
solution: Use a unique redirect URL scheme (e.g., com.yourapp.msauth://). Alternatively, set authorization_user_agent to WEBVIEW in MSAL config. Detect conflicting apps using PackageManager.queryIntentActivities().
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For Android/MSAL For Android TSG

symptom: MSAL Android: User is automatically signed in after explicitly signing out. Logout does not clear authentication state on non-shared devices.
rootCause: On non-shared devices, MSAL Android logout does not clear Microsoft Authenticator broker cache. This is by design.
solution: Expected behavior. For full sign-out across apps, configure Shared Device Mode: set shared_device_mode_supported to true and configure Authenticator for shared device mode.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For Android/MSAL For Android TSG

symptom: MSAL Android Manifest merger error: "uses-sdk:minSdkVersion 19 cannot be smaller than version 21 declared in library". Build fails.
rootCause: MSAL Android library requires minimum SDK version 21 (Android 5.0 Lollipop).
solution: Update AndroidManifest.xml: set uses-sdk android:minSdkVersion to 21 or higher.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For Android/MSAL For Android TSG

symptom: MSAL Android PKIX path building failed: SunCertPathBuilderException during broker or token acquisition. SSL handshake fails with certificate chain validation error on Android device.
rootCause: Android device is missing required intermediate CA or root CA certificates for Azure endpoints. Three scenarios: (A) server not sending intermediate CA, (B) root CA outdated/invalid, (C) root CA not in device trust store.
solution: Install all Root and Intermediate Certs from https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/azure-certificate-authority-details. For scenario A: ensure server sends complete certificate chain. Check inner exception for specific cause (chain validation failed vs trust anchor not found).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For Android/MSAL For Android TSG

symptom: Angular app throws "Maximum call stack size exceeded" error when loading MSAL configuration from a remote config service using HttpClient. Circular dependency during app initialization.
rootCause: HttpClient injection in MSAL config factory triggers all HTTP_INTERCEPTORS including MsalInterceptor, creating a circular dependency: MsalInterceptor -> MsalService -> MSAL_CONFIG -> HttpClient -> MsalInterceptor.
solution: Use HttpBackend instead of HttpClient in the config service constructor to bypass HTTP_INTERCEPTORS: this.http = new HttpClient(httpHandler). Load config via APP_INITIALIZER before MsalService initializes. See MSAL GitHub issue #1403.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL Angular Maximum call stack size exceeded

symptom: AADSTS50210 error during MSAL.js native broker bridge authentication on MacOS: "This web native bridge call resulted in a non-retriable error from the operating system."
rootCause: Browser called native broker (Company Portal) but broker returned PERSISTENT_ERROR. Most commonly caused by failed associated domain validation or SSO extension misconfiguration on MacOS.
solution: Review associated domain validation (section 3.1.1 of iOS/macOS broker TSG). Ensure SSO extension is configured. If SSO ext disabled, also remove Microsoft Single Sign-On Chrome extension. Company Portal 2603+ returns DISABLED status when SSO ext unavailable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS  Native broker bridge scenario

symptom: AADSTS9002325 during MSAL.js native broker bridge on MacOS/iOS: "Proof Key for Code Exchange is required for cross-origin authorization code redemption." PKCE validation fails.
rootCause: On Windows, PKCE bypassed for tb-env header requests. On MacOS/iOS the equivalent purpose=broker JWT claim is not recognized by ESTS.
solution: Retry the request. Known platform limitation tracked by engineering team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS  Native broker bridge scenario

symptom: BrowserAuthError: monitor_window_timeout - Token acquisition in iframe failed due to timeout. Silent token renewal via iframe does not complete.
rootCause: IdP not redirecting back to app within iframe, or redirect_uri page performs navigation that interrupts handleRedirectPromise. Or redirect_uri is protected and requires auth.
solution: Check Fiddler capture for IdP redirect. Ensure redirect_uri is a static blank page that does not redirect. For acquireTokenSilent failures, catch error and fallback to acquireTokenPopup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: BrowserAuthError: hash_empty_error - "Hash value cannot be processed because it is empty." The redirectUri clears the hash fragment before MSAL processes the auth response.
rootCause: Application or framework router updates window.location and clears URL hash fragment (#) before MSAL reads the authorization code. Common with Angular/React routers.
solution:
Configure redirect_uri to a static callback page without router logic. Angular: create empty auth.html in assets. React:
Completing auth
} />.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: AADSTS50058 during silent sign-in: "A silent sign-in request was sent but no user is signed in." InteractionRequiredAuthError. Session cookies not sent to Azure AD.
rootCause: No active user session in browser for Azure AD. Session cookies blocked by ITP/third-party restrictions, app domain and login.microsoftonline.com in different security zones, or session expired.
solution: Catch InteractionRequiredAuthError and fallback to acquireTokenPopup. Ensure app domain and login.microsoftonline.com are in same trusted zone. Add retry logic with acquireTokenPopup on failure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: MSAL.js error: "Interaction is currently in progress. Please ensure that this interaction has been completed before calling an interactive API." Login methods blocked.
rootCause: MSAL login methods called multiple times before previous attempt completes. Common with auto-login on page load without checking handleRedirectPromise first.
solution: Call handleRedirectPromise() before any interactive login. Check for existing accounts first. If stuck, clear sessionStorage "msal.<clientId>.interaction.status". MSAL v2.28.0+: use msalInstance.getBrowserStorage().clear().
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: MSAL Angular: MsalGuard not activating or protecting routes. Users can access guarded routes without authentication. Guard bypassed during initial navigation.
rootCause: initialNavigation property in RouterModule.forRoot() set to enabledNonBlocking, causing guard bypass during initial navigation in iframe/popup scenarios.
solution: Change initialNavigation from enabledNonBlocking to enabledBlocking in app-routing.module.ts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: Angular routing error: "Cannot match any routes. URL Segment: state". Occurs after Azure AD redirects back with state and code query parameters.
rootCause: Angular router has no routes for "state" and "code" URL segments appended by Azure AD during OAuth redirect.
solution: Add routes in app-routing.module.ts: { path: "code", component: HomeComponent } and { path: "state", component: HomeComponent }.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: MSAL React hooks (useMsalAuthentication, useIsAuthenticated) return incorrect state - user reported as not authenticated after successful login.
rootCause: MsalProvider renders before react-router-dom v6 completes first navigation. MSAL hooks evaluate before auth state is initialized. Navigation Client not configured.
solution: Wrap MsalProvider in ClientSideNavigation component that delays rendering using useState/useEffect. Set pca.setNavigationClient(navigationClient). Return null on first render.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: MSAL.js redirect results in infinite loop between app and login.microsoftonline.com. Browser keeps redirecting without completing authentication.
rootCause: App endpoint not in same trusted zone as login.microsoftonline.com (IE/Edge). Or x-ms-refreshTokenCredential header from broker plugin interfering.
solution: Ensure app domain and login.microsoftonline.com in same browser trusted zone. Verify redirect_uri callback does not perform additional redirects.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: MSAL.js CDN endpoint https://secure.aadcdn.microsoftonline-p.com returns HTTP 404. Auth library JS files cannot be loaded from deprecated CDN.
rootCause: CDN endpoint https://secure.aadcdn.microsoftonline-p.com/* deprecated. Microsoft stopped publishing newer library versions to CDN.
solution: Replace with https://alcdn.msauth.net/lib/{version}/js/ or https://alcdn.msftauth.net/lib/{version}/js/. For new projects, self-host auth libraries.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: Third-party security scanner flags Entra ID cookies as missing HttpOnly flag. Security audit reports cookies from login.microsoftonline.com without HttpOnly attribute.
rootCause: Entra ID intentionally sets some cookies without HttpOnly because JavaScript auth libraries (MSAL.js) need to read them.
solution: Expected behavior, by design. Document as accepted risk. Reference: "Web browser cookies used in Microsoft Entra authentication" on learn.microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS TSG

symptom: MSAL.js ClientAuthError: endpoints_resolution_error - "Endpoints cannot be resolved". Cloud Discovery Instance call fails or authority not validated.
rootCause: Authority URL malformed or not recognized. For B2C/CIAM/third-party IdP, knownAuthorities missing, causing MSAL to attempt Cloud Discovery which only works with standard Entra ID.
solution: Entra ID: authority = https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}. B2C: authority = https://contoso.b2clogin.com/{tenant}/{policy}, knownAuthorities: ["contoso.b2clogin.com"]. CIAM: authority = https://contoso.ciamlogin.com/{tenant} with matching knownAuthorities.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL JS endpoints_resolution_error

symptom: Angular app with Ivy compiler fails to use MsalModule.forRoot(). Compilation or runtime error during MSAL Angular module initialization with Ivy enabled.
rootCause: MsalModule.forRoot() incompatible with Angular Ivy compiler due to how Ivy handles module metadata and factory providers.
solution: Use FactoryProvider pattern instead of MsalModule.forRoot(). Create factory functions for MSAL_CONFIG, MSAL_CONFIG_ANGULAR, and MsalService as individual providers. See MSAL GitHub issue #1400.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL.js Angular Wrapper

symptom: Browser error: "Refused to display https://login.microsoftonline.com/... in a frame because it set X-Frame-Options to deny". Azure AD sign-in page cannot render in iframe.
rootCause: Azure AD sets X-Frame-Options: DENY on all sign-in pages as clickjacking protection. Not configurable, no exceptions.
solution: Use popup-based auth: loginPopup() and acquireTokenPopup(). For silent SSO in iframe, use prompt=none with login_hint. Never render Azure AD sign-in UI inside iframe.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/x frame options deny

symptom: X-Frame-Options deny during B2C authentication with SAML SSO, third-party IdP, or MFA. B2C custom policy fails to render in iframe.
rootCause: B2C custom policies involving SAML SSO, external IdP redirects, or MFA trigger iframe flows blocked by X-Frame-Options: DENY.
solution: Use loginPopup/acquireTokenPopup. For SAML SSO: configure HTTP Redirect (GET) binding instead of POST. Consider B2C Embedded sign-up/sign-in experience with custom policies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/x frame options deny

symptom: MS Graph SDK for Java: advanced queries using $count or directory object filters fail or return incomplete results
rootCause: ConsistencyLevel header not set to eventual when using advanced query capabilities like $count on directory objects
solution: Add ConsistencyLevel and $count via buildRequest options: graphClient.me().buildRequest(new QueryOption("$count", true), new HeaderOption("ConsistencyLevel", "eventual")).select("DisplayName,JobTitle").get(). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/aad-advanced-queries
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Java/MS Graph Client Java ConsistencyLevel

symptom: MS Graph Java SDK requests fail with 429 (throttled) or 503/504 (transient errors) without automatic retry, or retry count is insufficient
rootCause: Default retry handler not configured or has insufficient max retries for the workload
solution: For v5+ (Kiota): Create RetryHandlerOption, call setMaxRetries(N) and setDelay(seconds), add to OkHttpRequestAdapter middleware options. For v1-v4: Use HttpClients.createDefault(new RetryOptions(maxRetries, delay)) and pass to GraphServiceClient builder. Per-request override also possible via requestConfig.options.add(retryOption).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Java/MS Graph Java Max Retries

symptom: MS Graph API returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests / This request is throttled
rootCause: Application exceeds per-app per-tenant throttling limits for the specific MS Graph workload (e.g., Identity service: 5 requests per 10 seconds per app per tenant)
solution: 1) Check service-specific limits at learn.microsoft.com/graph/throttling-limits 2) Implement retry-after logic respecting Retry-After header 3) Use MS Graph SDKs with built-in retry 4) Verify via Kusto: cluster msgraphkus, db msgraphdb, table GlobalAggregatorServiceLogEvent - filter responseStatusCode==429 5) Check in ASC Microsoft Graph section with tenant/app/timestamp 6) If limits not exceeded but still throttled, escalate via AVA then IcM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/MS Graph Throttling

symptom: MS Graph API returns 401 Access token validation failure. Invalid audience
rootCause: The aud claim in the access token does not match MS Graph supported audience values
solution: Set correct scope/resource when acquiring token. Supported aud values: https://graph.microsoft.com, https://graph.microsoft.us, https://dod-graph.microsoft.us, 00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000. For 21Vianet: https://microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn. Decode token at jwt.ms to verify aud claim.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph API TSG

symptom: MS Graph API returns 403 Authorization_RequestDenied: Insufficient privileges to complete the operation
