symptom: AADSTS50059: No tenant-identifying information found in either the request or implied by any provided credentials. Silent sign-in fails without Azure AD redirecting the error back to the application.
rootCause: Silent request (prompt=none) uses a multi-tenant endpoint (common or organizations) without tenant-identifying info. The estsauth cookie is missing or expired. Azure AD cannot determine the tenant and does not redirect the error back to the app.
solution: Option 1: Use tenant-specific endpoint URL (login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant-id}/oauth2/authorize). Option 2: Configure app as multi-tenant. Then implement detection logic to catch the error and prompt for interactive sign-in (see MSAL error handling docs).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50059

symptom: AADSTS50070: Signout failed. The request specified session indexes which did not match the existing session(s). SAML logout fails when session index in LogoutRequest does not match the active SAML session.
rootCause: User has multiple SAML sessions active (each with different SessionIndex values). The logout request sends a SessionIndex from an earlier session that no longer matches the currently active session. This is a browser/app issue, not an ESTS issue.
solution: Ensure the SAML logout request uses the SessionIndex from the most recent SAML Response AuthnStatement. The application must track which SessionIndex is currently active and use that value in the LogoutRequest.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50070

symptom: AADSTS50196: The server terminated an operation because it encountered a client request loop. User stuck in MFA loop between mysignins.microsoft.com and login.microsoftonline.com after initial password login.
rootCause: Mismatch between authentication methods policy (UCP policy) configured by admin and the strong auth methods registered on user account. User default MFA method (e.g., NotificationOverAuthenticatorApp) is not in PolicyEnabledCredentials list, causing infinite MFA prompt loop.
solution: Align tenant authentication methods policy (UCP) with user registered/default MFA method. Either add user default method to PolicyEnabledCredentials, or change user default MFA to an allowed method. Diagnose via ASC auth troubleshooter: compare AllRegisteredMethods vs PolicyAllowedOrderedSAMs vs PolicyEnabledCredentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50072

symptom: AADSTS50076: Due to a configuration change made by your administrator, or because you moved to a new location, you must use multi-factor authentication to access the resource.
rootCause: Conditional Access policy requires MFA but the application is using a non-interactive flow (Windows Integrated Authentication or Resource Owner Password Credential) that cannot satisfy MFA interactively.
solution: 1) Use an interactive flow instead. 2) Ensure openid is in scopes during interactive sign-in (needed for auth code exchange). 3) Add client app to CA policy exception list. 4) Add user to CA policy exception. 5) Last resort: disable per-user MFA if not using CA policies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50076

symptom: User stuck during MFA/SSPR combined registration - no methods available when Security Defaults enabled and admin disabled app notification/code in MFA settings
rootCause: Conflict between Security Defaults (only allows Authenticator app) and MFA/authentication methods policy (disabled app notification/code). No registration methods remain.
solution: Re-adjust policy to allow mobile app notification/code, OR disable Security Defaults. Notify ISPDefaultProtection@service.microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/SSPR - Self Service Password Reset/SSPR - Windows Logon Screen

symptom: Reset Password link not showing on Windows 10/11 logon screen for SSPR
rootCause: Multiple possible causes including registry/policy/connectivity issues
solution: Run SSPR Win10 Logon Diagnostics script to identify specific cause.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/SSPR - Self Service Password Reset/SSPR - Windows Logon Screen

symptom: 3P app-only calls to Entra Graph APIs return stale/inconsistent data after mesh routing rollout disables app-only session consistency
rootCause: PG enabled mesh routing disabling app-only session consistency for 3P apps. Apps relying on session stickiness now see eventual consistency. Never guaranteed for app-only flows.
solution: (1) Point to docs: no session consistency for app-only, (2) Switch to delegated flows, (3) Design for eventual consistency, (4) S500: request allow-listing via Sev3 CRI to Programmability Infra.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/[Breaking Change] Enabling Mesh Routing (Disabling App Only Session Consistency) for 3P Apps calling Entra Graph APIs

symptom: AADSTS100033: Regional Cache Auth Service token requests for flows needing CA policy evaluation are forbidden
rootCause: App uses ESTSR regional endpoint instead of global ESTS. CA blocked on regional endpoints since late 2022. App Service REGION_NAME may override MSAL settings.
solution: (1) Switch to login.microsoftonline.com, (2) Check App Service REGION_NAME and set empty, (3) Update MSAL to latest, (4) Set ATTEMPT_REGION_DISCOVERY=False.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS100033

symptom: AADSTS1000901: Certificate cannot be used for requesting tokens - token_not_after expired. Managed Identity sign-in flow.
rootCause: Managed Identity certificate used by first-party resource has expired. Issuer is always managedidentitysnissuer.login.microsoft.com.
solution: Engage first-party resource support team and PG. Managed Identity must be recreated. NOT fixable by AAD AUTH team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS1000901

symptom: AADSTS1003031: Misconfigured required resource access when using tenant-wide admin consent URL for multi-tenant app
rootCause: App registration has no API permissions. Tenant-wide admin consent URL requires at least one API permission.
solution: (1) Add Microsoft Graph User.Read permission to app registration, OR (2) Use New-MgServicePrincipal or Graph Explorer POST /serviceprincipals to add SP manually.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS1003031

symptom: AADSTS1400001: Request nonce not provided. Common during WHFB sign-in. OAuth invalid_request.
rootCause: Device missing OS updates/patches, or nonce not generated correctly. Outdated OS builds commonly trigger this.
solution: (1) Update device OS, (2) Check app nonce generation, (3) If patched+correct, ICM to ESTS EEE. Check Event IDs 1085/1081 + Webauth.ETL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS1400001

symptom: AADSTS501051: Service principal gets error "Application isn't assigned to a role for the application" when requesting token via client_credentials grant for a resource API.
rootCause: The client service principal has not been granted an application permission (app role assignment) on the target resource service principal. Application permissions are implemented as app role assignments in Entra ID.
solution: Grant the calling service principal an app role assignment: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/{client-sp-id}/appRoleAssignments with principalId, resourceId, appRoleId. Or use New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment in Graph PowerShell. Requires AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All permission.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS501051

symptom: AADSTS50107: "The requested federation realm object does not exist" during On-Behalf-Of (OBO) flow with SAML assertion.
rootCause: The SAML assertion used in OBO flow is issued by Azure AD (http://sts.windows.net/{TenantID}) instead of the federated IdP (e.g., AD FS). Azure AD blocks its own issuer in this context.
solution: Ensure the SAML assertion is issued by the federated IdP (e.g., AD FS), not by Azure AD. Regenerate the assertion using the correct identity provider.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50107

symptom: AADSTS50107: "The requested federation realm object does not exist" for federated domain with third-party IdP returning incorrect IssuerURI.
rootCause: Third-party IdP returns an incorrect IssuerURI in the SAML response. Entra ID cannot match it to an existing federation realm object.
solution: Option 1: Contact third-party IdP to fix the IssuerURI. Option 2: Update IssuerURI in Entra ID using Update-MgDomainFederationConfiguration -DomainId "domain.com" -IssuerUri "{correct-value}". For SAML protocol add -PreferredAuthenticationProtocol SAMLP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50107

symptom: AADSTS50107: "The requested federation realm object does not exist" with multiple federated domains or child domains on same AD FS.
rootCause: Issuance Transform Rule is missing or outdated, or the AD FS issuer does not match what Entra ID expects when multiple domains/child domains are federated to the same AD FS instance.
solution: Review and fix the issuerid claim issuance transform rules in AD FS. See ADO Wiki: ADFS understanding the issuerid claim issuance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50107

symptom: AADSTS501209: "JWT signature is invalid" when accessing Office/Teams via browser. Error references expired MS-Organization-Access certificate (ThresholdJwtDeviceCredential.ValidateSignature failure).
rootCause: The device's MS-Organization-Access certificate (10-year Workplace Join cert) has expired. The ThresholdJwtDeviceCredential signature validation fails because the signing key is expired.
solution: 1) Check certificates via certlm.msc (Personal Store) for MS-Organization-Access certs. 2) Run dsregcmd /status and check WorkPlaceThumbprint and DeviceCertificateValidity. 3) Go to Settings > Accounts > Access work or school, select the expired account and Disconnect to remove the outdated certificate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS501209

symptom: AADSTS50126: "Invalid username or password" during interactive sign-in at Entra ID sign-in page.
rootCause: Username or password entered incorrectly, or if using Password Hash Sync, the password from on-premises AD has not yet synced to Entra ID.
solution: Verify credentials for typos. If using Password Hash Sync, check sync status and troubleshoot per MS docs on tshoot-connect-password-hash-synchronization.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50126

symptom: AADSTS50126: "Invalid username or password" during non-interactive sign-in using Resource Owner Password Credential (ROPC) grant_type=password flow.
rootCause: Federated user accounts are not supported for ROPC grant by default. Also may occur if Password Hash Sync has not completed, or if Public Client Flows is not enabled in the app registration.
solution: Use a managed (non-federated) account for ROPC, or switch to a supported auth flow. Enable Public Client Flows under app registration Authentication settings. Check PHS sync status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50126

symptom: AADSTS50140: Users must re-enter credentials and MFA each time they reopen Edge browser for third-party SAML apps, but Chrome works fine. Device credential ignored due to missing nonce.
rootCause: Edge Chromium sends POST request for BSSO but device credential nonce is missing. ESTS will not initiate nonce challenge (302 redirect) for POST requests because POST parameters would be lost. Chrome BSSO uses GET which supports nonce refresh.
solution: Check if customer can change HTTP POST request to GET for authentication flow. If POST is an absolute requirement, customer can raise a feature request with Microsoft.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50140

symptom: AADSTS50146: "This application is required to be configured with an application-specific signing key. It is either not configured with one, or the key has expired or is not yet valid."
rootCause: Application uses claims mapping policy but lacks a custom signing key, or the key has expired. Also triggered when AcceptMappedClaims is true but Identifier URI uses a non-verified/non-supported domain format.
solution: Option 1: Configure a custom signing key per MS docs. Option 2: Update app manifest with AcceptMappedClaims: true AND ensure Identifier URI uses a verified domain format. See jwt-claims-customization docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50146

symptom: AADSTS50158: "External security challenge not satisfied" during non-interactive sign-in to application with Terms of Use or third-party MFA conditional access policy.
rootCause: Terms of Use (and similar CA extensibility features) require interactive sign-in. Non-interactive sign-in (token endpoint) cannot verify ToU agreement. The client app receives interaction_required but does not handle it correctly.
solution: Client application must handle interaction_required error by prompting user for interactive sign-in. Refer to MSAL.js error handling docs. For CRI/ICM: confirm error relates to interactive sign-in requirement, then transfer to partner team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50158

symptom: AADSTS50177: "User account from identity provider does not exist in tenant and cannot access the application." External user trying to access resource tenant app.
rootCause: External user does not have a B2B Guest account with matching AlternativeSecurityID (PUID) or ProxyAddress in the resource tenant.
solution: Invite the external user to the resource tenant as a B2B guest first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50177

symptom: AADSTS50177 for passthrough user (B2B/CSP) accessing resource tenant. True error: ExternalChallengeNotSupportedForPassthroughusers.
rootCause: Resource tenant has conditional access policy with external challenge controls (e.g., Terms of Use) that are not supported for CSP/B2B passthrough users.
solution: Customer must exclude CSP service provider from CA policies enforcing external challenge controls. Use Auth Troubleshooter with correlation ID to identify blocking policy. See GDAP FAQ.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50177

symptom: AADSTS50178 for passthrough user (B2B/CSP) accessing resource tenant. True error: SessionControlNotSupportedForPassthroughUsers.
rootCause: Resource tenant has conditional access policy with session controls that are not supported for CSP/B2B passthrough users.
solution: Customer must exclude CSP service provider from CA policies enforcing session controls. Use Auth Troubleshooter with correlation ID to identify blocking policy. See GDAP FAQ.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50178

symptom: AADSTS50196: "The server terminated an operation because it encountered a loop while processing a request." Client app issues 15+ duplicate login requests in 5 minutes.
rootCause: Client application is misconfigured: missing token caching or exhibiting prompt loops. ESTS detects the loop (tracked per-instance via cookie on user hint, scopes, client ID, redirect URI, response type) and returns invalid_grant.
solution: Implement proper token caching in the client app. Handle invalid_grant by showing an interactive prompt instead of silently retrying. Ensure tokens with sufficient lifetime (10-60 min) are cached and not re-requested.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50196

symptom: AADSTS65005: The application asked for scope that does not exist. Occurs when app was created as SAML SSO (has applicationTemplateId for non-gallery SAML app) and tries to use OAuth2 scope request.
rootCause: Application was created as a SAML SSO application via applicationTemplateId. SAML apps cannot expose or use OAuth2 scopes.
solution: Recreate the application as an OAuth2 application using the App Registration portal or Microsoft Graph create Application API. Do not use applicationTemplate instantiate for OAuth2 apps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65005

symptom: AADSTS650051: The application needs access to a service the organization has not subscribed to. Resource API service principal missing in user tenant for multi-tenant app.
rootCause: Multi-tenant app references a resource API registered in another tenant. The resource API service principal has not been consented or created in the user tenant.
solution: Admin of user tenant must consent to resource API: 1) Admin Consent URL with resource API App ID, or 2) PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId {Resource-API-AppId}. Then create app role assignment if needed via New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650051

symptom: AADSTS650052: The app is trying to access a service that the organization lacks a service principal for. Tenant missing the required service principal.
rootCause: The application requires a service principal that does not exist in the user tenant. The app or its dependencies have not been provisioned.
solution: Create the missing service principal: Graph Explorer POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals with {appId: ClientID}, or PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -BodyParameter @{appId=ClientID}. Then grant admin consent in Entra portal Enterprise Applications.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650052

symptom: AADSTS650053 Scenario 1: Scope does not exist on the resource. Auth request sends scope parameter with incorrect format (commas instead of spaces separating scope values).
rootCause: The scope parameter in the OAuth2 authorization request uses incorrect separator characters (commas instead of spaces/%20) or contains special characters not recognized by Entra ID.
solution: Fix the scope parameter format: use spaces (%20 in URL encoding) to separate multiple scope values, not commas or other delimiters. Reference: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650053

symptom: AADSTS650053 Scenario 2: Scope does not exist on the resource. Admin consent URL used to consent application-type permissions (app roles), which is not supported.
rootCause: Application permissions (app roles) cannot be consented through the tenant-wide admin consent URL. Only delegated permissions support dynamic consent via admin consent URL.
solution: Add application permissions in the app registration API permissions blade and grant admin consent there. Do not use admin consent URL for application-type permissions. Alternative: use admin consent v2 endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650053

symptom: AADSTS650056 (OAuth): Misconfigured application. Client has not listed any permissions for the resource in app registration, or admin has not consented.
rootCause: The client app registration lacks API permissions for the requested resource, or admin consent has not been granted for the configured permissions.
solution: Add required API permissions (at minimum Microsoft Graph User.Read delegated) to the app registration for both client and resource apps. Then have admin grant consent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650056

symptom: AADSTS650056 (SAML): Misconfigured application. Issuer in SAMLRequest does not match the Identifier (Entity ID) configured on the Enterprise application.
rootCause: The Issuer element in the SAMLRequest does not match the Identifier URI / App ID URI configured on the Enterprise application in Entra ID.
solution: Either update the Enterprise application Identifier (Entity ID) to match the Issuer in the SAMLRequest, or update the SaaS application configuration to send the correct Issuer value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650056

symptom: AADSTS650057: Invalid resource. Client requested access to a resource not listed in its app registration required permissions.
rootCause: The resource API requested in the authentication flow is not added to the client app registration API permissions.
solution: Add the requested resource to the client app registration API permissions and grant admin consent. If resource and client are the same app, ensure the app has been consented to.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650057

symptom: AADSTS65006: Resource had no entitlements matching required permissions. A permission ID in the client app registration no longer exists on the resource.
rootCause: A scope or app role was deleted from the resource application, but the stale reference with the now-invalid permission ID remains in the client app registration API permissions.
solution: Remove the invalid permission from the client app registration API permissions. Check resource Application Manifest oauth2Permissions and appRoles for the missing permission ID. If resource has a replacement permission with same name, re-add it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65006

symptom: AADSTS70000: Provided grant is invalid or malformed. Occurs when redeeming a refresh token for a new access token.
rootCause: The ClientID or ClientSecret used in the token refresh request differs from the one that originally acquired the refresh token. Refresh tokens are bound to the client credentials used during initial acquisition.
solution: Ensure the same ClientID and ClientSecret that originally acquired the refresh token are used in the token refresh request. Verify no credential rotation occurred between token acquisition and refresh.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS70000

symptom: AADSTS700005: InvalidGrantRedeemAgainstWrongTenant. Authorization code redeemed in a different tenant than where it was issued. HTTP 400 invalid_grant.
rootCause: Tenant mismatch between /authorize and /token endpoints. Authorization code is tenant-bound. Common causes: mismatched authorities between frontend/backend, hard-coded tenant ID in SDK/middleware, mixed identity models (AD FS vs Entra).
solution: Align the tenant in /authorize and /token endpoints. For multi-tenant apps use /common or /organizations consistently. Capture browser trace to compare tenant IDs at both endpoints. Review MSAL/OIDC middleware configuration for hard-coded tenant IDs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS700005

symptom: AADSTS7000112: Application is disabled. Users cannot sign in to the application.
rootCause: The application 'Enabled for users to sign in' setting is set to No in the Enterprise application configuration in Entra ID.
solution: Go to Entra portal > Enterprise applications > find the app > Properties > set 'Enabled for users to sign in' to Yes. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/disable-user-sign-in-portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS7000112

symptom: AADSTS65005: The application asked for scope that does not exist on the resource. OAuth2 sign-in request specifying a scope fails even though the scope is valid for the app.
rootCause: The application was created as a SAML SSO app (has applicationTemplateId for non-gallery SAML SSO template 8adf8e6e-67b2-4cf2-a259-e3dc5476c621). SAML-created apps cannot use OAuth2 scopes.
solution: Recreate the application as an OAuth2 application using the App Registration portal or Microsoft Graph POST /applications API. Do not use applicationTemplate instantiate for OAuth2 apps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65005

symptom: AADSTS650051: The application needs access to a service that your organization has not subscribed to. Occurs when signing into a multi-tenant app and the resource API service principal does not exist in the user tenant.
rootCause: The resource API registered in tenant A has not been consented or provisioned in tenant B where the user is signing in. The service principal for the resource API is missing in the target tenant.
solution: Create the service principal for the resource API in the target tenant using: (1) Admin Consent URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/authorize?prompt=admin_consent&response_type=code&client_id={Resource-API-AppId}, or (2) PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId {Resource-API-AppId}. Then create app role assignments with New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650051

symptom: AADSTS650051: Application is requesting permissions that are either invalid or out of date. Misleading error during consent grant, actual cause is role assignment limit exceeded.
rootCause: User or service principal exceeded the limit of 1500 role assignments (Azure AD service limit). MSODS returns EntitlementValidationException but the error message is masked as AADSTS650051.
solution: Query MSODS tables in Kusto using the Correlation ID + Timestamp from the Audit Log to find the actual EntitlementValidationException. Reduce role assignments below 1500 limit per Azure AD Service Limits and Restrictions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650051

symptom: AADSTS650052: The app is trying to access a service that your organization lacks a service principal for. Tenant does not have the required application instance.
rootCause: The service principal for the target resource API does not exist in the tenant. The tenant has not subscribed to or enabled the required service.
solution: Create the service principal: (1) Graph Explorer: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals with body {appId: ClientID}, or (2) PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -BodyParameter @{appId=ClientID}. Then admin consent via URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/authorize?prompt=admin_consent&response_type=code&client_id={ClientID}.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650052

symptom: AADSTS650053: The application asked for scope that does not exist on the resource. Scope parameter uses commas instead of spaces to separate values.
rootCause: Invalid scope parameter format in the OAuth2 authentication request. The app developer used commas to separate scope values instead of spaces (space = %20 in URL encoding).
solution: Update the authentication request to use spaces (not commas) to separate scope values in the scope parameter. Reference: Microsoft identity platform OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650053

symptom: AADSTS650053: Scope does not exist on the resource. Occurs when trying admin consent via admin consent URL for Application-type API permissions.
rootCause: Application permissions (app roles) cannot be consented through the admin consent URL. The admin consent URL only works for delegated permissions.
solution: Add application permissions in the App Registration portal under API Permissions and consent from portal. Alternatively use the admin consent v2 endpoint for granting application permissions with dynamic scope.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650053

symptom: AADSTS650056: Misconfigured application in OAuth flow. Client has not listed any permissions for the resource in the requested permissions.
rootCause: The app registration for the client did not add API permissions for the requested resource, or admin has not consented to the permissions in the tenant.
solution: Add the required API permissions to the app registration (at minimum Microsoft Graph User.Read delegated permission for both client and resource). Have a tenant admin consent to the application.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650056

symptom: AADSTS650056: Misconfigured application in SAML authentication flow. The Issuer provided in the SAMLRequest does not match the Identifier (Entity ID).
rootCause: The Issuer element in the SAMLRequest does not match the Identifier (Entity ID) / Identifier URI / App ID URI configured on the Enterprise application in Entra ID.
solution: Either change the Identifier URI on the Enterprise application to match the Issuer in the SAMLRequest, or update the SaaS application configuration to send the correct Issuer value matching the configured Identifier.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650056

symptom: AADSTS650057: Invalid resource. The client has requested access to a resource which is not listed in the requested permissions in the client application registration.
rootCause: The resource API is not added to the client application's required API permissions in the app registration, or the resource API has not been consented to in the tenant.
solution: Scenario 1 (resource != client): Add the resource to the app's required API permissions and have admin consent. Scenario 2 (resource = client): Ensure the app itself has been consented to. For first-party Microsoft apps, the owning PG must provide instructions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650057

symptom: AADSTS65006: Resource had no entitlements matching required permissions for client. Requested permission IDs do not exist on the resource.
rootCause: A permission scope or app role was deleted from the resource application, but the client app registration still references the old Permission ID in its API permissions. The stale permission reference causes the error.
solution: Remove the invalid/stale permission from the client app registration's API permissions. Check the resource's Application Manifest under oauth2Permissions and appRoles to confirm the Permission ID no longer exists. Re-add the replacement permission if available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65006

symptom: AADSTS70000: Provided grant is invalid or malformed. Occurs when trying to acquire a new access token using a refresh token via the /oauth2/v2.0/token endpoint.
rootCause: The ClientID and/or ClientSecret used in the token refresh request is different from the one that originally acquired the refresh token. Refresh tokens are bound to the original client credentials.
solution: Ensure the same ClientID and ClientSecret that originally acquired the refresh token are used in the refresh request. Do not mix credentials from different app registrations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS70000

symptom: AADSTS700016: Application with identifier was not found in the directory. Auth request sent to wrong tenant or app not registered/consented.
rootCause: App does not exist in the directory (wrong AppId), or app is single-tenant but accessed from another tenant, or national cloud app accessed from global tenant.
solution: Verify the AppId (not ObjectId). If cross-tenant, convert app to multi-tenant via Azure portal. If single-tenant with guest user, use tenant-specific authority endpoint. For national clouds, register app in the correct cloud.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS700016

symptom: AADSTS7000215: Invalid client secret is provided when acquiring token via authorization code, client credentials, or refresh token grant.
rootCause: Client secret is expired, belongs to a different app registration, is not URL-encoded, or a different client_id/secret pair was used than the one that acquired the original refresh token.
solution: Verify client secret is not expired, matches the app registration client_id, is properly URL-encoded. For refresh token flows, ensure the same client_id and client_secret that acquired the original refresh token are used.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS7000215

symptom: AADSTS7000218: The request body must contain client_assertion or client_secret. Confidential client app fails to authenticate.
rootCause: App registration default client type is set to Confidential but using ROPC or device code flow (public client flows), or using authorization code flow without providing client_secret.
solution: For ROPC/device code: set Allow public client flows to Yes in App Registration > Authentication > Advanced Settings. For authorization code: provide client_secret or client_assertion in the token request body.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS7000218

symptom: AADSTS700027: Client assertion contains an invalid signature when using certificate-based authentication to acquire token.
rootCause: Wrong private key used to sign the client assertion, or wrong public key thumbprint sent to Azure AD. The private key does not match the certificate uploaded to the app registration.
solution: Verify private key matches the certificate by comparing modulus (openssl x509 -noout -modulus -in cert.crt | openssl md5 vs openssl rsa -noout -modulus -in key.pem | openssl md5). Ensure correct certificate is uploaded to App Registration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS700027

symptom: AADSTS7000272: Certificate used to sign federated credential could not be found in IdP metadata when using Federated Identity Credentials (FIC).
rootCause: FIC JWT header contains x5t (certificate thumbprint) but IdP JWKS metadata only publishes bare RSA key with kid, without x5t or x5c. Entra ID cannot fall back to key-based validation when x5t is present. Also possible: incorrect iss URL or inaccessible discovery endpoint.
solution: Ensure IdP JWKS metadata includes x5t and x5c fields matching the signing certificate. Verify iss in token is a fully qualified HTTPS URL. Confirm iss/.well-known/openid-configuration is publicly accessible and returns valid jwks_uri.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS7000272

symptom: AADSTS700054: response_type id_token is not enabled for the application. Sign-in request with response_type=id_token fails.
rootCause: The app registration has not enabled the ID token implicit grant flow. enableIdTokenIssuance is set to false in the application manifest.
solution: In App Registration > Manifest, set web.implicitGrantSettings.enableIdTokenIssuance to true. Or remove id_token from the response_type parameter. Allow up to 30 minutes for changes to reflect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS700054

symptom: AADSTS70011: The provided value for the input parameter scope is not valid. Scope format is invalid or scope does not exist.
rootCause: Scope parameter missing AppUriId prefix or scope_name suffix, invalid scope_name for the given AppUriId, or .default mixed with other non-OIDC scopes.
solution: Use format {AppUriId}/{scope_name} (e.g. https://api.contoso.com/Read.All). Check app registration oauth2PermissionScopes for valid scope names. Do not mix .default with other scopes except OIDC scopes (openid, profile, email, offline_access).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS70011

symptom: AADSTS701014: Cannot generate more one time passcodes (OneTimeCodeGenerationErrorGenerateLimit) during B2B email OTP authentication.
rootCause: Too many OTP codes requested within a 30-minute window. Maximum is 2 OTP codes per datacenter per 30 minutes.
solution: Wait at least 30 minutes before requesting new OTP codes. Inform user about the rate limit of 2 OTP codes per datacenter per 30-minute window.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS701014

symptom: AADSTS7500525: XML error in SAML message. The SAML request generated by the application is incorrect or invalid.
rootCause: Application vendor constructs a malformed SAML AuthnRequest that does not conform to SAML protocol specifications. May also occur when compressed SAML requests use wrong binding type.
solution: Collect Fiddler/SAML tracer to identify failing SAML request. Have app vendor fix SAML request format per SAML RFC. Ensure compression is only used for Redirect binding, not POST binding. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/single-sign-on-saml-protocol#authnrequest
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS7500525

symptom: AADSTS75008: Received SAML request with unexpected destination. Application sends incorrect optional destination attribute in SAML AuthnRequest.
rootCause: Application includes an unexpected destination attribute in the SAML request that does not match the expected Entra ID endpoint.
solution: Have the customer remove the destination attribute from the application-side SAML configuration. Collect Fiddler trace of working vs non-working scenarios. Decode SAMLRequest using Fiddler TextWizard (Transform From DeflatedSaml).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS75008

symptom: ADFS using non-English SQL Server backend throws Event ID 449 (AddFarmNodesIdentifierBackgroundTask StorageOperationException) every 5 minutes, OAuth token requests fail with MSIS7012/ADMIN0012, Get-AdfsAccessControlPolicy throws StorageOperationException, ADFS Management Console silently closes when adding Application Groups or Relying Party Trusts
rootCause: SQL Server installed with non-English language locale uses localized short month abbreviations (e.g. German Mr/Mai/Okt/Dez) causing HeartbeatTimeStamp parsing failures in ADFS stored procedures. WID databases are unaffected as they always use EN-US.
solution: Modify ADFS SQL stored procedures to handle non-English month abbreviations. Validate by running GetFarmNodes via PowerShell SQL query and checking Column1 (HeartbeatTimeStamp) uses English short month names. Stored procedures to modify: [IdentityServerPolicy].[GetFarmNodes] and related procedures (2016+).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Databases/ADFS SQL NonenglishCulture

symptom: Event ID 1021 "Encountered error during OAuth token request" logged repeatedly on ADFS 2016/2019/2022/2025 servers. MSIS9448: Interaction is required by the token broker. DeviceAuthenticationMethod SignedToken.
rootCause: Hybrid AD Joined or Azure AD Joined Windows 10/11 devices attempt OAuth JWT Bearer request for EnterprisePRT from ADFS, but Device Authentication is not configured and Device Writeback is not set up. The request is not signed by a registered device.
solution: Options: (1) Ignore if not using WHFB Cert Trust. (2) Deploy GPO registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\IdentityStore\LoadParameters\{B16898C6-A148-4967-9171-64D755DA8520} EnterpriseSTSTokenDisabled=dword:1. (3) Configure Device Writeback + Device Authentication in ADFS. (4) On Server 2019+, disable ADFS OAuth2 endpoint if no OAuth apps hosted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS EnterprisePRT and Event Id 1021

symptom: MSIS9426: Received invalid Oauth JWT Bearer. The JWT Bearer payload must contain scope. Logged in ADFS admin/debug logs.
rootCause: A Windows service on the client device makes OAuth calls to cloud resources through ADFS without including the required scope claim in the JWT Bearer payload.
solution: Check ADFS Debug logs Event ID 178 for request details and Event ID 151 for claims/displayname to identify the calling device. Disable unnecessary Windows call-home features on the offending device.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS EnterprisePRT and Event Id 1021

symptom: MSIS9699: GlobalAuthenticationPolicy on the Server does not allow this OAuth JWT Bearer request. Appears in dsregcmd /status SSO State section on Hybrid AD Joined devices with federated domain using ADFS 2016+.
rootCause: Device attempts EnterprisePRT acquisition from ADFS but Device Authentication is not configured in ADFS GlobalAuthenticationPolicy and Device Writeback is not set up.
solution: Run Add-AdfsScopeDescription -Name ugs to register UGS scope, then create ApplicationPermission and update AdfsGlobalAuthenticationPolicy with -DeviceAuthenticationMethod SignedToken. If not using WHFB Cert Trust, the error can be safely ignored.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS EnterprisePRT and Event Id 1021

symptom: After upgrading ADFS from Server 2016 to Server 2019+ via FBL raise, idpinitiatedsignon page not loading or WAP to ADFS trust failure. Event ID 54 in ADFS tracing: HTTPListener Unexpected exception Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
rootCause: Endpoints disabled in Server 2016 ADFS are carried over with disabled state during FBL upgrade to Server 2019+. Some of these endpoints are required for 2019+ functionality.
solution: After FBL upgrade to 2019+, review all ADFS endpoints with Get-AdfsEndpoint and enable required ones. Key required endpoints: /adfs/services/trust/mex, /adfs/ls/, /adfs/services/trust/2005/windowstransport, /adfs/services/trust/2005/certificatemixed, /adfs/services/trust/2005/usernamemixed, /adfs/services/trust/2005/kerberosmixed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS Endpoints - Purpose

symptom: ADFS fails or produces errors when SQL Server backend database is installed with a non-English language locale due to date format parsing issues with short month abbreviations
rootCause: SQL Server installed with non-English culture uses different short month abbreviations (e.g., German 'Mrz' instead of 'Mar', French 'janv' instead of 'Jan'), causing ADFS date parsing to fail. WID databases are unaffected as they always use EN-US locale.
solution: Ensure SQL Server is installed with English (US) locale for ADFS backend database, or reconfigure the SQL Server instance language to English. Alternatively, migrate to WID which does not have this issue. See the wiki for full language-to-abbreviation mapping table.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Databases/ADFS SQL NonenglishCulture

symptom: Event ID 1021 'Encountered error during OAuth token request' appears in ADFS Admin logs on Server 2016/2019 when Hybrid Azure AD Joined devices authenticate via ADFS
rootCause: Windows 10 client sends OAuth JWT Bearer request (OAuth 2.0 Protocol Extensions for Broker Clients) irrespective of whether device authentication is enabled on ADFS. ADFS expects the request from a registered device and checks AD, but device writeback is not enabled or device authentication is not configured, causing the error.
solution: This error can be safely ignored for Hybrid AD Joined devices. To suppress: (1) On Server 2016 only: disable OpenIDConfig endpoint, (2) If no OAuth/OIDC apps configured: disable ADFS/OAuth endpoint, (3) Set registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\IdentityStore\LoadParameters\{B16898C6-A148-4967-9171-64D755DA8520} EnterpriseSTSTokenDisabled=dword:1, (4) Enable Device Registration and Device Writeback in ADFS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS EnterprisePRT and Event Id 1021

symptom: After upgrading ADFS Farm Behavior Level (FBL) from Server 2016 to 2019+, idpinitiatedsignon page fails to load or WAP to ADFS trust fails. Event ID 54 HTTPListener Unexpected exception Object reference not set to an instance of an object in ADFS tracing.
rootCause: Endpoints disabled in older Server 2016 are carried over during FBL upgrade to Server 2019+. Some of these endpoints are required on the newer version but remain disabled, causing service outage.
solution: After FBL upgrade to Server 2019+, review all ADFS endpoints using Get-AdfsEndpoint and re-enable any required endpoints. Check the Required column in the ADFS endpoints documentation for Server 2019+ requirements. Pay special attention to endpoints that were manually disabled on the old farm.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS Endpoints - Purpose

symptom: Azure MFA Adapter in ADFS fails to authenticate users with MFA after certificate renewal or initial configuration. MFA requests fail or timeout.
rootCause: The self-signed certificate for Azure MFA Adapter is not properly registered with the Azure AD MFA client Service Principal (AppId 981f26a1-7f43-403b-a875-f8b09b8cd720), or the certificate has expired and not been renewed on all ADFS farm nodes.
solution: Run New-AdfsAzureMfaTenantCertificate -TenantID <tenantId> to generate a new cert. Use Microsoft.Graph module: Connect-MgGraph -Scopes Application.ReadWrite.All, get Service Principal by AppId 981f26a1-7f43-403b-a875-f8b09b8cd720, update KeyCredentials with new certificate. Ensure cert is deployed to ALL ADFS servers in the farm.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS Azure MFA Adapter Technical Deep Dive

symptom: AADSTS9002326: Cross-origin token redemption is permitted only for the Single-Page Application. Occurs when a non-SPA app attempts CORS token requests.
rootCause: ESTS blocks CORS for non-SPA apps created after 2021-02-15 to eliminate security vulnerability. Client credential flow from browser leaks secrets.
solution: Register Reply Address as SPA in app registration. Use Authorization Code flow + PKCE. Do not use client credential flow from browser frontend.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS9002326

symptom: AADSTS90038: Tenant request redirected to National Cloud. Public cloud user authenticates against government tenant endpoint.
rootCause: By design - redirection from microsoftonline.us to microsoftonline.com not supported. Cross-cloud auth requires targeting correct cloud.
solution: For third-party apps: use OIDC discovery endpoint to identify correct cloud. For first-party apps: engage owners for Instance_aware flow. Use MSAL National Cloud docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS90038

symptom: AADSTS900384: JWT token failed signature validation during certificate-based authentication.
rootCause: Private key used to sign ClientAssertion does not match public key uploaded to Entra ID.
solution: Verify key pair: openssl x509 -noout -modulus -in server.crt | openssl md5 vs openssl rsa -noout -modulus -in myserver.key | openssl md5. Modulus must match.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS900384

symptom: AADSTS900384: JWT token failed signature validation in cross-national-cloud scenario.
rootCause: Attempting to get access token for MS Graph across national clouds using incorrect endpoint.
solution: Use correct national cloud environment: Connect-AzAccount -Environment AzureUSGovernment or Connect-MgGraph -Environment USGov.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS900384

symptom: AADSTS90081: WS-Federation message error during IDP-initiated signout from external IDP (Okta/Shibboleth) via /login.srf endpoint.
rootCause: /login.srf endpoint only handles SAML/WS-Fed responses, not SAMLRequest from external IdP for logout.
solution: Cannot initiate SAML logout to /login.srf from external IDP. Platform limitation. File feature request if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS90081

symptom: AADSTS90093: Directory object quota limit exceeded for Principal. Non-admin user hits 250 object creation limit.
rootCause: By design. Non-admin: 250 objects/tenant. Admin default: 50k (Free) or 300k (verified domain).
solution: Admin increase quota via support request or delete unused objects. For service principals, contact support to raise per-principal limit.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS90093

symptom: AADSTS90094: AdminConsentRequired - app needs admin-level permissions. Non-admin user or risky permission detection.
rootCause: App requests admin-only permissions; or Microsoft detected risky request; or user assignment required without admin consent; or AllowedToReadOtherUsers=False.
solution: Admin grant consent via Entra portal. For AllowedToReadOtherUsers: Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -AllowedToReadOtherUsers $true. Configure user consent policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS90094

symptom: AADSTS900971: No reply address provided. Redirect URI missing or misconfigured during authentication.
rootCause: Reply URL missing/misconfigured in app registration, not in auth request, formatting errors, or mismatch with multiple URLs.
solution: Verify reply URL in App registrations > Authentication. Ensure redirect_uri matches registered URL exactly. Check formatting/typos.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS900971

symptom: AADSTS90099: Application not authorized in tenant. Partner delegated admin cannot use app.
rootCause: Partner has not pre-consented application to customer tenants in CSP/partner scenarios.
solution: Follow pre-consent guidance at docs.microsoft.com graph auth-cloudsolutionprovider. Engage Enterprise App Consent team if persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS90099

symptom: Kerberos authentication fails with KDC_ERR_ETYPE_NOTSUPP error in ADFS environment. Network trace shows AS Request followed by KRB_ERROR - KDC_ERR_ETYPE_NOSUPP.
rootCause: Encryption type mismatch between client, ADFS service account, and domain controller. Group Policy "Network security: Configure encryption types allowed for Kerberos" is misconfigured, or ADFS service account in AD does not have AES encryption support enabled (msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes).
solution: Ensure GPO encryption type settings match across all servers. For standard accounts: enable "This account supports Kerberos AES 128/256 bit encryption" in AD account properties. For gMSA accounts: verify msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes attribute is populated via Attribute Editor in ADUC Advanced Features view.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS TSG for KDC ERR ETYPE NOTSUPP

symptom: AVD SSO via ADFS fails with SendLogonCertRequest (-2146233029) error. ADFS service consumes excessive memory, Resource-Exhaustion-Detector events logged. LogonCertificate requests timeout or are significantly slow.
rootCause: ADFS acts as Enrollment Agent for AVD logon certificates. Each certificate request leaves a public key artifact in the ADFS service account profile under SystemCertificates\My\Certificates. Over time tens of thousands accumulate degrading performance. Unlike WHFB certificates, LogonCertificate template lacks the CTPRIVATEKEY_FLAG_HELLO_LOGON_KEY flag causing CertEnroll to install certs in the enrollment agent store.
solution: Set the CTPRIVATEKEY_FLAG_HELLO_LOGON_KEY flag on the LogonCertificate template: certutil.exe -dsTemplate <TemplateName> msPKI-Private-Key-Flag +CTPRIVATEKEY_FLAG_HELLO_LOGON_KEY. Then clean up accumulated certificates using PowerShell script with X509Store to find and remove certificates by template name. Run cleanup as ADFS service account (scheduled task for gMSA). Deletion may be slow (~10-166 certs/sec).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - AVD SSO failing with error SendLogonCertRequest (-2146233029)

symptom: After updating ADFS SSL certificate, web theme renders incorrectly, certificate-based authentication fails, WS-Trust auth (Hybrid Join, Azure PRT, federated sign-in) fails. Event ID 102 logged with HttpListenerException Access Denied for port 49443 endpoints.
rootCause: ADFS farm was initially configured with alternate host binding (certauth.sts.contoso.com on port 443) for CBA. Admin used Set-AdfsSslCertificate instead of Set-AdfsAlternateTlsClientBinding to replace SSL cert. This reconfigured ADFS to use default port 49443 for CBA but URL reservation for https://+:49443/adfs/ was never created during initial setup causing all endpoint registrations to fail.
solution: Verify binding mode: check Get-AdfsSslCertificate for certauth binding, (Get-AdfsProperties).TlsClientPort for 443 vs 49443, and netsh http show url for missing 49443 reservation. Fix by either: (1) switching back to alternate host binding with Set-AdfsAlternateTlsClientBinding if new cert has certauth SAN, or (2) adding missing URL reservation for 49443 and rebinding. Do NOT add ADFS service account to local Administrators group as workaround.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - After SSL Certificate change Endpoints are no longer published and Webpage may not render correctly

symptom: When setting ADFS certificate from MMC, the Windows Security certificate picker dialog appears blank/empty with no certificates listed. Incident probability: very low.
rootCause: Third-party credential provider filter (RSA SecureID) interfering with X509Certificate2UI.SelectFromCollection API. Registry entries under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Authentication\Credential Provider Filters block certificate display.
solution: Workaround: Use PowerShell Set-AdfsCertificate -CertificateType Service-Communications -Thumbprint <CERTTHUMBPRINT> instead of MMC. Fix: Review RSA SecureID GPO settings (RSA.Policy: Exclude all third-party Credential Providers) and relax filtering. Verify with test script using X509Certificate2UI to isolate whether issue is MMC-specific or system-wide.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - Certificate Selection Dialog Appears Blank

symptom: ADFS DeviceRegistrationService fails to initialize. Event ID 2076 in Device Registration Service Tracing/Debug log with error: Metadata contains a reference that cannot be resolved, MaxReceivedMessageSize (65536) exceeded. Windows Hello for Business (Hybrid and On-prem Cert Trust) deployments affected.
rootCause: Administrator enabled ALL WS-Trust Endpoints in ADFS Endpoint configuration causing the MEX document (/adfs/services/trust/mex) to grow beyond the 65536 byte MaxReceivedMessageSize buffer limit used by DRS MetadataExchangeClient during localhost MEX loading at service startup.
solution: Disable unnecessary WS-Trust Endpoints using ADFS Endpoint configuration. Refer to ADFS Endpoints - Purpose guide to identify which endpoints are required. Restart ADFS service after disabling unnecessary endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - DeviceRegistrationService initialization failures issues tracker

symptom: User resets password containing apostrophe, double quotes, or double hyphens. Password works on Windows/Web/Android/iPad but fails on iOS (iPhone). Sign-in error 0xc000006a STATUS_WRONG_PASSWORD in ADFS traces.
rootCause: iOS Smart Punctuation feature (since iOS 11) converts: vertical apostrophe (U+0027) to left/right quotation marks (U+2018/U+2019), double hyphens (--) to em dash, and straight double quotes to curly quotes. iPadOS keyboard handles differently explaining iPad success. The altered characters result in a different password being sent to the authentication service.
solution: Option 1: Long-press apostrophe key on iOS and select vertical apostrophe (rightmost). Option 2: Disable iOS Smart Punctuation: Settings > General > Keyboards > toggle Smart Punctuation off. Option 3 (server-side): Add spellcheck=false to ADFS password field via onload.js web theme customization: var pwdf=document.getElementById("passwordInput"); if(pwdf){pwdf.spellcheck=false}.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - Entra ID Authentication on IOS devices and Android not working after password reset

symptom: Android device (Samsung S23) sign-in fails with error 50126 (invalid username or password) across all apps (Outlook, Samsung email, 1st and 3rd party). Issue specific to default Samsung keyboard.
rootCause: Default Samsung keyboard alters special characters in password input similarly to iOS Smart Punctuation sending incorrect character codes to the authentication service.
solution: Change default Samsung keyboard to Gboard keyboard. Debug via Chrome remote debugging: enable Developer Options on Android, enable USB Debugging, connect via USB, use chrome://inspect/#devices on Windows to trace the mobile browser session.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - Entra ID Authentication on IOS devices and Android not working after password reset

symptom: ADFS service using gMSA fails to start or authenticate. Netlogon.log shows [MSA] NetpGMSAAddWrapper failed 0xc0000022 (STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED).
rootCause: The ADFS server computer account is not listed in the gMSA PrincipalsAllowedToRetrieveManagedPassword attribute so it cannot retrieve the managed password from AD.
solution: Check current permissions: Get-ADServiceAccount <gMSA> -Properties PrincipalsAllowedToRetrieveManagedPassword. Add missing server: Set-ADServiceAccount <gMSA> -PrincipalsAllowedToRetrieveManagedPassword Host1$,Host2$. Important: include ALL existing allowed principals when running Set command. Verify with Test-ADServiceAccount -Identity <gMSA>.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - GMSA Service account information and troubleshooting

symptom: gMSA password retrieval fails. Netlogon.log shows [MSA] NetpGMSAAddWrapper failed 0xc0000719 with message Supported encryption types did not match.
rootCause: msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes attribute on the ADFS server computer account does not match the gMSA account encryption types in Active Directory.
solution: Review msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes attribute on both the ADFS server computer object and the gMSA account in AD. Ensure encryption types match. Reference: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/core-infrastructure-and-security/decrypting-the-selection-of-supported-kerberos-encryption-types/ba-p/1628797
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - GMSA Service account information and troubleshooting

symptom: Users cannot sign in to Azure AD Joined/Hybrid Joined Windows 10 devices from outside corporate network when ADFS MFA is configured. Error 0xC00484C1 (AAD_CLOUDAP_E_WSTRUST_SAML_TOKENS_ARE_EMPTY) in AAD Analytic log. Event 1088 MSIS7068: Access denied.
rootCause: ADFS MFA policy (global or per-RPT) blocks Windows device sign-in because it uses non-interactive WS-Trust flow (user-agent: Windows-AzureAD-Authentication-Provider/1.0) which cannot present an MFA prompt.
solution: For classic AdditionalAuthenticationRules: add exclusion NOT exists([Type=="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2012/01/requestcontext/claims/x-ms-client-user-agent", Value=="Windows-AzureAD-Authentication-Provider/1.0"]) before the MFA issuance rule. For Access Control Policies: configure exception for the user-agent AND add explicit PERMIT condition. Also verify AuthorizationRules do not require authnmethodsreferences for MFA without the same exclusion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - MFA For Non Interactive Login

symptom: ADFS Extranet Smart Lockout (ESL) not working after enabling. Get-ADFSAccountActivity fails even though ExtranetLockoutEnabled shows True. Lockout not being enforced.
rootCause: ADFS service was not restarted on all farm nodes after enabling ESL. The new ESL mode requires a service restart on every node to take effect.
solution: Restart ADFS service on ALL nodes in the farm: Restart-Service adfssrv. For WID farms, wait 5 minutes between restarts for config sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS Extranet Smart Lockout (ESL)

symptom: Users from external trust domain cannot authenticate via ADFS extranet while internal access works. ADFS debug log shows CrackName DS_NAME_ERROR_NOT_FOUND for the user account.
rootCause: ADFS ESL uses DsCrackNames API which requires forest trust. External trust breaks CrackNames RPC calls because DsCrackNames only resolves accounts through GC lookups and forest trusts, not external trusts.
solution: Change trust type from external trust to two-way forest trust. Alternatively, disable ESL (not recommended as it reduces security). External Lockout feature explicitly does not support External Trusts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS Extranet Smart Lockout (ESL)

symptom: Get-AdfsAccountActivity throws Exception of type Microsoft.IdentityServer.User.UserActivityRestServiceException was thrown when querying ESL account activity.
rootCause: Misconfigured ADFS ServicePrincipalName (SPN) registration breaking Kerberos authentication on the ADFS REST API endpoint used by ESL management cmdlets.
solution: Verify that HOST SPN (HOST/<ADFSFarmName>) or explicit HTTP SPN (HTTP/<ADFSFarmName>) are only registered on the ADFS service account. Check for and remove any duplicate SPN registered on different accounts in Active Directory using setspn -X.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS Extranet Smart Lockout (ESL)

symptom: After running Reset-AdfsAccountLockout on primary ADFS server, user still cannot log in on secondary ADFS server. Secondary shows IsLockedOut=true and syncComplete=False despite sync infrastructure working correctly.
rootCause: ESL secondary nodes sync user activity from master only per user per hour (DefaultUserActivitySyncWindow = 1 hour, hardcoded). Reset-AdfsAccountLockout clears the counter on primary but is NOT auto-synced to secondaries. Secondary only contacts master when local data is stale (older than UserActivitySyncWindow).
solution: After Reset-AdfsAccountLockout: (1) Direct user auth to primary ADFS server first, OR (2) Wait up to 60 minutes for UserActivitySyncWindow to expire. If ExtranetObservationWindow < 1 hour, the lockout will clear when that window passes. Use PowerShell script to query AccountActivity table from AdfsArtifactStore DB to monitor LastUpdate timestamps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS Extranet Smart Lockout (ESL) unlock behavior

symptom: After FBL raise from 2012R2 to 2016, secondary ADFS server shows Event ID 344 and 345 synchronization errors. Get-AdfsSyncProperties shows last sync was with old 2012R2 ADFS server.
rootCause: FBL raise was executed while farm was still in mixed mode (2012R2 + 2016 servers). Secondary 2016 server retained old FBL 1 database (AdfsConfiguration) but FBL is set to version 3 (AdfsConfigurationV3), creating a database/FBL mismatch.
solution: Re-add the secondary node with Add-AdfsFarmNode -OverwriteConfiguration -PrimaryComputerName PrimaryWIDHost -PrimaryComputerPort 80 -ServiceAccountCredential $cred -CertificateThumbprint thumbprint. If AdfsArtifactStore.mdf files are missing from C:\Windows\WID\Data\, copy them from the primary server first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS FBL Raise

symptom: Error about rowguid issue when trying to raise the FBL on ADFS farm using SQL database backend.
rootCause: SQL database has replication enabled which conflicts with the FBL raise operation (Invoke-AdfsFarmBehaviorLevelRaise) because replication uses rowguid columns.
solution: Stop SQL database replication before raising the FBL. After ADFS farm upgrade completes, re-enable SQL replication. Engage SQL Replication Team for assistance with stopping/starting replication safely.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS FBL Raise

symptom: Event ID 180 is logged and ADFS endpoints are missing in Windows Server 2016 after installing March 2018 KB4088787 update on primary node of farm raised from FBL 1 (2012R2) to FBL 3 (2016).
rootCause: Known issue in KB4088787 causing regression and reordering of rows in the ADFS configuration database on farms that were raised from FBL 1 to FBL 3.
solution: Follow resolution in Microsoft KB article: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-server/identity/adfs-error-180-endpoints-missing
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS FBL Raise

symptom: ADFS farm FBL upgrade (Invoke-AdfsFarmBehaviorLevelRaise) fails, and retrying the command errors with message that the database already exists.
rootCause: The failed upgrade command may have partially created the new configuration database (e.g., AdfsConfigurationV3). Retrying conflicts with the already-existing partial database.
solution: Run Restore-AdfsFarmBehaviorLevel to delete the newly created database, then run Invoke-AdfsFarmBehaviorLevelRaise again. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/adfs/restore-adfsfarmbehaviorlevel
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Deep Dives - Features explained/ADFS FBL Raise

symptom: OAuth2/OIDC authentication fails with ADFS. The state parameter containing URL-encoded + (%2B) is returned by ADFS unencoded as literal + in the authorization code grant response, causing client-side state validation to fail because the values no longer match.
rootCause: Technical defect in ADFS 2016/2019/2022 where the OAuth2 authorization code response does not preserve URL encoding of the + character in the state parameter. ADFS decodes %2B to + before returning it.
solution: Application-side workaround: handle both encoded (%2B) and unencoded (+) versions when comparing state values, or avoid using + characters in state parameter values. This is a known ADFS product defect. Use Postman with the provided template to reproduce.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Known Issues/ADFS - Incorrectly formating State parameter

symptom: ADFS error MSIS9921: Received invalid UserInfo request. Audience in the access token is not same as the identifier of the UserInfo relying party trust urn:microsoft:userinfo. Occurs with RedHat Openshift and other OIDC apps calling the ADFS UserInfo endpoint.
rootCause: When OIDC client uses a WebAPI resource identifier (introduced via ALLAccessTokenClaims feature in KB4019472), ADFS issues the access token with the WebAPI audience instead of the built-in UserInfo audience (urn:microsoft:userinfo). The UserInfo endpoint rejects the token because audience does not match.
solution: Install the fix: KB5034770 (Server 2022) or KB5034768 (Server 2019), EnabledByDefault from June 2024 CU (KB5039227/KB5039217). Server 2016 not supported - must upgrade ADFS. If still DisabledByDefault, enable via GPO (install enablement MSI then configure in gpedit.msc) or registry: Server 2022 value 19489421, Server 2019 value 3544162445 under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides set to DWORD 1. Verify fix by checking AccessToken aud matches appid and new idsub claim is present.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Known Issues/ADFS - Redhat Openshift Access to ADFS Userinfo (archived)

symptom: ADFS MFA fails for OpenID/OAuth2 applications when Okta MFA Adapter is configured. Error MSIS7065: There are no registered protocol handlers on path /adfs/oauth2/authorize/. WS-Fed/SAML apps work fine.
rootCause: Okta MFA adapter posts a State parameter in the form body that conflicts with the State parameter already in the authorization request URL. ADFS creates a named collection with duplicate State values, triggering MSIS9460: Invalid Authorization Message with multiple values.
solution: Contact Okta support to fix the conflicting State parameter (known issue since Nov 2021). Alternatively, migrate to Azure MFA adapter which does not have this conflict.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - Okta MFA failing for OpenID Applications

symptom: ADFS sub claim issued in ID_Tokens changes value randomly for the same user across subsequent authentication requests to the same OpenID/OAuth2 application.
rootCause: The sub claim is a hash of ClientID + sourceanchor + PrivacyEntropy. The sourceanchor (WindowsAccountName) is resolved via LSA LookupCache which is case-insensitive for lookups but case-sensitive for storage. Cached values may have inconsistent casing causing different hash outputs.
solution: Disable LSA Lookup Caching on all ADFS servers: Registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa -> create DWORD LsaLookupCacheMaxSize = 0, then restart servers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - Sub claims issued in ID_Tokens may change for the same user randomly

symptom: ADFS Service fails to start with Error 1297: A privilege that the service requires to function properly does not exist in the service account configuration.
rootCause: ADFS setup grants required privileges via local GPO, but domain-based GPOs override local GPOs. A domain GPO removed the ADFS service account from required privileges such as Generate security audits.
solution: Run gpresult to identify overriding GPO. Ensure the ADFS service account (or NT Service\adfssrv) has: Logon as a Service and Generate security audits. Verify not in Deny Logon as a Service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - Troubleshooting ADFS Service startup failures

symptom: ADFS Service fails to start or management commands fail with Invalid namespace or Invalid class. PowerShell Get-AdfsProperties returns ManagementException. Debug log shows TypeInitializationException referencing WmiUtility.GetConfigurationDatabaseConnectionString.
rootCause: WMI corruption or WMI repository rebuild removed the ADFS WMI namespace. The ADFS MOF file resides in %windir%\ADFS (not standard WBEM folder), so WMI repairs do not re-register it.
solution: Re-register ADFS WMI namespace from elevated CMD: mofcomp.exe C:\Windows\ADFS\Microsoft.IdentityServer.Service.mof
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS - Troubleshooting ADFS Service startup failures

symptom: ADFS 2016: post_logout_redirect_uri is not honored when an External Claims Provider (SAML IDP) is used. User is not redirected to specified destination after logout.
rootCause: When ADFS processes logout through an external SAML Claims Provider, after receiving the LogoutResponse, the post_logout_redirect_uri from the original OAuth2 logout request is lost. ADFS constructs a new URI with only client-request-id.
solution: Known bug in Server 2016 (won't fix). Fixed in Server 2019 via KB5005625. Upgrade ADFS to 2019+ and apply KB5005625.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Troubleshooting/ADFS 2016 post_logout_redirect_uri= is not honored when an External Claims Provider is being used

symptom: ADFS 'Sign in as current user' (Windows Authentication) requires clicking twice before WIA authentication succeeds. Occurs when prompt=login without specifying auth method, or SAML AuthnRequest contains multiple AuthnContextClassRef including WIA RAC.
rootCause: Credential provider selection is lost during navigation between /ADFS/LS and /ADFS/LS/WIA endpoint. Known issue in ADFS 2016 and ADFS 2019.
solution: Add JavaScript to onload.js of custom web theme to auto-select WIA: check if path is /adfs/ls/wia, referrer is not /adfs/ls/wia, authOptions and WindowsAuthentication elements exist, then trigger WindowsAuthentication.onclick(). Deploy via New-AdfsWebTheme, Export-AdfsWebTheme, Set-AdfsWebTheme -AdditionalFileResource, Set-AdfsWebConfig -ActiveThemeName.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Webpage Customizations/ADFS "Sign in as current user" requires users to click the link two times for WIA

symptom: ADFS server fails HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) compliance check - no Strict-Transport-Security header in HTTP response when scanning root URL adfs.contoso.com.
rootCause: ADFS does not emit HSTS header by default. ADFS only serves HTTPS (no HTTP listener) so HSTS is not technically required. Root web site adfs.contoso.com is not a web page that sends response headers - only /adfs/ls/ does.
solution: For ADFS 2016+/2019: enable HSTS via Set-AdfsResponseHeaders. If compliance scanner checks root URL, configure IIS on all WAP servers: (1) install IIS, update web.config with custom headers, (2) add registry DisableServerHeader=2 at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters, (3) create HTTPS 443 binding in IIS and remove HTTP 80, (4) remove default SSL cert via netsh http delete sslcert ipport=0.0.0.0:443. Reboot all WAP servers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Webpage Customizations/ADFS HSTS Compliance Check

symptom: Web applications depending on HTTP Referer header stop working after Chrome 85 update. Referrer is truncated or omitted during cross-origin navigation through ADFS, breaking apps that rely on full referrer URL.
rootCause: Chrome 85 changed default referrer policy from no-referrer-when-downgrade to strict-origin-when-cross-origin, causing cross-origin requests to only include origin (not full path) in Referer header.
solution: Configure ADFS 2016+ Response Headers: (1) Verify ResponseHeadersEnabled via (get-adfsproperties).ResponseHeadersEnabled, (2) Enable if needed: Set-AdfsResponseHeaders -EnableResponseHeaders $true, (3) Set-AdfsResponseHeaders -SetHeaderName 'Referrer-Policy' -SetHeaderValue 'no-referrer-when-downgrade'. Note: mid-term fix only; apps should not depend on Referer header. Chrome will deprecate GPO ForceLegacyDefaultReferrerPolicy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Webpage Customizations/ADFS Referrer Headers

symptom: Cannot rename Active Directory Claims Provider display name on ADFS Home Realm Discovery (HRD) page - always shows 'Active Directory' regardless of configuration.
rootCause: ADFS 2012 R2 and higher does not support renaming the built-in Active Directory Claims Provider display name natively through any cmdlet or UI.
solution: Add JavaScript to onload.js of custom web theme: check for hrdArea element, iterate idpDescription elements with class 'float', find the one containing 'Active Directory' span, replace innerHTML with desired name. Deploy updated onload.js via Set-AdfsWebTheme -AdditionalFileResource and Set-AdfsWebConfig -ActiveThemeName.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Webpage Customizations/ADFS customize HRD page and rename the Active Directory Claims Provider

symptom: ADFS authentication fails for external/non-domain-joined clients when accessing through WAP
rootCause: Firewall between WAP and ADFS farm or between clients and WAP is blocking TCP port 443 (or port 49443 for client certificate auth on Server 2012 R2)
solution: Enable TCP port 443 inbound on firewalls between clients-WAP and WAP-ADFS farm. For client certificate (clientTLS) authentication on Server 2012 R2, also enable TCP port 49443 inbound between clients and WAP (not required between WAP and federation servers).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Workflow - Check Firewall

symptom: ADFS authentication intermittently fails through load balancer, especially with 3rd party MFA providers
rootCause: Load balancer lacks session persistence (sticky sessions), causing MFA state loss between requests. Also probe may be misconfigured.
solution: Enable session persistence/sticky sessions on the load balancer. Verify connectivity: Test-NetConnection -ComputerName sts.contoso.com -Port 443. For probes: install KB2975719 (Server 2012 R2), enable port 80 in firewall on WAP/ADFS servers, set probe endpoint to /adfs/probe.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/ADFS and WAP/ADFS Workflow - Check Load Balancer

symptom: Workplace Join, Device Registration, or Client Certificate Authentication fails when ADFS is behind a load balancer
rootCause: SSL Bridging on the load balancer interferes with these features which require end-to-end SSL passthrough
