sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: SSH login to Azure Linux VM with AADLoginForLinux extension shows 'Permission denied'. Auth log: pam_aad 'Failed to call back client. Make sure ChallengeResponseAuthentication in /etc/ssh/sshd_config is set to yes'. Device login URL not presented.
rootCause: ChallengeResponseAuthentication is set to 'no' in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, preventing pam_aad from sending device login callbacks. Often reverted to 'no' by Password Reset operations.
solution: Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config, set ChallengeResponseAuthentication to 'yes', restart sshd. Or remove and reinstall the VM extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: SSH login to Azure Linux VM with AADLoginForLinux repeatedly prompts for password instead of device login flow. Uppercase letters in UPN (e.g. username@Contoso.com) cause continuous password prompts.
rootCause: AADLoginForLinux requires the UPN to be entirely lowercase. Any uppercase character causes fallback to password authentication instead of device code flow.
solution: Use all-lowercase UPN: ssh username@contoso.com@<vm-ip> (not username@Contoso.com).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: SSH to Azure Linux VM fails with 'User account has expired for illegal user' despite successful Azure AD auth. PAM shows 'Login granted' but sshd reports account expired. Affects Guest users with MI, or Members with only User Assigned MI.
rootCause: PAS calls Graph using System Assigned MI to check user existence by UPN only (not email). Guest UPN lookup fails. Members with only User Assigned MI (no System Assigned) also fail this path.
solution: For Members: enable System Assigned MI. For Guests: pre-register with 'sudo aaduseradd --comment <name> --oid <objectId> <email>' to add to /etc/aadpasswd. PG deployed service-side fix for email parity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: Azure Linux VM Sign-In cloud app (AppId ce6ff14a-7fdc-4685-bbe0-f6afdfcfa8e0) not registered in Entra ID tenant. Cannot exclude/target in Conditional Access policies.
rootCause: The Azure Linux VM Sign-In cloud app SP is not auto-provisioned. Must be manually registered.
solution: Global Admin: Connect-MgGraph -Scopes Application.ReadWrite.All; New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId 'ce6ff14a-7fdc-4685-bbe0-f6afdfcfa8e0'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: ASP.NET Core web app hosted in multi-instance environment throws 'Unable to unprotect the message.State' or 'The oauth state was missing or invalid' during OAuth/OIDC sign-in flow
rootCause: Multiple app instances use different Data Protection Keys. Instance A encrypts the OAuth state parameter, but after authentication the Identity Provider redirects to Instance B which cannot decrypt with a different key.
solution: Solution 1: Configure ARR Affinity to ensure same instance handles the full flow. Solution 2: Configure a central Data Protection Store (e.g. Azure Blob Storage + Key Vault). Solution 3: Host only one instance. Note: This is an ASP.NET Core middleware issue, not Entra ID — route to ASP.NET support team if customer needs deeper help.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/AspNet AspNetCore/Unable to unprotect the message State.

symptom: Office 365 users prompted for credentials instead of seamless SSO when using Alternate ID with domain hint. Office 2016 worked fine but Office 365 with OneAuth does not.
rootCause: OneAuth does not pass the domain hint to WAM during silent requests, causing interactive prompt instead of silent SSO. Feature request tracked in Engineering backlog.
solution: Workaround: Do not use Alternate ID. OneAuth currently does not support passing domain hint to WAM for silent requests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Error 'the service cannot be started, either because it is disabled or because it has no enabled devices associated with it' when running Connect-MicrosoftTeams with AccountID on Windows Server 2019.
rootCause: Web Account Manager (WAM) service is not enabled or running on the device.
solution: Enable WAM by running services.msc, locate 'Web Account Manager' service, enable and start it, then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: OneDrive does not silently sign in on Virtual Machines. OneAuth log shows 'Account provider is null' with error tag 5akgo and StatusInternal::InteractionRequired.
rootCause: WAM account provider is missing on the VM, forcing OneAuth to require interactive UI flow to register the provider.
solution: Investigate with WAM team why the provider is missing. The interactive flow attempts to register the provider as part of the UI flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Silent token calls on WAM failing with wamcompat_id_token not present in WAM response on Windows 10 RS1. Error tag 62ubh.
rootCause: Obtaining wamcompat_id_token is only supported starting with Windows 10 RS2 and above. RS1 does not support this feature.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10 RS2 or later. This is expected behavior on RS1.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Authentication error 1200 in Teams App with error tag 657rx, error code 2148073494. TPM-related error requiring device recovery.
rootCause: TPM error (TPM_E_PCP_WRONG_PARENT). Windows authentication broker cannot complete request due to TPM issue.
solution: Restart the computer. If issue persists, perform manual recovery per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/office365/troubleshoot/authentication/connection-issue-when-sign-in-office-2016#manual-recovery
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Something went wrong [1001] with tags 7q6ch and 5fcl8 after upgrading Office 365 from version 2308 to 2402 on Windows Server 2019 with Citrix.
rootCause: REGDB_E_CLASSNOTREG and ERROR_NOT_FOUND on Windows Server 2019 + Citrix environment. Tag 5fcl8 (ERROR_PROCESS_ABORTED) may indicate antivirus blocking AAD/MSA WAM plugin.
solution: Apply Citrix TSG: https://support.citrix.com/s/article/CTX267071. For 5fcl8 errors, add exclusion in antivirus/protection software for AAD/MSA WAM plugin.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Users cannot sign in to Office desktop apps with error 1001 and tag 48v35. BrokerPlugin crash and svchost crash on Windows 10 22H2 hosted on AVD.
rootCause: Known Windows OS update bug on Windows 10 22H2 AVD: AppXSvc crash due to uninitialized m_targetUserSidString in OSIntegrationManagerHost::Initialize. Impacts both FSLogix and Broker Plugin.
solution: Engage Windows Performance team via collab. See internal Evergreen KB article for KIR.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Teams 'No network connection' error 2603. Typically indicates HTTP status 511 STATUS_NETWORK_AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED.
rootCause: Customer proxy setting is blocking or requiring network authentication. HTTP 511 indicates network authentication required by the proxy. Not an auth library issue.
solution: Review and fix customer proxy settings. Ensure proxy is not requiring additional authentication for Microsoft endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Something went wrong 1001 in Outlook after migrating to Exchange Online. Users hitting access denied when connecting to Entra endpoints behind proxy with Kerberos auth.
rootCause: Kerberos TGT (Ticket Granting Ticket) refresh failure behind proxy. After TGT expires, all requests to Entra fail with access denied. AAD/Operational log shows 'On-prem tgt error: On-prem configuration is missing'.
solution: 1) Restart machine to force new TGT acquisition. 2) Whitelist M365 endpoints per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/enterprise/urls-and-ip-address-ranges. 3) Engage AadCloudAPPlugin/WAM team if issue persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: AcquireCredentialInteractively prompts for password instead of going directly to MFA collection when WAM account is unavailable.
rootCause: When WAM account is not available (signed out or deleted), OneAuth calls WAM API with force prompt flag, requiring user to re-enter credentials instead of proceeding to MFA.
solution: Known limitation. OneAuth improvement planned to reduce/remove force prompt flags when UPN is known.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Users unable to sign-in to M365 apps after installing Windows patch KB5062554. WAM returns AADSTS9002341: User is required to permit SSO.
rootCause: AADSTS9002341 is a by-design DMA (Data Migration Authorization) block. Results in WAM UI being shown, which may fail for other reasons.
solution: Follow WAM SSO TSG DMA error section. If interactive sign-in also fails after user permits SSO, investigate further with WAM team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: 5z9gd error when signing into Word/Excel from AVD environment. Some AVD pools work while others fail with authorization failures.
rootCause: Server-side URL access restrictions in certain AVD pools. Specific Microsoft cloud URLs not whitelisted in customer network.
solution: Whitelist all required URLs per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/enterprise/urls-and-ip-address-ranges
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Teams and OneDrive frequently having sign-in issues caused by invalid/expired Primary Refresh Token. Error tag 657rx, error code 2148073494 (NTE_BAD_KEYSET).
rootCause: NTE_BAD_KEYSET error mapped to InteractionRequired by WAM. WAM has recovery logic but it does not trigger if device is in bad state.
solution: Check device state first and fix if needed. Collect recording and logs, then transfer to WAM team if recovery logic is not kicking in.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: VS Code logs into wrong account for MCP login. After WAM UI CBA flow, wrong account hint shown. Error code -895352830.
rootCause: Confirmed WAM bug: WAM shows wrong account hint after Certificate-Based Authentication (CBA) flow.
solution: Transfer to WAM team. This is a confirmed WAM bug.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: iOS users asked to login to multiple Microsoft apps several times a day. Keychain errors -25308 (save) and -25300 (find) in OneAuth-MSAL logs with error tag 4ut12.
rootCause: iOS device keychain is corrupted. OneAuth/MSAL cannot save or retrieve tokens from the keychain, forcing repeated interactive authentication.
solution: Collect sys.diagnose dump from affected device (instructions: https://it-training.apple.com/tutorials/support/sup075). Escalate to msal-objC (Apple) team for investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: iOS MAM-enabled app with MFA requirement shows multiple authentication prompts during login.
rootCause: MAM integration issue causing duplicate prompts when both MAM enrollment and MFA are required during sign-in flow.
solution: Update Microsoft Authenticator app to the latest version. Collect broker logs from Authenticator if issue persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: iOS Office app login fails with AADSTS50020: User account from identity provider does not exist in tenant after tenant migration.
rootCause: After tenant migration, old tenant information is cached in iOS keychain. Deleting app or Company Portal does not clear the cached tenant data.
solution: 1) Uninstall affected apps. 2) Open Edge, go to edge://signin-internals, click RemoveAllAccounts. 3) In Authenticator, remove all accounts then re-add. 4) Reinstall apps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: Edge for iOS 'sign out and forget' does not permanently clear used login accounts. Accounts reappear after signing out from non-broker apps.
rootCause: By design on iOS, non-broker apps cannot permanently remove accounts. Only the Authenticator app (as the broker) has permission to permanently remove accounts from the device keychain.
solution: Sign into Authenticator app with the account, then remove it from Authenticator. This is the only supported way to permanently remove an account on iOS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: Teams on iOS unable to sign in after tenant migration. Auth library still requesting resources from previous tenant even after deleting account from Company Portal.
rootCause: Tenant migration leaves stale tenant data in iOS keychain. Deleting account from Company Portal is not sufficient to clear iOS keychain storage.
solution: 1) Sign out from the app, Authenticator, and Company Portal. 2) Open Edge, sign out, go to edge://signin-internals, click RemoveAllAccounts. 3) Re-add account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: Power App mobile player v3.25065.4 does not logout when MAM Intune policy is configured. Sign-out confirmation dialog does not appear and app terminates prematurely.
rootCause: App not properly coordinating app data wipe and sign-out with MAM SDK in correct sequence. When MAM is active during sign-out, incorrect ordering prevents proper sign-out flow.
solution: 1) Investigate app MAM SDK integration focusing on sign-out + data wipe sequence. 2) Validate MAM policy behavior with Intune team. 3) Escalate to OneAuth team if others confirm their components work.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: Users are getting prompted to sign in repeatedly in New Outlook for Mac with OneAuth MSAL error tag 965ya (StatusInternal::InteractionRequired, invalid_grant, token_expired)
rootCause: SIF (Sign-In Frequency) Conditional Access policy causes interactive prompt every 18 hours. The token_expired suberror triggers InteractionRequired, but this is expected CA-driven behavior, not a OneAuth/MSAL library bug.
solution: Review the tenant Conditional Access policies for Sign-In Frequency settings. If the 18-hour prompt interval matches the configured SIF policy, this is by design. Adjust the CA policy if the frequency is undesirable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: Work Profile Error encountered after domain password change with OneAuth MSAL error tag 5pa42 indicating SSL handshake process error in Android broker
rootCause: The error originates from the Android broker side during SSL handshake (tag 5pa42). This is not related to the OneAuth/MSAL library itself.
solution: Route to Mobile broker team for diagnosis and resolution. This is an Android broker issue, not OneAuth/MSAL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: After the latest Android OS version 14 update, users cannot access Teams, OneDrive with OneAuth MSAL error tag 5ssab (Android broker returns CANCEL)
rootCause: Android broker returns CANCEL status (tag 5ssab) after OS update. This is an Android broker-side issue, not related to OneAuth/MSAL library.
solution: Route to Mobile broker team for investigation. The issue is on the Android broker side, not OneAuth/MSAL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: User sees Something went wrong window with OneAuth MSAL error tag 5objn on Android, corresponding to broker returning INVALID_REQUEST
rootCause: Tag 5objn corresponds to Android broker returning INVALID_REQUEST. This can be returned by the broker from the server for multiple reasons and is a broker-side issue.
solution: Route to Android broker team for investigation. Since INVALID_REQUEST comes from the broker, the Android broker team should investigate the root cause first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: Android Edge app Conditional Access does not prompt to register device, OneAuth MSAL error tag 5sr96 indicating interaction required error from Android broker
rootCause: Tag 5sr96 indicates an interaction required error from the Android broker. In observed cases, the error was caused by the customer Conditional Access policy, with Logsminer showing error code UserStrongAuthClientAuthNRequired.
solution: Check the customer Conditional Access policies. Use Logsminer to search for the specific error code. The interaction required error is driven by CA policy, not a OneAuth/MSAL bug.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: Getting invalid account (2201) errors when using SSO to sign into Edge on Android Shared Device Mode (SDM). Edge hits Invalid Account on startup but Teams gets an interrupt allowing login.
rootCause: Under SDM, Edge uses SignInSilently to load the current user profile. The account is in an interrupt state, and token acquisition fails with InteractionRequired, causing Edge to show Invalid account. Teams uses different startup logic that resolves the interrupt first.
solution: Upgrade to OneAuth 6.2.1 or later which introduces partial success status for SignIn* methods, where an account is returned along with an error. Edge should integrate with this new API behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: AirGap user running into authentication and connection issues testing from an Ubuntu Linux client after RDP client team moved from legacy auth library to OneAuth-MSAL
rootCause: RDP Linux client team removed the existing auth library approximately 6 months ago and moved to OneAuth-MSAL. Authentication does not work from Day 1 for partners using the RDP SDK in production in UsSec/UsNat environments. RDP team has no local test environment in these clouds.
solution: Route to RDP Linux client team to lead the investigation. While OneAuth-MSAL team may assist with fixes, the RDP Linux client team should own the issue since they integrated the SDK.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Linux/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Linux Broker sign-in silently is not working as expected: device-bound PRT is downgraded by eSTS to a device-less PRT during the call
rootCause: eSTS server bug: the device-bound PRT should not be downgraded to a device-less PRT. In Logsminer, searching the device keyword shows the device-less PRT does not have a DeviceId.
solution: This is a server-side (eSTS) bug. File/reference bug 3300735 in Identity Division Engineering. In Logsminer, verify by searching device keyword to confirm the PRT lacks DeviceId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Linux/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Teams cannot sign-in on macOS due to corrupted keychain with keychain error -25308 during interactive token acquisition
rootCause: A bug in OSQuery (github.com/osquery/osquery/issues/7780) corrupts the macOS keychain. Tools like MS Defender that use OSQuery can trigger this. The indicator is keychain error -25308 in logs and the Local Items keychain in Keychain Access app being locked or empty.
solution: 1) Open Keychain Access, check Local Items keychain - if locked or empty, proceed. 2) Quit all M365 apps and Keychain Access. 3) In Finder, Go To Folder: ~/Library/Keychains. 4) Delete all folders with long hex string names (keep .db files). 5) Restart Mac. 6) Verify Local Items keychain is unlocked, then sign in.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/MacOS/Learnings From ICMs - MacOS

symptom: User is unable to sign back in after signing out on MacOS with OneAuth MSAL error tag 6p73z Account has previously been signed out of this application despite completing a prompt
rootCause: This occurs most often with dual-headed accounts (email/UPN mapped to both an AAD and MSA account). Stale keychain entries prevent re-authentication even after the user completes a sign-in prompt.
solution: 1) Find the GUID of the user tenant ID. 2) Open Keychain Access on the Mac. 3) Search tenant ID GUID, select and delete all results. 4) Search MSA Tenant GUID 4c5b-b112-36a304b66dad, delete all results. 5) Restart Mac and sign in again. NOTE: This removes sign-in state from all MSFT apps and may require device re-enrollment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/MacOS/Learnings From ICMs - MacOS

symptom: User is asked for a password on MacOS despite the SSO Extension being available and configured with the account. OneAuth/MSAL SignInSilently returns Default account could not be found on Mac.
rootCause: As of early March 2025, this is expected behavior. OneAuth/MSAL SignInSilently API only works on Windows (or iOS/Android in SDM). On MacOS, it returns InteractionRequired. Additionally, SignInInteractively sends prompt=login flags that block the SSO Extension from bypassing the password prompt.
solution: This is a known feature gap. Work is scheduled in CY25 Q2/Q3 to make SignInSilently support Mac SSO Extension and optimize SignInInteractively parameters. Until then, the password prompt on initial sign-in is expected. Share the technical explanation with the customer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/MacOS/Learnings From ICMs - MacOS

symptom: FIDO key is not recognized in unbrokered passwordless authentication flow on Mac platform. webauthn=1 is being passed in the request but the key is not detected.
rootCause: This is a confirmed existing Apple issue (feedbackassistant.apple.com/feedback/15493539). When webauthn=1 is passed in the request and the expected dialog appears, the OneAuth/MSAL library is working correctly.
solution: Confirm the webauthn dialog appears correctly (library working as expected). The customer needs to work with Apple to fix this issue as it is an Apple platform bug.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/MacOS/Learnings From ICMs - MacOS

symptom: Customer encounters ADAL library issues or needs ADAL support for authentication in their application
rootCause: ADAL (Azure Active Directory Authentication Library) is deprecated and no longer supported by Microsoft
solution: Migrate from ADAL to MSAL following official guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/msal-migration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Support Boundaries/ADAL MSAL OWIN Support Boundaries

symptom: MSAL error occurs in first-party Microsoft application (Excel, Outlook, SharePoint) and case routing is unclear
rootCause: MSAL errors generated by first-party applications are owned by the respective workload team, not AAD Developer, as fixes require changes by that application's product group
solution: Route to the first-party application support team (SPO, EXO, etc.). AAD Developer only assists with MSAL token acquisition, not workload-specific MSAL errors. AAD Dev can be a collaboration partner.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Support Boundaries/ADAL MSAL OWIN Support Boundaries

symptom: OWIN or OpenIDConnect middleware error in ASP.NET application using Azure AD authentication, case routing needed
rootCause: OWIN/OpenIDConnect middleware development is owned by IIS Server team, not AAD Developer. AAD Developer only supports pipeline setup for token acquisition.
solution: Route OWIN/OpenIDConnect specific errors to IIS/ASP.NET server-side component support team. AAD Developer can set up the OWIN/OpenIDConnect pipeline for AAD Graph and MS Graph API token acquisition only.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Support Boundaries/ADAL MSAL OWIN Support Boundaries

symptom: Customer wants MSAL.js to not request refresh token or needs to remove offline_access from default OIDC scopes
rootCause: MSAL.js includes offline_access in OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES array by default, which always requests a refresh token
solution: Remove offline_access from OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES: import { OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES } from '@azure/msal-common'; OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES.splice(OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES.indexOf('offline_access'), 1);
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL js How to Scenarios

symptom: MSAL.NET application fails to authenticate when behind a corporate proxy; requests to login.microsoftonline.com time out or get blocked because default MSAL HttpClient has no proxy configured
rootCause: By default, MSAL.NET uses its own internal HttpClient that does not have proxy settings. Applications running behind a corporate proxy cannot reach Azure AD endpoints without explicit proxy configuration.
solution: Implement IMsalHttpClientFactory with a custom HttpClient configured with WebProxy (including credentials if needed), then pass it to PublicClientApplicationBuilder via .WithHttpClientFactory(new CustomMsalHttpClientFactory(httpClient)). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/msal/dotnet/advanced/httpclient
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/Add custom HttpClient

symptom: MS Graph .NET SDK sends requests to the default v1.0 commercial endpoint but the application needs to target the beta API, US Government cloud (graph.microsoft.us), or a specific partner tenant
rootCause: GraphServiceClient defaults to https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0. Developers must explicitly override the BaseUrl property to target different endpoints (beta, government, or partner tenant).
solution: Set graphClient.BaseUrl to the desired endpoint: https://graph.microsoft.com/beta for beta API, https://graph.microsoft.us/beta for US Government, or https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/{tenant-id} for partner scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/DotNet/Microsoft Graph DotNet Customize GraphServiceClient

symptom: MS Graph .NET SDK requests fail or time out when the application is behind a corporate proxy because GraphServiceClient uses default HttpClient without proxy settings
rootCause: The default GraphServiceClient does not configure proxy settings. Applications behind a corporate proxy need to provide a custom HttpClient with HttpClientHandler.Proxy configured.
solution: Create an HttpClient with HttpClientHandler configured with WebProxy (Proxy = new WebProxy(proxyUrl), UseProxy = true), then pass it to new GraphServiceClient(httpClient, authProvider).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/DotNet/Microsoft Graph DotNet Customize GraphServiceClient

symptom: Steps to Configure AD FS 2016 to authenticate users stored in an OpenLDAP directory can now be found at:https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server-docs/identity/ad-fs/operations/configure-ad-fs-to-authenticate-users-stored-in-ldap-directories
sourceRef: ContentIdea#56232

symptom: After machine joined in Azure AD and then restarted, the machine may get stuck during boot for about 3 hours (2.7 hours). After this duration it finally gets unstuck and the boot sequence completes. Complete dumps taken from system hung during boot show a dead lock in Services.exe and LSA initialization thread in Lsass.exe.
rootCause: This issue occurs in the following condition: The impacted computer is Azure AD joined, and the workgroup name is the same as the on-premises domain’s NetBIOS name. You have logged on to this computer with a federated user at least once. The ~3 hour hang occurs because in this condition, Windows unexpectedly evaluates a condition in which a wait time of 10,000 seconds is initiated. This timeout condition is manifested as a three-hour startup delay. (10,000 seconds = 167 minutes = 2 hours 47 minutes). LsapWaitForSamForAwhile() code.
solution: To resolve this issue, use a workgroup name that does not match the NetBIOS name of the on-premises domain
sourceRef: ContentIdea#57842

symptom: During installation AADC creates a set of four local groups: ADSyncAdmins, ADSyncOperators, ADSyncBrowsers, ADSyncPasswordReset
sourceRef: ContentIdea#58820

symptom: Full title not trunaced by our wonderful tools: AAD: Sync: Objects including group members from trusted domains fail to sync if FSP container not in sync scope Problem Statement An an on-prem Group syncronized to Azure Active Directory / Office 365 contains members from a trusted domain. The trusted users do not appears as members of the same group in O365 User accounts in the local doamin are correctedly synced to Azure AD but users from the trusted domain are not visible in O365 Specific example The saxobank. ch domain is part of one forest. The saxobank. com domain is part of another forest. There is a bi-directional trust between the two forests and the users are synced to tenant saxobank.onmicrosoft.com via one AADConnect server, from the saxobank. com domain. The users from the saxobank. ch domain which are part of a Group in the saxobank. com domain, are not synced into the cloud as Members of that group. But the users themselves are synched successfully into o365. Example: Group name: APPACC_LVE_WorkDay_Users (Group Scope � Domain local) Affected user: alventosa@saxobank. ch - not synced as a group member Working user: lba@saxobank. com - synced During our remote session we have created a new test group which is part of the same OU with the one listed above. Group name: APPACC_tst_lbatest (Group Scope � Domain local) Affected user: alventosa@saxobank. ch - not synced as a group member Working user: lba@saxobank. com - synced
rootCause: Short Answer: The ForeignSecurityPrincipals container was not part of the sync scope in AADConnect. Longer answer and problem backgrounder For the case when a Group in Forest1 has members in the current Forest1 and from a different Forest, Forest2I would expect to see : #1 in the AD Connector for Forest 1 the Group object (eg. G1 in Forest1) member references to Local Domain Users in this Forest that are members of this Group member references to ForeignSecurityPrincipal objects in this Forest. These FSPs point to the actual User objects in Forest2 the User objects in Forest1 ForeignSecurityPrincipal objects in this Forest1 (which point to User objects in Forest2) #2 in the AD Connector for Forest2 the User objects (which are pointed to by the FSPs in Forest1) When the Group F1:G1 should synchronized from AD Connector for Forest1 to the MV: - All member references will flow IFF there is a matching linked User object in the MV. So for all the member references to flow to MV, you need: - the User objects from Forest1 to flow to MV. - The User objects from Forest2 to flow to MV - The FSPs from Forest1 to link to the User object from Forest2 in the MV So the first thing I would check is what is in the MV for that particular Group which is having a problem, what do the member references look like?
solution: Add the FSP container to the sync scope in AADConnect Perform a full sync
sourceRef: ContentIdea#58917

symptom: Consider the following scenario: You have an ADFS 3.0 farm using a dedicated SQL instance to store the configuration data. You try to add an additional server to the existing farm using the Server Manager configuration wizard. On the Pre-requisite Checks page, the following error occurs: �One or more prerequisites failed. Please fix these issues and click �Rerun prerequisite check� The type initializer for 'System.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection' threw an exception. Unable to retrieve configuration from the SQL database. The type initializer for 'System.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection' threw an exception. Note: This same error occurs when using PowerShell command Add-AdfsFarmNode
rootCause: Not entirely known, but it seems to be a permission issue on the local machine for the ADFS service account. Possibly a problem in the local security policies or lack of permission to something on the local file system. Unfortunately, we were not able to run a Procmon trace the time, but we didn�t see anything wrong in the Security event log. Note: The error makes you think it�s a problem communicating with the SQL server, but in a Netmon trace, we saw no communication whatsoever with the SQL box. The error occurs when trying to create the SQL connection object on the ADFS server.
solution: Add the ADFS service account to the local Administrators group on the ADFS box.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#58976

symptom: This article describes the technical support boundaries for Azure AD Identity Governance. Support issues that are outside these guidelines should be redirected according to the appropriate support documentation. Important notes � Use the following article to determine how to transfer or redirect the case to the Supported by team: 2981981 Azure Active Directory support redirect resource sheet
sourceRef: ContentIdea#59020

symptom: Windows 7 Client machine in Forest A calls DClocator to discover a Domain Controller in Forest B Forest A and Forest B User and Client machine belongs to Forest A. Application is hosted on Forest B Users are accessing Application in Forest B from a client machine domain-joined in Forest A When users connect to office network via VPN, corresponding AD site is "PR" When user access Application hosted in Forest B, Network trace shows a Site-specific DNS query to locate a Domain controller in Forest B. The Trace shows client adds its own site name in the request but the site name referred in the request does not exist / is not defined in Forest B DNS communication Win 7 ---- > _ldap._tcp._sites.dc._msdcs.ForestBDomainName to Client primary DNS server but returned with no such name and returned the conditional forwarder DNS server name in response No Such name SRV _ldap._tcp._sites.dc._msdcs.ForestBDomainName SOA <Conditional forwarder Name in Forest B> Netlogon showing below error [CRITICAL] NetpDcGetDcNext: _ldap._tcp._sites.dc._msdcs.ForestBDomainName .: Cannot Query DNS. 9002 0x232a [CRITICAL] NetpDcGetNameIp: ForestBDomainName: No data returned from DnsQuery. [MISC] NetpDcGetName: NetpDcGetNameIp returned 1355
rootCause: Ideally, Win 7 should trigger a generic query with Forest B domain name but is not doing it Win 8 and onwards if Site specific query fails, fallback to Domain specific query, in this case observed Win8 machine connected on VPN send _ldap._tcp.dc._msdcs.ForestBDomainName and gets the response from DNS server.
solution: Workaround: Create an Empty site corresponding the "client domain site" in Forest B AD sites and services Next time Win7 send site specific query, it is being answered and DC locator discover the Domain controller in Forest B
sourceRef: ContentIdea#59302

symptom: SID showing up when we are change password field when logging into OWA via ADFS
rootCause: ADFS claim is sending SID and UPN, but Exchange Client is picking only SID
solution: > When we log into OWA (E2k13\E2k10)via ADFS and arrive at change password page, we see SID of the user instead of DomainName\username. > The ADFS token sent to Exchange also included SID, group SID, UPN > For some reason, although , UPN\SID both are defined to be sent in ADFS claim rule, only SID shows up >When the User has logged in to the Application and hits change Password ------� When the User hits change Password, on the Application page, we observe primary SID S-1-5-21-2242138424-1987062602-317689568-171614 which is Auto Filled and User is not able to change this and unable to change the Password as well > To mitigate this, drop the user SID in the ADFS claim rule.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#59354

symptom: When anyone else tried to run the ADMT console they get "Unable to check for failed actions. :DB.Manager.IManagerDB.1 : Cannot open database "ADMT" requested by the login. The login failed."
rootCause: SQL Permissions missing on the SQL Express database.
solution: Shut down SQL Express Server from services. Open cmd window (as admin) and run single-user mode as local admin with this command: c:\Program Files\Microsoft SQL Server\MSSQL10.SQLEXPRESS\MSSQL\Binn\sqlservr.exe" -m -s SQLEXPRESS Open another cmd window (as admin) and access this location. c:\Program Files\Microsoft SQL Server\MSSQL10.SQLEXPRESS\MSSQL\Binn\ Open sqlcmd: sqlcmd -S .\SQLEXPRESS Now add the sysadmin user: sp_addsrvrolemember 'domain\user', 'sysadmin' GO # Now Ctrl+C the single-user mode from the first cmd window to kill SQL Server. # Restart the SQL Express service from Services.msc in a normal way.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#59557

symptom: Title: AAD: UserCertificate attribute value enforcement rollout in March 2017 Microsoft will begin rolling out a stricter enforcement of the number of attribute values accepted by multi-valued binary properties (such as UserCertificate & UserSMIMECertificate) in March 2017 The new limit will be 15 values accepted for each multi-valued binary property (currently, this applies only to UserCertificate & UserSMIMECertificate). Exceeding the limit on a new user will result in a provisioning failure of that one user as opposed to the Exchange behavior which is to filter out the excess values. The optimal solution would be to resolve the attribute into certs, filter out all expired certs, and only flow active certs. That should really be fewer than 10. Resolving certs can be a perf impact so should only be done when there are many certs If an existing customer already has more than 15 values in place for an existing object, Microsoft will not block attempts to modify that user�s other properties. Microsoft will only prevent the creation of new objects with more than 15 values for those attributes Existing objects from increasing above 15 values. If an existing object has more than 15 values, Microsoft will not block attempts to decrease the number of values, even if the final number is above 15. (i.e. syncing a change on an existing object with 30 values to have 29 values will be allowed). A LargeObject failure today is equivalent to a bad request. It doesn�t show up as a failure, because it just means the client is trying to do something that is not allowed. This behavior change expands the definition of disallowed operation to better protect our service for all tenants. Microsoft is planning to implement another type of filter where we sum up the total byte size of the object across all multi-valued attributes to protect the service more broadly across all attributes The POR for rolling out this enforcement is shown in the table below: MT = megatenant partition Date In scope for enforcement Monday: 3/6 MSIT + 10 active tenants (detailed below) Tuesday: 3/7 Partition 38 3/8 Partition 37 + 36 (MT) + 35 (MT) + 34 (MT) 3/9 33+32+31+30(MT)+29(MT) 3/10 28+27+24+23+22(MT)+21 Monday: 3/13 19+18+17+15+14 3/14 16+13+12+11+10 3/15 9+8+7+6+5 3/16 4+3+2+1
solution: AAD Connect Syncs all objects Limit was previously not enforced in MSODS. Now it is. Errors surface in client. MSODS returns a "large object" error which shows up in old sync UI. Tenants get sync email notification. Eventually this and other errors will get exposed in the sync errors UI in the portals. Contact Phillip LeBlanc, Chun Yong Chua, Brad Craig; Bruce

symptom: Unable to configure ADFS 2016 Role on Windows Server 2016. End Goal:- First add ADFS Server 2016 to the existing farm of ADFS Server 2012 R2 and once it is done successfully, make it as primary Federation Server. ADFS Role is installed on the Domain Controller. There are 2 ADFS Servers 2016:- DCOLY02 and DCOLY01 This is the first time setup.
rootCause: All 3 ADFS Certificates were not present in Personal Computer Store of DCOLY02 . We have a scenario where for ADFS 3.0 all the three Certificates i.e ADFS Service Communication Certificate, Token- Signing and Token- Decrypting Certificate were all issued by Public CA.... In these scenario, if we are Configuring ADFS Server Role, all 3 Certificates shall be imported in Personal Computer Store of the respective ADFS Server which was not there in this scenario. Error Message observed was"- certificate with the thumbprint xxx2F5DA7321EC5CB78EB0FA6C332B58D65xxxxx was not found in the LocalMachine certificate store.
solution: We would need to export all the 3 Certificates from HBEADFS05 to DCOLY02 .Unfortunately we werent able to copy the ADFS Token-Decrypting Cert along with Private Key from HBEADFS05. In case , we are not able to export private key of the Token Signing or Token- Decryption Certificate, we need to create Self Signed certificate. Note:- If Token Signing and Token Decryption Certificates are SELF Signed, no need to copy them to Personal Computer Store of the respective ADFS Server where we are configuring ADFS role. If Token Signing and Token Decryption Certificates are issued by Public CA, we need to copy them along with Private Key to the Personal Computer Store of the respective ADFS Server where we are configuring ADFS role. Now,. if you have to generate Self Signed Certificate for Token- Decrypting Cert , we need to first create Certificate Sharing container in AD using following then create Self Signed Cert.... a) Set-AdfsCertSharingContainer -ServiceAccount <Service Account under which ADFS Service is running> Then create Self signed Cert b) Set-AdfsProperties -AutoCertificateRollover $True------> enable Auto Cert Rollver. c) Update-AdfsCertificate -CertificateType Token-Decrypting-----> Will create Token-Decrypting Cert. d) Update-AdfsCertificate -CertificateType Token-Signing---> Will create Token-signing Cert. e) Set-AdfsProperties -AutoCertificateRollover $False ----> Set auto rollover to false. Move the Primary Role to ADFS2016 1. Open Powershell as Administrator and type the following command to verify the ADFS server role: Get-AdfsSyncProperties 2. Move the Primary ADFS Server Role to ADFS2016 with the following command: Set-AdfsSyncProperties -Role PrimaryComputer Run the following command to set server ADFS2012R2 as secondary server Invoke-Command -ComputerName <FQDN Of Primary ADFS 2012 R2 Computer name> { Set-AdfsSyncProperties -Role SecondaryComputer -PrimaryComputerName <FQDN of ADFS 2016 Server which you want to configure as Primary> c. Invoke-Command -ComputerName hbeadfs05.corp.wahbexchange.org { Set-AdfsSyncProperties -Role SecondaryComputer -PrimaryComputerName dcoly02.corp.wahbexchange.org f) From GUi, make the self Signed Certs as primary.

symptom: The command used in : Method 1) step2 a) must be changed to � connect-AzureAD� instead of � connect-MSOLService� all command running graph V2 and the first step used to run AAD PS V1 Adding ========== the V1 as method 2 it was before and replace with V2 connect-MSOLService Note When you're prompted, enter non-federated cloud service global administrator credentials. 2.Set-MsolUserPrincipalName -UserPrincipalName [ExistingUPN] -NewUserPrincipalName [DefaultDomainUPN] Note In this command, [ExistingUPN] represents the current UPN of the user ID, and [DefaultDomainUPN] represents the UPN of the user ID that has the domain suffix changed to the default domain. For example, a Contoso administrator might use the following command: Set-MsolUserPrincipalName -UserPrincipalName user1@constoso.com -NewUserPrincipalName user1@contoso.onmicrosoft.com 3.Set-MsolUserPrincipalName -UserPrincipalName [DefaultDomainUPN] -NewUserPrincipalName [NewUPN] Note In this command, [DefaultDomainUPN] represents the UPN of the user ID after you run the command in step 2B, and [NewUPN] is the target UPN to which you are trying to migrate the user ID. Note If you get an error when you perform step 2C, close and then reopen the Azure Active Directory Module for Windows PowerShell. Then, run the command again Suggestion ========= adding a comments for each Method and the needed PowerShell version

symptom: This is a partner article to 3216953 AAD: Auth: Account Lockout mitigation for user accounts exposed on the extranet Office 365 tenants will soon be able to self-manage domain-wide IP Block Lists via PowerShell on: Standard IPv4 and IPv6 addresses IP ranges Customers with on-prem ADFS Servers that federate with Azure Active Directory or Office 365 may use this self-service block list to prevent basic auth request from tenant-specified IP addresses directed to EXO from being proxied to the tenants federation endpoint. The block list applies to successful and unsuccessful down-level authentication requests regardless of whether AAD or Office 365 services are consumed by the tenant or the account referenced in the logon requests. IP Block ranges may be submitted using the following formats: CIDR format 2001:0DB8::CD3/60 High-Low format 192.168.0.1-192.168.0.254 sub masking format 192.168.8.2(255.255.255.0) This article describes how a tenant could implement the block list and confirm the results of a self-service block list submission Note: Federated tenants ADFS Servers may receive modern authentication requests directly from the caller or proxied via EXO Windows Server2012 R2 and alter ADFS Servers log event 412 for successful authentication requests and Event 411 for unsuccessful authentication requests 411 + 412 events that contain a single IP address identify the IP address of the originating callers IP that negotiated using modern auth 411 + 412 events that contain two IP addresses identify the IP address of the originating caller followed by the EXO IP addresse that proxied a basic auth request to the tenants federation endpoint (ADFS server or 3rd party equivalent) This article is solely focused o the blocking of IPs proxied by EXO using basic auth Tenants wishing to bloc modern auth request should do so at their on-prem firewall.

symptom: SourceAnchor and ImmutableID hard match changes in March + May 2017. Customers use Hard-Match (SourceAnchor) to link objects between AD and AAD. Azure AD Sync Service uses SourceAnchor as pivot and won't allow global admin to update it while object is synchronized. Tenants open cases to update SourceAnchor due to design change or recovery from duplicate objects.
rootCause: As of March 2017, Azure AD team updated backend service code to prevent using scoping filter method to modify SourceAnchor attribute. After Premier customer escalation, PG extended deadline to May 15th 2017.
solution: 1) Don't use ObjectGUID as SourceAnchor for multi-forest - pick SourceAnchor not dependent on AD forest. 2) To change SourceAnchor for single/few objects: a) Deactivate DirSync on tenant (impact up to 72 hours), OR b) Run Set-MSOLUser -ImmutableID to update value.

symptom: Application integrated with Azure AD fails to get Access Token for SharePoint. Error AADSTS65005: The client application has requested access to resource. This request has failed because the client has not specified this resource in its requiredResourceAccess list. Also: AADSTS65005: Invalid resource - resource not listed in requested permissions in client application registration.
rootCause: Misconfiguration of Application ID URI in AppgraphInterchange application conflicting with SharePoint resource URL, affecting access of another application (Test ADAL Auth) via code.
solution: Changed Application ID URI from conflicting https://sohodragon.sharepoint.com to https://sohodragon.sharepoint.com/test in AppgraphInterchange app. Changed target URL in Test ADAL Auth from sohodragon-my.sharepoint.com to sohodragon.sharepoint.com. Both applications working after fix.

symptom: After in-place upgrading from DirSync to AAD Connect build 1.1.443.0, objects no longer synced from AD to AAD/O365. AAD Connector has no run profiles when there should be ~5. Event ID 905: Run profile Full Import not found. Scheduler stuck performing full import every 30 minutes.
rootCause: Build 1.1.443.0 (March 6 2017) contained a regression in the DirSync upgrade path.
solution: 1) Use CreateAADRunProfiles.ps1 script to repair AAD Connector run profiles (immediate recovery). 2) If script does not solve, perform parallel mode using preserved DirSync settings (AzureADConnect.exe /migrate). Settings found in %programData%/AADConnect. 3) Problem resolved in AAD Connect versions after 1.1.443.0.

symptom:
If an
organization has deployed AD RMS and wants to migrate to Azure Information
Protection, the normal migration process involves exporting the root keys from the
AD RMS cluster and importing them into Azure RMS, a service that is part of the
Azure Information Protection platform. 

 

In some
cases, an AD RMS cluster may have been configured in a way that prevents the
key from being exported and imported to the cloud. This may happen if the keys
have been marked as non-exportable, were protected with an HSM that is not
directly supported by Azure Information Protection or if the key is in a
Hardware Security Module for which the operator cards or other artifacts required
for a key export are not available.
solution:
A Fix
for the MSIPC RMS client used by Office has been released that allows these
clients to continue using AD RMS to consume previously protected content
without granting the ability to protect new content with these keys, while
allowing the clients to protect and consume content with Azure RMS. 

This
update is available from the Microsoft Update Web site:

http://update.microsoft.com

This
fix is available for Office 2013 and Office 2016, as well as other MSIPC
applications developed with the MSIPC SDK 2.1.

The following
versions of Office include this capability:

Office 2013          >= 15.0.4849.1000

Office 2016 MSI  >= 16.0.4496.1000 

Office 2016 C2R  >= 16.0.7407.1000

During a typical migration from AD RMS to Azure Information Protection, the root key of the AD RMS cluster used to protect content (called the Server Licensor Certificate, or SLC) is exported from the cluster and imported into the Azure RMS service part of the Azure Information Protection platform. Windows clients are then configured to redirect requests for licenses to open content protected with the AD RMS cluster to the Azure RMS service, which then issues the licenses as per the policy in the document and other artifacts that allow the client to use AD RMS for protecting content. Among these artifacts is the Client Licensor Certificate, or CLC for short, obtained by the client by calling APIs available under Publish.asmx in the AD RMS cluster. Once issued this certificate allows a client to protect content with keys associated to the AD RMS cluster�s SLC key.By denying access to these APIs, a client that has this fix installed that is configured to use Azure RMS can consume content from AD RMS without receiving a CLC by AD RMS. This allows the client to continue using Azure RMS to protect all new content while retaining access to content previously protected with AD RMS, even if the AD RMS key has not been imported into Azure RMS. This allows for a gradual phase-out of the AD RMS infrastructure as new content gets protected using Azure RMS, until the content protected with AD RMS has been re-protected with the new keys in Azure RMS, or it is no longer relevant, at which point the AD RMS cluster and its SLC can be decommissioned. After installing the fix on Windows clients running Office 2013 or Office 2016, and/or other applications developed using the MSIPC SDK, to set AD RMS in read-only mode you need to deny access to the Publish.asmx page. To do this, use the following steps:1)     Log on to each AD RMS server that is used by users to protect content, click Start, point to Administrative Tools, and then click Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager.2)     If the User Account Control dialog box appears, confirm that the action it displays is what you want, and then click Continue. 3)     Expand the domain node, expand Sites, expand Default Web Site, and then expand _wmcs.4)     Note that your installation of AD RMS may have been done in a web site other than the Default Web Site, in which case you will need to adjust the path above accordingly.5)     Right-click the licensing folder, and then click Switch to Content View.6)     Right-click Publish.asmx, and then click Switch to Features View.7)     Under IIS, double-click Authentication, check that Anonymous Authentication is disabled.8)     Right-click the licensing directory again, and then click Switch to Content View.9)     Right-click Publish.asmx, and then click Edit Permissions...10)  In the Security pane, click on Edit and click Add�11)  Enter the name of the group you want to prevent from protecting content using AD RMS. To deny all users the ability to protect content with AD RMS, enter Everyone.12)  Click OK13)  Close IIS ManagerOnce IIS is configured this way on all the AD RMS nodes, you can confirm a client can use AD RMS in Read-Only mode by starting with a client that is not configured to use either AD RMS or Azure RMS and opening content that was protected using AD RMS. After that, try to protect new content. You will not be able to perform this operation.A client that has been configured to use Azure RMS will also be able to consume content from AD RMS without affecting its prior configuration with Azure RMS. After consuming content from AD RMS such a client will continue to protect content using Azure RMS. If done as part of the de-provisioning process for AD RMS, you should also use the AD RMS console to unpublish the AD RMS Service Connection Point.

symptom:
Users
of domain tivit.com unable to access O365 Applicatin Externally and Internally.Error Observed:- Where it says Incorrect Username and PasswordError is seen on adfs/ls page of ADFS Server.
rootCause:
Since there is a 2 WAY External trust established between 2 Forests tivit.corp and syngbl.int.

On Domain Controller for syngbl.int Name Suffix routing for
Domain tivit.com is disabled

Since the Name Suffix routing for domain tivit.com is disabled on Domain Controller of syngbl.int, when the query reaches syngbl.int, it is not able to route it to Domain Controller for tivit.corp.


 There are 2 Forests:-
     tivit.corp and syngbl.int�
 Under the
     tivit.corp--------> tivit.com.br is added as UPN Suffix.. | Single
     Forest Single Domain 
 Under
     syngbl.int----------> tivit.com is added as UPN suffix .. | Single
     Forest Single Domain.
solution:
We enabled the name suffix for domain tivit.com on the Domain
Controller for syngbl.int post which the users with UPN tivit.com started to access Office 365 Application.

rootCause:
This
issue is being triggered by the PTA specific call to Msol-GetUser on the main
logon path of AAD Connect where multiple
separate commands issued on the same pipeline are being accidentally chained
together when PowerShell transcription is enabledEach
PowerShellProvider has its own local copy of a powerShell pipeline
object.  However it appears PowerShell itself has a common object for
transcriptionThe use of nested clauses also causes problems when transcription is enabled.
solution:
Install corrective updatesInstall Windows Management Framework 5.0 from https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/confirmation.aspx?id=50395 to enable the transcription functionality. Install the May 2017 or later release of AAD Connect which allows the basic express/custom flow for non-ADFS scenarios to work when transcription is enabledRelease notes for the May 2017 release of AAD Connect describe this fix as follows:

Previously, you must disable PowerShell transcription for Azure
AD Connect wizard to run correctly. This issue is partially resolved. You can
enable PowerShell transcription if you are using Azure AD Connect wizard to
manage sync configuration. You must disable PowerShell transcription if you are
using Azure AD Connect wizard to manage ADFS configuration.Install the June 2017 release of AAD Connect which includes the ADFS partition of the fix Workaround and action plan for versions of AAD Connect that precede 82346: ICM: Enabling the PS transcription policy makes the AAD Connect wizard grumpy (input object cannot be found).  PowerShell transcription is not supported with these versions of AAD Connect.The AAD Connect Wizard will not run correctly with PowerShell pipeline transactions enabled, since they fundamentally change how PowerShell works (as far as the AAD Connect Wizard is concerned ). Misc.reg file to enable PowerShell transcription.  This will enable PS transcription for the currently logged on user.  The transcription folder is called PSTranscript and will be located in My Documents.Copy text between "start here" and "end here" Save as PowerShellTranscription.regWindows Registry Editor Version 5.00[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription]"EnableTranscripting"=dword:00000001"OutputDirectory"="PsTranscript""EnableInvocationHeader"=dword:00000001
sourceRef: ContentIdea#61234

symptom:
There may be circumstances where the replica volume for a
protected data source is under allocated or a very large increase of protected
data results in synchronization or recovery point job failures due to
inadequate space on the replica.

Some common examples of where this might occur are explained
below.

Bare Metal
Restore (BMR) protection.    In
DPM, BMR protection covers protection for operating system files (System State)
and critical volumes (excluding user data). DPM does not calculate the
size of BMR data source, but assumes 20 GB for all servers. At the time of
protection this cannot be changed in the disk allocation page when using Modern
Backup Storage (MBS).
sourceRef: ContentIdea#61303

symptom:
After installing KB4015547 on Windows Server 2012 R2 DC, you may notice Kerberos Key Distribution Center (KDC) Service fails to start.   net start kdc command returns error 87: the parameter is incorrect 
 
  
  Screenshot 
  
  
   Textual copy of error 
  
 
 
  
   
  
  
  net start kdc The Kerberos key distribution center service is starting The Kerberos key distribution center service could not be startedA system error has occurred.System error 87 has occurred. The parameter is incorrect.  
  
  You may also notice the failure from system event log, an error like below will be logged.  Log Name:      System Source:        Service Control Manager Event ID:      7023 Task Category: None Level:         Error Keywords:      Classic Description: The Kerberos Key Distribution Center service terminated with the following error: The parameter is incorrect.
rootCause: This is a packaging issue, Because KB4015547 (April 11th 2017 security only update) only updates kdcsvc.dll, when a machine which has an old version of lsasrv.dll (older than 6.3.9600.18581, from March 2017 update), there will be a mismatch between the new kdcsvc.dll and old lsasrv.dll, a enumerate data structure used in both dlls has different element ranges, This causes KDC service failed to start.
solution: Workarounds:If you can consider installing monthly update, not security only update, you can install KB4015550 (April 2017 monthly update)  instead of KB4015547 (April 2017 security only update) . KB4015550 is April 2017 monthly update, it updates both lsasrv.dll to 6.3.9600.18581 and kdcsvc.dll to 6.3.9600.18652, which can fix the mismatch. OR If you want to only install security only update, you need to install both KB4015547 (April 2017 security only update) and KB4012213 (March 2017 Security only update). KB4012213 updates lsasrv.dll to v6.3.9600.18581
sourceRef: ContentIdea#61584

symptom:
Symptom from 117031715473638We have issues with Outlook clients, across all users, maintaining auth to Office 365. It's behaving as though the Refresh Tokens are getting revoked intermittently; sometimes it's weeks, sometimes it's days, and there have been reports of multiple times per day, and affects at least 6 Office 365 tenants federated through our Okta tenant. The issue didn't start out having a high impact to our business. But after an entire month of back-and-forth with Office 365 support on the issue, having received no help with the issue the impact to our business has increased significantly and is becoming extremely close to becoming damaging as we are about to add $2m/year in business for us to our Okta tenant and currently the user experience is already making people unhappy with the token revocations.The Refresh Token Max Inactive Time policy was set to  hour String: MaxInactiveTime Affects: Refresh tokens Summary: This policy controls how old a refresh token can be before a client can no longer use it to retrieve a new access/refresh token pair when attempting to access this resource. Because a new refresh token usually is returned when a refresh token is used, this policy prevents access if the client tries to access any resource by using the current refresh token during the specified period of time. At exactly one hour the refresh token is invalid and the user is force to reauthenticate.  This occurs even though the user is active in Outlook 2016 or other mail clientsFiddler traces show the following signature{"error":"invalid_grant","error_description":"AADSTS70002: Error validating credentials. AADSTS70008: The refresh token has expired due to inactivity. The token was issued on 2017-03-11T04:27:59.5264175Z and was inactive for 14.00:00:00.
Trace ID: 9d871ae9-7040-4dd7-ada5-5bd3ffb91100
Correlation ID: 6471cd7c-d45b-456b-8c91-34f6fd09afbd
Timestamp: 2017-03-25 04:50:19Z","error_codes":[70002,70008],"timestamp":"2017-03-25 04:50:19Z","trace_id":"9d871ae9-7040-4dd7-ada5-5bd3ffb91100","correlation_id":"6471cd7c-d45b-456b-8c91-34f6fd09afbd"} In a 2nd test tenant also using Octa as the IDP Access token lifetime set to 1 hourMaxInactiveTime set to 1 hour instead of 2 hours The client connected to that test tenant behaved exactly as one would normally expect it to. It did not arbitrarily disconnect every hour. The client remained persistently authed for several hours before reverting the settings back to default and ending the test. This tenant is apparently not affected by the same issue as our production tenant, but it is a similar environment; users are synced from our AD, using Okta as both the sync agent and our Idp, auth delegated to AD, with a federated domain. A near carbon-copy, minus the users and other tenant-specific data."Backgrounder on refresh token lifetime User authenticates to Azure-STS using ADALIf AzureSTS receives password change notifications, which can be sync�d form on premise. ADAL gets a Refresh token which is good for 2 weeks, it will be renewed if used before the two weeks are up until the 90 days are hit. Token Expiry Time = Min (Token Last Use Time + 14 days, Auth code redemption time + 90 days) If ADAL uses the refresh token within 2 weeks, SSP will get a new refresh token for 90 days every time it goes for a refreshed access token If ADAL does not use the refresh token within 2 weeks of token reissuance, User will be prompted to login to Azure-STS again If password for the User changes any time, IWs will be prompted to login only when the access token has burnt out (after its lifetime of an hour) If AuzreSTS does not receive password change notifications ADAL gets a Refresh token for 12 hours and access token for 1 hour, refresh token will be renewed every time the access token is refreshed within 12 hours. ser are prompted to login after every 12 hours of inactivity at the ADAL.
rootCause:
This behavior is by-design due to the LastPasswordChangeTimestamp not being synchronized for the user profiles. The issue stems from the fact that the code path diverged.  Tenants created prior to August 2016 follow this process for user profiles that do not synchronize the LastPasswordChangeTimestamp with regards to the Refresh Token. Consider the following workflow: User Authenticates User is issued a Refresh Token The tenant has the Refresh Token Max Inactive Time set to 2 hours The end user begins to use the Outlook 2016 thick client At two hours, the user is forced to authenticate and we notice that the Refresh Token is not "sliding" A decision was made to not issue new Refresh Tokens to users whose current password cannot be verified.  This is to prevent the reuse of "old" Refresh Token for users whose passwords might have expiredTenants that were created after August 2016 follow the following process for user profiles that do not synchronize the LastPasswordChangeTimestamp with regards to the Refresh Token. User Authenticates User is issued a Refresh Token The tenant has the Refresh Token Max Inactive Time set to 2 hours The end user begins to use the Outlook 2016 thick client We continue to issue new Refresh tokens for these users who are constantly active in the client.  The absolute lifetime for the Refresh Token is 12 hours, regardless of the value that you have configured for Refresh Token Max Inactive TimeLong versionUser authenticates to Azure-STS using ADAL If AzureSTS receives password change notifications, which
can be sync�d form on premise. ADAL gets a Refresh token which is good for 2
weeks, it will be renewed if used before the two weeks are up until the 90 days
are hit. Token Expiry Time = Min (Token Last Use Time + 14 days, Auth
code redemption time + 90 days) 

 If ADAL uses the refresh token within 2 weeks, SSP will
get a new refresh token for 90 days every time it goes for a refreshed access
token 

 If ADAL does not use the refresh token within 2 weeks of
token reissuance, User will be prompted to login to Azure-STS again 

 If password for the User changes any time, IWs will be
prompted to login only when the access token has burnt out (after its lifetime
of an hour) 

 If AuzreSTS does not receive password change
notifications 

 ADAL gets a Refresh token for 12 hours and access token
for 1 hour, refresh token will be renewed every time the access token is
refreshed within 12 hours. 

 User are prompted to login after every 12 hours of
inactivity at the ADAL.
solution: Change the Okta profile synchronization configuration to include the LastPasswordChangeTimestamp
sourceRef: ContentIdea#61898

symptom: After machine joined in Azure AD and then restart, machine hangs at boot up. After waiting for about 3 hours it finally boots up.
rootCause: The machine was in a workgroup before joining to Azure Domain, the workgroup name is either the same name with the Azure AD domain name, or there is a NetBIOS Alias using the same name on the AD domain.
solution: Move the machine back to workgroup, and then change the machine workgroup name to the default name "WORKGROUP".  then rejoin to Azure AD.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#61991

symptom: Summary This article is for support engineers and describes steps you can take to assist in troubleshooting issues with services that leverage the Azure AD Conditional Access service. for introductions to the Conditional Access product visit the LearningCentral training for Azure AD Conditional AccessIn summary, the following components are involved with MFA/Location Based Conditional Access:Policy in MSODS (able to be queried via the ViewPoint tool or DS Explorer)Azure MFA (either Cloud-Only or MFA provided by ADFS/MFA Server on-premises)Device Based Conditional AccessDevice Based Access Rules allows customers to target compliance of devices. Microsoft Intune to check compliance of devicesAzure AD Domain Join ++ for devices that must be "Domain Joined"This article is also used for documenting Known Issues/changes to Azure AD Conditional Access offering.

symptom: When clicking on Open with Explorer in a SharePoint document library, the error 'Access Denied' is received when authentication is handled by the Azure Application Proxy
rootCause: When Azure Application Proxy is used for authentication, the cookie created for authentication is a session cookie.
solution: Since Open in Explorer view (which relies on the WebClient service) requires persistent cookies, it will not function unless Azure App Proxy is set up for pass-through authentication.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#62950

symptom: https://social.technet.microsoft.com/wiki/contents/articles/19901.dirsync-list-of-attributes-that-are-synced-by-the-azure-active-directory-sync-tool.aspx describes the list of attributes synchronized by an AAD identify sync clients but is silent as to whether Account lockouts are synchronized from on-prem AD to O365 \ Azure Active DirectoryThe disabling of a user account in on-prem AD is synchronized to O365 \ AAD Existing documentation for attributes synchronized by AAD Connectindicate that the identity sync clients update the AccountEnabled attribute in Azure AD This content is less clear that the account disabled state is derived from UAC AccountDisabled flag.
