rootCause: Bug in Entra Connect versions 1.1.557.0 through 1.1.614.0 where the Change user sign-in task still enables Password Sync when enabling PTA, though this prerequisite was removed
solution: Upgrade to Entra Connect version 1.1.644.0 or later; to disable, run Entra Connect → Configure → Customize synchronization options → clear Password synchronization checkbox

symptom: Entra Connect upgrade fails with error code 1603 Unable to install the synchronization service at DetectServiceAccount action
rootCause: Service account configured using UPN format instead of DomainSamAccountName
solution: Open Services > Microsoft Azure AD Sync > Properties > Log On tab > set account using DomainSamAccountName format instead of UPN

symptom: Table-valued parameter (TVP) errors, password hash sync failures, or staging-error discovery errors after installing Entra Connect on Windows Server 2019; Event ID 6301 with 0x80004005 in rcvtvp.h
rootCause: The 'Beta: Use Unicode UTF-8 for worldwide language support' setting is enabled in Windows Region settings; the LocalDB database version in Server 2019 does not support this format
solution: Open Control Panel → Region → Administrative → Change System locale → clear 'Use Unicode UTF-8 for worldwide language support' checkbox → restart server

symptom: Running Azure AD Sync tool configuration wizard fails with error: Failed to get address for method: CreateIdentityHandle2 from library msoidcli.dll. GetLastError code: 127
rootCause: Earlier version of the Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant is installed on the directory sync computer
solution: Uninstall all versions of Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant, then install the latest version from the official Microsoft download link

symptom: Cannot install Azure Active Directory module for Windows PowerShell; error: You must have Windows PowerShell 2.0 or greater installed on this computer
rootCause: PowerShell 2.0 is not enabled or user is not logged on as local admin
solution: Log on as local admin (not just domain admin), ensure Windows PowerShell 2.0 feature is enabled in Windows Features, then install the Azure AD module

symptom: After changing domain-based filtering in AD management agent/connector, sync fails with 'missing-partition-for-run-step' error and Event ID 6020 is logged
rootCause: Run profile is not updated after domain-based filtering change; partition configuration references a removed or missing partition
solution: For removed domain: delete the step with alphanumeric Partition value in each run profile, then run Full Sync. For added domain: add a new step for the new partition in each run profile, then run Full Sync

symptom: High CPU usage (up to 100%) on Microsoft Entra Connect Health for Sync server; Microsoft.Online.Reporting.MonitoringAgent.Startup process consuming CPU
rootCause: June 2018 update for .NET Framework 4.7.2 causes incompatibility with the Connect Health for Sync monitoring agent
solution: For AD DS/AD FS: install Connect Health agent version 3.1.7.0+. For Entra Connect: install the latest version of Microsoft Entra Connect

symptom: After entering enterprise admin credentials in Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard, error: LogonUser() Failed with error code: 1789
rootCause: Computer running Directory Sync tool cannot authenticate with Active Directory
solution: Restart the computer, or unjoin from domain (join workgroup) then rejoin the domain and restart

symptom: Installing Azure Active Directory Sync Tool fails with error: The computer must be joined to a domain
rootCause: Computer is not joined to an AD DS domain, or the computer does not register a host (A) resource record with DNS
solution: Join the computer to an AD DS domain. If already joined, fix DNS settings: ensure correct DNS server configuration, run ipconfig /flushdns and ipconfig /registerdns, restart if needed

symptom: Identity sync client (Entra Connect / Azure AD Sync) cannot authenticate to Entra ID; error Unable to communicate with the Windows Azure Active Directory service with Error 906 or Event ID 0; wizard shows Failed even after 5 retries Action: PingProvisioningServiceEndPoint
rootCause: .NET Framework does not recognize the proxy settings; the sync appliance tries to make a direct connection to the Internet instead of using the unauthenticated proxy server
solution: Edit C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config\machine.config and add <system.net><defaultProxy><proxy usesystemdefault="true" proxyaddress="http://<PROXYIP>:80" bypassonlocal="true" /></defaultProxy></system.net> with the actual proxy IP

symptom: ADSync service fails to start on Entra Connect 2.x with SQL LocalDB; Event ID 528 from SQLLocalDB 15.0; Event IDs 2005/6226 from ADSync with 0x8023044a; error.log shows Error 9003 log scan number passed to log scan in database model is not valid
rootCause: Bug in SQL Server 2019 LocalDB backup logic creates inconsistent state in Model database; sets dbi_status to FULL recovery mode while Master database governs SIMPLE mode, causing log truncation mismatch after restart
solution: Mitigation: navigate to ADSync2019 instance folder, rename model.mdf/modellog.ldf to old_*, copy fresh template files from C:\Program Files\Microsoft SQL Server\150\LocalDB\Binn\Templates, restart ADSync service. Permanent fix: upgrade to Entra Connect version 2.1.1.0 or later

symptom: Directory sync error: This company has exceeded the number of objects that can be synchronized (Error 016)
rootCause: Objects exceed Entra ID directory quota (50k without verified domain, 300k with). Deleted objects in tombstone (30 days) also count at 25%
solution: Add verified domain to increase quota to 300k; or contact Microsoft Support for quota increase beyond 300k

symptom: User primary group membership not synced by Entra Connect
rootCause: Entra Connect does not support PrimaryGroupID attribute
solution: Known limitation. Avoid primary group for groups needing sync, use regular membership

symptom: Password hash synchronization stops working after updating global admin credentials in FIM; Event IDs 115, 0, 655, 6900 logged with Access denied or user name or password is incorrect
rootCause: Updated global administrator credentials in FIM cause password sync authentication failures
solution: Run the Azure Active Directory Sync tool Configuration Wizard; alternatively restart the Forefront Identity Manager Synchronization Service

symptom: Password is not synced from Entra ID to on-premises directory after password change or reset; user sees Your request could not be processed error during SSPR
rootCause: Multiple causes: password writeback prerequisites not met, permissions not configured, password reset agent not running, or network connectivity issue between Entra ID and local Entra Connect
solution: Verify password writeback prerequisites; check permissions; ensure password reset agent is running; verify network connectivity. Note: M365 admin center and PowerShell V1/V2 password resets do NOT support writeback

symptom: Password hash synchronization stops working; Event ID 611 logged: Password synchronization failed for domain
rootCause: Multiple known issues: RPC Error 8439 (Win2003 DC), RPC Error 8593 (domain rename), ArgumentOutOfRangeException, duplicate key, or RPC Error 8453 (AD DS Connector Account missing Replicating Directory Changes permissions)
solution: Update to latest Entra Connect version; for RPC 8453 grant Replicating Directory Changes and Replicating Directory Changes All extended permissions to AD DS Connector Account

symptom: Password not syncing from Entra ID to on-prem after change/reset; user sees request could not be processed
rootCause: Writeback prerequisites not met, permissions wrong, reset agent not running, or network issue
solution: Verify writeback prerequisites/permissions; ensure reset agent running; check connectivity; note M365 admin center resets do NOT support writeback

symptom: PHS stops after several days; Event ID 611 Password synchronization failed for domain
rootCause: RPC 8439 (Server 2003 DCs), RPC 8593 (domain rename), invalid FileTime, duplicate key, or RPC 8453 (missing replication permissions)
solution: Update to latest Entra Connect; for RPC 8453 grant Replicating Directory Changes + All permissions to AD DS Connector Account

symptom: Permission-issue export error 8344 Insufficient access rights on AD connector in Sync Service Manager Export
rootCause: MSOL_ connector account lacks permissions to write back object properties; AdminCount or SDProp issues can block propagation
solution: Use Connect wizard Troubleshooting > Configure AD DS Connector Account Permissions; or ADSyncConfig PowerShell; or dsacls

symptom: Users must use different passwords for computer logon and cloud service (Office 365/Azure/Intune) after password sync is enabled with Azure AD Sync tool
rootCause: Cloud service password was changed independently; password synchronization only syncs from on-premises AD to cloud, not the reverse
solution: Have user change their on-premises computer password (or admin resets it with 'User must change password at next logon' unchecked); after directory sync, passwords will match

symptom: Delta Import from on-premises AD connector fails in Synchronization Service Manager; Connection Log shows dropped-connection status with 'Size Limit Exceeded' error code 0x4; Event ID 6050 logged
rootCause: LDAP search hit the default 1000-record MaxPageSize limit, typically after restoring a large number of objects from AD Recycle Bin simultaneously
solution: Option 1: Run a Full Import on the AD DS connector instead of Delta Import. Option 2: Temporarily increase MaxPageSize via ntdsutil (e.g. to 5000), run sync, then restore to 1000

symptom: High CPU usage and slow performance from Microsoft.Online.Reporting.MonitoringAgent.Startup process on Entra Connect server
rootCause: Connect Health for Sync monitoring agent incompatible with .NET Framework 4.7.2 or July 2018 .NET updates
solution: Update Connect Health Agent to v3.1.7.0+ (for AD DS/AD FS); or install latest Entra Connect version (for sync); auto-upgrade will also resolve

symptom: New-MsolDomain cmdlet fails: 'Unable to add this domain. It is a subdomain and its authentication type is different from the authentication type of the root domain' when adding subdomain to federated domain
rootCause: New-MSOLDomain adds subdomains as standard authentication, conflicting with parent domain's federated authentication type
solution: Use New-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName:<subdomain> instead of New-MSOLDomain to add subdomains under federated root domains

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard fails with 'Error Unknown error (0x80005000)'
rootCause: The sync tool cannot configure the domain because one or more domain controllers are unreachable or not running
solution: Run PowerShell: $Forest = [System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Forest]::GetCurrentForest().domains; $Forest — examine output for domains with missing information (empty DomainControllers, Forest, DomainMode fields), then investigate and fix the unreachable domain controller

symptom: User object not synced to Entra ID — only AD connector shows in metaverse, no Entra connector listed, no error returned. msExchRecipientTypeDetails value is 2
rootCause: When msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 (Linked Mailbox), the sync rule sets sourceAnchor to NULL, causing AADSync to wait for the master account from account forest to join before provisioning. Without account-resource forest topology this blocks sync indefinitely
solution: 1) Ensure the master user account in Account forest is synchronized first (for account-resource forest topology), OR 2) Change msExchRecipientTypeDetails from 2 to 1 (or any non-filtered value) if not actually a linked mailbox scenario

symptom: New-MsolDomain command fails: Unable to add this domain. It is a subdomain and its authentication type is different from the root domain
rootCause: New-MSOLDomain tries to add subdomain as managed auth, but parent root domain is federated - auth type mismatch
solution: Use New-MSOLFederatedDomain instead: Connect to Entra ID via PowerShell, run New-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName:<subdomain>

symptom: Connect-MsolService fails: Unable to authenticate your credentials. Make sure user name is in format <username>@<domain>
rootCause: Incorrect username format (john or contosojohn instead of john@contoso.com), or account enabled for Entra MFA which legacy Azure AD PowerShell does not support
solution: Use correct UPN format user@domain.com. If MFA: disable MFA for admin, use non-MFA admin, or migrate to Graph PowerShell SDK with modern auth

symptom: Entra Connect wizard error: Unable to configure password writeback. Ensure you have the required license
rootCause: Admin account lacks Entra ID P1/P2 license, server time out of sync, or TLS 1.2 not enabled
solution: Enable TLS 1.2; use cloud-only admin with proper license (not federated); sync server time. Check logs at %appdata%LocalAADConnect

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard fails: Unknown error (0x80005000)
rootCause: Sync Tool cannot reach one or more domains in the forest - child domain controller offline or unreachable
solution: Run [System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Forest]::GetCurrentForest().domains to list domains; identify ones with empty fields; bring unreachable DC online

symptom: User object missing from Entra connector in Azure AD Sync - only AD connector on Connectors tab, no error; msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2
rootCause: msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 (Linked Mailbox) causes sourceAnchor rule to set NULL; AADSync waits for master account from account forest
solution: If not account-resource topology: change msExchRecipientTypeDetails from 2 to 1 in on-prem AD. If account-resource: ensure master account syncs first

symptom: Users in federated domain see yellow exclamation mark in Office 2013 apps when not synced to Entra ID
rootCause: Office 2013 IDCRL detects federated domain, tries Entra ID auth, but user not synced so does not exist there
solution: Set registry HKCUSoftwareMicrosoftOffice.0CommonSignInSignInOptions=3 (local AD only, non-synced users). Or upgrade to Office 2016+

symptom: New-MsolDomain fails adding subdomain to federated domain: Unable to add this domain. Authentication type differs from root domain
rootCause: New-MSOLDomain tries to add subdomain as standard auth but parent is federated
solution: Use New-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName:<subdomain> instead

symptom: Connect-MsolService fails with Unable to authenticate your credentials error (0x80048862)
rootCause: Incorrect username format (must be user@domain.com) or MFA enabled on account which MSOnline module does not support
solution: Use correct UPN format; if MFA enabled, disable MFA or use non-MFA admin account or migrate to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK

symptom: Entra Connect wizard: Unable to configure password writeback. Ensure you have the required license
rootCause: Admin account lacks Entra ID license, server time out of sync, or TLS 1.2 not enabled
solution: Enable TLS 1.2; use cloud admin account with proper license; sync server time with authoritative time server

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard fails with Unknown error (0x80005000)
rootCause: Config Wizard cannot reach one or more domains in the forest; domain controller is down or unreachable
solution: Run [System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Forest]::GetCurrentForest().domains in PowerShell to find unreachable domain; restore DC connectivity

symptom: User object not synced to Entra ID; Entra connector missing in metaverse; msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 (Linked Mailbox)
rootCause: msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 makes Entra Connect wait for master account from account forest before provisioning; without account-resource topology the object appears filtered
solution: Change msExchRecipientTypeDetails from 2 to 1 in on-premises AD if not using account-resource forest topology; or ensure master account syncs first

symptom: Users in federated domain not synced to Entra ID see yellow exclamation mark in Office 2013 apps
rootCause: Office 2013 IDCRL detects federated domain and tries Entra ID auth; user not synced so auth fails showing warning
solution: Set registry HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\15.0\Common\SignIn\SignInOptions to 3 (local AD auth only); or upgrade to Office 2016+. Do NOT set for synced users

symptom: Some users cannot sign in to M365/Entra/Intune; password appears not synced. 'User must change password at next logon' is checked on the AD account.
rootCause: Password hash sync does not sync temporary passwords when 'User must change password at next logon' is selected, unless ForcePasswordChangeOnLogOn feature is enabled.
solution: Clear the 'User must change password at next logon' checkbox, have the user change their password, or enable the ForcePasswordChangeOnLogOn feature in Entra Connect.

symptom: Users can sign in with old password but not new password after directory sync or password sync was disabled and re-enabled.
rootCause: Disabling and re-enabling directory/password sync may not trigger an immediate full password sync; old password hashes remain valid until new sync completes.
solution: Re-enable password sync via Entra Connect wizard (Customize synchronization options), then run Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Initial to force a full password sync.

symptom: Event ID 611 logged: Password synchronization failed for domain with RPC Error 8439 (distinguished name invalid) or RPC Error 8593 (different replication epochs).
rootCause: Windows Server 2003 domain controllers handle certain replication scenarios unexpectedly; or a domain rename is in progress causing epoch mismatch.
solution: For RPC 8439: investigate Windows Server 2003 DC behavior. For RPC 8593/ArgumentOutOfRange/duplicate key errors: update to latest version of Entra Connect.

symptom: Event ID 652/655 logged: 'Password Synchronization has not been activated for this company' during credential provisioning or ping.
rootCause: Password sync feature is not enabled for the tenant, or Entra credentials were updated through FIM without re-running the configuration wizard.
solution: Enable password sync via Entra Connect configuration wizard. If credentials were updated in FIM, re-run the Entra Connect wizard to refresh credentials.

symptom: New-MsolDomain fails with error: 'Unable to add this domain. It is a subdomain and its authentication type is different from the authentication type of the root domain.'
rootCause: New-MSOLDomain tries to add a subdomain as standard authentication, but the parent domain is configured for federated authentication.
solution: Use New-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName:<subdomain> instead of New-MSOLDomain to add subdomains under a federated parent domain.

symptom: Connect-MsolService fails with 'Unable to authenticate your credentials. Make sure that your user name is in the format: <username>@<domain>.'
rootCause: Either incorrect username format (must be user@domain, not domain\user) or the admin account has MFA enabled which is not supported by MSOnline PowerShell module.
solution: Use UPN format (user@domain.com) for username. If MFA is enabled, either disable MFA for the account or use a different admin account without MFA. Consider migrating to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK.

symptom: Entra Connect wizard shows error 'Unable to configure password writeback. Ensure you have the required license' during password writeback setup.
rootCause: Admin account lacks appropriate Entra license, server time is out of sync with Microsoft cloud services, or TLS 1.2 is not enabled.
solution: Enable TLS 1.2, ensure admin account is a cloud admin (not federated) with proper license, and verify server time is synchronized with authoritative time server.

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard fails with 'Error Unknown error (0x80005000)'.
rootCause: The sync tool cannot configure the domain because one or more domain controllers are unreachable or unhealthy.
solution: Check domain controller health using PowerShell: [System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Forest]::GetCurrentForest().domains. Identify and fix any unreachable domain controllers.

symptom: User object not synced to Entra ID; only AD connector shown in metaverse (no Entra connector). msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 on the user object. No sync error reported.
rootCause: When msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 (Linked Mailbox), Entra Connect sets sourceAnchor to NULL and waits for the master account from account forest to join. Without account-resource forest topology, the object appears stuck/filtered.
solution: If not using account-resource forest topology, change msExchRecipientTypeDetails to 1 or another non-filtered value. If using account-resource forest, ensure the master account in account forest is synchronized first.

symptom: Users in a federated domain see a yellow exclamation mark in Office 2013 applications. The user exists in federated domain but is not synced to Entra ID.
rootCause: Office 2013 uses IDCRL (Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant) which detects the federated domain and tries to authenticate to Entra ID. Since the user is not synced, authentication fails silently showing the yellow mark.
solution: Set registry key HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\15.0\Common\SignIn\SignInOptions=3 to force local AD authentication only. Alternatively, upgrade to Office 2016+ which does not have this issue.

symptom: Azure AD B2C local account passwords expire after 90 days unexpectedly. Users cannot sign in with expired passwords.
rootCause: B2C local accounts created via custom policy or Azure AD Graph API do not have passwordPolicies set to DisablePasswordExpiration by default. Only accounts created by built-in password policy get this automatically. Without this setting, passwords expire after 90 days.
solution: Run PowerShell: Connect-AzureAD → Get-AzureADUser -All $true | Set-AzureADUser -PasswordPolicies DisablePasswordExpiration. Alternatively use Graph API PATCH with {"passwordPolicies":"DisablePasswordExpiration"}. After patching, the original password can be used again immediately. For large tenants, pace writes to under 7000/5min to avoid 429 throttling.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.10 [B2C] AAD B2C password expiration.md

symptom: MFA Authenticator app displays Object ID instead of UPN when using notification-based verification in Azure China (Mooncake).
rootCause: This is by design (intentional). Azure MFA prevents EUII (end-user identifiable information) from leaving region boundaries, so Object ID is shown instead of UPN in push notifications on both iOS and Android.
solution: Use 'Verification code without notification' mode instead of 'Receive notifications for verification'. This shows UPN correctly. Setup URL for 21V: https://aka.ms/ChinaMFASetup. Note: if Google Play services are unavailable on Android, notification mode won't work anyway — use verification code mode.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.11 [MFA] MFA with Authenticator app.md

symptom: NPS extension for Azure MFA does not work in Azure China (21Vianet/Mooncake) environment with default configuration.
rootCause: Default NPS extension configuration points to global Azure endpoints. China cloud requires different endpoints for MFA hostname, resource hostname, and STS URL. Additionally, the setup script needs Connect-MgGraph with -Environment China parameter.
solution: 1) Modify AzureMfaNpsExtnConfigSetup.ps1: use Connect-MgGraph -Environment China. Register redirect URIs (https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient and http://localhost) and set Allow public client flows=Yes in app registration. 2) Set registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\AzureMfa: AZURE_MFA_HOSTNAME=strongauthenticationservice.auth.microsoft.cn, AZURE_MFA_RESOURCE_HOSTNAME=adnotifications.windowsazure.cn, STS_URL=https://login.chinacloudapi.cn/. 3) Restart IAS service. 4) Verify service principals exist (981f26a1-7f43-403b-a875-f8b09b8cd720 and 1f5530b3-261a-47a9-b357-ded261e17918).
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.14 [MFA on-prem] AAD MFA with NPS extension.md

symptom: ADFS Azure MFA authentication fails with error after AAD deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1. Schannel/DotNet errors in ADFS event logs when Azure MFA adapter tries to contact Azure MFA service.
rootCause: TLS 1.2 is not enabled on the ADFS server for .NET applications. AAD has deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1, so ADFS MFA adapter cannot establish secure connection to Azure MFA service.
solution: Enable strong authentication for .NET applications by setting registry: New-ItemProperty -path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NetFramework\v4.0.30319' -name 'SchUseStrongCrypto' -value '1' -PropertyType 'DWord' -Force. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-fs/operations/manage-ssl-protocols-in-ad-fs#enable-strong-authentication-for-net-applications
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.15 [ADFS] Configure 21V Azure MFA as auth provi.md

symptom: Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK fails in Azure China (Mooncake) with AADSTS700016
rootCause: MS Graph PS SDK app (14d82eec) is 3P, not provisioned in Mooncake. PG will not fix.
solution: Register custom app, grant MS Graph permissions, use cert auth with -Environment China.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.31 [MsGraph] Microsoftgraph PS SDK.md

symptom: SSL inspection/termination breaks auth for Azure services (AVD, AIP, ADFS, App Proxy, Kusto)
rootCause: SSL inspection intercepts and re-signs TLS traffic, breaking certificate pinning.
solution: Bypass SSL inspection for Azure service domains. Use Fiddler to test and identify impacted services.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.38 [MCVKB] [AAD] SSL Inspection impacting for A.md

symptom: Cannot access AAD sign-in/audit logs in Mooncake portal (requires Premium license)
rootCause: Mooncake only has AAD Free. MS Graph was unavailable, PG migrated reporting to AAD Graph beta.
solution: [DEPRECATED] Use AAD Graph API beta: graph.chinacloudapi.cn/<tenant>/activities/signinEvents or /audit with .
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.4 [Deprecated] Use AAD graph API to query AAD r.md

symptom: Cannot create dynamic groups with extension attributes via Azure China portal
rootCause: Azure China portal does not support selecting extension attributes in dynamic group rule builder.
solution: Use MS Graph API: create extension property, assign to users, then POST /beta/groups with membershipRule.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.40 [MsGraph] Write extension value & Create Dyn.md

symptom: Token lifetime policy does not take effect, tokens still ~1 hour
rootCause: Policy applied to wrong app type. ID Token needs policy on Caller App, Access Token on Resource App.
solution: Apply TokenLifetimePolicy to correct Service Principal. Multiple policies: first one wins. Avoid setting on MS Graph resource.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.41 [Token] Change token lifetime of access toke.md

symptom: NPS extension Azure MFA fails - cert on NPS server mismatches cert in AAD for SP 981f26a1.
rootCause: NPS extension cert locally out of sync with AAD-registered cert after expiration or reconfig.
solution: Compare certs via Get-MgServicePrincipal. If mismatch, re-run config script with -Environment China.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.46 NPS+Alternate ID.md

symptom: NPS extension MFA fails with ResourceNotFoundException using Alternate ID. MSODS 404 on user lookup.
rootCause: NPS passes alternate login (email) but MSODS looks up by UPN format which does not exist.
solution: Debug via Kusto IfxUlsEvents in MSODS. Use NTRadPing to test RADIUS. Check Azure MFA event logs on NPS server.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.46 NPS+Alternate ID.md

symptom: Convert-MsolDomainToFederated fails with Service not available in 21V. SOAP fault: TrustedRealm not found.
rootCause: MSOL deprecated. Backend returns TrustedRealm not found for new unfederated domains.
solution: Use Graph PowerShell New-MgDomainFederationConfiguration. Requires Domain.ReadWrite.All AND Directory.AccessAsUser.All (both needed - known issue). Get ADFS params from existing federated domain or metadata.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.48 [ADFS] use Microsoft Graph Powershell to ena.md

symptom: Windows shows Your account requires authentication toast. AAD event log: Universal Store Native Client (268761a2) auth error for onestore.microsoft.com.
rootCause: Subscription Activation uses Universal Store Native Client which does not work in 21V. New since Win11 23H2+KB5034848.
solution: Disable SA via registry: ClipSVC DisableSubscription=1. Disable MFA banner via MfaRequiredInClipRenew=0. SA is Windows DND scope.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.49[AAD][SA] Your account requires authenticatio.md

symptom: Customer wants IdP-initiated SSO to Azure Portal after signing into their IdP.
rootCause: Azure AD does not support true IdP-initiated SSO. Platform limitation.
solution: Use login_hint in OIDC implicit flow to simulate. For 21V Portal: client_id=c44b4083..., redirect_uri=management.chinacloudapi.cn, login_hint=user@domain.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.51 Using login hint to Simulate fake IdP-initia.md

symptom: B2B/CCB2B users cannot SSO on MDM iOS devices. Edge login always requires password; cross-tenant access shows device not compliant.
rootCause: Issue 1: Prompt=login forces fresh login. Issue 2: Device compliance not passed cross-tenant. Edge PG confirmed BUG.
solution: Check broker logs for Prompt=login. Cross-tenant issue: escalate to Edge PG. Debug via ESTS Kusto, iOS Fiddler, broker logs.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.54 SSO on ios and debug.md

symptom: B2B guest user authentication fails after migrating from Tango to CC B2B. Guest users in Tenant B can no longer authenticate because home tenant (Tenant A) was deprecated.
rootCause: After deprecating Tenant A (global), Tenant B's guest users still point to Tenant A as home tenant for authentication. Cross-tenant access with 21V Tenant C (same domain) is not set up.
solution: 1) Set up cross-tenant access between Tenant B and 21V Tenant C. 2) Reset redemption status for guest users in Tenant B. 3) Resend invitation — users will re-accept using Tenant C as home tenant.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.57 After migrate from Tango to CC B2B, how to m.md

symptom: MFA registration forces users to create App Password. During Enforced Per-user MFA registration, the App Password step is mandatory and cannot be skipped.
rootCause: Legacy Service Setting has 'Allow App Passwords' enabled, which auto-enables App Password in user's Authentication Methods policy (only visible via F12 browser dev tools).
solution: In MFA Service Settings, disable 'Allow App Passwords'. This automatically disables App Password in user Authentication Methods policy.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.58 [AAD][MFA]MFA 注册会强制要求AppPassword.md

symptom: MFA SMS/Voice verification fails with error BlockAllEnterpriseSmsTrafficInCountryExceptAllowedTenants or BlockAllEnterpriseVoiceTrafficInCountryExceptAllowedTenants. Telephony MFA blocked for certain country codes.
rootCause: Certain regions require opt-in for MFA telephony verification. The tenant's phone number country code is in a region that requires explicit opt-in via IcM to PG.
solution: 1) Verify in ESTS Kusto + MFA detail logs (idsharedmcsha AllSASCommonEvents) using correlation ID. 2) Raise CRI/IcM to PG: Identity Reputation Manage and Abuse Prevention > Telecom Queue. 3) Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/concept-mfa-regional-opt-in
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.59[MFA]Opt-In for MFA Telephony Verification.md

symptom: AADSTS500082: SAML assertion is not present in the token — when converting ADFS-AAD federation from WS-fed to SAML protocol.
rootCause: ADFS returns InvalidNameIDPolicy because it cannot provide NameID in the format AAD requests (urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:persistent). Also missing IDPEmail attribute in claim rules.
solution: 1) Remove extra RPID, keep only urn:federation:partner.microsoftonline.cn. 2) Change claim rule NameID format to persistent. 3) Add IDPEmail claim rule mapping UPN to IDPEmail. 4) To revert: Set-MsolDomainAuthentication Managed → delete RP → Convert-MsolDomainToFederated → Update-MsolFederatedDomain.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.6 [ADFS] How to convert ADFS-AAD federation pro.md

symptom: Cannot find AAD Connect download link. Old download locations deprecated.
rootCause: AAD Connect download location changed to Entra admin center.
solution: Download from: https://entra.microsoft.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_Connect_Provisioning/AADConnectMenuBlade/~/GetStarted
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.56 where to download aad connect.md

symptom: MFA requests fail due to AAD Gateway throttling — users receive HTTP 429 Too Many Requests during MFA sign-in. MFA service (SAS) never receives the request.
rootCause: AAD Gateway (Layer 1) throttles the request before it reaches SAS. EffectiveStatusCode=429 and IsThrottled=true in RequestSummaryEventCore. This is the highest-impact throttling layer.
solution: 1) Query AAD Gateway AllRequestSummaryEvents: filter EffectiveStatusCode startswith '429', TargetService contains 'mysignins' or 'strongauthenticationservice'. 2) Aggregate by time to identify throttling windows. 3) Gateway throttling blocks all MFA for affected services — escalate to identity platform team if persistent. 4) Kusto cluster (21V): idsharedmcbjb.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn / AADGatewayProd.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/1. Readiness/----Philip AAD MFA Deep Dive----/2.  Different level of throttling types.md

symptom: MFA SMS/Voice calls not delivered to users in specific countries. SAS logs show BlockAllEnterpriseSms/VoiceTrafficInCountry or RepMap/OptIn keywords.
rootCause: Telephony-level country hard block (RepMap) — Microsoft's fraud protection blocks SMS/Voice MFA delivery to certain countries flagged for telephony fraud. This is not rate-based throttling but a country-level policy.
solution: 1) Search SAS AllSASCommonEvents Msg for 'BlockAll', 'OptIn', 'RepMap' keywords. 2) If country is blocked, customer must use alternative MFA methods (Authenticator app TOTP/Push instead of SMS/Voice). 3) Reference: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/entra/identity/authentication/concept-mfa-telephony-fraud. 4) Kusto cluster (21V): idsharedmcsha.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn / idmfacne.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/1. Readiness/----Philip AAD MFA Deep Dive----/2.  Different level of throttling types.md

symptom: Global admin locked out of tenant due to lost/damaged MFA device (Authenticator app). Only one global admin with per-user MFA or Security Defaults enabled.
rootCause: MFA device lost/reinstalled/damaged. PG decommissioned LSM job to disable MFA (June 2024 IcM 507456130).
solution: 1) Identify other GA/Privileged Auth Admin to reset methods. 2) If no other admin, engage 21V T0 for phone/email update with authorization letter. 3) CSS creates IcM to MSODS to clear StrongAuthenticationMethod (user re-registers on next login). IcM template: tmpl=c52i3t
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.27 [Process][MFA] How to disable MFA for only o.md

symptom: Need to change AAD Connect synced domain UPN suffix (e.g. contoso.com to contoso.cn). No documentation for migrating domain UPN of existing synced users.
rootCause: AADC does not natively support changing domain suffix for already-synced objects. Custom sync rule only applies to new accounts.
solution: 1) Add new verified domain. 2) If ADFS, add as federation domain. 3) For O365: add both domains in proxyAddress. 4) Custom AADC filter rule for new accounts. 5) For existing: Set-MsolUserPrincipalName. 6) Update proxyAddress. Only tested for AD users.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.28 [Migrated][AADC] How to migrate domain UPN f.md

symptom: AAD Connect V2 requires Windows Server 2016+. Customer needs to upgrade from V1 to V2 with new server.
rootCause: AADC V2 dropped support for Windows Server 2008R2/2012/2012R2.
solution: 1) Export existing settings. 2) Install latest AADC on new server in staging mode, import settings. 3) Use AAD Connect Documenter to compare configs. 4) Switch new server to active, old to staging.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.29 [AADC] AAD Connect Upgrade from V1 to V2.md

symptom: Customer requests dual federation - single SSO covering both Global AAD and Mooncake AAD (sync on-prem AD to both clouds).
rootCause: No general solution available. PG must evaluate individually. Implementing without PG approval NOT supported.
solution: [DEPRECATED] Do NOT deliver dual-federation guidance. Pass-through: escalate to CSS. Non-pass-through: inform no general solution. Premier support required for PG evaluation/approval.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.3 [Deprecated] identity management across diffe.md

symptom: Cannot retrieve MFA device/phone info via Microsoft Graph API in Mooncake. Authentication methods resources not available.
rootCause: MS Graph authentication methods API not supported in Mooncake.
solution: Workaround: AAD Graph API 1.6-internal. 1) Get AADv1 token via password grant with AAD PS AppId (1b730954-...) against login.partner.microsoftonline.cn. 2) GET graph.chinacloudapi.cn/myorganization/users/{objectId}?api-version=1.6-internal. MFA info in strongAuthenticationDetail. Internal API - not for production.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.30 [MsGraph] How to get the device used in MFA.md

symptom: AADSTS50146 error when using claims mapping policy to add custom claims (e.g. onPremiseSamAccountName) to OIDC tokens.
rootCause: Application manifest missing acceptMappedClaims=true. Required for single-tenant apps using claims mapping policy.
solution: 1) Create claims mapping policy with ClaimsSchema. 2) Assign to service principal. 3) Set acceptMappedClaims=true in app manifest to fix AADSTS50146.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.34 [AppMgmt] customize attributes in OIDC(Acces.md

symptom: onPremiseSamAccountName not visible in Mooncake AAD despite being synced by AADC. Not shown via PowerShell, ASC, or Graph PS SDK.
rootCause: Many on-prem attributes synced as hint attributes. In Mooncake, hint attributes not exposed via PowerShell/ASC. Public Azure shows them normally.
solution: Use MS Graph REST API directly (Postman/Graph Explorer) to query user object. Verify in AADC MV and CS that attribute exists. Sync rule: AD accountName -> MV accountName -> AAD onPremiseSamAccountName.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.35 [AADC] how to find any hint attributes(onpre.md

symptom: Need to grant delegated permissions for specific AAD users only (not all via admin consent). ROPC flow cannot raise interactive consent prompts.
rootCause: Admin consent grants for all users. ROPC has no interactive UI. Need programmatic per-user consent.
solution: Use MS Graph oauth2permissiongrant API. Caller needs DelegatedPermissionGrant.ReadWrite.All. Create per-user consent grants via POST.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.36 [AAD] How to do user consent granting for sp.md

symptom: Configuring AAD authentication for multi-tenant Web App/Function App fails with error: The Application ID URI must be from a verified domain
rootCause: Doc steps only work for single-tenant apps. Multi-tenant apps require Application ID URI to use a verified domain, not the default *.azurewebsites.net domain.
solution: For multi-tenant: use domain+app name format URL as Application ID URI. If custom domain: add and verify in AAD Custom Domains first. Enable Access tokens and ID tokens. Ensure Application ID URI matches Allowed Token Audiences.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Web=======/5.1 configure Webapp_ Function app to use AAD logi.md

symptom: After AKS upgrade VMAS to VMSS, LB probes fail, traffic down. LB rules rewritten back to old IP. IP conflict errors PrivateIPAddressIsAllocated in cloud-controller-manager logs.
rootCause: UMI for AKS had no RBAC on VNet/PublicIP/LB. Cloud-controller-manager got AuthorizationFailed, could not manage LB. UMI also allocated IPs already assigned to LB causing conflicts.
solution: Assign Network Contributor to UMI on both Node RG and master RG. Reconcile AKS. Check logs for AuthorizationFailed/PrivateIPAddressIsAllocated.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Case Study###/[Aks]User Identity has no RBAC which caused severa.md

symptom: MSIX app attach fails on AVD session hosts joined to AADDS. Adding MSIX image to host pool returns permission errors on Azure Files.
rootCause: MSIX app attach does not support AADDS. AADDS computer objects not synced to AAD, RBAC for Azure Files cannot be granted.
solution: Use on-prem AD DS instead of AADDS for MSIX app attach.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/MSIX app attach/[Lab]MSIX+AADDS.md

symptom: AVD/WVD user repeatedly prompted to enter credentials when launching second RemoteApp.
rootCause: By design. All RemoteApps share one session ID. When all closed, session becomes Disconnected. New launch treated as new connection.
solution: Keep at least one RemoteApp active. Check RDAgent logs: AddOrUpdateConnectionForSession vs RdpStackConnectionEstablished.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Steve]2108110060002763 _ WVD keeps asking authent.md

symptom: AVD sign-in repeatedly asks for credentials despite Seamless SSO enabled. Error 70044 then Seamless SSO fallback fails with connectivity error to autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com.
rootCause: Sign-in frequency CA policy rejects PRT (70044 expected). Seamless SSO fallback fails because autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com blocked by firewall.
solution: Unblock autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com. Test by visiting URL directly. If still failing, capture Fiddler + Network Monitor.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/Concepts# Condition access_.md

symptom: ADFS SSO for AVD fails during user login with certificate authentication error.
rootCause: Certificate Authentication not enabled in ADFS. ADFS SSO for AVD uses virtual smart card which requires it.
solution: Enable Certificate Authentication in ADFS. Verify: Enterprise CA templates, Key Vault, relying party trust, host pool SSO params.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/ADFS SSO.md

symptom: Need to connect to Microsoft Graph PowerShell from Az module in Azure China (Mooncake)
rootCause: Az module and MgGraph module use different auth flows
solution: Connect-AzAccount -Environment AzureChinaCloud, Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn, ConvertTo-SecureString, Connect-MgGraph -AccessToken -Environment china
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/AAD PowerShell/Microsoft.Graph/Login from Az module.md

symptom: Need to authenticate Microsoft Graph PowerShell with service principal certificate in Azure China
rootCause: Default app ID not available in Mooncake
solution: Create self-signed cert, export .cer to app registration, Connect-MgGraph -ClientId -Environment China -TenantId -CertificateThumbprint
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/AAD PowerShell/Microsoft.Graph/Service Principal Sign-In with Certificate.md

rootCause: Without Exchange license, Exchange Admin Center cannot manage proxyAddresses
solution: Use MS Graph API beta: PATCH users/{userId} with {proxyAddresses:[]} to clear all, then re-add desired email. For batch mail update: Az PowerShell Update-AzADUser
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/AAD PowerShell/Microsoft.Graph/Update Azure AD user' s mail and ProxyAddress.md

symptom: AADSTS7000218: request body must contain client_assertion or client_secret when connecting MgGraph delegated auth
rootCause: App registration platform set to Web instead of Mobile and desktop applications
solution: Change app platform to Mobile and desktop applications, enable Allow public client flows
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/AAD PowerShell/Microsoft.Graph/User Delegated Sign-In.md

symptom: AADSTS50011: Redirect URI mismatch when using Connect-MgGraph with custom app
rootCause: Redirect URI not configured for Mobile platform
solution: Add redirect URIs: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient and http://localhost under Mobile platform
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/AAD PowerShell/Microsoft.Graph/User Delegated Sign-In.md

symptom: Entra Connect 2.4.129.0: custom domain UPN admin cannot login AADC wizard in Mooncake. Error: Unable to determine Azure instance, defaulting to WorldWide
rootCause: Bug in 2.4.129.0 DomainSuffixMapping - does not recognize custom MOERA domain in Mooncake
solution: Workaround: use user@*.partner.onmschina.cn to trigger login redirect to 21v, then sign in with admin. Fix pending PG
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/AAD PowerShell/MsOnline_AzureAD module deprecation/Actions for Entra Connect upgrade.md

symptom: Azure Files storage account AD DS join script runs successfully but FSLogix/Kerberos authentication fails afterward when accessing Azure Files shares.
rootCause: Join-AzStorageAccount script parameters silently accept wrong values: 1) EncryptionType must be exact string like 'AES256', not template format '<AES256|RC4|AES256,RC4>'. 2) SamAccountName must be 'domain\computerName' without trailing '$' sign. Script completes without errors even with wrong parameters but Kerberos auth fails at runtime.
solution: Re-run Join-AzStorageAccount with correct parameters: $EncryptionType = 'AES256' (exact string). $SamAccountName = 'domain\computerName' (no trailing $). After fixing, verify AD object was created correctly and test access.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/Storage Account Join AD Domain Issue.md

symptom: App role assignment to AAD group fails when trying to assign via Microsoft.Graph PowerShell.
rootCause: App role assignment must be performed on Service Principal (Enterprise Application), not the Application registration object. If Application was created via PowerShell without creating a corresponding Service Principal, assignment will fail.
solution: Create Service Principal first: New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId $app.AppId. Then assign: New-MgGroupAppRoleAssignment -GroupId <groupId> -AppRoleId <roleId> -ResourceId <spObjectId> -PrincipalId <groupId>. Also ensure AllowedMemberTypes includes 'User' for group-based assignment.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/AAD PowerShell/Microsoft.Graph/App Role Management.md

symptom: Azure AD B2C End of Sale: new customers cannot purchase B2C after May 1, 2025. B2C P2 tier retires March 15, 2026. Existing customers can continue using B2C until at least May 2030.
rootCause: Microsoft product lifecycle decision. B2C is being replaced by Microsoft Entra External ID. GoLocal for Japan/Australia expected GA Aug 2025; GCC and China timelines TBD.
solution: Existing customers: continue using B2C (P1 only for new tenants). New customers: evaluate Microsoft Entra External ID. Migration tooling: JIT/hybrid capabilities private preview May 2025, public preview EoQ1FY26. Mooncake: same timeline, details TBD.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/B2C/B2C retirement.md

symptom: Cannot create new Azure AD B2C tenant after B2C End of Sale (May 2025). Portal creation blocked for new customers.
rootCause: B2C End of Sale policy blocks new tenant creation. Only existing B2C customers with active tenants can create new B2C P1 tenants.
solution: If customer has business justification, work with account manager to submit an Ask.Entra request at https://aka.ms/askentra to get exception approval for new B2C tenant creation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/B2C/Cannot create new AAD B2C tenant.md

symptom: Unable to restore deleted B2C user. Error: 'A conflicting object with one or more of the specified property values is present in the directory' (Request_BadRequest). Browser trace may or may not show which attribute conflicts.
rootCause: The deleted B2C user's AlternativeSecurityId (AltSecID) conflicts with an existing active user. AltSecID is used by external IdP features - type 1=MSA, 5=AAD, 6=BYOI. When a new user is created with the same external identity after the original was deleted, both share the same AltSecID, blocking restore.
solution: 1) Check MSODS Kusto logs (IfxAuditLoggingCommon + IfxUlsEvents) using correlationId from audit log to identify the conflicting attribute. 2) If browser trace shows conflicted attribute name → search existing users for that value. 3) If no attribute shown → check AltSecID: use ASC/Graph API beta to get deleted user's alternativeSecurityIds, then search active users for matching values. 4) Customer must delete or modify the conflicting active user, then restore the deleted user.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/B2C/Case Study/2112020060001269 Unable to restore deleted B2C use.md

symptom: Azure AD B2C local account authentication fails with 'Invalid username or password' even though credentials are correct. Occurs in 21V (Mooncake) when using custom policy with LocalAccounts.
rootCause: Two issues: 1) The ESTS login endpoint in custom policy TrustFrameworkBase was pointing to global (login.microsoftonline.com) instead of 21V endpoint. 2) Issuer validation failed because login.partner.microsoftonline.cn is not recognized by B2C CPIM - must use sts.chinacloudapi.cn/{tenantId}/ as issuer.
solution: In B2C custom policy XML, change the login endpoint to the 21V-specific endpoint: use https://login.chinacloudapi.cn/ or https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/{tenantId}/ instead of login.partner.microsoftonline.cn. Verify via CPIM Kusto logs (cpimmcprod2.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/CPIM) using x-ms-cpim-trans correlation ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/B2C/Case Study/2307030040000167 AAD B2C local account authenticat.md

symptom: Azure AD B2C user flow does not return 'emails' claim in token for some users. Users created via Graph API don't get emails claim, but users created via B2C sign-up flow do.
rootCause: Users created via Graph API without proper 'identities' attribute are not recognized as B2C local/consumer accounts. The emails claim comes from the identities attribute (signInName without default domain) and otherMails attribute. Users must have creationType=LocalAccount to be treated as consumer accounts.
solution: When creating B2C users via Graph API, use Example 2 from MS docs (Create user with social and local account identities): include 'identities' array with signInType, issuer, and issuerAssignedId. Also set passwordProfile with forceChangePasswordNextSignIn=false and passwordPolicies. Note: use Graph API beta to view all user attributes (v1.0 omits some). The 'Mail' attribute does NOT contribute to emails claim, only identities signInName and otherMails do.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/B2C/Case Study/2311180030000169 AAD B2C emails claim of user flow.md

symptom: International social IDPs (Google, Facebook, Twitter) cannot be used as B2C identity providers in Mooncake (21V). Users cannot authenticate via these providers.
rootCause: China's national firewall blocks access to Google, Facebook, and Twitter services. These IDPs are still shown in B2C UI but are non-functional in Mooncake environment.
solution: PG is planning to hide blocked IDPs from the UI. Workaround: use Global AAD as IDP for Mooncake B2C - the user OID will be base64-converted into the AlternativeSecurityId attribute. Note: customized login UX might not work properly per B2C docs when using this approach.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/B2C/Known issue.md

symptom: Cross-tenant B2B collaboration fails when customer restricts allowed applications to Office365 only. Guest users cannot login to guest tenant portal even though Office365 app is allowed.
rootCause: MicrosoftAdminPortals app is implicitly required for portal access but not included in the Office365 app bundle. This app cannot be added via Portal UI directly - only via Graph API.
solution: Use Graph API to PATCH crossTenantAccessPolicy partner settings. Add MicrosoftAdminPortals app (AppId: c44b4083-3bb0-49c1-b47d-974e53cbdf3c) and the required portal app (80ccca67-54bd-44ab-8625-4b79c4dc7775) to the b2bCollaborationInbound.applications.targets array alongside Office365. Endpoint: PATCH https://microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn/v1.0/policies/crossTenantAccessPolicy/partners/{guestTenantId}. Prerequisites: register app with Graph permission to modify CrossTenant settings, obtain Graph token.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/Case Study/2408130030000612 Configure Application of b2b Coll.md

symptom: Azure Portal subscription overview page is always loading (infinite spinner) when accessed by a Cross-Cloud B2B guest account.
rootCause: Known platform issue (ICM-367805534) affecting B2B guest accounts accessing subscription overview in cross-cloud scenarios. No fix planned by PG.
solution: No fix available. Workaround: access subscription details via direct URL or use Azure CLI/PowerShell instead of Portal. This is a known limitation documented in ICM-367805534.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/Feature status/Known issues.md

symptom: Encrypted documents shared from Azure China cloud to Commercial cloud users are inaccessible via Office Client, Outlook, Office Web, or SharePoint Online.
rootCause: Cross-cloud AIP/sensitivity label encryption does not support document attachments across sovereign cloud boundaries. Only web-based link sharing works.
solution: Do NOT send encrypted documents as attachments across cloud boundaries. Share a link to the document in SharePoint/OneDrive instead. Recipient signs in as CC B2B guest and accesses via browser. Set up sensitivity labels with China cloud user domain in Encryption permissions.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/Technical details/AIP with CCB2B.md

symptom: Cross-cloud B2B guest user cannot access SharePoint documents. Authentication fails when using email address instead of UPN.
rootCause: SharePoint cross-cloud B2B authentication requires the UPN, not the email address. When UPN and email differ, using email will fail.
solution: Authenticate using UPN, not email address. Inform users to check their UPN in Azure AD portal if unsure.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/Technical details/AIP with CCB2B.md

symptom: B2B invitation cannot be sent when invited user UPN and email address differ. Portal and PowerShell only send invitations to accounts where UPN matches email.
rootCause: Azure AD B2B invitation mechanism uses the same address for both authentication lookup and email delivery.
solution: Workaround 1: Invite via Portal, manually copy InviteRedeemUrl and forward to actual email. Workaround 2: Use PowerShell bulk invite (New-AzureADMSInvitation) which returns InviteRedeemUrl in output.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/Technical details/Get RedeemURL.md

symptom: Cross-cloud B2B guest user cannot access Azure Portal when navigating to portal.azure.com or portal.azure.cn directly without specifying tenant.
rootCause: Default portal endpoint cannot determine which tenant the guest user should authenticate against in cross-cloud scenarios.
solution: MUST use tenanted endpoint: portal.azure.com/<tenant>.onmicrosoft.com or portal.azure.cn/<tenant_ID>. Direct portal.azure.com will NOT work.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/Technical details/PoC steps.md

symptom: Cross-cloud B2B guest invitation fails for consumer accounts (Microsoft personal accounts / LiveID).
rootCause: CC B2B only supports organizational accounts. Consumer accounts (outlook.com, hotmail.com, live.com) are not supported.
solution: Use organizational accounts (onmicrosoft.com or custom verified domain). Consumer/LiveID cannot be invited as CC B2B guests.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/Technical details/PoC steps.md

symptom: CC B2B does not support Azure Data Explorer (ADX) web UI authentication or Dynamics 365 access for guest users.
rootCause: ADX web UI and Dynamics 365 are not on the official supported app list for cross-cloud B2B. Only supported: SharePoint/OneDrive, Office Web, Teams, Power BI.
solution: No workaround for ADX web UI or Dynamics 365 via CC B2B. For ADX: use REST API or Kusto Explorer with explicit token.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/Technical details/Comparison with dual-fed.md

symptom: 21v Azure portal has a known issue blocking Cross-Cloud B2B guest user login. Guest users from another cloud instance cannot sign in to 21v Azure portal.
rootCause: Platform-level known issue in 21v (Mooncake) Azure portal that blocks CC B2B guest user authentication. PG investigated but no specific root cause published. Issue was marked as fixed.
solution: Issue has been fixed by PG. If recurring, escalate via ICM to Azure Portal team. After CC B2B GA, dual-federation will no longer be offered to new customers; existing dual-fed will still be supported.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Cross Cloud B2B/What is CC B2B.md

symptom: AADDS primary DC stops responding after Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) V2 migration maintenance. AADDS auto-healing does not detect this failure type, causing prolonged outage if DNS still points to the failed DC IP.
rootCause: PG maintenance operation migrating encryption-at-rest extension (ADE) from V1 to V2 caused corruption of the primary DC boot disk. AADDS auto-healing does not trigger for this specific failure mode — only PG manual intervention can fix it.
solution: 1) Raise ICM to PG to manually promote secondary DC and recreate failed DC. 2) Update VNet DNS settings to point to the new DC IPs. 3) Restart all domain-joined servers to pick up new DNS. 4) Do NOT put delete locks on AADDS resource group — locks prevent NIC cleanup during DC failover. Reference: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/active-directory-domain-services/network-considerations#network-resources-used-by-azure-ad-ds
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Entra Domain Service/## Case study/2206180060000017 Unable to connect to AADDS.md

symptom: AADDS DC failover creates new DC with different IP address instead of reusing the original IP. VNet DNS settings become stale, causing domain-joined machines to lose connectivity.
rootCause: Resource group delete lock prevents the old NIC from being deleted when AADDS auto-healing creates a new DC. Since the old NIC still holds the original IP, Azure DHCP assigns a new IP to the replacement DC's NIC. The VNet DNS is not auto-updated.
solution: 1) Remove delete locks from AADDS resource group before DC failover or as a preventive measure. 2) After DC failover, update VNet DNS to new DC IPs. 3) Restart domain-joined servers. 4) If customer needs original IPs back: remove lock, delete old NICs, then PG deletes and recreates DCs in sequence to reclaim IPs. 5) AADDS configuration diagnostics can detect VNet DNS mismatch — run diagnostics periodically.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Entra Domain Service/## Case study/2206180060000017 Unable to connect to AADDS.md

symptom: AADDS replica DC creation takes 3+ days or fails repeatedly. ARM logs show VM delete operations repeating every hour. Jarvis logs show PSRemoting access denied errors during DC promotion.
rootCause: Networking issue between Azure China regions (e.g., China East 2 and China North 3) causes RemotePowerShell connection drops during DC promotion. AADDS PG confirmed they cannot get root cause as they cannot reproduce in lab. Both successful and failed promotion events show PSRemoting errors, but some succeed after retries.
solution: Short-term: Manually reboot the affected VM during replicaCreate operation to unblock DC promotion. Long-term: PG is developing a remediator to automatically reboot the affected VM. Check ARM Kusto (armmcadx.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/armmc) EventServiceEntries for VM delete patterns. Check Jarvis for DC promotion success/failure correlation IDs. Reference ICM: 446491273.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Entra Domain Service/## Case study/2311240040001001 AADDS replica create failure due.md

symptom: Azure DevOps pipeline fails with AADSTS700212: No matching federated identity record found for presented assertion audience api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina. Service connection tests also fail.
rootCause: Azure DevOps backend changed the audience from api://AzureADTokenExchange to api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina for Mooncake environment. The federated identity credential on the Managed Identity still has the old audience value. Cloud-specific audiences: Public=api://AzureADTokenExchange, Mooncake=api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina, Fairfax=api://AzureADTokenExchangeUSGov.
solution: Update the audience in the federated identity credential (Azure Portal > Managed Identity > Federated credentials) to match the cloud environment: api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina for Mooncake. Note: DevOps PG may change the audience back, so monitor and adjust accordingly.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Managed Identity/[MCVKB] Azure DevOps Workload identity federation.md

symptom: Managed Identity token request returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error from IMDS endpoint (169.254.169.254). One pod on a VM gets tokens fine while another pod on a different VM fails. Error persists for hours then self-resolves.
rootCause: IMDS node-level issue: PFXImportCertStore failed with 'There is not enough space on the disk' on the hosting node. The MSI certificate store operation fails due to low disk space on the physical node, causing all MSI token requests on that node to return 500.
solution: 1) Check ImdsSpan Kusto table: cluster('azcore.chinanorth3.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn').database('Fa').ImdsSpan with NodeId and statusMessage filters. 2) Check Jarvis ImdsEvent table. 3) If FaultPoint=IdentityException:CertPFXImportCertStoreFailed, this is a node-level issue - file ICM to platform team. 4) Workaround: redeploy workload to different node. Note: ImdsApiReqResp table does NOT work in Mooncake.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Managed Identity/[MCVKB] RESPONSE 500 Internal Server Error for tok.md

symptom: PIM eligible assignment extend request fails with RoleAssignmentRequestPolicyValidationFailed in audit logs. No detailed error message shown in Azure Portal or ASC audit logs.
rootCause: The extend request duration exceeds the PIM policy MaximumGrantPeriod (default 365 days). The ExpirationRule policy validation fails silently with only RoleAssignmentRequestPolicyValidationFailed shown.
solution: 1) Check Jarvis logs with timestamp + correlation ID to see detailed failure reason. 2) Look for 'The duration in the request is greater than maximum allowed duration' in Jarvis logs. 3) Adjust the request StopDateTime to be within MaximumGrantPeriod, or increase MaximumGrantPeriod in PIM role settings. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/authorization/includes/privileged-role-common-errors
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Privileged Identity Managemnet/Case study/[Case study] PIM - how to check request failure.md

symptom: Customer needs to grant full database access across multiple database types (SQL Database + DBForMySQL) within a specific resource group. No single built-in role covers multiple DB types.
rootCause: Built-in RBAC roles are database-type-specific (e.g. SQL DB Contributor only covers Microsoft.Sql/*). There is no built-in role that covers both Microsoft.Sql/* and Microsoft.DBforMySQL/* simultaneously.
solution: Create a custom RBAC role combining operations: Microsoft.Sql/*, Microsoft.DBforMySQL/*, plus common ops (Authorization/*/read, Insights/alertRules/*, Insights/metrics/read, ResourceHealth/*, Resources/deployments/*, Support/*). Set assignableScopes at subscription level for reuse. Assign at resource group scope.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/RBAC/Case study/Custom RBAC role for multiple DB access in resourc.md

symptom: az role assignment create fails with 403 Forbidden when logged in as Service Principal; debug shows 403 on graph.chinacloudapi.cn getObjectsByObjectIds API call
rootCause: Azure CLI role assignment command first queries AAD Graph to resolve the assignee identity. The SP lacks permission to read AAD user/SP information
solution: Assign Directory readers role (or other role with AAD read permission) to the Service Principal, then retry
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Role and admin/Kusto/#1 Add a role assignment after Az login with a SP(.md

symptom: Sudden ADFS service outage; system log shows Event 4231 from Tcpip source indicating all ephemeral TCP ports in use (port exhaustion)
rootCause: AAD Connect Health Agent for ADFS version 3.0.244.0 has a connection buffer leak in Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.PshSurrogate.exe
solution: Upgrade AAD Connect Health Agent for ADFS to version 3.1.2.0 or later. Immediate mitigation: restart ADFS server/service
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/AD FS-[Case Sharing]AAD Connect Health Agent for A.md

symptom: ADFS issues windowsaccountname claim with different case than AD samAccountName; case-sensitive apps fail to recognize user. Format persists in cache until computer restart
rootCause: ADFS reads windowsaccountname from Windows/Kerberos logon session context, preserving case as typed at login. Cached until computer restart
solution: Configure additional ADFS claim rules to query AD directly for sAMAccountName instead of using session context. Note: domain name hard-coded, multi-domain needs extra rules
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - AD FS windowsaccountname claim format.md

symptom: Entra Domain Services (AADDS) may stop working after TLS 1.0/1.1 deprecation. Need to identify apps/clients still using deprecated TLS versions.
rootCause: Microsoft deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1 for Entra Domain Services. Apps authenticating with older TLS versions will fail. Cannot identify source machine/IP, only app name visible in Data2 column.
solution: Check Jarvis telemetry (Geneva shortlink CFDC69AA) to identify TLS 1.0/1.1 traffic. Apps in Data2 column need TLS 1.2 upgrade. ADO Wiki: AzureAD/_wiki/2201046/TLS-1.0-1.1-Deprecation-For-Entra-Domain-Services. PG notifies impacted tenants via ICM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Entra Domain Service/## TS Workflow/TLS 1.0_1.1 Deprecation.md

symptom: AD FS 2016 with Azure MFA does not support Alternate Login ID. Authentication fails when using alternate login ID with Azure MFA adapter.
rootCause: Known limitation by design: ADFS 2016 Azure MFA adapter does not support alternate login ID.
solution: Known limitation. Workaround: use UPN instead of alternate login ID, or use a different MFA method.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Alternate login ID] AD FS 2016 with Azure MFA to.md

symptom: OAuth ROPC grant fails for federated users against Azure AD. Error code 80047860 in EvoSTS LoginResponse.
rootCause: Azure AD does not forward password to AD FS for ROPC flow. Federated users must have password hash synced. Error 80047860 = federated user auth failure; e5a = managed user wrong password.
solution: Enable Password Hash Sync (PHS) for federated users who need ROPC. For ADFS 2016 direct ROPC: configure Application Group, use grant_type=password with client_id and resource. Post to /adfs/oauth2/token.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Auth flow] OAuth Resource Owner Password Credenti.md

symptom: Chrome always prompts certificate selection dialog during ADFS/Azure AD device or client certificate authentication, even with only one cert.
rootCause: Chrome does not auto-select client certificates by default. IE/Edge on Windows 10 auto-selects. iOS/Android Chrome/Safari prompts once and remembers.
solution: Set Chrome policy AutoSelectCertificateForUrls via registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome\AutoSelectCertificateForUrls. Use pattern https://device.login.microsoftonline.com with filter ISSUER CN=MS-Organization-Access for AAD.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Browsers related] Suppress the cert list prompted.md

symptom: Connect-EXOPSSession with -Credential uses basic auth (usernamemixed endpoint) to ADFS despite being modern auth module, hitting ADFS claim rules blocking basic auth.
rootCause: Get-Credential + -Credential sends creds to ADFS usernamemixed endpoint (basic auth) instead of passive /adfs/ls/ (modern auth browser flow). Blocked by ADFS claim rules.
solution: Use Connect-EXOPSSession without -Credential to trigger browser-based modern auth. If automation needed: add PS client IP exception to basic auth blocking claim rule.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Case sharing] Legacy (Basic) auth OR Modern auth.md

symptom: WAP resets TLS client hello from applications. Application cannot establish SSL connection to ADFS through WAP.
rootCause: Application/WAF does not include SNI extension in TLS Client Hello. Http.sys on WAP filters traffic because it cannot determine which certificate to serve without SNI.
solution: Add fallback certificate to 0.0.0.0:443 on every ADFS and WAP: netsh http show sslcert, then netsh http add sslcert ipport=0.0.0.0:443 certhash=<hash> appid={5d89a20c-beab-4389-9447-324788eb944a}. Long-term: upgrade app for SNI.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Cert] ADFS with SSL TLS Server Name Indication.md

symptom: ADFS claim rule for x-ms-forwarded-client-ip does not work. MDM managed devices cannot access Exchange Online despite claim rule allowing their IPs.
rootCause: ADFS claim rule parameters (x-ms-*) are case-sensitive. Uppercase letters cause silent failure. x-ms-forwarded-client-ip shows WAP/proxy IP, not actual client IP.
solution: Ensure exact lowercase for claim type URIs. Use regex with word boundaries for IP matching. Collect WAP/ADFS debug+admin+audit logs. Test regex at regex101.com.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Claim rules] Claim Rule Parameter is Case Sensiti.md

symptom: Conditional Access geo-based block policy does not block sign-in from blacklisted country/IP. Attacker still accesses Exchange Online despite CA policy.
rootCause: Legacy/basic auth apps (e.g., iOS default Mail, AppId bfc44fc5-...) use OrgID traffic. CA is skipped for Exchange Server OrgID traffic. Only modern auth evaluated by CA.
solution: Mitigations: (1) app-based CA, (2) disable basic auth via Set-AuthenticationPolicy, (3) Set-OrganizationConfig -IPListBlocked, (4) Client Access Rules in EXO, (5) ADFS claim rules to block non-modern auth, (6) Set-SPOTenant -LegacyAuthProtocolsEnabled false.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Conditional access integration] Geo based conditi.md

symptom: ADFS Extranet Smart Lockout (ESL) cmdlets (Get-ADFSAccountActivity, Set-ADFSAccountActivity, Reset-ADFSAccountLockout) fail when executed remotely, even with domain admin credentials. Error: Only AD FS Service can access this endpoint. The client was authenticated as NT AUTHORITY ANONYMOUS LOGON. Event ID 1100 in ADFS Admin log.
rootCause: ESL REST endpoint requires ADFS local administrator privilege executed locally on the primary ADFS server. Remote PSH uses IWA auth then ESL cmdlets reconnect to the REST endpoint using IWA again (double-hop), causing authentication to fail. This is by design in ADFS delegation model.
solution: Use PowerShell Just Enough Administration (JEA) to delegate ESL commands to service desk workers: 1) Create JEA session configuration allowing only ESL cmdlets; 2) Register the JEA endpoint on the primary ADFS server; 3) Service desk connects via Enter-PSSession to the JEA endpoint.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - Using Powershell _Just Enough Administratio.md

symptom: ADFS federation service fails to start after replacing SSL certificate. ADFS Admin log shows error 356: Failed to register notification to the SQL database. ADFS 2.0/2.1 on Windows Server 2012 (without R2) with SQL configuration database.
rootCause: ADFS 2.0/2.1 has no Set-AdfsSslCertificate cmdlet; certificate replacement must be done manually via IIS console. Additionally SQL Service Broker may be disabled on the configuration database preventing ADFS from registering change notifications.
solution: 1) Use IIS Management Console to replace ADFS SSL cert (not PowerShell). 2) For PS commands on ADFS 2.0/2.1: Get-PSSnapin -Registered then Add-PSSnapin Microsoft.Adfs.PowerShell. 3) Check SQL Service Broker: SELECT is_broker_enabled FROM sys.databases WHERE name=AdfsConfiguration. 4) If disabled: ALTER DATABASE AdfsConfiguration SET ENABLE_BROKER WITH ROLLBACK IMMEDIATE.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] 119033019781878 ADFS fail to.md

symptom: Chrome and Firefox cannot access ADFS IDP-initiated page with connection reset error. IE works fine. Network trace shows Client Hello is immediately RST by ADFS server. SSL ETL shows: none of the cipher suites supported by the client application are supported by the server.
rootCause: A local Group Policy SSL Cipher Suite Order was configured on the ADFS server with a restrictive cipher suite list that excluded cipher suites supported by Chrome/Firefox. IE succeeded because it supports TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256 which was in the list.
solution: 1) On ADFS server open gpedit.msc - Computer Configuration - Administrative Templates - Network - SSL Configuration Settings. 2) Double-click SSL Cipher Suite Order and set to Disabled (or fix the order). 3) Run gpupdate /force. 4) Restart ADFS server.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] 119040822000085 ADFS 2016 浏览.md

symptom: Login failure in 2-farm ADFS scenario (account provider + resource provider, both ADFS 2016) when using IE11 on Win10/Server 2016 or Edge on Win10. Works in Chrome, Win7 IE11, InPrivate mode, or with Fiddler. Event 364: Token Binding claim in token must match the binding provided by the channel. Event 441: A token with a bad token binding key was found.
rootCause: Known bug in ADFS 2016 Token Binding feature. In 2-farm scenarios, the Token Binding ID from the SAML token does not match the Request Provided ID from the HTTP request. IE11/Edge on Win10 sends Sec-Token-Binding header which triggers the issue; Chrome and older browsers do not support Token Binding so they work fine.
solution: Disable Token Binding on ADFS 2016: Set-AdfsProperties -EnableTokenBinding $false, then restart ADFS service. PG confirmed this is a known issue and Token Binding feature is being deprecated.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] ADFS Token Binding issue -11.md

symptom: ADFS integration with RSA Authentication Agent (custom MFA provider) fails to start secondary auth process for iOS and Mac OS platform. Error: No style sheet is configured in the active theme for default locale [en-GB/2057]. Windows platform works fine.
rootCause: System locale mismatch between ADFS service account and the third-party MFA agent. ADFS service using EN-GB locale while RSA agent configured for EN-US only. iOS/Mac detect locale from server while Windows may handle it differently.
solution: Align system locale: 1) Verify ADFS service account. 2) Get SID: Get-ADUser -Identity service_account | select SID. 3) Configure the ADFS service account to use the same system locale as the RSA Authentication Agent by modifying user profile registry entries.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] System locale failed in inte.md

symptom: Chrome cannot access ADFS sign-in page from external network (via WAP/ADFS proxy). IE and other browsers work fine externally and internally. ADFS proxy system event log: A fatal alert was generated, TLS fatal error code 20. SSL ETL: SslDecryptPacket() FAILED: 0x8009002c.
