rootCause: After a Windows update to Windows Server 2012 R2, the ADFS proxy server negotiated a weak cipher suite (e.g. TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA) with Chrome. Chrome dropped support for 3DES ciphers causing TLS handshake failure.
solution: 1) Disable weak cipher suites (especially 3DES-based) on the ADFS proxy/WAP server. 2) Ensure strong cipher suites like TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 are enabled and prioritized. 3) Use IISCrypto tool or GPO to manage cipher suite order.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] User is unable to access ADF.md

symptom: Cross-forest users can sign in to ADFS with DomainBIOS\User but fail with UPN. Error: incorrect username and password. Environment: multiple AAD instances federated with single ADFS. ADFS debug log shows domain location failure; Netlogon log shows error code 1355.
rootCause: UPN suffix routing is not enabled for the external forest domains. Without UPN suffix routing the NetLogon service cannot locate the correct domain when cross-forest users authenticate with UPN format.
solution: Enable UPN suffix routing for external domains: 1) Open Active Directory Domains and Trusts. 2) Right-click the forest trust - Properties - Name Suffix Routing tab. 3) Enable routing for the UPN suffix of the external forest.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] [119042623000069]Cross Fores.md

symptom: Application requires HS256 (symmetric key) JWT token signing algorithm from ADFS 2016 but gets RS256 signed tokens instead. No documentation about supported signing algorithms.
rootCause: ADFS 2016 only supports RS256 (RSA-SHA256) for JWT token signing using the private key of the ADFS token signing certificate. HS256 (HMAC-SHA256 with symmetric key) is not supported. Confirmed by PG but not documented publicly.
solution: Redesign the application to accept RS256 signed JWT tokens from ADFS. If HS256 is absolutely required consider using a different IdP or adding a token transformation layer. No workaround on ADFS side.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case sharing] Supported JWT token signing.md

symptom: Third-party desktop app prompts for credentials when authenticating via ADFS SSO, while browser-based access works fine.
rootCause: ADFS ExtendedProtectionTokenCheck set to Required blocks desktop apps not using Schannel for SSL from WIA authentication.
solution: Set-ADFSProperties -ExtendedProtectionTokenCheck None. Alt: enable Form-based auth and modify app User Agent string.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Extended token protection] RP authentication fail.md

symptom: All O365 Basic Auth clients unable to access email. Modern Auth and OWA work fine. ADFS external auth fails on usernamemixed endpoint.
rootCause: ADFS Extranet Lockout depends on PDC. When PDC unavailable, lockout blocks all external Basic Auth. Debug logs show AccountLockoutPolicy errors.
solution: Disable Extranet Lockout: Set-AdfsProperties -EnableExtranetLockout $false. Fix PDC/Kerberos. Diagnostic: test IDP-initiated page internally vs externally.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Extranet lockout] Client unable to access emails.md

symptom: Cannot convert federated domain to managed after ADFS server uninstalled. Set-MsolADFSContext fails.
rootCause: ADFS server removed before domain conversion. Standard cmdlets require active ADFS.
solution: Use Set-MsolDomainAuthentication or Get-MsolUser | Convert-MsolFederatedUser directly without ADFS context. Passwords lost after conversion.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Fed config] Convert Federated Domain to Managed w.md

symptom: Cannot add child federated domain with New-MsolFederatedDomain when parent uses third-party STS (Shibboleth). Error asks for ADFS context.
rootCause: New-MsolFederatedDomain only works with AD FS as IDP. Fails for 3rd-party IdPs.
solution: Use New-MsolDomain -Name child.domain -Authentication Federated. For non-ADFS: use New-MsolDomain, Set-MsolDomainAuthentication, Set-MsolDomainFederationSettings.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Fed config] New-MsolFederatedDomain.md

symptom: Update-MsolFederatedDomain -SupportMultipleDomain fails. IssuerUri mismatch between ADFS service identifier and domain-based URI.
rootCause: First federated domain set up without -SupportMultipleDomain. HAADJ script with multipleVerifiedDomainNames=true adds claim rule changing issuerURI format.
solution: Delete claim rules, rerun with multipleVerifiedDomainNames=false. Or reconfigure with -SupportMultipleDomain. Check via Get-MsolDomainFederationSettings.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Fed config] Update-FederatedDomain report error.md

symptom: SAML auth to ADFS RP fails with Event ID 364/303: MSIS0038 SAML Message has wrong signature.
rootCause: Mismatch between signing cert in SAML request and cert in ADFS RP Trust config. RP vendor using wrong cert.
solution: Verify ADFS via IDP-initiated page. Capture Fiddler + Debug logs. Compare certs. RP vendor must fix signing cert.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[SAML fed] SAML wrong signature.md

symptom: ADFS Health Agent synthetic transactions fail with 503 on /adfs/probe. Test-AdfsServerHealth reports failure.
rootCause: /adfs/probe endpoint not listened. Known .NET Framework bug (fixed in 4.7).
solution: Restart AD FS service. Upgrade .NET to 4.7+. Use ETL trace script to diagnose.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Knowledge Sharing] Script to collect traces for A.md

symptom: SAML authentication to ADFS RP fails when POST binding is used and the SAML request does not contain a signing certificate. ADFS uses only the first certificate from the RP Trust signature certificate array (unsorted from DB).
rootCause: For POST binding, ADFS expects the signing certificate embedded in the SAML request. If missing, ADFS picks the first cert from the database (not sorted). If that cert does not match the one used by the RP to sign, validation fails.
solution: Ensure RP includes signing certificate in the SAML request body. For Redirect binding, ADFS scans all certs. For POST binding without embedded cert, verify the first cert in ADFS RP Trust matches the RP signing cert.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Quick Sharing] - How does ADFS select cert.md

symptom: adfshelp.microsoft.com GenerateClaims tool produces invalid RegEx for IssuerURI claim rule when multiple federated domains include subdomains registered before parent domain.
rootCause: Bug in adfshelp tool: generates RegEx with consecutive asterisks instead of pipe separator when complex subdomain hierarchy exists.
solution: Validate generated RegEx at regex101.com before deploying. Fix by replacing consecutive asterisks with pipe separator. Manually verify domain-to-IssuerURI mapping.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-Possible bug of https___adfshelp.microsoft.co.md

symptom: WAP client certificate authentication fails with CERT_E_UNTRUSTEDROOT (-2146762487) when SendTrustedIssuerList=0; or no client cert provided when SendTrustedIssuerList=1.
rootCause: Non-self-signed certificate (e.g., Intermediate CA) installed in Local Computer Trusted Root Certification Authorities store on WAP server corrupts the trusted issuer list.
solution: Remove/move non-self-signed certificates from Local Computer Trusted Root Certification Authorities store on WAP. Ref: KB2802568, KB2795828.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[ Sharing] [REG_115042312669490] Client certi.md

symptom: WAP configuration wizard fails with 401 Unauthorized. WAP proxy cannot download configuration from ADFS server.
rootCause: Exact IP:port SSL bindings on ADFS server take precedence over ADFS hostname:port binding. The IP binding uses default Trusted Root CA store (not AdfsTrustedDevices), so WAP self-signed client cert fails validation.
solution: Remove exact IP:port SSL bindings on ADFS/WAP servers. Add IP wildcard binding: netsh http add sslcert ipport=0.0.0.0:443 certhash=<hash> appid={5d89a20c-beab-4389-9447-324788eb944a} for non-SNI client support.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[ Sharing] [REG_115042312669490] Client certi.md

symptom: ADFS service fails to start after SSL certificate replacement. Event 133: certificate private key cannot be accessed. CNG key error.
rootCause: ADFS does not support CNG (Cryptographic Next Generation) keys. Certificate with ProviderType=0 and KeySpec=0 is CNG-keyed.
solution: Replace certificate with one NOT using CNG keys. Check with: certutil -v -store my <serial>. If ProviderType=0 and KeySpec=0 -> CNG keyed -> not supported by ADFS.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[Case Sharing] ADFS fail to start due to cert.md

symptom: ADFS service running but /adfs/services/trust/mex returns 503. Office client SSO fails. IDP-initiated sign-on works. Error: certificate not capable of key exchange.
rootCause: Certificate KeySpec=2 (AT_SIGNATURE) instead of AT_KEYEXCHANGE. Cannot be seen in certificate UI, only via certutil -v -store my.
solution: Delete certificate and reimport with: certutil -importpfx certfile.pfx AT_KEYEXCHANGE. No need for new certificate.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[Case Sharing] ADFS fail to start due to cert.md

symptom: ADFS alternate login ID with Employee Number fails. Set-AdfsClaimsProviderTrust succeeds but authentication with employee number (e.g., 5555) fails.
rootCause: Alternate login ID attribute must conform to UPN format (e.g., 5555@domain.com). Plain digits are not valid. Attribute must also be indexed, in Global Catalog, and GC-replicated.
solution: 1) Ensure AD attribute is GC-replicated (AD Schema snap-in, check Replicate this attribute to the Global Catalog). 2) Format employee number as UPN: 5555@domain.com. 3) Workaround: customize ADFS onload.js to auto-append domain suffix for non-UPN input.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[Case Sharing] Use Employee Number as alterna.md

symptom: One specific federated user cannot access SharePoint Online. SAML token IssuerURI contains trailing newline. Other O365 apps work for same user.
rootCause: User UPN in Active Directory stored in Base64-encoded format (ldifde export shows encoded value). The encoded UPN contains trailing newline character which gets embedded in SAML token IssuerURI.
solution: Revert user UPN in Active Directory from Base64-encoded format to normal plain text UPN. Verify via ldifde -f export.txt -r samaccountname=<user>.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[Case Sharing] _ #118100219133650 _ ADFS issu.md

symptom: ADFS Relying Party requires claim value (e.g., username) in all uppercase. No built-in ADFS function for string case conversion.
rootCause: ADFS claim language has no native toUpper/toLower function. Requires custom attribute store DLL.
solution: Deploy StringProcessingAttributeStore DLL to C:\windows\ADFS on all ADFS servers. Register as Custom Attribute Store. Use claim rule with query=toUpper. NOTE: input claim type must match previous rule output type; store name and query must match DLL code exactly (case-sensitive).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[Case Sharing] _ #118110519304247 _ Passing t.md

symptom: ADFS Windows Integrated Authentication (WIA) fails for workgroup (non-domain-joined) machines accessing O365 from intranet. ADFS attempts Kerberos auth which fails for workgroup machines.
rootCause: ADFS checks WIASupportedUserAgents property against the client user agent. If matched, ADFS attempts WIA (Kerberos). Workgroup machines cannot perform Kerberos auth, causing failure.
solution: Use Group Policy to set a specific custom user agent on domain-joined machines, then restrict ADFS WIASupportedUserAgents to only that custom agent. This way workgroup machines fall back to forms auth.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[case sharing]INTRANET SSO.md

symptom: User is prompted with login form instead of SSO in intranet when accessing O365 through federated ADFS. Expected behavior is Windows Integrated Authentication (silent SSO).
rootCause: The application sends prompt=login parameter to AAD. AAD translates this to wauth=urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.0:am:password and wfresh=0, which forces ADFS to use Forms Authentication instead of WIA.
solution: Set ADFS PromptLoginBehavior to Disabled so ADFS ignores the wauth/wfresh parameters translated from prompt=login. Also ensure: DNS A record for ADFS, correct SPN, and ADFS URL in IE Local Intranet Zone (https: not http:).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[case sharing]prompt=login parameter.md

symptom: ADFS configuration to authenticate users stored in AD LDS (LDAP directories) fails when using Get-Credential approach from official documentation.
rootCause: The official Microsoft documentation PowerShell example for creating LDAP server connection uses Get-Credential incorrectly for AD LDS scenarios. The doc approach does not work with AD LDS.
solution: Use PSCredential directly: $ldapuser='CN=admin,OU=...'; $ldappassword=ConvertTo-SecureString -String 'pwd' -AsPlainText -Force; $Credential=New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -ArgumentList $ldapuser,$ldappassword. Then use $Credential in New-AdfsLdapServerConnection.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[quick sharing]Configure AD FS to authenticat.md

symptom: ADFS LDAP query fails when UserContainer OU name contains a space (e.g. OU=User Container). ADFS URL-encodes the space as %20 which LDAP server cannot resolve.
rootCause: Known ADFS bug: ADFS incorrectly URL-encodes spaces in LDAP UserContainer parameter, translating spaces to %20 in the LDAP query, which the LDAP server cannot interpret.
solution: No workaround available. Known bug reported to ADFS product team. Avoid spaces in OU names if possible.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[quick sharing]Configure AD FS to authenticat.md

symptom: Update-MsolFederatedDomain fails with 'Invalid length for a Base-64 char array or string' when trying to update O365 RPT token-signing certificate. ADFS metadata is not publicly accessible.
rootCause: When ADFS metadata is not publicly accessible from the internet, the O365 RPT cannot auto-update the token-signing cert. The Update-MsolFederatedDomain cmdlet fails with base64 parsing errors.
solution: Use Set-MsolDomainFederationSettings as workaround: 1) Export token-signing cert from ADFS console in base64 format, 2) Read cert file, strip BEGIN/END headers and newlines, 3) Run Set-MsolDomainFederationSettings -DomainName 'domain' -SigningCertificate '$cert'. Can run from any PC.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[sharing] update token-signing cert for O365.md

symptom: ADFS Smart Lockout PowerShell cmdlets return 401 Unauthorized error on ADFS 4.0 (Windows Server 2016). ESL works but management cmdlets fail.
rootCause: Duplicate SPN registered on two different AD accounts. ADFS internal REST API calls /adfs/users/ endpoint. Kerberos auth fails due to duplicate SPN, falling back to NTLM which is denied.
solution: Check for duplicate SPNs using setspn -Q. Remove the duplicate SPN registration from the incorrect account. After removing duplicate, ADFS management cmdlets work correctly.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[sharing]Prem_LN_Windows Svr 2016 Standard_AD.md

symptom: New-MgDomainFederationConfiguration fails with 403 Forbidden (Authorization_RequestDenied) when using app-only auth with Domain.ReadWrite.All permission. Converting managed domain to federated fails.
rootCause: SetDomainAuthentication action requires Domain-InternalFederation.ReadWrite.All or IdentityProvider.ReadWrite.All, NOT Domain.ReadWrite.All. MS Learn doc incorrectly lists Domain.ReadWrite.All as sufficient.
solution: Add Domain-InternalFederation.ReadWrite.All (or IdentityProvider.ReadWrite.All) to the app registration. Verify via DSExplorer that the required permission maps to SetDomainAuthentication action. ICM: 670746109.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/Aode [ADFS]Domain.readWrite.All is not sufficient.md

symptom: WAP trust rebuild fails immediately with 'Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel' after sudden WAP service crash. All standard checks pass. Network trace shows successful TLS 1.2 handshake.
rootCause: SCHANNEL registry key DisableRenegoOnServer=1 on ADFS server. WAP trust uses client certificate auth performed during TLS renegotiation. Disabling renegotiation blocks WAP from authenticating to ADFS after trust establishment.
solution: Set HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\DisableRenegoOnServer to 0 or delete the key. Diagnose with SCHANNEL ETL trace: look for SEC_E_ILLEGAL_MESSAGE errors indicating blocked renegotiation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/WAP-[case sharing] AAD - Tricky WAP Trust issue.md

symptom: D365 Finance & Operations users must enter different account formats across multiple login pages (D365->AAD->ADFS->custom). UPN domain differs from email domain, federated domain is email domain.
rootCause: D365 only passes tenant info (not domain) to AAD. AAD needs domain to route to ADFS. UPN domain registered in international tenant but removed from 21V during CC B2B migration. Email domain used as federated domain. ADFS not configured with AlternateLoginID.
solution: WA1: Configure ADFS AlternateLoginID (Set-AdfsClaimsProviderTrust -AlternateLoginID mail -LookupForests domain). WA2: Extend SSO with KMSI persistent cookie. D365 token=1 day, AAD refresh token=14d/90d max.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[ADFS] D365 jump to AAD need to change login user.md

symptom: Mooncake myapps portal (myapps.windowsazure.cn) cannot launch SAML SSO applications. Error AADSTS50011: redirect URI mismatch for myapps application.
rootCause: Mooncake myapps portal does not support launching SAML SSO apps the same way as global AAD. The myapps app registration in Mooncake does not have correct redirect URIs.
solution: Forge SAML sign-in URL manually: 1) Capture SAMLRequest from global AAD test login via F12. 2) Decode at samltool.com/decode.php. 3) Modify AssertionConsumerServiceURL (=reply URL), Issuer (=Entity ID), IssueInstant. 4) Re-encode and URL-encode. 5) Construct: https://login.partner.microsoftonline.cn/{tenant}/saml2?SAMLRequest={encoded}. Reply URL in Mooncake: App registration > Authentication > Redirect URIs. Entity ID: Expose an API > Application ID URI.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SAML App SSO/How to verify the SAML app SSO.md

symptom: Graph API servicePrincipal AddKey operation fails silently. MSODS logs show no clear error. Requires proof of possession token.
rootCause: AddKey API requires proof of possession (PoP) token from existing valid cert on SP. If no existing cert or PoP token incorrectly generated, operation fails silently.
solution: 1) Generate PoP token per MS Learn doc. 2) Install NuGet packages, configure parameters. 3) Alternative: use PATCH with full keyCredentials/passwordCredentials instead of AddKey. 4) Debug via Kusto IfxUlsEvents table with SP objectId for internalCorrelationId.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SAML App SSO/Service Principal Signing Cert Configuration/Service Principal AddKey operation not working.md

symptom: Azure web app fails to get access token. AADSTS50194: Application not configured as multi-tenant. /common endpoint not supported for apps created after 10/15/2018.
rootCause: Single-tenant app created after 10/15/2018 cannot use /common endpoint. Microsoft enforced this restriction from that date. Older apps with same config work fine.
solution: Either: 1) Use tenant-specific endpoint (login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/...) instead of /common, or 2) Configure app as multi-tenant in App Registration > Authentication.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS - [Case Sharing]  Azure web app cannot get ac.md

symptom: AWS SAML SSO fails with invalid SAML response error on AWS side after setup. AAD shows no errors. Provisioning shows no export activity.
rootCause: User assigned Default Access role instead of provisioned AWS role. AWS SSO requires Role and RoleSessionName claims with actual AWS IAM roles. AWS user provisioning from AAD NOT supported - only role sync (AWS->AAD). AWS uses JIT provisioning for users.
solution: 1) Configure auto-provisioning to sync AWS roles to AAD (not users). 2) Assign users with actual AWS role (synced) instead of Default Access. 3) Ensure Role and RoleSessionName claims configured in SAML attributes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS - [Case Sharing] AWS SSO and Provisioning.md

symptom: Third-party SaaS apps appear in MyApps portal but not in Office 365 portal/waffle. Customized app icons show in MyApps but do not sync to Office 365 portal.
rootCause: Backend architectural difference between MyApps and Office 365 waffle app display systems. PG confirmed known backend issue.
solution: Workaround: Use MyApps portal (myapps.microsoft.com) to access apps. PG was working on rearchitecture for parity between MyApps and Office waffle.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS - [Case Sharing] Apps display difference betw.md

symptom: Password-based SSO browser extension cannot capture sign-in fields for non-gallery application. Login page loads but fields not detected.
rootCause: Sign-in page uses iframe to embed login form. Password SSO field capture only supports standard HTML pages, not iframes, Flash, or non-HTML technologies.
solution: Use direct sign-in URL (iframe src URL) that renders login form without iframe wrapper. Check with F12 if login page has iframe elements. Configure app with direct URL as sign-on URL.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS - [Case Sharing] Password Based SSO not captu.md

symptom: Picturepark SAML-based SSO integration fails. SSO does not work after following the official Microsoft tutorial for Picturepark SAML SSO configuration.
rootCause: Picturepark only supports WS-Federation protocol, not SAML. The official Microsoft documentation incorrectly provides SAML SSO configuration steps for Picturepark.
solution: Change the SAML endpoint to WS-Federation endpoint in the Azure AD enterprise application configuration. Configure Picturepark to use the WS-Fed endpoint URL instead of the SAML endpoint. All other configurations from the tutorial remain valid after the endpoint change.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application - 118120626003463 _ SSO - Picture.md

symptom: AAD enterprise application does not appear in My Apps Portal after assigning users. App registration uses wildcard reply URLs (e.g., http://localhost:3000/*, https://dev.example.com/*).
rootCause: By design. Wildcard reply URLs have limited support in Azure AD v1 and are not supported in v2. Apps with only wildcard reply URLs will not show in My Apps Portal. The new App Registration blade treats wildcards as illegal.
solution: Remove wildcard reply URLs and replace with exact redirect URIs. Wildcards increase security surface area (XSS, referrer header leaks). In the new App Registration blade, wildcards cannot be saved.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application - A quick sharing on If the AAD A.md

symptom: Customer wants to use dynamic parameters in redirect URI (e.g., https://xxx.com?aaa={a}&bbb={b}). Wildcard redirect URIs with query parameters do not work as expected.
rootCause: Azure AD redirect URI wildcard matching has limitations: wildcards (*) can match path characters but cannot contain ? or & characters. Supported wildcard formats are: https://*.domain.com and https://www.domain.com/* only.
solution: Use supported wildcard formats only: https://*.mstest.com or https://www.mstest.com/*. For dynamic parameters, handle them in the application code after receiving the redirect. To add wildcard redirect URIs, use the application manifest editor. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/reply-url#restrictions-on-wildcards-in-redirect-uris
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application - [Case Sharing]  redirect url 里是.md

symptom: User cannot add an enterprise application in AAD portal. Error from AAD Graph: AADGraphException. New application button appears greyed out.
rootCause: The user account does not have the required directory role (Global Administrator, Application Administrator, or Cloud Application Administrator). The portal overview links to Enterprise Applications directly, making users think they can add apps, but the button is greyed out without proper roles.
solution: Assign the appropriate directory role (Global Admin, Application Admin, or Cloud Application Admin) to the user account. The issue resolves immediately after role assignment. Tip: always do a remote session to verify UI state rather than relying solely on logs.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application - [Case Sharing] Cannot add an en.md

symptom: Need to allow a specific user to grant admin consent only for a specific app (App B) to access a specific API (App A exposed delegation permission), without granting Cloud App Admin role.
rootCause: By default, granting admin consent requires at least Cloud App Admin role, which is too broad. Azure AD supports custom app consent policies to scope admin consent to specific app-to-app combinations.
solution: Create custom app consent policy: 1) New-MgPolicyPermissionGrantPolicy with specific client/resource app IDs. 2) New-MgPolicyPermissionGrantPolicyInclude for conditions. 3) Create custom Entra ID role with microsoft.directory/servicePrincipals/managePermissionGrantsForAll.{policyId}. 4) Assign role to user, wait ~15 min. For 21V: Connect-MgGraph -Environment China.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application - [Case Sharing] app consent poli.md

symptom: Enterprise application SSO blade is not available/not visible in Azure portal. App Type shows N/A in ASC. Fiddler shows currentSingleSignOnMode is none.
rootCause: By design. When an application is created through App Registration (not from gallery), the SSO blade is not available in Enterprise Applications. App Type = N/A instead of SAML App (FederatedSsoIdp).
solution: Configure SSO through the Enterprise Applications blade directly (not App Registration). Add the application from gallery or manually configure SAML/WS-Fed SSO settings. Verify in ASC that App Type shows FederatedSsoIdp after configuration.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application - [Quick Sharing] Unable to see E.md

symptom: Internal Microsoft colleagues request to update application ownership on microsoft.onmicrosoft.com tenant for signing key rollover. Support team does not have permission.
rootCause: Applications on the Microsoft corporate tenant require internal processes for ownership changes. Regular support engineers do not have permissions to modify these apps.
solution: Refer requestor to: 1) Open support incident via http://aka.ms/filloutaform, select Application & Service > AAD Identity and Access, include App ID and display name. 2) If slow, escalate via ICM to Identity SRE / corp tenant(Azure AD) team, usually resolved within 1 day.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application - [Quick Sharing] update the owne.md

symptom: SAML SSO fails with AADSTS75011: Authentication method WindowsIntegrated does not match requested authentication method Password, ProtectedTransport.
rootCause: SaaS app SAML request has RequestedAuthnContext Comparison="exact" requesting PasswordProtectedTransport (forms-based auth). Internal users authenticate via Windows Integrated Authentication (WIA), causing exact comparison to fail.
solution: Contact SaaS app vendor to: 1) Change RequestedAuthnContext Comparison from "exact" to less restrictive value, or 2) Allow other authentication methods in SAML request besides PasswordProtectedTransport. Verify by checking SAMLRequest in Fiddler for RequestedAuthnContext element.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Access m.md

symptom: Azure AD to SaaS app (e.g., Salesforce) provisioning syncs unexpected/incorrect timezone for user accounts.
rootCause: Azure AD user accounts do not have a timezone attribute. The attribute mapping for TimeZoneSidKey is missing or incorrectly configured in enterprise application provisioning settings.
solution: Edit or create attribute mapping: Enterprise app > Provisioning > Mappings. For TimeZoneSidKey: Mapping type=None, Default value=desired timezone (e.g., Australia/Sydney), Target attribute=TimeZoneSidKey, Apply=Always. If not listed, click Add New Mapping.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Applicat.md

symptom: Google App provisioning configuration fails to save with error: Size of input ExternalSecret data is invalid. Credentials could not be saved due to internal storage issue.
rootCause: Combined configuration data (credentials + settings like notification email) exceeds Azure AD internal storage limit for ExternalSecret.
solution: Two workarounds: 1) Use two separate gallery app instances - one for SSO, one for provisioning, to reduce per-app storage. 2) Remove optional fields like Notification Email from provisioning page to reduce data size.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Google A.md

symptom: AADSTS65005: Invalid resource error when accessing SaaS application via SAML SSO. Client requested access to resource not listed in requested permissions.
rootCause: The Identifier (Entity ID) URL was not configured in the Enterprise Application SSO advanced URL settings. The SAML request Issuer from application side did not match any configured identifier.
solution: Go to Enterprise Application SSO configuration page > Advanced URL settings, manually enter the correct Identifier URL matching the SAML request Issuer. Use Fiddler to capture SAML request and verify the Issuer value.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Invalid.md

symptom: Enterprise application SAML SSO failure with redirect loop. User sign-in redirects cyclically between two URLs.
rootCause: Duplicate application registrations in the directory. The configured Enterprise Application was not the correct one with the proper attribute mapping. Identifier URL mismatch between duplicate apps.
solution: 1) Check App Registrations for duplicate apps with same name. 2) Delete duplicate registrations. 3) Verify the correct app has proper identifier and attribute configuration. 4) For multi-tenant apps that cannot be deleted, set availableToOtherTenants=false in Manifest first.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Single s.md

symptom: AADSTS65001: User or administrator has not consented to use the application. Web app fails to get access token silently after deployment.
rootCause: Admin consent was not granted for the app. Silent token acquisition (no user interaction) cannot prompt interactive consent. Consent status can be verified via MSODS Explorer delegation entries.
solution: Grant admin consent via Azure Portal Grant Permissions button. Verify consent with MSODS Explorer delegation entries (Source=app SP, Target=resource SP). Use eSTS Logs Viewer/LogsMiner to confirm DelegationDoesNotExist error stopped. Track who granted consent via Jarvis MSODS activity logs using Service Principal Object ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Tracking.md

symptom: SaaS application (Replicon) SAML SSO fails with Azure AD. Error shows permission issue from a different/unknown tenant.
rootCause: The Identifier URL in SAML request was missing a path segment (/saml2/). Mismatch between app-configured identifier and Azure AD Enterprise App identifier caused authentication to hit wrong tenant.
solution: Capture Fiddler trace of SAML SSO flow. Compare SAML request identifier with Enterprise App SSO configuration. Fix the identifier URL to include the missing path segment. Use Test SAML SSO feature in Azure Portal to validate.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS- Replicon.md

symptom: Optional claims customization not taking effect or being overwritten. Claims configured in Azure Portal SSO page show warning about being overridden by claims mapping policy.
rootCause: Azure AD has 3 hierarchical levels for claims customization: Level 1 (App Manifest optional claims) < Level 2 (Enterprise App SSO portal config/ClaimIssuancePolicy) < Level 3 (Claims Mapping Policy via Graph/PowerShell). Higher levels override lower ones. IncludeBasicClaimSet=false in ClaimIssuancePolicy removes basic claims unless individually added.
solution: Check all 3 levels: 1) App Registration > Token configuration (manifest). 2) Enterprise App > SSO > Attributes & Claims (backed by ClaimIssuancePolicy). 3) Claims Mapping Policy via PowerShell Get-AzureADPolicy. If Level 3 policy exists, portal config (Level 2) is ignored. Remove claims mapping policy or configure all claims at same level.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Training Material/Optional Claims.md

symptom: AADSTS90033: A transient error has occurred when performing admin consent to web app. Error persists for days despite retries.
rootCause: Application has too many API permissions configured (25+). Directory Proxy (DPX) has a 5-second timeout threshold. EvoSTS queries each API permission in directory; combined lookup time exceeds timeout when app objects are remote to request landing site.
solution: Reduce the number of API permissions per consent request or use Dynamic consent to request permissions incrementally. If issue persists, escalate to EvoSTS/DPX team via ICM. DPX timeout is a known architectural limitation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/[Quick Sharing]  Unable to admin consent to web ap.md

symptom: ADFS Extranet Lockout feature does not work for user accounts from a trusted (resource) domain. Only accounts from the ADFS farm's own domain are soft-locked.
rootCause: Extranet Lockout requires PDC of the user's domain to be reachable. Cross-domain scenarios fail when PDC is unavailable or DNS resolution between domains is broken. The feature only protects against brute-force via WAP (extranet); it does not cover intranet auth.
solution: 1) Ensure PDC of each trusted domain is reachable from ADFS servers. 2) Check ADFS Audit log event IDs: 512 (ExtranetLockoutAccountThrottledAudit), 515 (ExtranetLockoutUserThrottleTransitionAudit), 516 (ExtranetLockoutAccountRestrictedAudit). 3) Verify settings: ExtranetObservationWindow > AD 'Reset Account Lockout Counter After'; ExtranetLockoutThreshold < AD 'Account Lockout Threshold'.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Feature_specific configuration/Extranet lockout.md

symptom: Guest user cross-tenant SSO shows GUID as NameID instead of email address. NameID format is persistent with random GUID.
rootCause: Claims mapping policies do not apply to guest users (B2B). For B2B users, mapping NameID and UPN cross-tenant is prevented for security reasons. The mail claim is on the NameID cross-tenant block list.
solution: Cannot use mail as NameID for guest users via claims mapping. Change the application to SP-initiated and configure NameID format request in SAML request as emailAddress format.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/application token-[case sharing] Cross-tenant acce.md

symptom: Refresh token renewed despite MaxInactiveTime exceeded and Outlook was idle/closed longer than configured.
rootCause: Outlook requests tokens for multiple SPs (Exchange + OfficeClientService). Token Lifetime Policy only applied to Exchange SP. Relaunching Outlook first requests token for OfficeClientService (no policy), renewing the refresh token.
solution: Apply Token Lifetime Policy to ALL service principals the client may request tokens for. Use Jarvis/ESTS ITData logs to identify which ResourceIDs are accessed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/refresh token-[case sharing]   get details of refr.md

symptom: Az CLI login fails with AADSTS53003 blocked by CA even though browser auth succeeds from same machine.
rootCause: Browser uses PAC-file proxy (corporate IP in CA trusted locations), but Az CLI does not inherit proxy. Az CLI sends token redemption directly without proxy, using different IP not in trusted locations.
solution: Configure proxy env vars before az login: HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY. Also check WinHTTP proxy (netsh winhttp show proxy).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Conditional Access/Case study/Az CLI received AADSTS53003_ Access has been block.md

symptom: AADSTS50076 MFA required unexpectedly when accessing PIM in Azure portal. Blue screen session expired targeting Microsoft Graph.
rootCause: PIM uses .default scope evaluating all pre-authorized scopes. Some scopes (RoleManagement.Read.Directory, Group.Read.All) are unmapped in ESTS. Unmapped scopes default CA evaluation to EXO/SPO. CA policy requiring MFA for SPO triggers for PIM.
solution: Expected behavior per CA PG. Workaround: exclude PIM users from SPO MFA CA policy, or escalate to PIM/Portal PG to handle MFA challenge.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Conditional Access/Case study/CA evaluation on EXO and SPO caused by unmapped sc.md

symptom: ADFS token signing certificate recently renewed but relying party (AAD or app) still has old cert public key. SAML/WS-Fed signature verification fails. SSO breaks for federated users.
rootCause: ADFS signs tokens with new token signing cert private key after auto-renewal, but relying party (AAD, or another SP) hasn't fetched the updated metadata containing the new cert public key. Signature verification fails.
solution: 1) Update relying party with new cert: for AAD run Update-MsolFederatedDomain; for other SPs update their metadata endpoint or manually import the new cert. 2) Check ADFS metadata endpoint is accessible: https://<adfs>/FederationMetadata/2007-06/FederationMetadata.xml. 3) Consider disabling AutoCertificateRollover if causing recurrent issues.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Log collection_tools/Start with ADFS troubleshooting.md

symptom: Customer asks about AAD optional claims enfpolids and in_corp — unclear what they represent and how they work.
rootCause: enfpolids = Object ID of Conditional Access policy hit during sign-in, mainly for MS internal services, not useful for external app developers. in_corp = signal indicating if client is logging in from corporate network, determined by MFA trusted IPs list and Named Locations marked as trusted, NOT by ADFS/customer internal network definition.
solution: Explain enfpolids is CA policy object ID (useless for non-MS apps, no Graph/PowerShell query available). Explain in_corp is based on MFA trusted IPs and Named Locations, not ADFS network boundary. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/active-directory-optional-claims
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/AAD Optional Claims - [quick Sharing] Question abo.md

symptom: Auto-provisioning (SCIM) for non-gallery app fails with SystemForCrossDomainIdentityManagementCredentialValidationFailure. Test Connection intermittently returns 'Invalid credentials' or succeeds.
rootCause: The SCIM endpoint URL provided by the app vendor has an invalid/mismatched SSL certificate (e.g., cert for *.lastpass.com but URL is different subdomain). Azure AD SCIM provisioning agent rejects untrusted remote certificate during TLS handshake. Jarvis log shows: 'The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure'.
solution: Use the correct SCIM endpoint URL that has a valid SSL certificate. For LastPass example: change from old URL format to https://lastpass.com/scimapi/{accountId}. Verify the URL's SSL cert in browser before configuring. Restart full sync after URL change.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case sharing  _ Auto Provisioning.md

symptom: Need to export expiration dates of all Service Principal and App Registration secrets/certificates in a 21V (Mooncake) Entra tenant to audit or plan rotation.
rootCause: No built-in portal feature to bulk-view all SP/App credential expiry dates. Manual checking is impractical for large tenants.
solution: Use official PowerShell script from Azure China docs: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/scripts/powershell-export-apps-with-expiring-secrets — dedicated for 21V, no modification needed. Alternative: GitHub gist https://gist.github.com/torumakabe/779490238f238d0bece355daddb37b05
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/Export expiration date of SP_APP.md

symptom: Outlook Report Phishing add-in fails on VDI (VMware) with CAE error: InteractionRequired / LocationConditionEvaluationSatisfied when accessing microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn
rootCause: VDI routes login and Graph traffic through different outbound IPs (firewall vs Zscaler). CAE detects IP change mid-session: token issued from trusted IP but Graph call from untrusted IP.
solution: Option 1: Add VDI outbound IP (Zscaler) to CA trusted IP-based Named Location. Option 2: Fix VDI network so both endpoints use same outbound IP. Debug: decode access token at jwt.ms, base64-decode claims in 401 for xms_rp_ipaddr.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Conditional Access/Case study/[CAE]  Continuous access evaluation resulted in ch.md

symptom: RDP to AADJ VM fails with internal error when using FQDN for Entra ID web account auth. Succeeds with NetBIOS name or IP.
rootCause: Custom domain FQDN suffix missing ICP record in 21Vianet. Azure China blocks connections to domains without ICP. TCP RST sent by 21v-bluecloud.
solution: Option 1: Complete ICP registration at support.azure.cn. Option 2: Change VM FQDN suffix to domain with ICP record. Workaround: Use NetBIOS name or IP for RDP.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/2506120050003842 - Fail to RDP with AAD Account.md

symptom: On AADJ machine, users can still login with old password after password change. CachedLogonsCount=0 does not prevent cached login.
rootCause: By design. AADJ uses cloudAP cache separate from on-prem lsass. CachedLogonsCount=0 only affects on-prem. PG aware, no fix planned.
solution: Option 1: Login with new password to update cloudAP cache. Option 2: Delete CloudAPCache folder. Find alias via HKLM IdentityStore LogonCache Alias2Name.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/Cached logon in AAD joined machine.md

symptom: On HAADJ device (Mooncake), Office client shows cached credentials expired. Fix Me wizard loops. WAM renews PRT/RT but Office uses old expired RT from wrong cache entry.
rootCause: Bug in Win10 2004. WAM writes renewed RT to new cache entry but Office reads from old cache. Authority URL mismatch causes wrong cache lookup. Fixed in Outlook, not SharePoint.
solution: Outlook: Update to latest. OneDrive/SharePoint: PG fix pending. Workaround: WAM diagnostics (aka.ms/wamhot) to clear token cache.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/SSO - Office client still used expired RT though W.md

symptom: HAADJ device cannot get PRT after OS reimage. Device registration fails with error: The verification of the signature failed. PRT not obtainable until AAD Connect syncs the new UserCertificate.
rootCause: After OS reimage, the device generates a new self-signed UserCertificate, but AAD still has the old certificate. Device registration authentication fails because the certificate mismatch. AAD Connect default sync interval is 30 minutes, and registration task is only triggered by user logon or event 4096.
solution: Wait ~30 minutes for AAD Connect to sync the new UserCertificate to AAD, then re-login to the device to trigger device registration task again. After successful device registration, re-login once more to obtain PRT. If AAD Connect Wizard is open in debug mode, sync will be paused - close it first.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/Unable to get PRT after OS reimage.md

symptom: After running Convert-MsolDomainToFederated for Mooncake domain, the ADFS relying party configuration for global AAD gets overwritten by Mooncake configuration. Global AAD federation breaks.
rootCause: Old version of MSOnline PowerShell module cannot distinguish public AAD from Mooncake AAD. It searches relying party by name "Microsoft Office 365 Identity Platform" and overwrites the entire configuration. Latest module uses distinct names: "...Worldwide" for public and "...China" for Mooncake.
solution: Always update MSOnline module to latest before running federation commands: Install-Module MSOnline -Force. The latest module creates separate relying party trusts for public ("Worldwide") and Mooncake ("China") AAD, preventing overwrite.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Case study/2111220040001272 Out-of-dated MSOnline module caus.md

symptom: In dual-federation environment with same UPN in both public and 21v clouds, iOS users get "Org Data Removal" prompt in Outlook/PowerApps, or auth fails when mis-selecting wrong cloud credential in Authenticator. Affects all Microsoft SaaS apps on the device.
rootCause: Intune SDK for iOS changed device primary user determination from UPN to OID (since 2022/05/25). In dual-fed environments with same UPN across clouds, the SDK cannot correctly distinguish users, causing MAM policy conflicts and data removal prompts.
solution: Short-term: (1) Use Edge web apps instead of native PowerApps app. (2) Use Teams add-in apps for PowerApps. (3) PG publishes private version under different publisher. Long-term: (1) Use cross-cloud B2B (different UPN for 21v). (2) Change UPN in one cloud by modifying ADFS config + AAD Connect sync rules + on-prem AD attributes. (3) Wait for Intune MAM "Multiple Managed Account" feature.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Case study/Dual-fed with Intune compatibility.md

symptom: AAD issues access token for application whose Service Principal was deleted. Token contains appid but missing oid/puid/altsecid claims. ARM returns InvalidAuthenticationToken error, AAD Graph returns Authorization_IdentityNotFound.
rootCause: By design: AAD can issue client_credentials tokens (ClientOnlyJsonWebToken) for multi-tenant applications even when the service principal does not exist in the tenant. The token is issued based on the application registration in the home tenant, not the local SP.
solution: Recreate the service principal in the tenant: New-AzureADServicePrincipal -AppId <appId>. This is by design per Microsoft documentation on service principal provisioning. Without SP, the token will lack oid claim and be rejected by resource APIs like ARM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/Service Principal got deleted but AAD still issues.md

symptom: Numerous AAD audit log failures for 'adding service principal' triggered by 'Windows Azure Service Management API'. Error: appId does not reference a valid application object. Occurs when registering/unregistering resource providers.
rootCause: ARM automatically attempts to create service principals for resource provider apps when processing subscription operations. If the first-party app (e.g., China CDN Resource Provider, Azure Machine Learning Singularity) has not been deployed to Mooncake, the SP creation fails because the application object doesn't exist in the Mooncake directory.
solution: This is expected behavior and can be safely ignored. The audit log failures indicate first-party apps not yet available in Mooncake. No customer action needed. ARM will continue to retry periodically but these failures do not affect other operations.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/[First Party App] Numerous failure of adding servi.md

symptom: On-premise data gateway login fails because Power BI service application SP gets automatically disabled. SP must be manually re-enabled before each sign-in.
rootCause: Power BI service SP has ServicePrincipalLifecyclePolicy set to SubscriptionManaged(1). Since the tenant has no Power BI license/subscription, the ordering management system periodically disables the SP.
solution: Manually re-enable the SP before each use. PG acknowledges this is a design issue and improvement is on their roadmap. No ETA available. Long-term: customer should acquire PBI license if using data gateway.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/[First Party App] application “Power BI service”.md

symptom: AADSTS500014: Service principal for resource is disabled. Affects multiple services: Power BI Desktop login, Logic App on-premise data gateway, AIP/MIP service. SP gets disabled unexpectedly and periodically without admin action.
rootCause: First-party apps with ServicePrincipalLifecyclePolicy=SubscriptionManaged(1) are managed by the ordering management system. It periodically checks if the tenant has a paid subscription associated with the service. If no valid subscription found, the SP is disabled. E.g., PBI requires PBI Pro/Premium license, AIP requires EMS/AIP license, MFA requires MFA/AAD Premium license.
solution: 1. Verify customer has valid paid subscription at portal.partner.microsoftonline.cn. 2. Check first-party portal for ServicePrincipalLifecyclePolicy setting. 3. Workaround: Schedule PowerShell task every 30 min to re-enable SP if disabled (Get-MsolServicePrincipal + Set-MsolServicePrincipal -AccountEnabled $true). 4. For AIP Sync service in Mooncake, known bug that it's not bound to any SKU (ICM-281185138).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/[TSG] Service principal got disabled unexpectedly.md

symptom: Microsoft Authenticator app cannot handle two accounts with same UPN from different cloud instances (e.g. public Azure and Mooncake). Users cannot register both accounts in one Authenticator app.
rootCause: Authenticator app identifies accounts by UPN and cannot distinguish multiple accounts with the same UPN across different Azure cloud instances.
solution: Register one of the accounts in a different authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator). The OTP/TOTP method uses open standard OATH algorithm compatible across authenticator apps.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Technical details/Limitation.md

symptom: PowerApps mobile app fails to authenticate Mooncake account in dual-federation environment when OneAuth is enabled for public tenant.
rootCause: OneAuth feature bug: when enabled for public tenant, PowerApps mobile uses OneAuth instead of Authenticator. OneAuth cannot distinguish dual-fed accounts with same UPN.
solution: (1) Select Use legacy sign-in in Power Apps mobile login UI. (2) Engage PowerApps PG to disable OneAuth feature flag on the tenant. (3) Users must re-install PowerApps mobile app after PG change.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Technical details/Limitation.md

symptom: Custom domain unexpectedly appears in public Azure AD due to viral tenant created by Power BI trial sign-up. Causes dual-federation issues in Mooncake.
rootCause: When a user signs up for PBI trial using corporate email, Azure AD auto-creates a viral tenant in public Azure with that domain, causing it to exist in both clouds unintentionally.
solution: (1) Customer performs admin takeover of viral tenant per MS docs. (2) File ICM (template: m2Q2t1) to block domain adoption BEFORE deleting the domain. (3) Contact Peter Shi / nagendran.sankaralingam to add to adoption blocklist. (4) Verify by testing PBI sign-up - should show personal email.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Viral tenant take over and restriction.md

symptom: NPS MFA extension auth fails. Multiple certificates exist locally from repeated configuration script runs without proper parameter changes.
rootCause: First run generated cert locally but failed to upload to AAD. Subsequent runs created additional certs without cleaning old ones, causing cert confusion.
solution: Delete excess local certs, ensure only one valid cert matching AAD. Verify AAD cert via Connect-MgGraph and Get-MgServicePrincipal for appid 981f26a1-7f43-403b-a875-f8b09b8cd720.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/MFA On-prem (not MFA server)/MFA extension with NPS server/2601260040000488 Case Study - NPS MFA.md

symptom: NPS MFA extension fails to find user in AAD. On-prem AD UPN format differs from AAD UPN.
rootCause: NPS extension defaults to on-premises AD UPN for AAD user lookup. When on-prem UPN (e.g. user@domain.local) differs from AAD UPN, lookup fails.
solution: Configure registry LDAP_ALTERNATE_LOGINID_ATTRIBUTE to specify AD attribute matching AAD UPN (typically mail). Restart NPS service. Ref: howto-mfa-nps-extension-advanced.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/MFA On-prem (not MFA server)/MFA extension with NPS server/2601260040000488 Case Study - NPS MFA.md

symptom: NPS MFA triggers SMS verification but VPN client cannot input verification code. NPS log: Challenge requested in Authentication Ext for User.
rootCause: Azure MFA defaults to SMS. VPN clients using RADIUS cannot handle challenge-response for SMS/OTP input. After number matching update, default behavior changed.
solution: (1) Change user default MFA method to Authenticator push in AAD portal. (2) Configure NPS registry OVERRIDE_NUMBER_MATCHING_WITH_OTP=FALSE for legacy Approve/Deny push. (3) Restart NPS service.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/MFA On-prem (not MFA server)/MFA extension with NPS server/2601260040000488 Case Study - NPS MFA.md

symptom: NPS primary auth fails before MFA extension is invoked. Event 6273 in Security log. VPN client cannot connect despite NPS MFA extension installed.
rootCause: VPN client auth method does not match NPS network policy auth method. Mismatch causes NPS layer-1 primary auth failure before MFA extension (layer-2) is reached.
solution: (1) Enable NPS audit: auditpol /set /subcategory:Network Policy Server /success:enable /failure:enable. (2) Check event 6273 for details. (3) Temporarily remove MFA DLL registry (AuthorizationDLLs/ExtensionDLLs under authsrv\parameters) to isolate. (4) Configure matching auth (e.g. PEAP-MSCHAPv2) on both NPS and VPN client.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/MFA On-prem (not MFA server)/MFA extension with NPS server/2601260040000488 Case Study - NPS MFA.md

symptom: Enabling Certificate-Based Authentication (CBA) in Azure China (21Vianet) fails when using Microsoft Graph v1.0 API endpoint. No UI available for CBA configuration in Mooncake portal.
rootCause: 21Vianet Entra ID CBA configuration requires the beta API endpoint (microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn/beta/), not the standard v1.0 endpoint. The CBA management UI is not yet available in Mooncake environment.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph beta API with PowerShell to enable CBA: Connect-MgGraph -Environment China, then Update-MgBetaPolicyAuthenticationMethodPolicyAuthenticationMethodConfiguration with X509Certificate config. Set authenticationModeConfiguration to x509CertificateMultiFactor for MFA-equivalent CBA. Upload root/intermediate CA certs to PKI Trust Store via Graph API.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Authentication methods/Certificate based auth.md

symptom: Bulk enable MFA via MFA portal for more than 50 users partially fails — some users remain unenabled but the portal reports all operations successful.
rootCause: MFA portal bulk enable operation has a known limitation when processing large batches (>50 users), silently failing to enable MFA for some users without reporting errors.
solution: Use PowerShell script with Set-MsolUser -StrongAuthenticationRequirements to bulk enable MFA. After execution, verify each user's status with Get-MsolUser checking StrongAuthenticationRequirements.State. Export results to CSV and retry users with empty State values.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Case study/MFA-[case sharing]  bulk MFA enable via PowerShell.md

symptom: Unable to configure 'Verification options' under Service settings of per-user MFA in Azure portal. The settings page fails to save changes.
rootCause: The rememberMfaOnTrustedDevice.allowedNumberOfDays field has an invalid value (0 or null) in the mfaServicePolicy, preventing the verification options configuration from being saved.
solution: Submit a PATCH request to the mfaServicePolicy endpoint with rememberMfaOnTrustedDevice.allowedNumberOfDays set to a value between 1 and 90. Use F12/Insomnia to send the PATCH with the full policy body including mfaPolicySettings and trustedIps.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Case study/[AAD] Unable to configure _Verification options_.md

symptom: Customer concerned that MFA phone calls/SMS for 21Vianet (Mooncake) M365 services originate from international numbers outside China mainland, raising data compliance and PIPL cross-border transfer concerns.
rootCause: 21Vianet uses centralized global telecom services to deliver MFA SMS/voice calls. End users' phone numbers are transferred to global telecom service providers outside mainland China during MFA authentication.
solution: Share CELA official response: 21Vianet has obtained prior authorization under customer agreement DPA for cross-border transfers. The DPA incorporates PIPL Article 21 entrusted processing requirements. 21Vianet commits to conducting personal information protection impact assessments and adopting appropriate transfer mechanisms. No current non-compliance under effective PRC law. If PRC law changes, 21Vianet will revisit and take measures. Mark communication as internal-only reference.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Mooncake MFA with international caller ID.md

symptom: Cannot query MFA logs from Jarvis SASPerRequestEvent / SASCommonEvent tables for Mooncake. Data not available in these Jarvis tables since Dec 2025.
rootCause: Jarvis SAS tables (SASPerRequestEvent, SASCommonEvent) were deprecated for MFA log queries in Mooncake as of Dec 2025.
solution: Use Kusto queries instead: cluster('idsharedmcsha.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn').database('idmfacne'). SAS logs: AllSASCommonEvents table (filter by env_time, userObjectId/TrackingID). CAPP logs: AllCappLogEvents table (filter by env_time, userObjectId, smsMessageId). TrackingID = ESTS correlation ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Kusto.md

symptom: Customer asks about MFA enforcement timeline for Azure admin portals in Mooncake (21Vianet).
rootCause: Microsoft rolling out mandatory MFA for all Azure users globally. Mooncake enforcement date not yet determined as of Aug 2024.
solution: No Mooncake enforcement date set yet. Customer can proactively prepare by enabling MFA now. Refer to MS blog and MFA FAQ. Internal wiki: Azure Portal MFA Enforcement (AzureAD wiki pageid 1509472).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Service update/MFA Enforcement on Admin portals.md

symptom: 21Vianet Teams users prompted for MFA registration endlessly. No CA/per-user MFA/security defaults configured. Other apps (OWA) work fine.
rootCause: China network law requires real-name auth for Teams in 21Vianet. Enforced by Identity Protection, cannot be disabled via AAD MFA/security defaults/CA policy.
solution: 1) Confirm MFA works for other apps (OWA). 2) Check ASC if user has phone auth method registered. 3) Register phone at https://mysignins.windowsazure.cn/security-info. 4) Admin check per-user MFA config to ensure phone method allowed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Service update/MFA limitation with 21v Teams.md

symptom: 21v Teams login fails when both Call and Text MFA methods disabled in per-user MFA config. Admin disabled phone-based MFA but Teams requires them.
rootCause: Teams 21v requires Call or Text MFA enabled for real-name verification. Disabling both blocks auth flow required by China regulatory law. Confirmed by Teams PG.
solution: Admin must enable at least one phone-based auth method (Call or Text) in per-user MFA service config. This is a government regulatory requirement.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Service update/Teams 实名认证.md

symptom: WHfB PIN prompt keeps appearing at sign-in despite WHfB disabled in both GPO and Intune MDM. Registry PassportForWork and AllowSignInOptions set to 0.
rootCause: WHfB PIN prompt triggered by SCCM Windows Hello for Business settings, which override GPO and Intune.
solution: Disable WHfB from SCCM and deploy changes to all users/devices. Check SCCM WHfB settings in addition to GPO and Intune.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Windows Hello for business/Case study/Disable Windows Hello for Business PIN prompt.md

symptom: AADJ device cannot SSO to on-premise share folder with WHFB PIN. Works with username+password. Error: cannot find DC or domain not available.
rootCause: WHFB key trust or certificate trust not configured for hybrid deployment. SSO with PIN requires: WS2016+ DC, Kerberos certs on DCs, PKI with HTTP CRL, root cert on devices.
solution: Configure WHFB key trust or cert trust. Requirements: 1) WS2016+ DC, 2) Kerberos certs with HTTP CRL on DCs, 3) Root cert deployed to devices. Verify with netmon trace.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Windows Hello for business/Case study/Not able to SSO to on-premise Share folder from AA.md

symptom: Kerberos auth fails with KDC_ERR_CLIENT_NAME_MISMATCH when AADJ user accesses on-prem share with WHFB PIN. KDC ETL shows user not found, LDAP query on msDS-KeyCredentialLink returns empty.
rootCause: msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute empty in on-prem AD. Should be written back by AAD Connect. Common causes: Connect account lacks write permission or sync rule issue.
solution: 1) Verify msDS-KeyCredentialLink exists for user in on-prem AD. 2) If empty, grant Connect account permission: add to Key Admins group or run dsacls. 3) Check AADConnect sync rules and operations.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Windows Hello for business/Case study/Not able to SSO to on-premise Share folder from AA.md

symptom: AADJ device cannot SSO to on-prem share folder after initial join. Only works after rebooting (sometimes multiple reboots). Netmon shows no DC locator attempt.
rootCause: By design, AADJ device needs reboot after initial setup to cache domain info (DNS domain, NetBIOS, SAM account name from AAD Connect). Without reboot, no DC locator, falls back to NTLM.
solution: Reboot the AADJ device after initial join. By-design behavior. After reboot, device performs DC locator via DNS, obtains Kerberos TGT for SSO.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Windows Hello for business/Case study/Not able to SSO to on-premise Share folder from AA.md

symptom: msDS-KeyCredentialLink in on-prem AD keeps being written back by AAD Connect sync after manual deletion. Customer cannot access original Windows devices to remove WHfB enrollment.
rootCause: AAD Connect sync rule 'Out to AD - User NGCKey' writes Azure AD searchableDeviceKey to on-prem msDS-KeyCredentialLink. Deleting on-prem only is restored by next sync cycle.
solution: Delete source in Azure AD: 1) Verify WHfB via browser F12 checking windowsHelloForBusinessAuthenticationMethod. 2) DELETE via Graph API: https://microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn/beta/users/<USERID>/authentication/windowsHelloForBusinessMethods/<methodID>. Works in 21Vianet despite docs saying unsupported.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Windows Hello for business/Case study/Remove msDS-KeyCredentialLink_WHfB without accessi.md

symptom: WHFB PIN provisioning appears on AAD Registered (workplace joined) device, which is undocumented. Official docs only mention AAD Joined devices support WHFB. Convenience PIN vs WHFB PIN confusion on registered devices.
rootCause: When a Win10 machine is registered in Azure AD, the system prompts WHFB provisioning. Users can skip initial prompt but can manually set WHFB PIN via Settings. Convenience PIN uses password auth backend and will be deprecated.
solution: 1) WHFB on AAD Registered is by-design but not officially supported for accessing Azure resources. 2) To remove WHFB PIN: run 'certutil -deleteHelloContainer'. 3) Closing the WHFB blue window creates a convenience PIN instead (not recommended). 4) Supported deployment: AAD Joined devices for full WHFB.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Windows Hello for business/Case study/WHFB can work on AAD registered device.md

symptom: SMS-based primary authentication (SMS Sign-in) is not available in Mooncake.
rootCause: No plans to support SMS as primary authentication in Mooncake, per PG (anjusingh, March 2025).
solution: No plan to support. Use other auth methods.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/Microsoft Entra ID feature parity in Mooncake.md

symptom: Sign-in with email as alternate login ID not available in Mooncake. Being replaced by Alternate Sign-in Identifier (ASI).
rootCause: Current feature has security issues. PG replacing with ASI supporting email, employee IDs, usernames. ASI private preview CY25Q2 public cloud.
solution: ASI parity across clouds planned once GA (later CY25). Not clear if Mooncake can join private preview. Customer: Deloitte.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/Customer feature ask collection.md

symptom: Email one-time passcode (EOTP) is not delivered to B2B guest users whose account is not registered with MSA in Mooncake (21Vianet).
rootCause: In Mooncake, the EOTP flow relies on live.com (GetOneTimeCode.srf). If the B2B guest account is not an MSA account, the EOTP code will not be sent. This is a known feature gap (PBI 3362888).
solution: Verify whether the B2B guest user account is MSA-registered. If not, EOTP will not work. Track PBI 3362888. Use Kusto on estscnn2 cluster ESTS.DiagnosticTracesIfx to confirm failure.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/Email OTP does not support Non-MSA B2B account in.md

symptom: Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) using OTP method fails in Mooncake. User sees error when attempting password reset with only OTP method registered.
rootCause: SSPR OTP method is not supported in Mooncake. Known feature gap tracked in ICM 650180669 and PG backlog 3316633. No ETA.
solution: Inform customer SSPR OTP is not supported in Mooncake. Recommend alternative SSPR methods (e.g., mobile phone SMS). Track PG backlog 3316633.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/SSPR OTP is not supported in MC.md

symptom: Microsoft Authenticator passwordless phone sign-in and Certificate-Based Authentication (CBA) are not available in Mooncake (21Vianet).
rootCause: Prerequisite infrastructure for device-bound passkeys has not landed in China. FIDO2 ETA ~2027. Authenticator phone sign-in and CBA share same prerequisite.
solution: Features not available in Mooncake. Device-bound passkeys (FIDO2) rough ETA 2027. Recommend alternative auth methods (password + MFA via SMS/OATH token).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/Customer feature ask collection.md

symptom: Customer asks about SCIM provisioning availability for non-gallery/BYOA applications in Mooncake.
rootCause: SCIM provisioning via non-gallery app was a feature gap. As of Feb 2025, confirmed GA. Supported: Custom/BYOA/non-gallery app. Not yet: specific gallery apps. Outbound SCIM: CY25H2. Sync Fabric: CY25Q3.
solution: SCIM provisioning for non-gallery apps is GA in Mooncake (Feb 2025). For gallery apps (e.g., Databricks confirmed working), test directly. Track ADO 10155 for timeline.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/Customer feature ask collection.md

symptom: Number matching in Microsoft Authenticator push notification is not available in Mooncake (21Vianet).
rootCause: Feature gap in Mooncake. No ETA from PG. Related to case 2406180040001262.
solution: Not available in Mooncake. No ETA. Use SMS or OATH token for MFA verification as alternative.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/Feature tracking for MorganStanley.md

symptom: Temporary Access Pass (TAP) support in Azure portal UI is not available in Mooncake (21Vianet).
rootCause: TAP UI support is a feature gap in Mooncake. No ETA. Related to case 2404230040000986.
solution: TAP UI not available in Mooncake portal. No ETA. Explore PowerShell/Graph API alternatives if available.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/Feature tracking for MorganStanley.md

symptom: Pass-through Authentication (PTA) is not available in Mooncake. Cloud authentication only supports Password Hash Synchronization.
rootCause: PTA is a known feature gap. Feature parity shows Cloud Authentication as Partial. Seamless SSO works via PowerShell but not officially announced.
solution: Use Password Hash Sync (PHS) or Federated Auth (ADFS/3rd party). Seamless SSO configurable via PowerShell (public preview). Do not promise PTA availability.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Feature gap/Microsoft Entra ID feature parity in Mooncake.md

solution: Extend: portal.partner.microsoftonline.cn -> Billing -> Subscriptions -> support ticket to 21v Office commerce. Max 1yr/time, 3yr total. Purchase: contact PM Shawn Wang for web-direct whitelist -> Billing -> Purchase services -> free trial.

symptom: 21V tenant promo code redemption fails when the target tenant already has an Expired M365 subscription for the same offer
rootCause: 21V commerce portal blocks new promo code redemption if an Expired subscription for the same offer already exists on the tenant
solution: File ticket to 21V O365 commerce team to cancel the Expired subscription from backend (status changes to Disabled) → delete subscription via admin portal → verify no "You own this" mark on the offer in Purchase Services → redeem promo code with AliPay billing set to CNY 0.00. M365 E5 IP&G trial activation: portal.partner.microsoftonline.cn/Commerce/Trial.aspx

symptom: Cannot access Azure AD Support Wiki, permission denied
rootCause: User not member of Azure AD Wiki Entitlement group
solution: Request Reader role at coreidentity entitlement URL (auto-approved). Log out/in to refresh tokens.

symptom: AAD Connect export fails with Insufficient access rights for Exchange hybrid writeback attributes
rootCause: MSOL connector account missing Write Property on Exchange attrs for user/iNetOrgPerson/group/contact
solution: Grant permissions using dsacls for proxyAddresses, msExch* attrs on all object types.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Connector account] Insufficient access rights to.md
